They are equal in the basic principle of inheritance, even though they differ according to what Allah [Most High] has apportioned to each of them, depending on the connection by which each is linked to the deceased, whether by kinship, marriage, or wala' (bond of clientage/manumission). For that bond is a tie like the tie of blood kinship.

Ibn Marduyah related, by way of Ibn Harasah, from Sufyan al-Thawri, from Abdullah ibn Muhammad ibn Aqil, from Jabir, who said: Umm Kujjah came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said, "O Messenger of Allah, I have two daughters, and their father has died, and they have nothing." So Allah Most High revealed: {For men is a share of what parents and near relatives leave...} the verse. This hadith will come again at the two verses of inheritance in another wording. And Allah knows best.

Regarding His saying: {And when the division is attended [by relatives, orphans, and the poor, give them thereof and speak to them a kind word]}: it is said that the meaning is: when those relatives who are not heirs, along with orphans and the poor, are present at the division of an inheritance, then something of the estate should be given to them as a token portion, and that this was obligatory at the beginning of Islam. It is also said that it is merely recommended. They differed as to whether it is abrogated or not, on two views.

Al-Bukhari said: Ahmad ibn Humayd told us, Ubaydullah al-Ashja'i informed us, from Sufyan, from al-Shaybani, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas concerning {And when the division is attended by relatives, orphans, and the poor}, he said: it is muhkamah (a settled, unabrogated ruling), and it is not abrogated. Sa'id followed him in narrating this from Ibn Abbas.

Ibn Jarir said: al-Qasim told us, al-Husayn told us, Abbad ibn al-Awwam told us, from al-Hajjaj, from al-Hakam, from Miqsam, from Ibn Abbas, who said: it stands firm and is to be acted upon.

Al-Thawri said, from Ibn Abi Najih, from Mujahid, concerning this verse: it is obligatory upon the heirs, to the extent that their souls are content with. The same has been related from Ibn Mas'ud, Abu Musa, Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Bakr, Abu al-Aliyah, al-Sha'bi, al-Hasan, Ibn Sirin, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, Makhul, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, Ata' ibn Abi Rabah, al-Zuhri, and Yahya ibn Ya'mar: that it is obligatory.

Ibn Abi Hatim related from Abu Sa'id al-Ashajj, from Isma'il ibn Ulayyah, from Yunus ibn Ubayd, from Muhammad ibn Sirin, who said: Ubaydah oversaw an estate, and he ordered a sheep to be slaughtered, and he fed those entitled under this verse, and he said: were it not for this verse, this would have been from my own wealth.

Malik said, in what is related from him in tafsir gathered in a compiled portion, from al-Zuhri: that Urwah gave from the wealth of Mus'ab when he divided his estate. Al-Zuhri said: and it (the verse) is muhkamah.

Malik said, from Abd al-Karim, from Mujahid, who said: it is an obligatory right, to the extent that souls are content with it.

Mention of those who held that this is a command to make bequest (wasiyyah) for them:

Abd al-Razzaq said: Ibn Jurayj informed us, Ibn Abi Mulaykah informed me: that Asma bint Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Bakr al-Siddiq and al-Qasim ibn Muhammad informed him: that Abdullah ibn Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Bakr divided the inheritance of his father Abd al-Rahman while Aisha was still alive. They said: he left no poor person or relative in the household without giving him something from his father's inheritance. They said: and he recited {And when the division is attended by relatives...}. Al-Qasim said: I mentioned that to Ibn Abbas, and he said: he did not get it right, that is not what is meant by it, rather that refers to bequest, and this verse only concerns bequest, that is, the deceased is to add a bequest for them. Ibn Abi Hatim related it.

Mention of those who said that this verse is abrogated entirely:

Sufyan al-Thawri said, from Muhammad ibn al-Sa'ib al-Kalbi, from Abu Salih, from Ibn Abbas: {And when the division is attended...} he said: it is abrogated.

Isma'il ibn Muslim al-Makki said, from Qatadah, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas, that he said concerning this verse, {And when the division is attended by relatives...}: it was abrogated by the verse that comes after it, {Allah instructs you concerning your children...}.

Al-Awfi said, from Ibn Abbas concerning this verse, {And when the division is attended by relatives...}: that was before the verses of the fixed shares (fara'id) were revealed. Then Allah revealed after that the fara'id, and gave every rightful claimant his due, and so the charitable share was made dependent on what the deceased named in bequest. Ibn Marduyah related these.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: al-Hasan ibn Muhammad ibn al-Sabbah told us, Hajjaj told us, from Ibn Jurayj, and Uthman ibn Ata', from Ata', from Ibn Abbas, concerning His saying: {And when the division is attended by relatives, orphans, and the poor}: it was abrogated by the verse of inheritance, so that every person was given his share of what parents and near relatives leave, whether it be little or much, an obligatory share.

And he told us: Usayd ibn Asim told us, Sa'id ibn Amir told us, from Hammam, Qatadah told us, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, that he said: it is abrogated, it was before the fara'id. Whatever wealth a man left, the orphan, the poor, the destitute, and relatives who were present at the division would be given something from it. Then that was abrogated afterward, the verses of inheritance abrogated it, and Allah assigned to every rightful claimant his due, and bequest became a matter of one's own wealth, which one may will to whichever relatives one wishes.

Malik said, from al-Zuhri, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab: it is abrogated, the verses of inheritance and of bequest abrogated it.

The same has been related from Ikrimah, Abu al-Sha'tha, al-Qasim ibn Muhammad, Abu Salih, Abu Malik, Zayd ibn Aslam, al-Dahhak, Ata' al-Khurasani, Muqatil ibn Hayyan, and Rabi'ah ibn Abi Abd al-Rahman: that they said it is abrogated. This is the view of the majority of jurists and the four imams and their followers.

Ibn Jarir has chosen here a very strange, unusual view, the substance of which is: that the meaning of the verse according to him, {And when the division is attended}, means: when the relatives of the deceased are present at the division of the bequeathed wealth, {give them thereof and speak to them}, that is, to the orphans and the poor when they are present, {a kind word}. This is the gist of what he attempted after a lengthy and repetitive exposition, and there is question to be raised about it. And Allah knows best.

Al-Awfi said, from Ibn Abbas: {And when the division is attended}, that is, the division of inheritance. Several others said the same, and the meaning follows this rather than the path taken by Abu Ja'far ibn Jarir, may Allah have mercy on him. Rather the meaning is: that when these poor relatives who do not inherit, along with orphans and the poor, are present at the division of a substantial estate, their souls yearn for something of it, when they see this one taking and that one taking and another taking, while they themselves are left with no hope, receiving nothing. So Allah Most High, who is the Most Kind, the Most Merciful, commanded that a token portion from the middle range be given to them, as an act of kindness toward them, a charity upon them, a favor to them, and a consolation for their disappointment. As Allah Most High said: {Eat of its fruit when it yields fruit, and give its due on the day of its harvest} [al-An'am 6:141]. And He condemned those who move wealth secretly, fearing lest the needy and the destitute become aware of them, as He informed concerning the People of the Garden: {When they swore they would surely cut its fruit in the early morning} [al-Qalam 68:17], that is, by night. And He said: {So they set off, murmuring to one another: let no poor person enter it upon you today} [al-Qalam 68:23-24], {Allah destroyed them utterly, and the like awaits the disbelievers} [Muhammad 47:10]. So whoever denies Allah's due upon him, Allah punishes him in the very thing he holds dearest and most precious. For this reason it is stated in the hadith: "Charity never mixes with wealth without corrupting it," meaning: withholding it becomes the cause of the utter ruin of that wealth.

Regarding His saying: {And let those fear who, were they to leave behind them [weak offspring, would fear for them, let them fear Allah]}: Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas: this concerns a man on whom death has descended, and another man hears him making a bequest that would harm his heirs. Allah Most High commanded the one who hears this to fear Allah, and to guide and set him right toward what is correct, and to have regard for his heirs just as he himself would love to have his own heirs cared for, were he to fear neglect befalling them.

Mujahid and more than one other said the same. It is established in the two Sahihs that when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ entered upon Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas to visit him during his illness, Sa'd said: "O Messenger of Allah, I am a man of wealth and none will inherit from me except a daughter. Shall I give two thirds of my wealth in charity?" He said: "No." He said: "Then half?" He said: "No." He said: "Then a third?" He said: "A third, and a third is much." Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Indeed for you to leave your heirs wealthy is better than leaving them destitute, begging from people with open hands."

It is established in the Sahih that Ibn Abbas said: "If people had reduced from a third to a quarter, for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A third, and a third is much.'"

The jurists said: if the heirs of the deceased are wealthy, it is recommended for the deceased to make full use of the third in his bequest, and if they are poor, it is recommended that he reduce below the third.

It has also been said that what is meant by His saying, {And let those who, if they left behind them weak offspring, would fear for them, fear Allah}, [that is,] in handling the wealth of orphans, {and let them not consume it wastefully and hastily, fearing that they will grow up}.

Ibn Jarir related this from the route of al-Awfi from Ibn Abbas. It is a good statement, supported by what follows it, the warning against consuming the wealth of orphans unjustly. That is: just as you would love for your own offspring to be treated well after you, so treat other people's offspring when you are placed in charge of them.

Then He informs them that whoever consumes an orphan's wealth unjustly is only consuming fire in his belly. Hence He said: {Indeed, those who consume the wealth of orphans unjustly are only consuming fire in their bellies, and they will burn in a blazing fire}. That is: when they consume the wealth of orphans without right, they are only consuming a fire that will blaze in their bellies on the Day of Resurrection.

It is established in the two Sahihs, from the hadith of Sulayman ibn Bilal, from Thawr ibn Zayd, from Salim Abu al-Ghayth, from Abu Hurayrah, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Avoid the seven destructive sins." It was said: "O Messenger of Allah, what are they?" He said: "Associating partners with Allah, sorcery, killing a soul whom Allah has forbidden to be killed except by right, consuming usury, consuming the wealth of an orphan, fleeing from battle, and slandering chaste believing women who are heedless [of accusation]."

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Abu Sa'id al-Khudri said: We said: "O Messenger of Allah, what did you see on the night you were taken on the Night Journey?" He said: "I was taken to a great multitude of Allah's creation, men, each of them having two lips like the lips of a camel, and men were appointed over them who would pry open the lips of each of them, then a rock of fire would be brought and thrown into the mouth of each of them until it emerged from below him, and they had a bellowing and screaming. I said: 'O Gabriel, who are these?' He said: 'These are those who consume the wealth of orphans unjustly; they are only consuming fire in their bellies, and they will burn in a blazing fire.'"

Al-Suddi said: the one who consumes the wealth of an orphan will be raised on the Day of Resurrection with the flame of fire coming out of his mouth, his ears, his nose, and his eyes, so that whoever sees him will recognize him as one who consumed the wealth of an orphan.

Abu Bakr ibn Mardawayh said: [chain] from Abu Barzah, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "On the Day of Resurrection a people will be raised from their graves with their mouths blazing with fire." It was said: "O Messenger of Allah, who are they?" He said: "Have you not seen that Allah has said: {Indeed, those who consume the wealth of orphans unjustly [are only consuming fire in their bellies]}," the verse.

Ibn Abi Hatim related it from Abu Zur'ah, from Uqbah ibn Mukram, and Abu Hatim ibn Hibban brought it out in his Sahih, from Ahmad ibn Ali ibn al-Muthanna, from Uqbah ibn Mukram.

Ibn Mardawayh said: [chain] from Abu Hurayrah, who said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "I declare forbidden the wealth of the two weak ones: the woman and the orphan," meaning, I charge you to avoid [wrongfully taking] their wealth.

It was mentioned previously in Surat al-Baqarah, by the route of Ata' ibn al-Sa'ib, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas, who said: when Allah revealed {Indeed, those who consume the wealth of orphans unjustly [are only consuming fire in their bellies]}, whoever had an orphan in his care went and separated the orphan's food from his own food, and his drink from his own drink, and would set aside anything extra and keep it for him until he ate it or it spoiled. This became a great burden for them, so they mentioned this to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and Allah revealed: {And they ask you about orphans. Say: setting things right for them is good. [And if you mix your affairs with theirs, they are your brothers. And Allah knows the one who corrupts from the one who sets right]} [al-Baqarah: 220].

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

قَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ وَقَتَادَةُ: كَانَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ يَجْعَلُونَ الْمَالَ لِلرِّجَالِ الْكِبَارِ، وَلَا يُوَرِّثُونَ النِّسَاءَ وَلَا الْأَطْفَالَ شَيْئًا، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿لِلرِّجَالِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوَالِدَانِ وَالأقْرَبُونَ [وَلِلنِّسَاءِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوَالِدَانِ وَالأقْرَبُونَ مِمَّا قَلَّ مِنْهُ أَوْ كَثُرَ نَصِيبًا مَفْرُوضًا] ﴾ أَيِ: الْجَمِيعُ فِيهِ سَوَاءٌ فِي حُكْمِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى، يَسْتَوُونَ فِي أَصْلِ الْوِرَاثَةِ وَإِنْ تَفَاوَتُوا بِحَسَبِ مَا فَرَضَ اللَّهُ [تَعَالَى] لِكُلٍّ مِنْهُمْ، بِمَا يُدْلِي بِهِ إِلَى الْمَيِّتِ مِنْ قَرَابَةٍ، أَوْ زَوْجِيَّةٍ، أَوْ وَلَاءٍ. فَإِنَّهُ لُحْمَة كَلُحمة النَّسَبِ. وَقَدْ رَوَى ابْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ مِنْ طَرِيقِ ابْنِ هَرَاسة عَنْ سُفْيَانَ الثَّوْرِيِّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَقِيلٍ، عن جابر قَالَ: جَاءَتْ أُمُّ كُجَّة إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَقَالَتْ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنَّ لِيَ ابْنَتَيْنِ، وَقَدْ مَاتَ أَبُوهُمَا، وَلَيْسَ لَهُمَا شَيْءٌ، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿لِلرِّجَالِ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ الْوَالِدَانِ وَالأقْرَبُونَ﴾ الْآيَةَ، وَسَيَأْتِي هَذَا الحديثُ عِنْدَ آيَتَيِ الْمِيرَاثِ بِسِيَاقٍ آخَرَ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَإِذَا حَضَرَ الْقِسْمَةَ [أُولُو الْقُرْبَى وَالْيَتَامَى وَالْمَسَاكِينُ فَارْزُقُوهُمْ مِنْهُ وَقُولُوا لَهُمْ قَوْلا مَعْرُوفًا] ﴾ قِيلَ: الْمُرَادُ: وَإِذَا حَضَرَ قِسْمَةَ الْمِيرَاثِ ذَوُو الْقُرْبَى مِمَّنْ لَيْسَ بِوَارِثٍ وَالْيَتَامَى وَالْمَسَاكِينُ فَلْيَرْضَخْ لَهُمْ مِنَ التَّرِكَةِ نَصِيبٌ، وَأَنَّ ذَلِكَ كَانَ وَاجِبًا فِي ابْتِدَاءِ الْإِسْلَامِ. وَقِيلَ: يُسْتَحَبُّ وَاخْتَلَفُوا: هَلْ هُوَ مَنْسُوخٌ أَمْ لَا؟ عَلَى قَوْلَيْنِ، فَقَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ حُمَيد أَخْبَرَنَا عُبَيدُ اللَّهِ الْأَشْجَعِيُّ، عَنْ سُفْيان، عَنِ الشَّيْباني، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿وَإِذَا حَضَرَ الْقِسْمَةَ أُولُوا الْقُرْبَى وَالْيَتَامَى وَالْمَسَاكِينُ﴾ قَالَ: هِيَ مُحْكَمَة، وَلَيْسَتْ بِمَنْسُوخَةٍ. تَابَعَهُ سَعيد عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا الْقَاسِمُ، حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَيْنُ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّاد بْنُ العَوَّام، عَنِ الْحَجَّاجِ، عَنِ الحَكَم، عَنْ مِقْسم، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: هِيَ قَائِمَةٌ يَعْمَلُ بِهَا.

وَقَالَ الثَّوْرِيُّ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيح، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ فِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ، قَالَ: هِيَ وَاجِبَةٌ عَلَى أَهْلِ الْمِيرَاثِ، مَا طَابَتْ بِهِ أَنْفُسُهُمْ. وَهَكَذَا رُوِيَ عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، وَأَبِي مُوسَى، وَعَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ، وَأَبِي الْعَالِيَةِ، وَالشَّعْبِيِّ، وَالْحَسَنِ، وَابْنِ سِيرِينَ، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَير، وَمَكْحُولٍ، وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ النَّخَعي، وَعَطَاءِ بْنِ أَبِي رَبَاحٍ، وَالزُّهْرِيِّ، وَيَحْيَى بْنِ يَعْمَرَ: إِنَّهَا وَاجِبَةٌ.

وَرَوَى ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْأَشَجِّ، عَنِ اسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ بْنِ عُبَيد، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِيرِينَ قَالَ: وَلِيَ عُبَيْدَةُ وَصِيَّةً، فَأَمَرَ بِشَاةٍ فَذُبِحَتْ، فَأَطْعَمَ أَصْحَابَ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ، وَقَالَ: لَوْلَا هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ لَكَانَ هَذَا مِنْ مَالِي.

وَقَالَ مَالِكٌ، فِيمَا يُرْوَى عَنْهُ مِنَ التَّفْسِيرِ فِي جُزْءٍ مَجْمُوعٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ: أَنَّ عُرْوَةَ أعْطى مِنْ مَالِ مُصْعَبٍ حِينَ قَسَّمَ مَالَهُ. وَقَالَ الزُّهْرِيُّ: وَهِيَ مُحْكَمَةٌ.

وَقَالَ مَالِكٌ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: هُوَ حَقٌّ وَاجِبٌ مَا طَابَتْ بِهِ الْأَنْفُسُ.

ذِكْرُ مَنْ ذَهَبَ إِلَى أَنَّ ذَلِكَ أَمْرٌ بِالْوَصِيَّةِ لَهُمْ:

قَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ: أَخْبَرَنَا ابْنُ جُرَيج أَخْبَرَنِي ابْنُ أَبَى مُلَيكة: أَنَّ أَسْمَاءَ بِنْتَ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ الصِّدِّيقِ، وَالْقَاسِمَ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ أَخْبَرَاهُ: أَنَّ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ قَسَّمَ مِيرَاثَ أَبِيهِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ وَعَائِشَةُ حَية قَالَا فَلَمْ يَدَعْ فِي الدَّارِ مِسْكِينًا وَلَا ذَا قَرَابَةٍ إِلَّا أَعْطَاهُ مِنْ مِيرَاثِ أَبِيهِ. قَالَا وَتَلَا ﴿وَإِذَا حَضَرَ الْقِسْمَةَ أُولُوا الْقُرْبَى﴾ قَالَ الْقَاسِمُ: فَذَكَرْتُ ذَلِكَ لِابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فَقَالَ: مَا أَصَابَ، لَيْسَ ذَلِكَ لَهُ، إِنَّمَا ذَلِكَ إِلَى الْوَصِيَّةِ، وَإِنَّمَا هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ فِي الْوَصِيَّةِ يزيد الْمَيِّتُ [أَنْ] يُوصِي لَهُمْ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ .

ذِكْرُ مَنْ قَالَ: إِنَّ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ مَنْسُوخَةٌ بِالْكُلِّيَّةِ:

قَالَ سُفْيَانُ الثَّوْرِيُّ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنُ السَّائِبِ الْكَلْبِيُّ، عَنْ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، عن ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿وَإِذَا حَضَرَ الْقِسْمَةَ﴾ قَالَ: مَنْسُوخَةٌ.

وَقَالَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ الْمَكِّيُّ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ فِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: ﴿وَإِذَا حَضَرَ الْقِسْمَةَ أُولُوا الْقُرْبَى﴾ نَسَخَتْهَا الْآيَةُ الَّتِي بَعْدَهَا: ﴿يُوصِيكُمُ اللَّهُ فِي أَوْلادِكُمْ﴾

وَقَالَ العَوْفي، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاس فِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: ﴿وَإِذَا حَضَرَ الْقِسْمَةَ أُولُوا الْقُرْبَى﴾ كَانَ ذَلِكَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَنزل الْفَرَائِضُ، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ الْفَرَائِضَ، فَأَعْطَى كُلَّ ذِي حَقِّ حَقَّهُ، فَجُعِلَتِ الصَّدَقَةُ فِيمَا سَمى الْمُتَوَفَّى. رَوَاهُنَّ ابْنُ مَرْدُويه.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الصبَّاح، حَدَّثَنَا حَجَّاج، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيج وَعُثْمَانُ بْنُ عَطَاءٍ عَنْ عَطاء، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَإِذَا حَضَرَ الْقِسْمَةَ أُولُوا الْقُرْبَى وَالْيَتَامَى وَالْمَسَاكِينُ﴾

نَسَخَتْهَا آيَةُ الْمِيرَاثِ، فَجُعِلَ لِكُلِّ إِنْسَانٍ نُصِيبُهُ مِمَّا تَرك الْوَالِدَانِ وَالْأَقْرَبُونَ -مِمَّا قَلَّ مِنْهُ أَوْ كَثُرَ -[نَصِيبًا مَفْرُوضًا] 

وَحَدَّثَنَا أُسَيْدُ بْنُ عَاصِمٍ، حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ عَامِرٍ، عَنْ هَمَّامٍ، حَدَّثَنَا قَتَادَةُ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: إِنَّهَا مَنْسُوخَةٌ، كَانَتْ قَبْلَ الْفَرَائِضِ، كَانَ مَا تَرَكَ الرَّجُلُ مِنْ مَالٍ أُعْطِيَ مِنْهُ الْيَتِيمُ وَالْفَقِيرُ وَالْمِسْكِينُ وَذَوِي الْقُرْبَى إِذَا حَضروا الْقِسْمَةَ، ثُمَّ نُسِخَ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ، نَسَخَتْهَا الْمَوَارِيثُ، فَأَلْحَقَ اللَّهُ بِكُلِّ ذِي حَق حَقَّهُ، وَصَارَتِ الْوَصِيَّةُ مِنْ مَالِهِ، يُوصِي بِهَا لِذَوِي قَرَابَتِهِ حَيْثُ يَشَاءُ.

وَقَالَ مَالِكٌ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ: هِيَ مَنْسُوخَةٌ، نَسَخَتْهَا الْمَوَارِيثُ وَالْوَصِيَّةُ.

وَهَكَذَا رُوِيَ عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، وَأَبِي الشَّعْثَاءِ، وَالْقَاسِمِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ، وَأَبِي صَالِحٍ، وَأَبِي مَالِكٍ، وزيد ابن أَسْلَمَ، وَالضَّحَّاكِ، وَعَطَاءٍ الْخُرَاسَانِيِّ، وَمُقَاتِلِ بْنِ حَيَّان، وَرَبِيعَةَ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ: أَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا: إِنَّهَا مَنْسُوخَةٌ. وَهَذَا مَذْهَبُ جُمْهور الْفُقَهَاءِ وَالْأَئِمَّةِ الْأَرْبَعَةِ وَأَصْحَابِهِمْ.

وَقَدِ اخْتَارَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ هَاهُنَا قَوْلًا غَرِيبًا جَدًا، وَحَاصِلُهُ: أَنَّ مَعْنَى الْآيَةِ عِنْدَهُ ﴿وَإِذَا حَضَرَ الْقِسْمَةَ﴾ أَيْ: وَإِذَا حَضَرَ قِسْمَةَ مَالِ الْوَصِيَّةِ أُولُو قُرَابَةِ الْمَيِّتِ ﴿فَارْزُقُوهُمْ مِنْهُ وَقُولُوا لَهُمْ﴾ لِلْيَتَامَى وَالْمَسَاكِينِ إِذَا حَضَرُوا ﴿قَوْلا مَعْرُوفًا﴾ هَذَا مَضْمُونُ مَا حَاوَلَهُ بَعْدَ طُول الْعِبَارَةِ وَالتَّكْرَارِ، وَفِيهِ نَظَرٌ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَقَدْ قَالَ العَوْفي عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿وَإِذَا حَضَرَ الْقِسْمَةَ﴾ وَهِيَ قِسْمَةُ الْمِيرَاثِ. وَهَكَذَا قَالَ غَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ، وَالْمَعْنَى عَلَى هَذَا لَا عَلَى مَا سَلَكَهُ أَبُو جَعْفَرِ بْنِ جَرِيرٍ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ، بَلِ الْمَعْنَى: أَنَّهُ إِذَا حَضَرَ هَؤُلَاءِ الْفُقَرَاءُ مِنَ الْقَرَابَةِ الَّذِينَ لَا يَرثون، وَالْيَتَامَى وَالْمَسَاكِينُ قِسْمَةَ مَالٍ جَزِيلٍ، فَإِنَّ أَنْفُسَهُمْ تَتُوقُ إِلَى شَيْءٍ مِنْهُ، إِذَا رَأَوْا هَذَا يَأْخُذُ وَهَذَا يَأْخُذُ وَهَذَا يَأْخُذُ، وَهُمْ يَائِسُونَ لَا شَيْءَ يُعْطَوْنَ، فَأَمَرَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى -وَهُوَ الرَّءُوفُ الرَّحِيمُ -أَنْ يُرضَخ لَهُمْ شَيْءٌ مِنَ الوسَط يَكُونُ بِرًّا بِهِمْ وَصَدَقَةً عَلَيْهِمْ، وَإِحْسَانًا إِلَيْهِمْ، وَجَبْرًا لِكَسْرِهِمْ. كَمَا قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿كُلُوا مِنْ ثَمَرِهِ إِذَا أَثْمَرَ وَآتُوا حَقَّهُ يَوْمَ حَصَادِهِ﴾ [الْأَنْعَامِ:١٤١] وَذَمَّ الَّذِينَ يَنْقُلُونَ الْمَالَ خِفْيَةً؛ خَشْيَةَ أَنْ يَطَّلِعَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْمَحَاوِيجُ وَذَوُو الْفَاقَةِ، كَمَا أَخْبَرَ عَنِ أَصْحَابِ الْجَنَّةِ ﴿إِذْ أَقْسَمُوا لَيَصْرِمُنَّهَا مُصْبِحِينَ﴾ [الْقَلَمِ:١٧] أَيْ: بِلَيْلٍ. وَقَالَ: ﴿فَانْطَلَقُوا وَهُمْ يَتَخَافَتُونَ. أَنْ لَا يَدْخُلَنَّهَا الْيَوْمَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِسْكِينٌ﴾ [الْقَلَمِ:٢٣، ٢٤] ﴿دَمَّرَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلِلْكَافِرِينَ أَمْثَالُهَا﴾ [مُحَمَّدٍ:١٠] فَمَنْ جَحَد حَقَّ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ عَاقَبَهُ فِي أَعَزِّ مَا يَمْلِكُهُ؛ وَلِهَذَا جَاءَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ: "مَا خَالَطَتِ الصَّدَقَةُ مَالًا إِلَّا أَفْسَدَتْهُ" أَيْ: مَنْعُهَا يَكُونُ سَبَبَ مِحَاقِ ذَلِكَ الْمَالِ بالكلية.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلْيَخْشَ الَّذِينَ لَوْ تَرَكُوا مِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ [ذُرِّيَّةً ضِعَافًا خَافُوا عَلَيْهِمْ فَلْيَتَّقُوا اللَّهَ] ﴾ قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عباس: هَذَا فِي الرَّجُلِ يَحْضُره الْمَوْتُ، فَيَسْمَعُهُ الرَّجُلُ يُوصِي بِوَصِيَّةٍ تَضر بِوَرَثَتِهِ، فَأَمَرَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى الَّذِي يَسْمَعُهُ أَنْ يَتَّقِيَ اللَّهَ، وَيُوَفِّقَهُ وَيُسَدِّدَهُ لِلصَّوَابِ، وَلْيُنْظَرْ لِوَرَثَتِهِ كَمَا كَانَ يُحِبُّ أَنْ يُصْنَعَ بِوَرَثَتِهِ إِذَا خَشِيَ عَلَيْهِمُ الضَّيْعَةَ.

وَهَكَذَا قَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ وَغَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ، وَثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى الله عليه وسلم لَمَّا دَخَلَ عَلَى سَعْد بْنِ أَبِي وَقَاصٍّ يَعُودُهُ قَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنِّي ذُو مَالٍ وَلَا يَرِثُنِي إِلَّا ابْنَةً، أَفَأَتَصَدَّقُ بِثُلُثَيْ مَالِي؟ قَالَ: "لَا". قَالَ: فالشَّطْر؟ قَالَ: "لَا". قَالَ: فَالثُّلُثُ؟ قَالَ: "الثُّلُثُ، وَالثُّلُثُ كَثِيرٌ". ثُمَّ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إنك أن تَذر وَرَثَتَك أغنياء خَيْر من أَنْ تَذَرَهم عَالةً يتكَفَّفُون النَّاسَ" .

وَفِي الصَّحِيحِ أَنَّ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: لَوْ أَنَّ النَّاسَ غَضّوا مِنَ الثُّلُثِ إِلَى الرُّبُعِ فَإِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "الثُّلُثُ، وَالثُّلُثُ كَثِيرٌ" .

قَالَ الْفُقَهَاءُ: إِنْ كَانَ وَرَثَةُ الْمَيِّتِ أَغْنِيَاءَ استُحب لِلْمَيِّتِ أَنْ يَسْتَوفي الثُّلُثَ فِي وَصِيَّتِهِ وَإِنْ كَانُوا فَقُرَاءَ استُحب أَنْ يَنْقُص الثُّلُثَ.

وَقِيلَ: الْمُرَادُ بِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَلْيَخْشَ الَّذِينَ لَوْ تَرَكُوا مِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّةً ضِعَافًا خَافُوا عَلَيْهِمْ فَلْيَتَّقُوا اللَّهَ﴾ [أَيْ] فِي مُبَاشَرَةِ أَمْوَالِ الْيَتَامَى ﴿وَلا تَأْكُلُوهَا إِسْرَافًا وَبِدَارًا أَنْ يَكْبَرُوا﴾

حَكَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ مِنْ طَرِيقِ العَوْفي، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: وَهُوَ قَوْلٌ حَسَنٌ، يَتَأَيَّدُ بِمَا بَعْدَهُ مِنَ التَّهْدِيدِ فِي أَكْلِ مَالِ الْيَتَامَى ظُلْمًا، أَيْ: كَمَا تُحِبُّ أَنْ تُعَامَلَ ذَرِّيَّتُكَ مِنْ بَعْدِكَ، فَعَامِلِ النَّاسَ فِي ذُرِّيَّاتِهِمْ إِذَا وَلِيتَهُمْ.

ثُمَّ أَعْلِمْهُمْ أَنَّ مَنْ أَكَلَ مَالَ يَتِيمٍ ظُلْمًا فَإِنَّمَا يَأْكُلُ فِي بَطْنِهِ نَارًا؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوَالَ الْيَتَامَى ظُلْمًا إِنَّمَا يَأْكُلُونَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ نَارًا وَسَيَصْلَوْنَ سَعِيرًا﴾ أَيْ: إِذَا أَكَلُوا أَمْوَالَ الْيَتَامَى بِلَا سَبَبٍ، فَإِنَّمَا يَأْكُلُونَ نَارًا تَأجَّج فِي بُطُونِهِمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ. وَثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ مِنْ حَدِيثِ سليمان ابن بِلَالٍ، عَنْ ثَوْر بْنِ زَيْدٍ عَنْ سَالِمٍ أَبِي الغَيْث، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "اجْتَنبوا السَّبْعَ الْمُوبِقَاتِ" قِيلَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، وَمَا هُنَّ؟ قَالَ: "الشِّرْكُ بِاللَّهِ، والسِّحْر، وَقَتْلُ النَّفْسِ الَّتِي حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ وَأَكْلُ الرِّبَا، وَأَكْلُ مَالِ الْيَتِيمِ، والتولِّي يَوْمَ الزَّحْفِ، وقَذْفُ الْمُحْصَنَاتِ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ الْغَافِلَاتِ".

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدَةُ أَخْبَرَنَا أَبُو عَبْدِ الصَّمَدِ عَبْدُ الْعَزِيزِ بْنُ عَبْدِ الصَّمَدِ العمِّى، حدثنا أبو هاروي العَبْدي عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ قَالَ: قُلْنَا: يا رسول الله، ما رأيت لَيْلَةَ أُسَرِيَ بِكَ؟ قَالَ: "انطَلَق بِي إِلَى خَلْقٍ مِنْ خَلْقِ اللَّهِ كَثِيرٍ، رِجَال، كُلُّ رَجُلٍ لَهُ مِشْفَران كَمِشْفَرَيِ الْبَعِيرِ، وَهُوَ موَكَّل بِهِمْ رِجَالٌ يَفُكُّونَ لِحَاءَ أَحَدِهِمْ، ثُمَّ يُجَاءُ بِصَخْرَةٍ مِنْ نَارٍ فَتُقْذَف فِي فِي أَحَدِهِمْ حَتَّى يَخْرُجَ مِنْ أَسْفَلِهِ وَلَهُمْ خُوار وصُرَاخ. قُلْتُ يَا جِبْرِيلُ، مَنْ هَؤُلَاءِ؟ قَالَ: هَؤُلَاءِ الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوَالَ الْيَتَامَى ظُلْمًا إِنَّمَا يَأْكُلُونَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ نَارًا وسَيَصْلَوْن سَعِيرًا" .

وَقَالَ السُّدِّيُّ: يُبْعَثُ آكِلُ مَالِ الْيَتِيمِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَلَهَبُ النَّارِ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ فِيهِ وَمِنْ مَسَامِعِهِ وَأَنْفِهِ وَعَيْنَيْهِ، يَعْرِفُهُ مَنْ رَآهُ بِأَكْلِ مَالِ الْيَتِيمِ.

وَقَالَ أَبُو بَكْرِ ابْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عَمْرٍو، حَدَّثَنَا عُقْبَةُ بْنُ مُكْرَمٍ، حَدَّثَنَا يُونُسُ بْنُ بُكَير، حَدَّثَنَا زِيَادُ بْنُ الْمُنْذِرِ، عَنْ نَافِعِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ عَنْ أَبِي بَرْزَةَ؛ أَنَّ رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم قال: "يُبْعَثُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ الْقَوْمُ مِنْ قُبُورِهِمْ تَأَجَّج أَفْوَاهُهُمْ نَارًا" قِيلَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، مَنْ هُمْ؟ قَالَ: "أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ اللَّهَ قَالَ: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوَالَ الْيَتَامَى ظُلْمًا [إِنَّمَا يَأْكُلُونَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ نَارًا] ﴾ الْآيَةَ.

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي زُرْعة، عَنْ عُقْبة بْنِ مُكْرَمٍ وَأَخْرَجَهُ أَبُو حَاتِمِ بْنُ حِبَّانَ فِي صَحِيحِهِ، عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْمُثَنَّى، عَنْ عُقْبَةَ بْنِ مُكْرَمٍ .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ مَردويه: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جعفر، أحمد بْنُ عِصَامٍ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَامِرٍ الْعَبْدِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ الزُّهْرِيُّ، عَنْ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنِ المقبرِيّ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "أُحَرِّجُ مَالَ الضَّعِيفيْن: الْمَرْأَةِ وَالْيَتِيمِ" أَيْ أُوصِيكُمْ بِاجْتِنَابِ مَالِهِمَا.

وَتَقَدَّمَ فِي سُورَةِ الْبَقَرَةِ مِنْ طَرِيقِ عَطَاءِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بن جبير، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: لَمَّا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوَالَ الْيَتَامَى ظُلْمًا [إِنَّمَا يَأْكُلُونَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ نَارًا] ﴾ انْطَلَقَ مَنْ كَانَ عِنْدَهُ يَتِيمٌ، فَعَزَل طَعَامَهُ مِنْ طَعَامِهِ، وَشَرَابَهُ مِنْ شَرَابِهِ، فَجَعَلَ يُفْضِلُ الشَّيْءَ فَيُحْبَس لَهُ حَتَّى يَأْكُلَهُ أَوْ يَفْسَدَ فَاشْتَدَّ ذَلِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ، فَذَكَرُوا ذَلِكَ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿وَيَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْيَتَامَى قُلْ إِصْلاحٌ لَهُمْ خَيْرٌ [وَإِنْ تُخَالِطُوهُمْ فَإِخْوَانُكُمْ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ الْمُفْسِدَ مِنَ الْمُصْلِحِ] ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ٢٢٠] .

---

## 4:11 — verse 11

### Translation

This noble verse, and the one following it, and the verse that is the closing verse of this surah, are the verses of the science of al-Fara'id (the science of inheritance shares). This science is derived from these three verses and from the hadiths transmitted concerning that matter, which serve as an explanation of them. We shall mention here what pertains to the explanation of that. As for the establishment of the detailed rulings, the presentation of differences and proofs, and the argumentation among the imams, its proper place is the book of "al-Ahkam" (Rulings). God is the One from whom help is sought.

Encouragement has been transmitted regarding learning the fara'id, and these specific shares are among the most important of that matter. Abu Dawud and Ibn Majah related, from the hadith of Abd al-Rahman ibn Ziyad ibn An'um al-Ifriqi, from Abd al-Rahman ibn Rafi' al-Tanukhi, from Abdullah ibn Amr, may God be pleased with him, that the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Knowledge is three things, and whatever is beyond that is surplus: a clear verse, an established sunnah, or a just [prescribed] share of inheritance."

And from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "O Abu Hurayrah, learn the fara'id and teach it, for it is half of knowledge, and it is forgotten, and it is the first thing to be taken away from my nation."

Ibn Majah related it, and there is weakness in its chain.

It has also been narrated from the hadith of Abdullah ibn Mas'ud and Abu Sa'id, though there is question regarding each of them. [Sufyan] ibn Uyaynah said: He only named the fara'id "half of knowledge" because all people are tested by it.

Al-Bukhari said, in the course of explaining this verse: [chain] Ibrahim ibn Musa told us, Hisham told us, that Ibn Jurayj informed them, saying: Ibn al-Munkadir informed me, from Jabir ibn Abdullah, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ and Abu Bakr came to visit me, walking, in Banu Salamah, and the Prophet ﷺ found me unconscious. He called for water and performed ablution with it, then sprinkled it on me, and I regained consciousness. I said: "What do you command me to do with my wealth, O Messenger of God?" Then was revealed: {God instructs you concerning your children: for the male, the equivalent of the share of two females.}

Muslim and al-Nasa'i likewise related it, from the hadith of Hajjaj ibn Muhammad al-A'war, from Ibn Jurayj with the same content. And the whole group [of hadith compilers] related it from the hadith of Sufyan ibn Uyaynah, from Muhammad ibn al-Munkadir, from Jabir.

Another hadith from Jabir concerning the occasion of the revelation of the verse: Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Zakariyya ibn Adi told us, Ubaydullah, that is, Ibn Amr al-Raqqi, told us, from Abdullah ibn Muhammad ibn Aqil, from Jabir, who said: The wife of Sa'd ibn al-Rabi' came to the Messenger of God ﷺ and said: "O Messenger of God, these are the two daughters of Sa'd ibn al-Rabi'. Their father was killed alongside you at Uhud as a martyr, and their uncle has taken their wealth, leaving them nothing, and they cannot be married unless they have wealth." He said: "God will decide concerning that." He said: Then the verse of inheritance was revealed, and the Messenger of God ﷺ sent word to their uncle, saying: "Give the two daughters of Sa'd two thirds, and their mother an eighth, and whatever remains is yours."

Abu Dawud, al-Tirmidhi, and Ibn Majah related it through various routes, from Abdullah ibn Muhammad ibn Uqayl, with the same content. Al-Tirmidhi said: It is not known except through his hadith.

It appears that the first hadith of Jabir was actually the occasion for the revelation of the last verse of this surah, as will come later, for at that time he had sisters and no daughters, and he was inherited from as one having no direct heirs (kalalah). But we have mentioned the hadith here in following al-Bukhari, may God have mercy on him, since he mentioned it here. The second hadith from Jabir is more fitting as the occasion for the revelation of this verse, and God knows best.

As for His saying, exalted is He: {God instructs you concerning your children: for the male, the equivalent of the share of two females}, meaning: He commands you to deal justly with them. The people of the Jahiliyyah (pre-Islamic era) used to give the entire inheritance to the males, excluding the females. So God, exalted is He, commanded equality between them in the basic right to inherit, while differentiating between the two categories, giving the male the equivalent of the share of two females. This is because of the man's need for the expense of maintenance, the burden, the toil of trade and earning a livelihood, and the bearing of hardship, so it was fitting that he be given twice what the female takes.

Some of the perceptive scholars have derived from His saying, exalted is He: {God instructs you concerning your children: for the male, the equivalent of the share of two females}, that He, exalted is He, is more merciful to His creation than a parent is to his child, since He instructed the parents concerning their children, from which it is known that He is more merciful to them than they are, as has come in the authentic hadith.

He saw a woman among the captives searching anxiously for her child, and when she found him, she took him and pressed him to her chest and suckled him. The Messenger of God ﷺ said to his companions: "Do you think this woman would cast her child into the fire while she is able to prevent it?" They said: "No, O Messenger of God." He said: "Then by God, God is more merciful to His servants than this woman is to her child."

Al-Bukhari said here: [chain] Muhammad ibn Yusuf told us, from Warqa', from Ibn Abi Najih, from Ata', from Ibn Abbas, who said: The wealth used to belong to the child, and the bequest used to be for the parents, then God abrogated of that what He willed, and made for the male the equivalent of the share of two females, and made for the two parents, for each one of them, a sixth or a third, and made for the wife an eighth or a fourth, and for the husband a half or a fourth.

Al-Awfi said, from Ibn Abbas, concerning His saying: {God instructs you concerning your children: for the male, the equivalent of the share of two females}: This was because when the shares of inheritance were revealed, in which God prescribed what He prescribed for the male child, the female child, and the two parents, the people, or some of them, disliked it and said: "The woman is given a fourth or an eighth, while the daughter is given a half, and the young boy is given [a share], yet none of these can fight the enemy nor take possession of the spoils. Keep quiet about this matter; perhaps the Messenger of God ﷺ will forget it, or we will speak to him and he will change it." Some of them said: "O Messenger of God, shall we give the girl half of what her father left, when she does not ride a horse nor fight the enemy, and give the boy an inheritance when he is of no use at all?" They used to do that in the Jahiliyyah, giving inheritance only to one who fought the enemy, and giving it to the eldest after the eldest. Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Jarir also related this.

As for His saying: {But if there are more than two women, then for them two thirds of what he left}, some people said: the word {fawqa} (more than) is superfluous, and the meaning is: "if there are two women," as in His saying, exalted is He: {Then strike above the necks} [al-Anfal: 12]. This is not accepted, neither here nor there, for there is nothing in the Qur'an that is superfluous without benefit; that is impossible. Moreover, His saying {then for them two thirds of what he left}, if the intended meaning were what they claimed, He would have said "then for them both [dual] two thirds of what he left." Rather, the fact that two thirds belong to two daughters is derived from the ruling concerning two sisters in the final verse [of this surah], for He, exalted is He, ruled therein that two sisters receive two thirds. And since two sisters inherit two thirds, then it follows all the more that two daughters inherit two thirds. It has already been mentioned in the hadith of Jabir that the Messenger of God ﷺ ruled that the two daughters of Sa'd ibn al-Rabi' receive two thirds. Thus the Book and the Sunnah both indicate this. Furthermore, He said: {And if there is only one, then for her is half}. If two daughters were also to receive half, He would have stated it explicitly. Since He ruled for the single daughter alone, this indicates that two daughters fall under the ruling of three or more. And God knows best.

Regarding His saying: {And for his parents, for each one of them a sixth of what he left if he has a child. But if he has no child and his parents inherit from him, then for his mother a third. But if he has brothers, then for his mother a sixth} and so on to the end: the two parents have several conditions regarding inheritance:

The first: that they come together with children [of the deceased], in which case a sixth is prescribed for each one of them. If the deceased has only one daughter, she is prescribed a half, and for the two parents, each one of them a sixth, and the father takes the remaining sixth by way of residuary inheritance (ta'sib). Thus, in this situation, he combines a prescribed share with residuary inheritance.

The second condition: that the two parents inherit alone [without children or spouse], in which case, in this situation, a third is prescribed for the mother, and the father takes the remainder by pure residuary inheritance, thereby taking twice what was prescribed for the mother, which is two thirds. But if, in this situation, there were with them a husband or a wife, the husband would take a half and the wife a fourth. Then the scholars differed as to what the mother takes after the prescribed share of the husband or wife, into three opinions:

The first: that she takes a third of the remainder in both cases, because the remainder is, as it were, the entire inheritance in relation to the two of them [the parents].

Allah has made her share half of what He made for the father, so she takes a third of what remains and he takes two thirds of it. This is the position of Umar and Uthman, and it is the more correct of the two reports from Ali. Ibn Mas'ud and Zayd ibn Thabit hold this view as well, and it is the position of the seven jurists, the four imams, and the majority of scholars, may Allah have mercy on them.

The second position is that she takes a third of the entire estate, based on the general wording of His statement: {But if he has no child and his parents inherit from him, then for his mother is a third}, for the verse is more general than being restricted to whether there is a husband or wife present or not. This is the position of Ibn Abbas. Something similar has been reported from Ali and Mu'adh ibn Jabal. Shurayh and Dawud ibn Ali al-Zahiri held this view, and Imam Abu al-Husayn Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn al-Labban al-Basri chose it in his book "al-Ijaz fi 'Ilm al-Fara'id" (The Concise Treatise on the Science of Inheritance Shares).

There is something questionable in this, indeed it is weak, because the apparent meaning of the verse only applies to the case where she takes sole possession of the whole estate. But in this particular matter, the husband or wife takes their fixed share first, and what remains is treated as though it were the whole estate, so she takes a third of it, as mentioned above.

The third position is that she takes a third of the whole estate in the case involving a wife, for the wife takes a quarter, which is three out of twelve, and the mother takes a third, which is four, leaving five for the father. But in the case involving a husband, she takes a third of what remains, so that she does not end up taking more than the father would if she took a third of the whole estate. In that case the matter would be reckoned out of six: the husband gets half, which is three, the mother gets a third of what remains, which is one share, and the father gets what remains after that, which is two shares. This is attributed to Muhammad ibn Sirin, may Allah have mercy on him, and it is a composite of the first two positions, agreeing with each of them in one scenario. It too is weak. The first position is the correct one, and Allah knows best.

The third state among the states of the two parents is when they are combined with siblings, whether these siblings are full siblings, siblings from the father's side only, or siblings from the mother's side only. They do not inherit anything alongside the father, but nonetheless they restrict the mother's share from a third down to a sixth. So her fixed share, when they are present, becomes a sixth. If there is no heir besides her and the father, the father takes what remains.

The ruling concerning two siblings in what we have mentioned is the same as the ruling concerning a larger group of siblings according to the majority. Al-Bayhaqi reported through the route of Shu'bah, the freed slave of Ibn Abbas, from Ibn Abbas, that he came to Uthman and said: "Two siblings do not restrict the mother from the third. Allah says: {But if he has siblings}, and two siblings are not called 'siblings' (ikhwah, a plural form) in the language of your people." Uthman replied: "I cannot change what was established before my time," and the matter proceeded in the various regions, and people continued to inherit according to it.

There is something questionable about the soundness of this report, for this Shu'bah was criticized by Malik ibn Anas. Had this been sound from Ibn Abbas, his closest companions would have adopted it, yet what has been transmitted from them is the opposite.

Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi al-Zinad reported from Kharijah ibn Zayd, from his father, that he said: "Two siblings are called 'siblings' (ikhwah)." I have devoted a separate section to this matter.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Qatadah said regarding His statement: {But if he has siblings, then for his mother is a sixth}: "They harm the mother's share but do not inherit themselves. A single brother does not restrict her from the third; what is more than that does restrict her." The people of knowledge held that the reason they restricted their mother's share from the third was that their father bore responsibility for arranging their marriages and for their maintenance, not their mother.

This is a fine statement. However, it has been reported from Ibn Abbas, with a sound chain of transmission, that he held the view that the sixth which the siblings caused to be withheld from their mother should go to them. This is an anomalous position. Ibn Jarir reported it in his tafsir, saying:

[chain] Ibn Tawus, from his father, from Ibn Abbas, who said: "The sixth which the siblings from the mother's side caused to be withheld from her, they only withheld it from her so that it would belong to them rather than to their father."

Ibn Jarir then said: "This is a position that contradicts the entire community (of scholars)." He also said: [chain] al-Hasan ibn Muhammad, from Ibn Abbas, that he said: "Kalalah (a person with no direct heirs) refers to one who has no child and no parent."

Regarding His statement: {after any bequest he makes or debt}, the scholars, both earlier and later, are unanimously agreed that debt takes precedence over the bequest. This, upon careful consideration, is understood from the sense conveyed by the noble verse. Imam Ahmad, al-Tirmidhi, Ibn Majah, and the authors of the tafsir works reported, through the hadith of Abu Ishaq, from al-Harith ibn Abdullah al-A'war, from Ali ibn Abi Talib [may Allah be pleased with him], that he said: "You recite {after any bequest he makes or debt}, yet the Messenger of Allah ﷺ ruled that debt comes before the bequest. And full siblings inherit from one another to the exclusion of half-siblings from different mothers; a man inherits from his brother who shares his father and mother rather than from his brother who shares only his father." Al-Tirmidhi then said: "We know it only through the hadith of al-Harith al-A'war, and some scholars have criticized him."

I say: but he was known for retaining and being attentive to the science of inheritance shares and to calculation, and Allah knows best.

Regarding His statement: {Your parents and your children, you do not know which of them is nearer to you in benefit}, that is: We have only made fixed shares for parents and children, and We made them equal to one another in the basic principle of inheritance, contrary to what was the practice in pre-Islamic times (al-Jahiliyyah), and contrary to what was the practice at the beginning of Islam, when the property went to the child and a bequest was made for the parents, as was mentioned previously from Ibn Abbas. Allah abrogated that former practice with this one, establishing fixed shares for these and for those according to their due, because a person may receive worldly benefit, or benefit in the next life, or both, from his father that he does not receive from his son, and it may also be the reverse. For this reason He said: {Your parents and your children, you do not know which of them is nearer to you in benefit}, meaning: as though benefit is anticipated and hoped for from this one just as it is anticipated and hoped for from the other. For this reason We established a fixed share for this one and for that one, and made the two categories equal in the basic principle of inheritance, and Allah knows best.

Regarding His statement: {an obligation from Allah}, that is: this detailed apportionment of inheritance that We have mentioned, giving some heirs more than others, is an obligation from Allah which He has decreed and settled. And Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise, He who places things in their proper places and gives to each what he deserves according to his due. For this reason He said: {Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise}.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ الْكَرِيمَةُ وَالَّتِي بَعْدَهَا وَالْآيَةُ الَّتِي هِيَ خَاتِمَةُ هَذِهِ السُّورَةِ هُنَّ آيَاتُ عِلْمِ الْفَرَائِضِ، وَهُوَ مُسْتَنْبَطٌ مِنْ هَذِهِ الْآيَاتِ الثَّلَاثِ، وَمِنَ الْأَحَادِيثِ الْوَارِدَةِ فِي ذَلِكَ مِمَّا هِيَ كَالتَّفْسِيرِ لِذَلِكَ وَلنذْكُرْ مِنْهَا مَا هُوَ مُتَعَلِّقٌ بِتَفْسِيرِ ذَلِكَ، وَأَمَّا تَقْرِيرُ الْمَسَائِلِ وَنَصْبُ الْخِلَافِ وَالْأَدِلَّةِ، وَالْحِجَاجُ بَيْنَ الْأَئِمَّةِ، فَمَوْضِعُهُ كِتَابُ "الْأَحْكَامِ" فَاللَّهُ الْمُسْتَعَانُ .

وَقَدْ وَرَدَ التَّرْغِيبُ فِي تَعَلُّمِ الْفَرَائِضِ، وَهَذِهِ الْفَرَائِضُ الْخَاصَّةُ مِنْ أَهَمِّ ذَلِكَ. وَقَدْ رَوَى أَبُو دَاوُدَ وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ زِيَادِ بْنِ أَنْعَمَ الْإِفْرِيقِيِّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ رَافِعٍ التَّنُوخِيِّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "العِلْمُ ثَلَاثَةٌ، وَمَا سِوَى ذَلِكَ فَهُوَ فَضْلٌ: آيَةٌ مُحْكَمَةٌ، أَوْ سُنَّةٌ قائمةٌ، أَوْ فَريضةٌ عَادَلةٌ" .

وَعَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "يَا أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ، تَعلَّمُوا الفرائِضَ وعلِّموهُ فَإِنَّهُ نصْف الْعِلْمِ، وَهُوَ يُنْسَى، وَهُوَ أَوَّلُ شَيْءٍ يُنْتزَع مِنْ أُمَّتِي".

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَاجَهْ، وَفِي إِسْنَادِهِ ضَعْفٌ .

وَقَدْ رُوي مِنْ حَدِيثِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ وَأَبِي سَعِيدٍ وَفِي كُلٍّ مِنْهُمَا نَظَرٌ. قَالَ [سُفْيَانُ] ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ: إِنَّمَا سَمَّى الْفَرَائِضَ نصفَ الْعِلْمِ؛ لِأَنَّهُ يُبْتَلَى بِهِ النَّاسُ كُلُّهُمْ.

وَقَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ عِنْدَ تَفْسِيرِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ مُوسَى، حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامٌ: أن ابن جُرَيج أَخْبَرَهُمْ قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي ابْنُ المُنْكدِر، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: عَادَنِي رسولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ وَأَبُو بَكْرٍ فِي بَنِي سَلمَةَ مَاشِيَيْنِ، فوجَدَني النَّبِيُّ ﷺ لَا أَعْقِلُ شَيْئًا، فَدَعَا بِمَاءٍ فَتَوَضَّأَ مِنْهُ، ثُمَّ رَش عَلَيَّ، فَأَفَقْتُ، فَقُلْتُ: مَا تَأْمُرُنِي أَنْ أَصْنَعَ فِي مَالِي يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ؟ فَنَزَلَتْ: ﴿يُوصِيكُمُ اللَّهُ فِي أَوْلادِكُمْ لِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلُ حَظِّ الأنْثَيَيْنِ﴾ .

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ وَالنَّسَائِيُّ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ حَجَّاجِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الْأَعْوَرِ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ بِهِ، وَرَوَاهُ الجماعةُ كُلّهم مِنْ حَدِيثِ سُفْيَانَ بْنِ عُيَينة، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْمُنْكَدِرِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ .

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ عَنْ جَابِرٍ فِي سَبَبِ نُزُولِ الْآيَةِ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدّثنا زَكَرِيَّا بْنُ عَدِيٍّ، حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ -هُوَ ابْنُ عَمْرو الرَّقِّيُّ -عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَقيل، عن جابر قَالَ: جَاءَتِ امْرَأَةُ سَعْدِ بْنِ الرَّبيع إِلَى رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم فقالت: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، هَاتَانِ ابْنَتَا سَعْدِ بْنِ الرَّبِيعِ، قُتل أَبُوهُمَا مَعَكَ فِي أحُد شَهِيدًا، وَإِنَّ عَمَّهُمَا أَخَذَ مَالَهُمَا، فَلَمْ يَدَعْ لَهُمَا مَالًا وَلَا يُنْكَحَان إِلَّا وَلَهُمَا مَالٌ. قَالَ: فَقَالَ: "يَقْضِي اللَّهُ فِي ذَلِكَ". قَالَ: فَنَزَلَتْ آيَةُ الْمِيرَاثِ، فَأَرْسَلَ رسولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ إِلَى عَمِّهِمَا فَقَالَ: "أعْطِ ابْنَتي سَعْدٍ الثُّلُثَيْنِ، وأُمُّهُمَا الثُّمُنَ، وَمَا بَقِيَ فَهُوَ لَكَ".

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ، مِنْ طُرُقٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عُقَيل، بِهِ. قَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: وَلَا يُعْرَفُ إِلَّا مِنْ حَدِيثِهِ .

وَالظَّاهِرُ أَنَّ حَدِيثَ جَابِرٍ الْأَوَّلَ إِنَّمَا نَزَلَ بِسَبَبِهِ الْآيَةُ الْأَخِيرَةُ مِنْ هَذِهِ السُّورَةِ كَمَا سَيَأْتِي، فَإِنَّهُ إِنَّمَا كَانَ لَهُ إِذْ ذَاكَ أَخَوَاتٌ، وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ بَنَاتٌ، وَإِنَّمَا كَانَ يُورَثُ كَلَالَةً، وَلَكِنْ ذَكَرْنَا الْحَدِيثَ هَاهُنَا تَبَعًا لِلْبُخَارِيِّ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ، فَإِنَّهُ ذَكَرَهُ هَاهُنَا. وَالْحَدِيثُ الثَّانِي عَنْ جَابِرٍ أَشْبَهُ بِنُزُولِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

فَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿يُوصِيكُمُ اللَّهُ فِي أَوْلادِكُمْ لِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلُ حَظِّ الأنْثَيَيْنِ﴾ أَيْ: يَأْمُرُكُمْ بِالْعَدْلِ فِيهِمْ، فَإِنَّ أَهْلَ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ كَانُوا يَجْعَلُونَ جَمِيعَ الْمِيرَاثِ لِلذُّكُورِ دُونَ الْإِنَاثِ، فَأَمَرَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى بِالتَّسْوِيَةِ بَيْنَهُمْ فِي أَصْلِ الْمِيرَاثِ، وَفَاوَتَ بَيْنَ الصِّنْفَيْنِ، فَجَعَلَ لِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلَ حَظِّ الْأُنْثَيَيْنِ؛ وَذَلِكَ لِاحْتِيَاجِ الرَّجُلِ إِلَى مُؤْنَةِ النَّفَقَةِ وَالْكُلْفَةِ وَمُعَانَاةِ التِّجَارَةِ وَالتَّكَسُّبِ وتجشُّم الْمَشَقَّةِ، فَنَاسَبَ أَنْ يُعْطَى ضعْفَيْ مَا تَأْخُذُهُ الْأُنْثَى.

وَقَدِ اسْتَنْبَطَ بَعْضُ الْأَذْكِيَاءِ مِنْ قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿يُوصِيكُمُ اللَّهُ فِي أَوْلادِكُمْ لِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلُ حَظِّ الأنْثَيَيْنِ﴾ أَنَّهُ تَعَالَى أَرْحَمُ بِخَلْقِهِ مِنَ الْوَالِدِ بِوَلَدِهِ، حَيْثُ أَوْصَى الْوَالِدَيْنِ بِأَوْلَادِهِمْ، فَعُلِمَ أَنَّهُ أَرْحَمُ بِهِمْ مِنْهُمْ، كَمَا جَاءَ في الحديث الصحيح.

وَقَدْ رَأَى امْرَأَةً مِنَ السَّبْي تَدُورُ عَلَى وَلَدِهَا، فَلَمَّا وَجَدَتْهُ أَخَذَتْهُ فألْصَقَتْه بصَدْرها وَأَرْضَعَتْهُ. فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ لِأَصْحَابِهِ: "أتَروْن هذهِ طارحةَ وَلَدَهَا فِي النَّارِ وَهِيَ تَقْدِرُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ؟ " قَالُوا: لَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ: قَالَ: "فَوَاللهِ للَّهُ أًرْحَمُ بعبادِهِ مِنْ هَذِهِ بِوَلَدِهَا".

وَقَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ هَاهُنَا: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يُوسُفَ، عَنْ وَرْقَاءَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيح، عَنْ عَطاء، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: كَانَ الْمَالُ لِلْوَلَدِ، وَكَانَتِ الْوَصِيَّةُ لِلْوَالِدَيْنِ، فنَسَخ اللَّهُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ مَا أَحَبَّ، فَجَعَلَ لِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلَ حَظِّ الْأُنْثَيَيْنِ، وَجَعَلَ لِلْأَبَوَيْنِ لِكُلِّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا السُّدُسَ وَالثُّلُثَ، وَجَعَلَ لِلزَّوْجَةِ الثَّمُنَ وَالرُّبُعَ، وَلِلزَّوْجِ الشَّطْرَ وَالرُّبُعَ .

وَقَالَ العَوفي، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿يُوصِيكُمُ اللَّهُ فِي أَوْلادِكُمْ لِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلُ حَظِّ الأنْثَيَيْنِ﴾ وَذَلِكَ أَنَّهُ لَمَّا نَزَلَتِ الْفَرَائِضُ الَّتِي فَرَضَ اللَّهُ فِيهَا مَا فَرَضَ، لِلْوَلَدِ الذَّكَرِ وَالْأُنْثَى وَالْأَبَوَيْنِ، كَرِهَهَا النَّاسُ أَوْ بَعْضُهُمْ وَقَالُوا: تُعطَى الْمَرْأَةُ الرُّبُعَ أَوِ الثُّمُنَ وَتُعْطَى الْبِنْتُ النِّصْفَ. وَيُعْطَى الْغُلَامُ الصَّغِيرُ. وَلَيْسَ أَحَدٌ مِنْ هَؤُلَاءِ يُقَاتِلُ الْقَوْمَ، وَلَا يَحُوزُ الْغَنِيمَةَ.. اسْكُتُوا عَنْ هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ لَعَلَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَنْسَاهُ، أَوْ نَقُولُ لَهُ فَيُغَيِّرُ، فَقَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، نُعْطِي الْجَارِيَةَ نِصْفَ مَا تَرَكَ أَبُوهَا، وَلَيْسَتْ تَرْكَبُ الفَرَس، وَلَا تُقَاتِلُ الْقَوْمَ ونُعطِي الصَّبِيَّ الْمِيرَاثَ وَلَيْسَ يُغني شَيْئًا.. وَكَانُوا يَفْعَلُونَ ذَلِكَ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ، لَا يُعْطُونَ الْمِيرَاثَ إِلَّا لِمَنْ قَاتَلَ الْقَوْمَ، وَيُعْطُونَهُ الْأَكْبَرَ فَالْأَكْبَرَ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ وَابْنُ جَرِيرٍ أَيْضًا.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَإِنْ كُنَّ نِسَاءً فَوْقَ اثْنَتَيْنِ فَلَهُنَّ ثُلُثَا مَا تَرَكَ﴾ قَالَ بَعْضُ النَّاسِ: قَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَوْقَ﴾ زَائِدَةٌ وَتَقْدِيرُهُ: فَإِنْ كُنَّ نِسَاءً اثْنَتَيْنِ كَمَا فِي قَوْلِهِ [تَعَالَى] ﴿فَاضْرِبُوا فَوْقَ الأعْنَاقِ﴾ [الْأَنْفَالِ:١٢] وَهَذَا غَيْرُ مُسَلَّم لَا هُنَا وَلَا هُنَاكَ؛ فَإِنَّهُ لَيْسَ فِي الْقُرْآنِ شَيْءٌ زَائِدٌ لَا فَائِدَةَ فِيهِ وَهَذَا مُمْتَنِعٌ، ثُمَّ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَلَهُنَّ ثُلُثَا مَا تَرَكَ﴾ لَوْ كَانَ الْمُرَادُ مَا قَالُوهُ لَقَالَ: فَلَهُمَا ثُلُثَا مَا تَرَكَ. وَإِنَّمَا اسْتُفِيدَ كَوْنُ الثُّلُثَيْنِ لِلْبِنْتَيْنِ مِنْ حُكْمِ الْأُخْتَيْنِ فِي الْآيَةِ الْأَخِيرَةِ، فَإِنَّهُ تَعَالَى حَكَمَ فِيهَا لِلْأُخْتَيْنِ بِالثُّلُثَيْنِ. وَإِذَا وَرِثَ الْأُخْتَانِ الثُّلُثَيْنِ فَلَأَنْ يَرِثَ الْبِنْتَانِ الثُّلُثَيْنِ بِطْرِيقِ الْأَولَى وَقَدْ تَقَدَّمَ فِي حَدِيثِ جَابِرٍ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ حَكَمَ لِابْنَتَيْ سَعْدِ بْنِ الرَّبِيعِ بِالثُّلُثَيْنِ، فَدَلَّ الْكِتَابُ وَالسُّنَّةُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ، وَأَيْضًا فَإِنَّهُ قَالَ: ﴿وَإِنْ كَانَتْ وَاحِدَةً فَلَهَا النِّصْفُ﴾ فَلَوْ كَانَ لِلْبِنْتَيْنِ النِّصْفُ [أَيْضًا] لَنَصَّ عَلَيْهِ، فَلَمَّا حَكَمَ بِهِ لِلْوَاحِدَةِ عَلَى انْفِرَادِهَا دَلَّ عَلَى أَنَّ الْبِنْتَيْنِ فِي حُكْمِ الثَّلَاثِ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلأبَوَيْهِ لِكُلِّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا السُّدُسُ [مِمَّا تَرَكَ إِنْ كَانَ لَهُ وَلَدٌ فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ وَلَدٌ وَوَرِثَهُ أَبَوَاهُ فَلأمِّهِ الثُّلُثُ فَإِنْ كَانَ لَهُ إِخْوَةٌ فَلأمِّهِ السُّدُسُ] ﴾

إِلَى آخِرِهِ، الْأَبَوَانِ لَهُمَا فِي الْمِيرَاثِ أَحْوَالٌ:

أَحَدُهَا: أَنْ يَجْتَمِعَا مَعَ الْأَوْلَادِ، فَيُفْرَضُ لِكُلِّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا السُّدُسُ فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لِلْمَيِّتِ إِلَّا بِنْتٌ وَاحِدَةٌ، فُرِضَ لَهَا النِّصْفُ، وَلِلْأَبَوَيْنِ لِكُلِّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا السُّدُسُ، وَأَخَذَ الْأَبُ السُّدُسَ الْآخَرَ بِالتَّعْصِيبِ، فَيُجْمَعُ لَهُ -وَالْحَالَةُ هَذِهِ -بَيْنَ هَذِهِ الْفَرْضُ وَالتَّعْصِيبُ.

الْحَالُ الثَّانِي: أَنْ يَنْفَرِدَ الْأَبَوَانِ بِالْمِيرَاثِ، فَيُفْرَضُ لِلْأُمِّ -وَالْحَالَةُ هَذِهِ -الثُّلُثُ وَيَأْخُذُ الْأَبُ الْبَاقِي بِالتَّعْصِيبِ الْمَحْضِ، وَيَكُونُ قَدْ أَخَذَ ضَعْفَيِ مَا فُرِضَ لِلْأُمِّ، وَهُوَ الثُّلُثَانِ، فَلَوْ كَانَ مَعَهُمَا -وَالْحَالَةُ هَذِهِ -زَوْجٌ أَوْ زَوْجَةٌ أَخَذَ الزَّوْجُ النِّصْفَ وَالزَّوْجَةُ الرُّبُعَ. ثُمَّ اخْتَلَفَ الْعُلَمَاءُ: مَا تَأْخُذُ الْأُمُّ بَعْدَ فَرْضِ الزَّوْجِ وَالزَّوْجَةِ عَلَى ثَلَاثَةِ أَقْوَالٍ:

أَحَدُهَا: أَنَّهَا تَأْخُذُ ثُلُثَ الْبَاقِي فِي الْمَسْأَلَتَيْنِ؛ لِأَنَّ الْبَاقِيَ كَأَنَّهُ جَمِيعُ الْمِيرَاثِ بِالنِّسْبَةِ إِلَيْهِمَا. وَقَدْ جَعَلَ اللَّهُ لَهَا نِصْفَ مَا جَعَلَ لِلْأَبِ فَتَأْخُذُ ثُلُثَ الْبَاقِي وَيَأْخُذُ ثُلُثَيْهِ وَهُوَ قَوْلُ عُمَرَ وَعُثْمَانَ، وَأَصَحُّ الرِّوَايَتَيْنِ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ. وَبِهِ يَقُولُ ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ وَزَيْدُ بْنُ ثَابِتٍ، وَهُوَ قَوْلُ الْفُقَهَاءِ السَّبْعَةِ، وَالْأَئِمَّةِ الْأَرْبَعَةِ، وَجُمْهُورِ الْعُلَمَاءِ -رَحِمَهُمُ اللَّهُ.

وَالْقَوْلُ الثَّانِي: أَنَّهَا تَأْخُذُ ثُلُثَ جَمِيعِ الْمَالِ لِعُمُومِ قَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ وَلَدٌ وَوَرِثَهُ أَبَوَاهُ فَلأمِّهِ الثُّلُثُ﴾ فَإِنَّ الْآيَةَ أَعَمُّ مِنْ أَنْ يَكُونَ مَعَهَا زَوْجٌ أَوْ زَوْجَةٌ أَوْ لَا. وَهُوَ قَوْلُ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ. وَرُوِيَ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، وَمُعَاذِ بْنِ جَبَلٍ، نَحْوُهُ. وَبِهِ يَقُولُ شُرَيْحٌ وَدَاوُدُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ الظَّاهِرِيُّ وَاخْتَارَهُ الْإِمَامُ أَبُو الْحُسَيْنِ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ اللَّبَّانِ الْبَصْرِيُّ فِي كِتَابِهِ "الْإِيجَازُ فِي عِلْمِ الْفَرَائِضِ".

وَهَذَا فِيهِ نَظَرٌ، بَلْ هُوَ ضَعِيفٌ؛ لِأَنَّ ظَاهِرَ الْآيَةِ إِنَّمَا هُوَ [مَا] إِذَا اسْتَبَدَّ بِجَمِيعِ التَّرِكَةِ، فَأَمَّا فِي هَذِهِ الْمَسْأَلَةِ فَيَأْخُذُ الزَّوْجُ أَوِ الزَّوْجَةُ الْفَرْضَ، وَيَبْقَى الْبَاقِي كَأَنَّهُ جَمِيعُ التَّرِكَةِ، فَتَأْخُذُ ثُلُثَهُ، كَمَا تَقَدَّمَ.

وَالْقَوْلُ الثَّالِثُ: أَنَّهَا تَأْخُذُ ثُلُثَ جَمِيعِ الْمَالِ فِي مَسْأَلَةِ الزَّوْجَةِ، فَإِنَّهَا تَأْخُذُ الرُّبُعَ وَهُوَ ثَلَاثَةٌ مِنِ اثْنَيْ عَشَرَ، وَتَأْخُذُ الْأُمُّ الثُّلُثَ وَهُوَ أَرْبَعَةٌ، فَيَبْقَى خَمْسَةٌ لِلْأَبِ. وَأَمَّا فِي مَسْأَلَةِ الزَّوْجِ فَتَأْخُذُ ثُلُثَ الْبَاقِي؛ لِئَلَّا تَأْخُذَ أَكْثَرَ مِنَ الْأَبِ لَوْ أَخَذَتْ ثُلُثَ الْمَالِ، فَتَكُونُ الْمَسْأَلَةُ مِنْ سِتَّةٍ: لِلزَّوْجِ النِّصْفُ ثَلَاثَةٌ وَلِلْأُمِّ ثُلُثُ مَا بَقِيَ وَهُوَ سَهْمٌ، وَلِلْأَبِ الْبَاقِي بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ وَهُوَ سَهْمَانِ. وَيُحْكَى هَذَا عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِيرِينَ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ، وَهُوَ مُرَكَّبٌ مِنَ الْقَوْلَيْنِ الْأَوَّلِينَ، مُوَافِقٌ كُلَّا مِنْهُمَا فِي صُورَةٍ وَهُوَ ضَعِيفٌ أَيْضًا. وَالصَّحِيحُ الْأَوَّلُ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَالْحَالُ الثَّالِثُ مِنْ أَحْوَالِ الْأَبَوَيْنِ: وَهُوَ اجْتِمَاعُهُمَا مَعَ الْإِخْوَةِ، وَسَوَاءٌ كانوا من الأبوين، أو من الْأَبِ، أَوْ مِنَ الْأُمِّ، فَإِنَّهُمْ لَا يَرِثُونَ مَعَ الْأَبِ شَيْئًا، وَلَكِنَّهُمْ مَعَ ذَلِكَ يَحْجُبُونَ الْأُمَّ عَنِ الثُّلُثِ إِلَى السُّدُسِ، فَيُفْرَضُ لَهَا مَعَ وُجُودِهِمُ السُّدُسُ، فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ وَارِثٌ سِوَاهَا وَسِوَى الْأَبِ أَخَذَ الْأَبُ الْبَاقِي.

وَحُكْمُ الْأَخَوَيْنِ فِيمَا ذَكَرْنَاهُ كَحُكْمِ الْإِخْوَةِ عِنْدَ الْجُمْهُورِ. وَقَدْ رَوَى الْبَيْهَقِيُّ مِنْ طَرِيقِ شُعْبة مَوْلَى ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ أَنَّهُ دَخَلَ عَلَى عُثْمَانَ فَقَالَ: إِنَّ الْأَخَوَيْنِ لَا يَردان الْأُمَّ عَنِ الثُّلُثِ، قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَإِنْ كَانَ لَهُ إِخْوَةٌ﴾ فَالْأَخَوَانِ لَيْسَا بِلِسَانِ قَوْمِكَ إِخْوَةٌ. فَقَالَ عُثْمَانُ: لَا أَسْتَطِيعُ تَغْيِيرَ مَا كَانَ قَبْلِي، وَمَضَى فِي الْأَمْصَارِ، وَتَوَارَثَ بِهِ النَّاسُ.

وَفِي صِحَّةِ هَذَا الْأَثَرِ نَظَرٌ، فَإِنَّ شُعْبَة هَذَا تكلَّم فِيهِ مَالِكُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ، وَلَوْ كَانَ هَذَا صَحِيحًا عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ لَذَهَبَ إِلَيْهِ أَصْحَابُهُ الْأَخِصَّاءُ بِهِ، وَالْمَنْقُولُ عَنْهُمْ خِلَافُهُ.

وَقَدْ رَوَى عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ أَبِي الزِّنَادِ، عَنْ خَارِجَةَ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: الْأَخَوَانِ تُسَمَّى إِخْوَةٌ وَقَدْ أَفْرَدْتُ لِهَذِهِ الْمَسْأَلَةِ جُزءًا عَلَى حِدَةٍ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْعَزِيزِ بْنُ الْمُغِيرَةِ، حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ زُرَيْعٍ عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَإِنْ كَانَ لَهُ إِخْوَةٌ فَلأمِّهِ السُّدُسُ﴾ أَضَرُّوا بِالْأُمِّ وَلَا يَرِثُونَ، وَلَا يَحْجُبُهَا الْأَخُ الْوَاحِدُ مِنَ الثُّلُثِ وَيَحْجُبُهَا مَا فَوْقَ ذَلِكَ، وَكَانَ أَهْلُ الْعِلْمِ يَرون أَنَّهُمْ إِنَّمَا حَجَبُوا أُمَّهُمْ مِنَ الثُّلُثِ أَنَّ أَبَاهُمْ يَلِي إِنْكَاحَهُمْ وَنَفَقَتَهُ عَلَيْهِمْ دُونَ أُمِّهِمْ.

وَهَذَا كَلَامٌ حَسَنٌ. لَكِنْ رُوِيَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ بِإِسْنَادٍ صَحِيحٍ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَرَى أَنَّ السُّدُسَ الَّذِي حَجَبُوهُ عَنْ أُمِّهِمْ يَكُونُ لَهُمْ، وَهَذَا قَوْلٌ شَاذٌّ، رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ فَقَالَ:

حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ يَحْيَى، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، أَخْبَرَنَا مَعْمَر عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: السُّدُسُ الَّذِي حَجَبَتْه الْإِخْوَةُ لِأُمٍّ لَهُمْ، إِنَّمَا حَجَبُوا أُمَّهُمْ عَنْهُ لِيَكُونَ لَهُمْ دُونَ أَبِيهِمْ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: وَهَذَا قَوْلٌ مُخَالِفٌ لِجَمِيعِ الْأُمَّةِ، وَقَدْ حَدَّثَنِي يُونُسُ، أَخْبَرَنَا سُفْيَانُ، أَخْبَرَنَا عَمْرو، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: الْكَلَالَةُ مَنْ لَا وَلَدَ لَهُ وَلَا وَالِدَ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿مِنْ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ يُوصِي بِهَا أَوْ دَيْنٍ﴾ أَجْمَعَ الْعُلَمَاءُ سَلَفًا وَخَلَفًا: أَنَّ الدَّيْن مُقَدَّمٌ عَلَى الْوَصِيَّةِ، وَذَلِكَ عِنْدَ إِمْعَانِ النَّظَرِ يُفْهَمُ مِنْ فَحْوَى الْآيَةِ الْكَرِيمَةِ. وَقَدْ رَوَى الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ وَأَصْحَابُ التَّفَاسِيرِ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْأَعْوَرِ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ [رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عنه] قَالَ: إِنَّكُمْ تَقْرَءُونَ ﴿مِنْ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ يُوصِي بِهَا أَوْ دَيْنٍ﴾ وَإِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَضَى بِالدَّيْنِ قَبْلَ الْوَصِيَّةِ، وَإِنَّ أَعْيَانَ بَنِي الْأُمِّ يَتَوَارَثُونَ دُونَ بَنِي العَلات، يَرِثُ الرَّجُلُ أَخَاهُ لِأَبِيهِ وَأُمِّهِ دُونَ أَخِيهِ لِأَبِيهِ. ثُمَّ قَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: لَا نَعْرِفُهُ إِلَّا مِنْ حَدِيثِ الْحَارِثِ الْأَعْوَرِ، وَقَدْ تَكَلَّمَ فيه بعض أهل العلم .

قُلْتُ: لَكِنْ كَانَ حَافِظًا لِلْفَرَائِضِ مُعْتَنِيًا بِهَا وَبِالْحِسَابِ فَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿آبَاؤُكُمْ وَأَبْنَاؤُكُمْ لَا تَدْرُونَ أَيُّهُمْ أَقْرَبُ لَكُمْ نَفْعًا﴾ أَيْ: إِنَّمَا فَرَضْنَا لِلْآبَاءِ وَلِلْأَبْنَاءِ، وَسَاوَيْنَا بَيْنَ الْكُلِّ فِي أَصْلِ الْمِيرَاثِ عَلَى خِلَافِ مَا كَانَ عَلَيْهِ الْأَمْرُ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ، وَعَلَى خِلَافِ مَا كَانَ عَلَيْهِ الْأَمْرُ فِي ابْتِدَاءِ الْإِسْلَامِ مِنْ كَوْنِ الْمَالِ لِلْوَلَدِ وَلِلْوَالِدَيْنِ الْوَصِيَّةُ، كَمَا تَقَدَّمَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، إِنَّمَا نَسَخَ اللَّهُ ذَلِكَ إِلَى هَذَا، فَفَرَضَ لِهَؤُلَاءِ وَلِهَؤُلَاءِ بِحَسَبِهِمْ؛ لِأَنَّ الْإِنْسَانَ قَدْ يَأْتِيهِ النَّفْعُ الدُّنْيَوِيُّ -أَوِ الْأُخْرَوِيُّ أَوْ هُمَا -مِنْ أَبِيهِ مَا لَا يَأْتِيهِ مِنِ ابْنِهِ، وَقَدْ يَكُونُ بِالْعَكْسِ؛ فَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿آبَاؤُكُمْ وَأَبْنَاؤُكُمْ لَا تَدْرُونَ أَيُّهُمْ أَقْرَبُ لَكُمْ نَفْعًا﴾ أَيْ: كَأَنَّ النَّفْعَ مُتَوَقَّعٌ وَمَرْجُوٌّ مِنْ هَذَا، كَمَا هُوَ مُتَوَقَّعٌ وَمَرْجُوٌّ مِنَ الْآخَرِ؛ فَلِهَذَا فَرَضْنَا لِهَذَا وَلِهَذَا، وَسَاوَيْنَا بَيْنَ الْقِسْمَيْنِ فِي أَصْلِ الْمِيرَاثِ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَرِيضَةً مِنَ اللَّهِ﴾ أَيْ: [مِنْ] هَذَا الَّذِي ذَكَرْنَاهُ مِنْ تَفْصِيلِ الْمِيرَاثِ، وَإِعْطَاءِ بَعْضِ الْوَرَثَةِ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ بَعْضٍ -هُوَ فَرْضٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ حَكَمَ بِهِ وَقَضَاهُ، وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ الَّذِي يَضَعُ الْأَشْيَاءَ فِي مَحَالِّهَا، وَيُعْطِي كُلًّا مَا يَسْتَحِقُّهُ بِحَسَبِهِ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا﴾

---

## 4:12 — verse 12

### Translation

The Almighty says: to you, O men, belongs half of what your wives leave behind if they die without a child; but if they have a child, then to you belongs a quarter of what they leave, after any bequest they make or debt. It has already been established that debt takes precedence over the bequest, and after that comes the bequest, then the inheritance. This is a matter upon which the scholars are agreed, and the ruling regarding the children of sons, however far down they descend, is the same as the ruling regarding one's direct offspring.

Then He says: {And to them belongs a quarter of what you leave if you have no child; but if you have a child, then to them belongs an eighth of what you leave} and so on. Whether it is the quarter or the eighth, a single wife, two wives, three, or four share in it equally.

His saying {after any bequest} and so on has already been discussed as mentioned before.

His saying {And if a man or woman leaves neither ascendants nor descendants but has a brother or a sister}: kalalah (one who leaves no direct heir, ascendant or descendant) is derived from al-iklil, which is that which encircles the head from its sides. What is meant here is one who is inherited from by his collateral relatives, not his ascendants nor his descendants, as al-Sha'bi narrated from Abu Bakr al-Siddiq: he was asked about kalalah, and he said, "I will speak concerning it according to my own opinion; if it is correct, it is from Allah, and if it is wrong, it is from me and from Satan, and Allah and His Messenger are free of it: kalalah is one who has neither child nor parent." Then when Umar ibn al-Khattab took charge, he said, "I would be ashamed to oppose Abu Bakr in an opinion he held." Narrated by Ibn Jarir and others.

Ibn Abi Hatim, may Allah have mercy on him, said in his tafsir: [chain] Tawus said: I heard Abdullah ibn Abbas say: I was the last of the people to have contact with Umar ibn al-Khattab, and I heard him say, "The correct statement is what I said, what I said, and what I said." He said: kalalah is one who has neither child nor parent.

This is likewise what Ali ibn Abi Talib and Ibn Mas'ud said, and it is authentically established through more than one route from Abdullah ibn Abbas and Zayd ibn Thabit. This is also the view of al-Sha'bi, al-Nakha'i, al-Hasan al-Basri, Qatadah, Jabir ibn Zayd, and al-Hakam. It is the view held by the people of Medina, Kufa, and Basra, and it is the view of the seven jurists, the four imams, and the majority of the early and later scholars, indeed nearly all of them. More than one scholar has related that there is consensus on this, and a marfu' (elevated) hadith has come concerning it. Abu al-Husayn ibn al-Lubban said: something contrary to this has been narrated from Ibn Abbas, namely that kalalah means one who has no child. But the correct position from him is the first one, and perhaps the narrator did not understand correctly what he intended.

His saying {and he has a brother or a sister}, meaning from the mother, as is found in the recitation of some of the early scholars, among them Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas, and this is likewise how Abu Bakr al-Siddiq interpreted it as narrated from him by Qatadah: {then to each of them belongs a sixth; but if they are more than that, they are partners in a third}.

The siblings from the mother differ from the rest of the heirs in several respects. First: they inherit alongside the one through whom they are related, namely the mother. Second: their males and females are equal in share. Third: they inherit only when the deceased is inherited from as kalalah, so they do not inherit alongside a father, a grandfather, a child, or a son's child. Fourth: their share is never increased beyond a third, however numerous their males and females may be.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] al-Zuhri said: Umar ibn al-Khattab, may Allah be pleased with him, ruled that the inheritance of siblings from the mother is to be divided among them with the male receiving the same as the female. Muhammad ibn Shihab al-Zuhri said: I do not think Umar ruled thus except upon knowledge of it from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and because of this verse in which Allah the Almighty says: {but if they are more than that, they are partners in a third}.

The scholars differed concerning the "shared case" (al-mas'alah al-mushtarakah), which is: a husband, and a mother or grandmother, and two or more children of the mother, together with one or more children of both parents. According to the view of the majority: the husband receives a half, the mother or grandmother receives a sixth, and the children of the mother receive a third, and the children of both parents share with them in that third by virtue of what they have in common with them, namely being siblings from the mother.

This case arose in the time of the Commander of the Faithful, Umar ibn al-Khattab. He gave the husband a half, the mother a sixth, and assigned the third to the children of the mother. Then the children of both parents said to him, "O Commander of the Faithful, suppose our father had been a donkey, are we not still from the same mother?" So he made them share equally.

This practice of sharing equally is authentically established from him and from the Commander of the Faithful Uthman, and it is one of the two narrations from Ibn Mas'ud, Zayd ibn Thabit, and Ibn Abbas, may Allah be pleased with them. This is also the view of Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, Shurayh the judge, Masruq, Tawus, Muhammad ibn Sirin, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz, al-Thawri, Sharik, and it is the school of Malik, al-Shafi'i, and Ishaq ibn Rahwayh.

Ali ibn Abi Talib, however, did not make them share equally; rather, he assigned the third to the children of the mother and gave nothing to the children of both parents in this situation, because they are considered agnate heirs (asabah). Waki' ibn al-Jarrah said: no differing report has come from him on this matter. This is also the view of Ubayy ibn Ka'b and Abu Musa al-Ash'ari, and it is the well-known position from Ibn Abbas, and it is the school of al-Sha'bi, Ibn Abi Layla, Abu Yusuf, Muhammad ibn al-Hasan, al-Hasan ibn Ziyad, Zufar ibn al-Hudhayl, the Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal, Yahya ibn Adam, Nu'aym ibn Hammad, Abu Thawr, and Dawud ibn Ali al-Zahiri. Abu al-Husayn ibn al-Lubban al-Faradi, may Allah have mercy on him, chose this view in his book al-Ijaz.

His saying {after any bequest that is made, or debt, without causing harm}: meaning, that his bequest should be made with justice, not with the intent to cause harm, injustice, or wrong, such as depriving some of the heirs, or diminishing or increasing their shares beyond what Allah has decreed for them as their fixed portion. Whenever someone pursues that, he becomes like one who opposes Allah in His wisdom and His apportionment. Hence Ibn Abi Hatim said:

[chain] Ibn Abbas reported from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: "Causing harm through a bequest is among the grave sins (kaba'ir)."

Ibn Jarir likewise narrated this through the route of this same Umar ibn al-Mughirah, who is Abu Hafs al-Basri, who settled in al-Massisah. Abu al-Qasim ibn Asakir said: he is known as "the mufti of the poor." More than one of the imams narrated from him. Abu Hatim al-Razi said of him: he is a shaykh (of acceptable standing). Ali ibn al-Madini said: he is majhul (unknown), I do not know him. However, al-Nasa'i narrated it in his Sunan from Ali ibn Hujr, from Ali ibn Mushir, from Dawud ibn Abi Hind, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas, as mawquf (halted report, i.e., attributed only to the Companion): "Causing harm through a bequest is among the grave sins." Ibn Abi Hatim likewise narrated it from Abu Sa'id al-Ashajj, from A'idh ibn Habib, from Dawud ibn Abi Hind. Ibn Jarir narrated it through a group of hadith masters, from Dawud, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas, as mawquf, and in some versions: Ibn Abbas used to recite {ghayra mudarrin, "without causing harm"}.

Ibn Jurayj said: the correct version is the mawquf one.

Because of this, the scholars differed concerning acknowledgment (iqrar) in favor of an heir: is it valid or not? There are two views. The first: it is not valid, because it raises the suspicion that it may actually be a bequest made in the guise of an acknowledgment, and it has been established in an authentic hadith that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Allah has given each rightful claimant his due right, so there is no bequest for an heir." This is the school of Abu Hanifah, Malik, and Ahmad ibn Hanbal, and it is the older view of al-Shafi'i, may Allah have mercy on them. In his newer view, al-Shafi'i held that such acknowledgment is valid. This is also the school of Tawus, Ata', al-Hasan, and Umar ibn Abd al-Aziz.

This is the position chosen by Abu Abdullah al-Bukhari in his Sahih. He argued that Rafi' ibn Khadij made a bequest that al-Fazariyyah should not be questioned about what she had locked behind her door. He said: some people have said that such acknowledgment is not permissible, out of suspicion of ill intent toward the heirs, but the Prophet ﷺ said: "Beware of suspicion, for suspicion is the most untruthful of speech." And Allah the Almighty says: {Indeed, Allah commands you to render trusts to their rightful owners} [al-Nisa: 58], without distinguishing between an heir and anyone else. This concludes what he mentioned.

So whenever the acknowledgment is genuinely true and corresponds to the actual reality of the matter, this difference of opinion applies to it. But whenever it is used as a stratagem and a means to increase the share of some heirs and diminish that of others, then it is forbidden by consensus, and by the explicit text of this noble verse: {without causing harm. This is an injunction from Allah, and Allah is All-Knowing, Most Forbearing} [Then Allah says]

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى: وَلَكُمْ -أَيُّهَا الرِّجَالُ-نِصْفُ مَا تَرَكَ أَزْوَاجُكُمْ إِذَا مُتْن عَنْ غَيْرِ وَلَدٍ، فَإِنْ كَانَ لَهُنَّ وَلَدٌ فَلَكُمُ الرُّبُعُ مِمَّا تَرَكْنَ مِنْ بَعْدِ [وَصِيَّةٍ] يُوصِينَ بِهَا أَوْ دِينٍ. وَقَدْ تَقَدَّمَ أَنَّ الدَّيْنَ مُقَدَّمٌ عَلَى الْوَصِيَّةِ، وَبَعْدَهُ الْوَصِيَّةُ ثُمَّ الْمِيرَاثُ، وَهَذَا أَمْرٌ مُجْمَعٌ عَلَيْهِ بَيْنَ الْعُلَمَاءِ، وَحُكْمُ أَوْلَادِ الْبَنِينَ وَإِنْ سَفُلُوا حُكْمُ أَوْلَادِ الصُّلْبِ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿وَلَهُنَّ الرُّبُعُ مِمَّا تَرَكْتُمْ [إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَكُمْ وَلَدٌ فَإِنْ كَانَ لَكُمْ وَلَدٌ فَلَهُنَّ الثُّمُنُ مِمَّا تَرَكْتُمْ] ﴾ إِلَخْ، وَسَوَاءٌ فِي الرُّبُعِ أَوِ الثُّمُنِ الزَّوْجَةُ والزوجتان الاثنتان والثلاث والأربع يشتركن فيه.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿مِنْ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ﴾ إِلَخْ، الْكَلَامُ عَلَيْهِ كَمَا تَقَدَّمَ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَإِنْ كَانَ رَجُلٌ يُورَثُ كَلالَةً﴾ الْكَلَالَةُ: مُشْتَقَّةٌ مِنَ الْإِكْلِيلِ، وَهُوَ الَّذِي يُحِيطُ بِالرَّأْسِ مِنْ جَوَانِبِهِ، وَالْمُرَادُ هُنَا مَنْ يَرِثُهُ مِنْ حَوَاشِيهِ لَا أُصُولِهِ وَلَا فُرُوعِهِ، كَمَا رَوَى الشَّعْبِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرٍ الصَّدِيقِ: أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنِ الْكَلَالَةِ، فَقَالَ: أَقُولُ فِيهَا بِرَأْيِي، فَإِنْ يَكُنْ صَوَابًا فَمِنَ اللَّهِ، وَإِنْ يَكُنْ خَطَأً فَمِنِّي وَمِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ، وَاللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ بَرِيئَانِ مِنْهُ: الْكَلَالَةُ مَنْ لَا وَلَدَ لَهُ وَلَا وَالِدَ. فَلَمَّا وَلِيَ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ قَالَ: إِنِّي لِأَسْتَحْيِيَ أَنْ أُخَالِفَ أَبَا بَكْرٍ فِي رَأْيٍ رَآهُ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ وَغَيْرُهُ .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ، فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ الْأَحْوَلِ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عَبَّاسٍ يَقُولُ: كُنْتُ آخِرَ النَّاسِ عَهْدًا بِعُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ، فَسَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ: الْقَوْلُ مَا قُلْتُ، وَمَا قُلْتُ وَمَا قُلْتُ. قَالَ: الْكَلَالَةُ مَنْ لَا وَلَدَ لَهُ وَلَا وَالِدَ .

وَهَكَذَا قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ وَابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ، وَصَحَّ عَنْ غَيْرِ وَجْهٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، وَزَيْدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، وَبِهِ يَقُولُ الشَّعْبِيُّ وَالنَّخَعِيُّ، وَالْحَسَنُ الْبَصْرِيُّ، وَقَتَادَةُ، وَجَابِرُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، وَالْحَكَمُ. وَبِهِ يَقُولُ أَهْلُ الْمَدِينَةِ وَالْكُوفَةِ وَالْبَصْرَةِ. وَهُوَ قَوْلُ الْفُقَهَاءِ السَّبْعَةِ وَالْأَئِمَّةِ الْأَرْبَعَةِ وَجُمْهُورِ السَّلَفِ وَالْخَلْفِ بَلْ جَمِيعِهِمْ. وَقَدْ حَكَى الْإِجْمَاعَ عَلَى ذَلِكَ غَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ، وَوَرَدَ فِيهِ حَدِيثٌ مَرْفُوعٌ. قَالَ أَبُو الْحُسَيْنِ بْنُ اللَّبَّانِ: وَقَدْ رُوِيَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ مَا يُخَالِفُ ذَلِكَ، وَهُوَ أَنَّهُ لَا وَلَدَ لَهُ. وَالصَّحِيحُ عَنْهُ الْأَوَّلُ، وَلَعَلَّ الرَّاوِيَ مَا فُهِمَ عَنْهُ مَا أَرَادَ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلَهُ أَخٌ أَوْ أُخْتٌ﴾ أَيْ: مِنْ أُمٍّ، كَمَا هُوَ فِي قِرَاءَةِ بَعْضِ السَّلَفِ، مِنْهُمْ سَعْدُ بْنُ أَبِي وَقَّاصٍ، وَكَذَا فَسَّرَهَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ الصَّدِيقُ فِيمَا رَوَاهُ قَتَادَةُ عَنْهُ، ﴿فَلِكُلِّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا السُّدُسُ فَإِنْ كَانُوا أَكْثَرَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ فَهُمْ شُرَكَاءُ فِي الثُّلُثِ﴾

وَإِخْوَةُ الْأُمِّ يُخَالِفُونَ بَقِيَّةَ الْوَرَثَةِ مِنْ وُجُوهٍ، أَحَدُهَا: أَنَّهُمْ يَرِثُونَ مَعَ مَنْ أَدْلَوْا بِهِ وَهِيَ الْأُمُّ. الثَّانِي: أَنَّ ذَكَرَهُمْ وَأُنْثَاهُمْ سَوَاءٌ. الثَّالِثُ: أَنَّهُمْ لَا يَرِثُونَ إِلَّا إِذَا كَانَ مَيِّتُهُمْ يُورَثُ كَلَالَةً، فَلَا يَرِثُونَ مَعَ أَبٍ، وَلَا جَدٍّ، وَلَا وَلَدٍ، وَلَا وَلَدِ ابْنٍ. الرَّابِعُ: أَنَّهُمْ لَا يُزَادُونَ عَلَى الثُّلُثِ، وَإِنْ كَثُرَ ذُكُورُهُمْ وَإِنَاثُهُمْ.

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا يُونُسُ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ وَهْب، أَخْبَرَنَا يُونُسُ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ قَالَ: قَضَى عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، أَنَّ مِيرَاثَ الْإِخْوَةِ مِنَ الْأُمِّ بَيْنَهُمْ، لِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلُ الْأُنْثَى قَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ شِهَابٍ الزُّهْرِيُّ: وَلَا أَرَى عُمَرَ قَضَى بِذَلِكَ حَتَّى عَلِمَ بِذَلِكَ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ، وَلِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ الَّتِي قَالَ الله تَعَالَى: ﴿فَإِنْ كَانُوا أَكْثَرَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ فَهُمْ شُرَكَاءُ فِي الثُّلُثِ﴾

وَاخْتَلَفَ الْعُلَمَاءُ فِي الْمَسْأَلَةِ الْمُشْتَرَكَةِ، وَهِيَ: زَوْجٌ، وَأَمٌّ أَوْ جَدَّةٌ، وَاثْنَانِ مَنْ وَلَدِ الْأُمِّ وَوَاحِدٌ أَوْ أَكْثَرُ مَنْ وَلَدِ الْأَبَوَيْنِ. فَعَلَى قَوْلِ الْجُمْهُورِ: لِلزَّوْجِ النِّصْفُ، وَلِلْأُمِّ أَوِ الْجَدَّةِ السُّدُسُ، وَلِوَلَدِ الْأُمِّ الثُّلُثُ، وَيُشَارِكُهُمْ فِيهِ وَلَدُ الْأَبِ وَالْأُمِّ بِمَا بَيْنَهُمْ مِنَ الْقَدْرِ الْمُشْتَرَكِ وَهُوَ إخوةُ الْأُمِّ.

وَقَدْ وَقَعَتْ هَذِهِ الْمَسْأَلَةُ فِي زَمَنِ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ، فَأَعْطَى الزَّوْجَ النِّصْفَ، وَالْأُمَّ السُّدُسَ، وَجَعَلَ الثُّلُثَ لِأَوْلَادِ الْأُمِّ، فَقَالَ لَهُ أَوْلَادُ الْأَبَوَيْنِ: يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ، هَبْ أَنَّ أَبَانَا كَانَ حِمَارًا، أَلَسْنًا مِنْ أُمٍّ وَاحِدَةٍ؟ فَشَرَّكَ بَيْنَهُمْ.

وَصَحَّ التَّشْرِيكُ عَنْهُ وَعَنْ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عُثْمَانَ، وَهُوَ إِحْدَى الرِّوَايَتَيْنِ عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، وَزَيْدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، وَابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ. وَبِهِ يَقُولُ سَعِيدُ بْنُ الْمُسَيَّبِ، وَشُرَيْحٌ الْقَاضِي، وَمَسْرُوقٌ، وَطَاوُسٌ، وَمُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سِيرِينَ وَإِبْرَاهِيمُ النَّخَعِيُّ، وَعُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، وَالثَّوْرِيُّ، وشريك وهو مذهب مالك والشافعي، وإسحاق بن رَاهْوَيْهِ.

وَكَانَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ لَا يُشَرِّكُ بَيْنَهُمْ، بَلْ يَجْعَلُ الثُّلُثَ لِأَوْلَادِ الْأُمِّ، وَلَا شَيْءَ لِأَوْلَادِ الْأَبَوَيْنِ، وَالْحَالَةُ هَذِهِ، لِأَنَّهُمْ عَصَبَةٌ. وَقَالَ وَكِيع بْنُ الْجَرَّاحِ: لَمْ يَخْتَلِفْ عَنْهُ فِي ذَلِكَ، وَهَذَا قَوْلُ أُبَيِّ بْنِ كَعْبٍ وَأَبِي مُوسَى الْأَشْعَرِيِّ، وَهُوَ الْمَشْهُورُ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، وَهُوَ مَذْهَبُ الشَّعْبِيِّ وَابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، وَأَبِي يُوسُفَ، وَمُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَالْحَسَنِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ، وزُفَر بْنِ الهُذيل، وَالْإِمَامِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ حَنْبَلٍ، وَيَحْيَى بْنِ آدَمَ وَنُعَيْمِ بْنِ حَمَّادٍ، وَأَبِي ثَوْرٍ، وَدَاوُدَ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ الظَّاهِرِيِّ، وَاخْتَارَهُ أَبُو الْحُسَيْنِ بْنُ اللَّبَّانِ الْفَرَضِيُّ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ، فِي كِتَابِهِ "الْإِيجَازُ".

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿مِنْ بَعْدِ وَصِيَّةٍ يُوصَى بِهَا أَوْ دَيْنٍ غَيْرَ مُضَارٍّ﴾ أَيْ: لِتَكُونَ وَصِيَّتُهُ عَلَى الْعَدْلِ، لَا عَلَى الْإِضْرَارِ وَالْجَوْرِ وَالْحَيْفِ بِأَنْ يَحْرِمَ بَعْضَ الْوَرَثَةِ، أَوْ يَنْقُصَهُ، أَوْ يَزِيدَهُ عَلَى مَا قدرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ مِنَ الْفَرِيضَةِ فَمَتَى سَعَى فِي ذَلِكَ كَانَ كَمَنْ ضَادَّ اللَّهَ فِي حِكْمَتِهِ وَقِسْمَتِهِ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ:

حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو النَّضْرِ الدِّمَشْقِيُّ الْفَرَادِيسِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا عُمَر بْنُ الْمُغِيرَةِ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ أَبِي هِنْدٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: "الْإِضْرَارُ فِي الْوَصِيَّةِ مِنَ الْكَبَائِرِ".

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ مِنْ طَرِيقِ عُمَرَ بْنِ الْمُغِيرَةِ هَذَا وَهُوَ أَبُو حَفْصٍ بَصْرِيٌ سَكَنَ الْمِصِّيصَةَ، قَالَ أَبُو الْقَاسِمِ ابْنُ عَسَاكِرَ: وَيُعْرَفُ بِمُفْتِي الْمَسَاكِينِ. وَرَوَى عَنْهُ غَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ مِنَ الْأَئِمَّةِ. وَقَالَ فِيهِ أَبُو حَاتِمٍ الرَّازِيُّ: هُوَ شَيْخٌ. وَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْمَدِينِيِّ: هُوَ مَجْهُولٌ لَا أَعْرِفُهُ. لَكِنْ رَوَاهُ النَّسَائِيُّ فِي سُنَنِهِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ ابْنِ حُجْرٍ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مُسْهِر، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ أَبِي هند، عن عكرمة، عن ابن عباس، موقوفًا: "الْإِضْرَارُ فِي الْوَصِيَّةِ مِنَ الْكَبَائِرِ". وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْأَشَجِّ، عَنْ عَائِذِ بْنِ حَبِيبٍ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ أَبِي هِنْدٍ. وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ مِنْ حَدِيثِ جَمَاعَةٍ مِنَ الْحُفَّاظِ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ مَوْقُوفًا وَفِي بَعْضِهَا: وَيَقْرَأُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿غَيْرَ مُضَارٍّ﴾

قَالَ ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ وَالصَّحِيحُ الْمَوْقُوفُ.
