وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَأَخَذْنَ مِنْكُمْ مِيثَاقًا غَلِيظًا﴾ رُوِيَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ وَمُجَاهِدٍ، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ: أَنَّ الْمُرَادَ بِذَلِكَ العَقْد.

وَقَالَ سُفْيَانُ الثَّوْرِيُّ، عَنْ حَبِيبِ بْنِ أَبِي ثَابِتٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَأَخَذْنَ مِنْكُم مِّيثَاقًا [غَلِيظًا] ﴾ قَالَ: قَوْلُهُ: إِمْسَاكٌ بِمَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ تَسْرِيحٌ بِإِحْسَانٍ.

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: وَرُوِيَ عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، وَأَبِي الْعَالِيَةِ، وَالْحَسَنِ، وَقَتَادَةَ، وَيَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي كَثِيرٍ، وَالضَّحَّاكِ وَالسُّدِّيِّ-نَحْوُ ذَلِكَ.

وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ الرَّازِيُّ، عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ فِي الْآيَةِ هُوَ قَوْلُهُ: أَخَذْتُمُوهُنَّ بِأَمَانَةِ اللَّهِ، وَاسْتَحْلَلْتُمْ فُرُوجَهُنَّ بِكَلِمَةِ اللَّهِ، فَإِنَّ "كَلِمَةَ اللَّهِ" هِيَ التَّشَهُّدُ فِي الْخُطْبَةِ. قَالَ: وَكَانَ فِيمَا أَعْطَى النَّبِيُّ ﷺ لَيْلَةَ أُسْرِيَ بِهِ قَالَ لَهُ: جَعَلْتُ أُمَّتَكَ لَا تَجُوزُ لَهُمْ خُطبة حَتَّى يَشْهَدُوا أَنَّكَ عَبْدِي وَرَسُولِي. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ.

وَفِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ فِي خُطبة حِجة الْوَدَاعِ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ فِيهَا: "وَاسْتَوْصُوا بِالنِّسَاءِ خَيْرًا، فَإِنَّكُمْ أَخَذْتُمُوهُنَّ بِأَمَانِ اللَّهِ، وَاسْتَحْلَلْتُمْ فُروجهن بِكَلِمَة اللَّهِ" .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَلا تَنْكِحُوا مَا نَكَحَ آبَاؤُكُمْ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ [إِلا مَا قَدْ سَلَفَ إِنَّهُ كَانَ فَاحِشَةً وَمَقْتًا وَسَاءَ سَبِيلا] ﴾ يُحَرم تَعَالَى زَوْجَاتِ الْآبَاءِ تَكْرِمَةً لهم، وإعذظامًا وَاحْتِرَامًا أَنْ تُوطَأَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ، حَتَّى إِنَّهَا لَتَحْرُمُ عَلَى الِابْنِ بِمُجَرَّدِ الْعَقْدِ عَلَيْهَا، وَهَذَا أَمْرٌ مُجْمَعٌ عَلَيْهِ.

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا مَالِكُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، حَدَّثَنَا قَيْسُ بْنُ الرَّبِيعِ عَنْ أَشْعَثَ بْنِ سَوَّار، عَنْ عَدِيِّ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ قَالَ: لَمَّا تُوُفِّيَ أَبُو قَيْس -يَعْنِي ابْنَ الْأَسْلَتِ-وَكَانَ مِنْ صَالِحِي الْأَنْصَارِ، فَخَطَبَ ابنَه قَيْسٌ امْرَأَتَهُ، فَقَالَتْ: إِنَّمَا أعُدُّكَّ وَلَدًا وَأَنْتَ مِنْ صَالِحِي قَوْمِكَ، وَلَكِنْ آتِي رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَأَسْتَأْمِرُهُ. فَأَتَتْ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ فقالت: إِنَّ أَبَا قَيْسٍ تُوفِّي. فَقَالَ: "خَيْرًا". ثُمَّ قَالَتْ: إِنَّ ابْنَهُ قَيْسًا خَطَبَنِي وَهُوَ مِنْ صَالِحِي قَوْمِهِ. وَإِنَّمَا كُنْتُ أَعُدُّهُ وَلَدًا، فَمَا تَرَى؟ فَقَالَ لَهَا: "ارْجِعِي إِلَى بَيْتِكِ". قَالَ: فَنَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ ﴿وَلا تَنْكِحُوا مَا نَكَحَ آبَاؤُكُمْ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ [إِلا مَا قَدْ سَلَفَ] ﴾ الآية.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا الْقَاسِمُ، حَدَّثَنَا، حُسَيْنٌ، حَدَّثَنَا حَجَّاج، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْج، عَنْ عِكْرمة فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَلا تَنْكِحُوا مَا نَكَحَ آبَاؤُكُمْ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ إِلا مَا قَدْ سَلَفَ﴾ [الْآيَةَ] قال: نزلت في أبي قيس ابن الْأَسْلَتِ، خُلِّفَ عَلَى أُمِّ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بِنْتِ صَخْرٍ وَكَانَتْ تَحْتَ الْأَسْلَتِ أَبِيهِ، وَفِي الْأَسْوَدِ بْنِ خَلَفَ، وَكَانَ خَلَفَ عَلَى ابْنَةِ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ بْنِ عَبْدِ العُزّى بْنِ عُثمان بْنِ عَبْدِ الدَّارِ، وَكَانَتْ عِنْدَ أَبِيهِ خَلَف، وَفِي فاخِتَة ابْنَةِ الْأَسْوَدِ بْنِ الْمُطَّلِبِ بْنِ أَسَدٍ، كَانَتْ عِنْدَ أُمَيَّةَ بْنِ خَلَف، فخُلِّف عَلَيْهَا صفوان ابن أُمَيَّةَ .

وَقَدْ زَعَمَ السُّهيلي أَنَّ نِكَاحَ نِسَاءِ الْآبَاءِ كَانَ مَعْمُولًا بِهِ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿إِلا مَا قَدْ سَلَفَ﴾ كَمَا قَالَ ﴿وَأَنْ تَجْمَعُوا بَيْنَ الأخْتَيْنِ إِلا مَا قَدْ سَلَفَ﴾ قَالَ: وَقَدْ فَعَلَ ذَلِكَ كِنَانة بْنُ خُزَيْمَةَ، تَزَوَّجَ بِامْرَأَةِ أَبِيهِ، فَأَوْلَدَهَا ابْنَهُ النضْر بْنَ كِنَانَةَ قَالَ: وَقَدْ قَالَ ﷺ: "وُلِدتُ مِنْ نِكاحٍ لَا مِنْ سِفَاحٍ". قَالَ: فَدَلَّ عَلَى أنَّه كَانَ سَائِغًا لَهُمْ ذَلِكَ، فَإِنْ أَرَادَ أَنَّ ذَلِكَ كَانَ عِنْدَهُمْ يَعُدُّونَهُ نِكَاحًا فِيمَا بَيْنَهُمْ، فَقَدْ قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْمُخَرَّمِيُّ حَدَّثَنَا قُرَاد، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ عَنْ عُمَرَ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: كَانَ أَهْلُ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ يُحَرِّمون مَا حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ، إِلَّا امْرَأَةَ الْأَبِ وَالْجَمْعَ بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿وَلا تَنْكِحُوا مَا نَكَحَ آبَاؤُكُمْ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ﴾ ﴿وَأَنْ تَجْمَعُوا بَيْنَ الأخْتَيْنِ﴾ وَهَكَذَا قَالَ عَطَاءٌ وَقَتَادَةُ. وَلَكِنْ فِيمَا نَقَلَهُ السُّهَيْلِيُّ مِنْ قِصَّةِ كِنَانَةَ نَظَرٌ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ. عَلَى كُلِّ تَقْدِيرٍ فَهُوَ حَرَامٌ فِي هَذِهِ الْأُمَّةِ، مُبَشَّع غَايَةَ التَّبَشُّعِ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿إِنَّهُ كَانَ فَاحِشَةً وَمَقْتًا وَسَاءَ سَبِيلا﴾ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ [تَعَالَى] ﴿وَلا تَقْرَبُوا الْفَوَاحِشَ مَا ظَهَرَ مِنْهَا وَمَا بَطَنَ﴾ [الْأَنْعَامِ:١٥١] وَقَالَ ﴿وَلا تَقْرَبُوا الزِّنَا إِنَّهُ كَانَ فَاحِشَةً وَسَاءَ سَبِيلا﴾ [الْإِسْرَاءِ:٣٢] فَزَادَ هَاهُنَا: ﴿وَمَقْتًا﴾ أَيْ: بُغْضًا، أَيْ هُوَ أَمْرٌ كَبِيرٌ فِي نَفْسِهِ، وَيُؤَدِّي إِلَى مَقْتِ الِابْنِ أَبَاهُ بَعْدَ أَنْ يَتَزَوَّجَ بِامْرَأَتِهِ، فَإِنَّ الْغَالِبَ أَنَّ مَنْ تَزَوَّجَ بِامْرَأَةٍ يُبْغِضُ مَنْ كَانَ زَوْجَهَا قَبْلَهُ؛ وَلِهَذَا حُرِّمَتْ أُمَّهَاتُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَى الْأُمَّةِ؛ لِأَنَّهُنَّ أُمَّهَاتٌ، لِكَوْنِهِنَّ زَوْجَاتِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، وَهُوَ كَالْأَبِ [لِلْأُمَّةِ] بَلْ حَقُّهُ أَعْظَمُ مِنْ حَقِّ الْآبَاءِ بِالْإِجْمَاعِ، بَلْ حُبُّهُ مُقَدَّمٌ عَلَى حُبِّ النُّفُوسِ صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ وَسَلَامُهُ عَلَيْهِ.

وَقَالَ عَطاء بْنُ أَبِي رَباح فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَمَقْتًا﴾ أَيْ: يَمْقُتُ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ ﴿وَسَاءَ سَبِيلا﴾ أَيْ: وَبِئْسَ طَرِيقًا لِمَنْ سَلَكَهُ مِنَ النَّاسِ، فَمَنْ تَعَاطَاهُ بَعْدَ هَذَا فَقَدِ ارْتَدَّ عَنْ دِينِهِ، فَيُقْتَلُ، وَيَصِيرُ مَالُهُ فَيْئًا لِبَيْتِ الْمَالِ. كَمَا رَوَاهُ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ وَأَهْلُ السُّنَنِ، مِنْ طُرُقٍ، عَنِ الْبَرَاءِ بْنِ عَازِبٍ، عَنْ خَالِهِ أَبِي بُرْدَةَ -وَفِي رِوَايَةِ: ابْنِ عُمَرَ-وَفِي رِوَايَةٍ: عَنْ عَمِّهِ: أَنَّهُ بَعَثَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ إِلَى رَجُلٍ تَزَوَّجَ امْرَأَةَ أَبِيهِ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ أَنْ يَقْتُلَهُ وَيَأْخُذَ مَالَهُ.

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْم، حَدَّثَنَا أَشْعَثُ، عَنْ عَدِيِّ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، عَنِ الْبَرَاءِ بْنِ عازب قال: مرَّ بِي عَمِّي الْحَارِثُ بْنُ عَمْرٍو، وَمَعَهُ لِوَاءٌ قَدْ عَقَدَهُ لَهُ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ: أَيْ عَمِّ، أَيْنَ بَعَثَكَ النَّبِيُّ [ﷺ] ؟ قَالَ: بَعَثَنِي إِلَى رَجُلٍ تَزَوَّجَ امْرَأَةَ أَبِيهِ فَأَمَرَنِي أَنْ أَضْرِبَ عُنُقَهُ .

مَسْأَلَةٌ:

وَقَدْ أَجْمَعَ العلماءُ عَلَى تَحْرِيمِ مَنْ وَطَئَهَا الأبُ بِتَزْوِيجٍ أَوْ مِلْكٍ أَوْ بِشُبْهَةٍ أَيْضًا، وَاخْتَلَفُوا فِيمَنْ بَاشَرَهَا بِشَهْوَةٍ دُونَ الْجِمَاعِ، أَوْ نَظَرَ إِلَى مَا لَا يَحِلُّ لَهُ النَّظَرُ إِلَيْهِ مِنْهَا لَوْ كَانَتْ أَجْنَبِيَّةً. فَعَنِ الْإِمَامِ أَحْمَدَ رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ أَنَّهَا تَحْرُمُ أَيْضًا بِذَلِكَ. قَدْ رَوَى [الْحَافِظُ] ابْنُ عَسَاكِرَ فِي تَرْجَمَةِ خُدَيْج الحِصْنِيّ مَوْلَى مُعَاوِيَةَ قَالَ: اشْتَرَى لِمُعَاوِيَةَ جَارِيَةً بَيْضَاءَ جَمِيلَةً، فَأَدْخَلَهَا عَلَيْهِ مُجَرَّدَةً وَبِيَدِهِ قَضِيبٌ. فَجَعَلَ يَهْوِي بِهِ إِلَى مَتَاعِهَا وَيَقُولُ: هَذَا الْمَتَاعُ لَوْ كَانَ لَهُ مَتَاعٌ! اذْهَبْ بِهَا إِلَى يَزِيدَ بْنِ مُعَاوِيَةَ. ثُمَّ قَالَ: لَا ادْعُ لِي رَبِيعَةَ بْنَ عَمْرٍو الجُرَشِي -وَكَانَ فَقِيهًا-فَلَمَّا دَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ قَالَ: إِنَّ هَذِهِ أُتِيتُ بِهَا مُجَرَّدَةً، فَرَأَيْتُ مِنْهَا ذَاكَ وَذَاكَ، وَإِنِّي أَرَدْتُ أَنْ أَبْعَثَ بِهَا إِلَى يَزِيدَ. فَقَالَ: لَا تَفْعَلْ يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ، فَإِنَّهَا لَا تَصْلُحُ لَهُ. ثُمَّ قَالَ: نِعْمَ مَا رَأَيْتَ. ثُمَّ قَالَ: ادْعُ لِي عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ مَسْعَدَةَ الْفَزَارِيَّ، فَدَعَوْتُهُ، وَكَانَ آدَمَ شَدِيدَ الْأُدْمَةِ، فَقَالَ: دُونَكَ هَذِهِ، بَيض بِهَا وَلَدَكَ. قَالَ: وَ [قَدْ] كَانَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مَسْعَدَةَ هَذَا وَهَبَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ لِابْنَتِهِ فَاطِمَةَ فَرَبَّتْهُ ثُمَّ أَعْتَقَتْهُ ثُمَّ كَانَ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ مَعَ مُعَاوِيَةَ مِنَ النَّاسِ عَلَى عَلِي [بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ] رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ.

---

## 4:23-24 — verses 23-24 (shared commentary)

### Translation

This noble verse is the verse establishing the prohibition of marriage to those forbidden by blood relation, along with what follows from it concerning fosterage and those forbidden through marriage relation, as Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Ahmad ibn Sinan told us, Abd al-Rahman ibn Mahdi told us, from Sufyan ibn Habib, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas, who said: seven are forbidden to you through blood relation and seven through marriage relation, and he recited: {Forbidden to you are your mothers, your daughters, your sisters} to the end of the verse.

And he told us: [chain] Abu Sa'id ibn Yahya ibn Sa'id told us, Abu Ahmad told us, Sufyan told us, from al-A'mash, from Isma'il ibn Raja, from Umayr the freedman of Ibn Abbas, from Ibn Abbas, who said: seven are forbidden through blood relation and seven through marriage relation, then he recited: {Forbidden to you are your mothers, your daughters, your sisters, your paternal aunts, your maternal aunts, the daughters of a brother, and the daughters of a sister}, these are the blood relations.

The majority of scholars have inferred the prohibition of marrying the daughter conceived from the water of an adulterer, upon him, from the general applicability of His statement: {your daughters}, for she is indeed a daughter and thus enters into the general meaning. This is the position of Abu Hanifa, Malik, and Ahmad ibn Hanbal. Something has been related from al-Shafi'i permitting it, on the ground that she is not a legally recognized daughter, and just as she does not enter into His statement {Allah instructs you concerning your children}, since by consensus she does not inherit, likewise she does not enter into this verse. And Allah knows best.

His statement: {and your mothers who nursed you, and your sisters through fosterage}, meaning: just as your mother who bore you is forbidden to you, likewise your mother who nursed you is forbidden to you. For this reason al-Bukhari and Muslim narrated in the two Sahihs, from the hadith of Malik ibn Anas, from Abdullah ibn Abi Bakr ibn Muhammad ibn Amr ibn Hazm, from Amra bint Abd al-Rahman, from A'isha, Mother of the Believers, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Fosterage forbids what birth forbids." And in a wording of Muslim: "What is forbidden through fosterage is what is forbidden through blood relation."

Some jurists have said: just as prohibition occurs through blood relation, so it occurs through fosterage, except in four cases. Others said: six cases, which are mentioned in the books of derived rulings (furu'). The sound view is that nothing at all is to be excepted from this, because the like of some of these cases is found in blood relation itself, and some of them are only forbidden through the aspect of marriage relation, so nothing at all comes in contradiction to the hadith. Praise be to Allah.

The scholars then differed regarding the number of nursings that establish prohibition. Some held that mere fosterage establishes prohibition, based on the general wording of this verse. This is the view of Malik, and it is related from Ibn Umar, and Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, Urwa ibn al-Zubayr, and al-Zuhri held to it as well.

Others said that fewer than three nursings do not establish prohibition, based on what is established in Sahih Muslim, by the route of Hisham ibn Urwa, from his father, from A'isha, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "A single suckling or two sucklings do not forbid."

Qatada said, from Abu al-Khalil, from Abdullah ibn al-Harith, from Umm al-Fadl, who said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Neither one nursing nor two nursings forbid, nor one suckling nor two sucklings forbid." And in another wording: "Neither one milk-feeding nor two milk-feedings forbid." Muslim narrated it.

Among those who held this view were the Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal, Ishaq ibn Rahwayh, Abu Ubayd, and Abu Thawr. It is also related from Ali, A'isha, Umm al-Fadl, Ibn al-Zubayr, Sulayman ibn Yasar, and Sa'id ibn Jubayr, may Allah have mercy on them.

Others said that fewer than five nursings do not establish prohibition, based on what is established in Sahih Muslim, by the route of Malik, from Abdullah ibn Abi Bakr, from Amra, from A'isha, may Allah be pleased with her, who said: among what was revealed of the Qur'an was: ten known nursings establish prohibition. Then this was abrogated by five known nursings, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ died while this remained among what was recited of the Qur'an.

Abd al-Razzaq narrated, from Ma'mar, from al-Zuhri, from Urwa, from A'isha, something similar to that.

And in the hadith of Sahla bint Suhayl: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ commanded her to nurse the freedman of Abu Hudhayfa five nursings. And A'isha used to command whoever wished to visit her to have been nursed five nursings. This is what al-Shafi'i, may Allah have mercy on him, and his companions held. It should further be known that the fosterage must occur during the period of infancy, within the two years, according to the majority view. As we have already discussed this matter in Surat al-Baqara, at His statement: {Mothers shall nurse their children for two complete years, for one who wishes to complete the nursing} [verse 233].

They differed as to whether the milk of the husband (that is, prohibition extending to the father's relatives through the wife's milk) establishes prohibition, as is the position of the majority of the four leading imams and others, or whether fosterage is restricted to the mother's side alone and does not extend to the father's side, as held by some of the early scholars. There are two views on this, and the full treatment of all this is in the book al-Ahkam al-Kabir.

His statement: {and the mothers of your wives, and your stepdaughters who are in your care, from among your wives with whom you have consummated marriage, but if you have not consummated marriage with them then there is no blame upon you}: as for the mother of the wife, she becomes forbidden by mere contract of marriage to her daughter, whether or not consummation occurs. As for the stepdaughter, who is the daughter of the wife, she does not become forbidden by mere contract of marriage to her mother until consummation occurs with the mother. So if he divorces the mother before consummation, it is permissible for him to marry her daughter. For this reason He said: {and your stepdaughters who are in your care, from among your wives with whom you have consummated marriage, but if you have not consummated marriage with them then there is no blame upon you}, meaning in marrying them. This is specific to stepdaughters alone.

Some have understood the pronoun to refer back to both the mothers and the stepdaughters, saying: neither the mother nor the daughter becomes forbidden by mere contract with the other until consummation occurs with her, based on His statement: {but if you have not consummated marriage with them then there is no blame upon you}.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Ibn Bashshar told us, Ibn Abi Adi and Abd al-A'la told us, from Sa'id, from Qatada, from Khilas ibn Amr, from Ali, may Allah be pleased with him, concerning a man who married a woman then divorced her before consummating with her, may he marry her mother? He said: she is in the position of the stepdaughter.

And he told us: [chain] Ibn Bashshar told us, Yahya ibn Sa'id told us, from Qatada, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, from Zayd ibn Thabit, who said: if a man divorces his wife before consummating with her, there is no harm in his marrying her mother.

And in another report from Qatada, from Sa'id, from Zayd ibn Thabit: he used to say, if she died while married to him and he took her inheritance, it was disliked for him to then marry her mother in succession, but if he divorced her before consummating with her, then if he wished he could do so.

Ibn al-Mundhir said: [chain] Ishaq told us, from Abd al-Razzaq, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: Abu Bakr ibn Hafs informed me, from Muslim ibn Uwaymir al-Ajda', that Bakr ibn Kinana informed him that his father had married him to a woman in al-Ta'if. He said: I did not consummate the marriage with her until my paternal uncle, who was married to her mother, died. And her mother was a woman of great wealth. My father said: are you interested in her mother? He said: so I asked Ibn Abbas and told him the story, and he said: marry her mother. He said: then I asked Ibn Umar, and he said: do not marry her. So I informed my father of what Ibn Abbas said and what Ibn Umar said, and he wrote to Mu'awiya and informed him in his letter of what Ibn Umar and Ibn Abbas had said. Mu'awiya wrote back: I do not make lawful what Allah has forbidden, nor do I forbid what Allah has made lawful, and you and that matter, and there are many other women besides her. So he neither forbade nor permitted it to me, and my father turned away from marrying her mother and did not marry her.

Abd al-Razzaq said: Ma'mar informed us, from Simak ibn al-Fadl, from a man, from Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr, who said: the stepdaughter and the mother are the same, there is no harm in it if consummation has not occurred with the woman. In its chain there is an unnamed man who is not identified.

Ibn Jurayj said: Ikrima ibn Khalid informed me that Mujahid said to him regarding {and the mothers of your wives, and your stepdaughters who are in your care}: he meant by both of them that consummation applies to both together. This view, as you see, is related from Ali, Zayd ibn Thabit, Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr, Mujahid, Ibn Jubayr, and Ibn Abbas. Mu'awiya held back from deciding on it, and among the Shafi'is, Abu al-Hasan Ahmad ibn Muhammad al-Sabuni adopted it, as al-Rafi'i related from al-Abbadi.

The majority of the scholars, both the early and later generations, opposed him, holding that the step-daughter is not made unlawful by the mere contracting of marriage with the mother, and that she is not made unlawful except by consummation with the mother. This is unlike the mother, for she is made unlawful by the mere contracting of marriage with the step-daughter.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] from Ibn Abbas, that he used to say: if a man divorced a woman before consummating with her, or she died, her mother was not permitted to him. He said: it (the verse) is unqualified (mubhamah), so he disliked it (marrying her).

He then said: something similar has been related from Ibn Mas'ud, Imran ibn Husayn, Masruq, Tawus, Ikrimah, Ata, al-Hasan, Makhul, Ibn Sirin, Qatadah, and al-Zuhri. This is the position of the four imams, the seven jurists, and the majority of jurists, past and present. Praise and grace belong to God.

Ibn Jarir said: the correct view, meaning the statement of those who said "the mother is among the unqualified (mubhamat) prohibitions," is correct, because God did not stipulate consummation with regard to them as He stipulated it with regard to the mothers of step-daughters, even though this too is a matter of consensus among the authoritative proofs whose agreed position it is not permissible to contradict. A report has also been related on this matter from the Prophet ﷺ, though there is some question regarding its chain of transmission. It is what was related to me by al-Muthanna: [chain] from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "When a man marries a woman, it is not lawful for him to marry her mother, whether he has consummated with the daughter or not. But if he marries the mother and does not consummate with her, then divorces her, he may, if he wishes, marry the daughter."

He then said: even though there is something questionable in the chain of this report, the consensus of the authoritative proofs on the correctness of holding this view makes it unnecessary to seek further corroboration for its validity.

As for His statement, {and your step-daughters who are in your care}, the majority of the imams hold that the step-daughter is forbidden whether she is in the man's care or not. They said: this address is phrased according to the common case, so it carries no restrictive implication, just as in His statement, {And do not compel your slave girls to prostitution if they desire chastity} [al-Nur: 33].

In the two Sahihs, it is related that Umm Habibah said: "O Messenger of God, marry my sister, the daughter of Abu Sufyan," in one wording of Muslim's: "Azzah, the daughter of Abu Sufyan." He said: "Do you love that?" She said: "Yes, I am not the only one for you, and the one I would most love to share in good with me is my sister." He said: "That is not lawful for me." She said: "We have been told that you intend to marry the daughter of Abu Salamah." He said: "The daughter of Umm Salamah?" She said: "Yes." He said: "Even if she were not my step-daughter under my care, she would not be lawful for me. She is indeed the daughter of my brother by fosterage; Thuwaybah nursed me and Abu Salamah both. So do not offer me your daughters or your sisters." In a version of al-Bukhari: "Even if I had not married Umm Salamah, she would not have been lawful for me."

So he made the basis of the prohibition the mere fact of his having married Umm Salamah, and ruled the prohibition on that basis. This is the position of the four imams, the seven jurists, and the majority of the later and early scholars. It has also been said that the step-daughter is not forbidden except if she is in the man's care, and that if she is not so, she is not forbidden.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Malik ibn Aws ibn al-Hadathan said: I had a wife who died, and she had borne children for me, and I grieved over her. Ali ibn Abi Talib met me and said: "What is wrong with you?" I said: "The woman has died." Ali said: "Does she have a daughter?" I said: "Yes, and she is in al-Ta'if." He said: "Was she in your care?" I said: "No, she is in al-Ta'if." He said: "Then marry her." I said: "Then where does the statement of God [Mighty and Majestic] {and your step-daughters who are in your care} come in?" He said: "She was not in your care; that (prohibition) applies only when she is in your care."

This is a strong, established chain of transmission back to Ali ibn Abi Talib, meeting the standard of Muslim, and it is a very unusual (gharib) opinion. Dawud ibn Ali al-Zahiri and his followers held to this. Abu al-Qasim al-Rafi'i related it from Malik, may God have mercy on him, and Ibn Hazm chose it. Our teacher, the hafiz Abu Abdullah al-Dhahabi, told me that he presented this to the shaykh, the imam Taqi al-Din Ibn Taymiyyah, may God have mercy on him, who found it problematic and hesitated over it. God knows best.

Ibn al-Mundhir said: [chain] from Abu Ubaydah, regarding His statement, {who are in your care}: he said, "in your houses."

As for the step-daughter arising from ownership (concubinage), the imam Malik ibn Anas related from Ibn Shihab that Umar ibn al-Khattab was asked about a woman and her daughter, both slaves owned by a man, being had intercourse with, one after the other. Umar said: "I would not like to have known them both," meaning that he would have intercourse with both of them by right of ownership. This report is disconnected (munqati').

Sunayd ibn Dawud said in his tafsir: [chain] from Qays, who said: I said to Ibn Abbas: "May a man have intercourse with a woman and her daughter, both of them his slaves?" He said: "One verse permitted them both and another verse forbade them both, and I would not do it."

The shaykh Abu Umar ibn Abd al-Barr, may God have mercy on him, said: there is no disagreement among the scholars that it is not lawful for anyone to have intercourse with a woman and her daughter from ownership, because God forbade that in the case of marriage, as He said: {and the mothers of your wives, and your step-daughters who are in your care, from your wives}, and ownership follows the rule of marriage, except for what has been related from Umar and Ibn Abbas, and none of the leading authorities of legal opinion nor their followers hold to that. Hisham related from Qatadah: "The daughter of the step-daughter, and the daughter of her daughter, are not permissible, even if she is many generations removed." Qatadah said the same from Abu al-Aliyah.

The meaning of His statement, {whom you have gone in unto}, is: whom you have married (consummated marriage with). This was said by Ibn Abbas and others.

Ibn Jurayj related from Ata: it means that she is brought to him and he uncovers her, examines her, and sits between her legs. I said: "What if he did that in her family's house?" He said: "It is the same; it is enough that this has made her daughter forbidden to him."

Ibn Jarir said: there is a consensus among all that a man's being alone in privacy with his wife does not make her daughter forbidden to him if he divorces her before consummating with her or engaging in intimate contact with her, or before looking at her private parts with desire. This indicates that the meaning intended is reaching her through sexual intercourse.

His statement, {and the wives of your sons who are from your own loins}, means: forbidden to you are the wives of your sons whom you have fathered from your own loins. This serves to exclude the adopted sons whom people used to adopt in the pre-Islamic period, as God said: {So when Zayd had no more need for her, We married her to you, so that there would be no discomfort for the believers regarding the wives of their adopted sons when they have no more need of them} [al-Ahzab: 37].

Ibn Jurayj said: I asked Ata about His statement, {and the wives of your sons who are from your own loins}. He said: we used to relate, and God knows best, that when the Messenger of God ﷺ married the wife of Zayd, the polytheists in Mecca spoke about that, so God [Mighty and Majestic] revealed {and the wives of your sons who are from your own loins}, and there was revealed {He has not made your adopted sons your (real) sons} [al-Ahzab: 4], and there was revealed {Muhammad is not the father of any of your men} [al-Ahzab: 40].

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] from al-Hasan ibn Muhammad, that these verses are unqualified (mubhamat): {and the wives of your sons} and {the mothers of your wives}. He then said: something similar has been related from Tawus, Ibrahim, al-Zuhri, and Makhul.

I say: the meaning of "unqualified" is that it applies generally, whether consummation has occurred or not, so she becomes forbidden by the mere contracting of the marriage. This is agreed upon. If it is asked: on what basis is the wife of one's son by fosterage made forbidden, as is the majority position, and some relate it as a matter of consensus, even though he is not from his own loins? The answer is found in his ﷺ statement: "What is made unlawful by fosterage is what is made unlawful by blood relation."

His statement, {and that you take together two sisters in marriage, except what has already occurred; indeed God is Ever-Forgiving, Merciful}.

That is: it is forbidden for you to combine two sisters together in marriage, and likewise in concubinage (milk al-yamin), except for what occurred among you in your Jahiliyyah (pre-Islamic era), for We have pardoned that and forgiven it. This indicates that there is no dispensation for what lies ahead, nor any exception regarding what has passed, just as He said: {They will not taste death therein except the first death} [al-Dukhan: 56], which indicates that they will never taste death therein at all. The scholars among the Companions, the Successors, and the leading authorities, both early and late, have agreed unanimously that combining two sisters in marriage is forbidden, and that whoever embraces Islam while having two sisters as wives must choose: he keeps one of them and must divorce the other, without any alternative.

Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal said: Musa ibn Dawud told us, Ibn Lahi'ah told us, from Abu Wahb al-Jayshani, from al-Dahhak ibn Fayruz, from his father, who said: "I embraced Islam while I had two wives who were sisters, so the Prophet ﷺ commanded me to divorce one of them."

Imam Ahmad, al-Tirmidhi, and Ibn Majah then all narrated it from the hadith of Ibn Lahi'ah. Abu Dawud and al-Tirmidhi also reported it from the hadith of Yazid ibn Abi Habib, both of them from Abu Wahb al-Jayshani. Al-Tirmidhi said: his name is Daylam ibn al-Hawshab, from al-Dahhak ibn Fayruz al-Daylami, from his father, with the same content. In one wording given by al-Tirmidhi: the Prophet ﷺ said, "Choose whichever of them you wish." Al-Tirmidhi then said: this is a hasan (sound) hadith.

Ibn Majah also narrated it with another chain, saying: Abu Bakr ibn Abi Shaybah told us, Abd al-Salam ibn Harb told us, from Ishaq ibn Abd Allah ibn Abi Farwah, from Abu Wahb al-Jayshani, from Abu Khirash al-Ru'ayni, who said: "I came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ while I had two sisters whom I had married during the Jahiliyyah, so he said: 'When you return, divorce one of them.'"

I [Ibn Kathir] say: it is possible that this Abu Khirash is the same as al-Dahhak ibn Fayruz, and it is possible that he is someone else, in which case Abu Wahb would have narrated it from two different men, from Fayruz al-Daylami. And Allah knows best.

Ibn Marduyah said: Abd Allah ibn Yahya ibn Muhammad ibn Yahya told us, Ahmad ibn Yahya al-Khawlani told us, Haytham ibn Kharijah told us, Yahya ibn Ishaq told us, from Ishaq ibn Abd Allah ibn Abi Farwah, from Ruzayq ibn Hakim, from Kathir ibn Murrah, from al-Daylami, who said: "I said, O Messenger of Allah, I have two wives who are sisters. He said: 'Divorce whichever of them you wish.'"

The al-Daylami mentioned first is al-Dahhak ibn Fayruz al-Daylami, may Allah be pleased with him. Abu Zur'ah al-Dimashqi said: he used to accompany Abd al-Malik ibn Marwan. The second is Abu Fayruz al-Daylami, may Allah be pleased with him, who was among the commanders in Yemen appointed to kill al-Aswad al-Ansi, the false prophet, may Allah curse him.

As for combining two sisters in concubinage, this too is forbidden, on account of the general wording of the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim said:

Abu Zur'ah told us, Musa ibn Isma'il told us, Hammad ibn Salamah told us, from Qatadah, from Abd Allah ibn Abi Inabah, or Utbah, from Ibn Mas'ud: he was asked about a man combining two sisters, and he disliked it. The questioner said to him: "Allah, Mighty and Majestic, says: {except what your right hands possess}." Ibn Mas'ud said to him: "And your camel too is among what your right hand possesses."

This is the well known position of the majority and of the four imams and others, even though some of the early scholars hesitated on this matter. Imam Malik related, from Ibn Shihab, from Qabisah ibn Dhu'ayb: that a man asked Uthman ibn Affan about two sisters held in concubinage, whether they could be combined. Uthman said: "One verse makes them permissible and another verse makes them forbidden, and I would not do such a thing." The man left him and met one of the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ, and asked him about that, and he said: "If I had any authority in the matter and then found someone who did that, I would make an example of him as a deterrent." Malik said: Ibn Shihab said: "I think he meant Ali ibn Abi Talib." He said: and it reached me that al-Zubayr ibn al-Awwam said something similar.

Shaykh Abu Umar ibn Abd al-Barr al-Namari, may Allah have mercy on him, said in his book al-Istidhkar: Qabisah ibn Dhu'ayb only alluded indirectly to Ali ibn Abi Talib because of his association with Abd al-Malik ibn Marwan, for they used to find it burdensome to mention Ali ibn Abi Talib, may Allah be pleased with him, by name.

Abu Umar, may Allah have mercy on him, then said: Khalaf ibn Ahmad, may Allah have mercy on him, related to me by reading, that Khalaf ibn Mutarrif told them: Ayyub ibn Sulayman and Sa'id ibn Sulayman and Muhammad ibn Umar ibn Lubabah said: Abu Zayd Abd al-Rahman ibn Ibrahim told us, Abu Abd al-Rahman al-Muqri told us, from Musa ibn Ayyub al-Ghafiqi, my uncle Iyas ibn Amir told me, who said: "I asked Ali ibn Abi Talib, may Allah be pleased with him, and said: I have two sisters among those whom my right hand possesses. I took one of them as a concubine and she bore me children, then I came to desire the other. What should I do?" Ali, may Allah be pleased with him, said: "You free the one you have been having intercourse with, then you may have intercourse with the other." I said: "But some people say instead that you should marry her off, and then have intercourse with the other." Ali said: "What do you think, if her husband divorced her or died, would she not return to you? It is safer for you to free her." Then Ali took my hand and said to me: "What your right hand possesses is forbidden to you according to what is forbidden to you in the Book of Allah, Mighty and Majestic, regarding free women, except for the matter of number," or he said, "except for the four," "and what is forbidden to you through breastfeeding (rada'ah) is what is forbidden to you in the Book of Allah through blood lineage."

Abu Umar then said: this hadith is worth a journey; even if a man traveled from the furthest east or west to Mecca and obtained nothing else but this, his journey would not have been in vain.

I [Ibn Kathir] say: something similar to what has been related from Uthman has also been related from Ali. Abu Bakr ibn Marduyah said:

Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Ibrahim told us, Muhammad ibn al-Abbas told us, Muhammad ibn Abd Allah ibn al-Mubarak al-Mukharrimi told me, Abd al-Rahman ibn Ghazwan told us, Sufyan told us, from Amr ibn Dinar, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas, who said: Ali ibn Abi Talib said to me: "One verse forbids them and another verse permits them," meaning the two sisters. Ibn Abbas said: "What is forbidden to me among them is my own kinship with them, but the kinship of some of them with one another does not make them forbidden to me," meaning female slaves. And in the Jahiliyyah they used to forbid what you forbid, except for the wife of one's father and the combining of two sisters. Then when Islam came, Allah, Mighty and Majestic, revealed {And do not marry those women whom your fathers married, except what has already occurred} and {and that you combine two sisters, except what has already occurred}, meaning: in marriage.

Abu Umar then said: Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal narrated: Muhammad ibn Salamah told us, from Hisham, from Ibn Sirin, from Ibn Mas'ud, who said: "What is forbidden among female slaves is what is forbidden among free women, except for the matter of number." And something similar has been related from Ibn Sirin and al-Sha'bi.

Abu Umar, may Allah have mercy on him, said: something similar to the statement of Uthman has also been related from a group of the early scholars, among them Ibn Abbas, but there is disagreement reported concerning them, and none of the jurists of the various regions paid any attention to that, neither in the Hijaz, nor in Iraq, nor in the lands beyond them to the east, nor in Syria, nor in the Maghrib, except for someone who deviated from their consensus by following the literal wording and rejecting analogical reasoning (qiyas). Whoever acts upon that has abandoned what we have agreed upon. The body of jurists are agreed that it is not lawful to combine two sisters through concubinage in intercourse, just as it is not lawful in marriage. The Muslims have agreed unanimously that the meaning of His statement, Exalted is He, {Forbidden to you are your mothers, your daughters, your sisters, your paternal aunts, and your maternal aunts} to the end of the verse, is that marriage and concubinage are equal with regard to all of these women. Likewise, then, by consideration and analogy, it must be the case with combining two sisters, the mothers of one's wives, and stepdaughters. And such is indeed the position of the majority of them, and they are the authoritative proof against whoever opposes them and deviates from them. And praise belongs to Allah.

Regarding His saying, Exalted is He: {and married women, except those whom your right hands possess}, that is: forbidden to you are unrelated women who are already married (muhsanat, meaning married women), {except those whom your right hands possess}, meaning: except those whom you come to possess through captivity, for it is lawful for you to have intercourse with them once you have observed their waiting period (istibra'), for the verse was revealed concerning that matter.

Imam Ahmad said: Abd al-Razzaq narrated to us, Sufyan (that is, al-Thawri) informed us, from Uthman al-Batti, from Abu al-Khalil, from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, who said: We obtained women from among the captives of Awtas, and they had husbands. We disliked having relations with them while they had husbands, so we asked the Prophet ﷺ, and this verse came down: {And [forbidden to you are] married women, except those whom your right hands possess} [Al-Nisa: 24]. [He said] So we deemed their private parts lawful for us.

Al-Tirmidhi likewise narrated it from Ahmad ibn Mani', from Hushaym. Al-Nasa'i narrated it from the hadith of Sufyan al-Thawri and Shu'bah ibn al-Hajjaj, all three from Uthman al-Batti. Ibn Jarir narrated it from the hadith of Ash'ath ibn Sawwar, from Uthman al-Batti. Muslim narrated it in his Sahih from the hadith of Shu'bah, from Qatadah, both of them from Abu al-Khalil Salih ibn Abi Maryam, from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, mentioning it as above. Abd al-Razzaq likewise narrated it from Ma'mar, from Qatadah, from Abu al-Khalil, from Abu Sa'id, with the same content.

It has also been narrated from another route, from Abu al-Khalil, from Abu Alqamah al-Hashimi, from Abu Sa'id. Imam Ahmad said:

Ibn Abi Adi narrated to us, from Sa'id, from Qatadah, from Abu al-Khalil, from Abu Alqamah, from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri: that the companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ took captive women on the day of Awtas, who had husbands among the polytheists. It seems that some of the companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ held back and felt it to be sinful to have relations with them. He said: So this verse came down concerning that: {And [forbidden to you are] married women, except those whom your right hands possess}.

Muslim, Abu Dawud, and al-Nasa'i likewise narrated it from the hadith of Sa'id ibn Abi Arubah (Muslim added: and Shu'bah), and al-Tirmidhi narrated it from the hadith of Hammam ibn Yahya, all three from Qatadah, with his chain, in similar wording. Al-Tirmidhi said: This is a hasan (sound) hadith, and I do not know of anyone who mentioned Abu Alqamah in this hadith except what Hammam mentioned from Qatadah. So he said. But Sa'id and Shu'bah corroborated him, and Allah knows best.

Al-Tabarani narrated, by way of al-Dahhak from Ibn Abbas, that it came down concerning the captive women of Khaybar, and he mentioned something similar to the hadith of Abu Sa'id. A group of the predecessors held the view that the sale of a female slave constitutes her divorce from her husband, taking the generality of this verse as their basis. Ibn Jarir said: Ibn al-Muthanna narrated to us, Muhammad ibn Ja'far narrated to us, from Shu'bah, from al-Mughirah, from Ibrahim, that he was asked about a female slave being sold while she has a husband. He said: Abdullah used to say: her sale is her divorce, and he would recite this verse: {And [forbidden to you are] married women, except those whom your right hands possess}.

Sufyan likewise narrated it from Mansur, al-Mughirah, and al-A'mash, from Ibrahim, from Ibn Mas'ud, who said: her sale is her divorce. But this chain is munqati' (broken).

Sufyan al-Thawri said, from Khalid, from Abu Qilabah, from Ibn Mas'ud, who said: If a female slave is sold while she has a husband, then her master has greater right to her intimate relations.

Sa'id narrated it, from Qatadah, who said: Ubay ibn Ka'b, Jabir ibn Abdullah, and Ibn Abbas said: her sale is her divorce.

Ibn Jarir said: Ya'qub narrated to me, [Ibn Ulayyah narrated to us], from Khalid, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas, who said: the divorce of a female slave has six [forms]: her sale is her divorce, her manumission is her divorce, her being given as a gift is her divorce, her being freed from a claim is her divorce, and her husband's divorcing her is her divorce.

Abd al-Razzaq said: Ma'mar informed us, from al-Zuhri, from Ibn al-Musayyab regarding His saying: {And married women among the women}, he said: they are those who have husbands; Allah has forbidden marrying them except what your right hand possesses, so her sale is her divorce. Ma'mar said: al-Hasan said the same.

Sa'id ibn Abi Arubah likewise narrated it, from Qatadah, from al-Hasan, regarding His saying: {And [forbidden to you are] married women, except those whom your right hands possess}, he said: if she has a husband, then her sale is her divorce.

Awf said, from al-Hasan: the sale of a female slave is her divorce, and his sale is her divorce.

This is the view of these predecessors, may Allah have mercy on them. The majority, both in earlier and later times, opposed them, holding that the sale of a female slave is not her divorce, because the buyer stands as a substitute for the seller, and the seller had already removed this benefit from his ownership and sold her stripped of it. They relied in this on the hadith of Barirah, recorded in the two Sahihs and elsewhere: that Aisha, Mother of the Believers, purchased her and immediately freed her, yet her marriage to her husband Mughith was not dissolved. Rather, the Prophet ﷺ gave her the choice between annulment and remaining married, and she chose annulment. Her story is well known. Had the sale of a female slave been her divorce, as those others said, the Prophet ﷺ would not have given her the choice. Since he gave her the choice, this indicates that the marriage remained intact, and that what is meant by the verse is only the captive women, and Allah knows best.

It has also been said that what is meant by His saying {And married women among the women} is: chaste women are forbidden to you until you gain possession of them through marriage, with witnesses, dowries, and a guardian, whether one, two, three, or four. Ibn Jarir related this from Abu al-Aliyah, Tawus, and others. Umar and Ubaydah said: {And married women among the women}, meaning: those beyond the four are forbidden to you, except what your right hands possess.

As for His saying: {It is a decree of Allah upon you}, meaning: this prohibition is a decree that Allah has written upon you, so adhere to His decree, do not depart from its limits, and adhere to His law and what He has made obligatory.

Ubaydah, Ata, and al-Suddi said regarding His saying {It is a decree of Allah upon you}: it means the four [wives]. Ibrahim said: {It is a decree of Allah upon you} means: what He has forbidden to you.

As for His saying: {And lawful to you are those beyond that}, meaning: those beyond the ones mentioned among the forbidden relatives are lawful to you. Ata and others said this. Ubaydah and al-Suddi said: {And lawful to you are those beyond that} means: those fewer than four. This is a far-fetched interpretation, and the correct view is that of Ata, as mentioned above. Qatadah said {And lawful to you are those beyond that} means: what your right hands possess.

This verse is the one used as evidence by those who argued for the permissibility of combining two sisters in marriage, and by those who said: one verse permitted them and another verse forbade them.

As for His saying: {That you seek them in marriage with your wealth, in honest wedlock, not in fornication}, meaning: obtain, with your wealth, wives up to four, or as many concubines as you wish, through the lawful path. Hence He said: {in honest wedlock, not in fornication}.

As for His saying: {And for whatever you enjoy of them, give them their due compensation as an obligation}, meaning: as you enjoy them, give them their dowries in exchange for that, as in His saying: {And how could you take it while you have gone in unto each other} [Al-Nisa: 21], and as in His saying {And give the women their dowries as a gift} [Al-Nisa: 4], and as in His saying: {And it is not lawful for you to take back anything of what you have given them} [Al-Baqarah: 229]. The generality of this verse has been used as evidence for the permissibility of mut'ah marriage (temporary marriage). There is no doubt that it was permitted at the beginning of Islam, then was later abrogated. Al-Shafi'i and a group of scholars held that it was permitted, then abrogated, then permitted again, then abrogated again, twice in total. Others said it happened more times than that. Others said: it was permitted only once, then abrogated, and was not permitted again after that.

It has been narrated from Ibn Abbas and a group of the companions that they held it to be permissible in cases of necessity, and this is also a report from Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal, may Allah have mercy on them. Ibn Abbas, Ubay ibn Ka'b, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, and al-Suddi used to recite: "And for whatever you enjoy of them for a specified term, give them their due compensation as an obligation." Mujahid said: it came down concerning mut'ah marriage. But the majority hold the opposite view, and the basis is what is established in the two Sahihs, from the Commander of the Believers, Ali ibn Abi Talib (may Allah be pleased with him), who said: The Prophet ﷺ forbade mut'ah marriage and forbade the meat of domestic donkeys, on the day of Khaybar. This hadith has established wordings found in the Book of Rulings (Kitab al-Ahkam).

In Sahih Muslim, from al-Rabi ibn Sabrah ibn Ma'bad al-Juhani, from his father: that he campaigned with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ at the conquest of Mecca, and he said: "O people, I had permitted you to engage in temporary enjoyment with women, but Allah has now forbidden that until the Day of Resurrection. So whoever has any of them still with him, let him release her, and do not take back anything of what you have given them." In another report by Muslim, this occurred during the Farewell Pilgrimage, and it has wordings whose place is in the Book of Rulings.

As for His saying: {And there is no blame upon you for what you mutually agree to beyond the obligation}...

Whoever interprets this verse as referring to the temporary marriage (mut'ah) for a specified term says: "There is no blame upon you, when the term has expired, if you mutually agree upon an increase in it and an increase in the payment."

Al-Suddi said: If he wishes, he may satisfy her further, beyond the first stipulated payment, meaning the wage he gave her for his enjoyment of her, before the expiry of the term between them, saying: "I will also enjoy you for such and such," and he increased the payment before he had let her womb clear itself on the day the term expires. This is His saying: {And there is no blame upon you in what you mutually agree upon after the stipulated term}.

Al-Suddi said: When the term expires, he has no claim over her, and she is free of him, and it is upon her to let her womb clear itself, and there is no inheritance between them; neither of them inherits from the other.

Those who hold the first view make its meaning like His saying: {And give the women their dowries as a free gift; but if they, of their own free will, give up any of it to you, then consume it in ease and pleasure} [al-Nisa: 4], meaning: if you have stipulated a dowry for her and she then absolves you of it, or of part of it, there is no blame upon you or upon her in that.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] ... al-Hadrami claimed that there were men who used to stipulate the dowry, and then it might happen that one of them fell into hardship, so He said: {And there is no blame upon you}, O people, {in what you mutually agree upon after the stipulated term}, meaning: if she remits something of it to you, then it is lawful for you. Ibn Jarir preferred this view. And [Ali] ibn Abi Talhah reported from Ibn Abbas: {And there is no blame upon you in what you mutually agree upon after the stipulated term}, and mutual agreement is that he pay her dowry in full and then give her the choice, meaning the choice to remain or to separate.

His saying, {Indeed, Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise}, is fitting to mention these two attributes after the legislation of these grave prohibitions.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ الْكَرِيمَةُ هِيَ آيَةُ تَحْرِيمِ الْمَحَارِمِ مِنَ النَّسَبِ، وَمَا يَتْبَعُهُ مِنَ الرَّضَاعِ وَالْمَحَارِمِ بِالصِّهْرِ، كَمَا قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ سِنان، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ بْنِ حَبِيبٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قال: حُرمت عَلَيْكُمْ سَبْعٌ نَسَبًا، وَسَبْعٌ صِهْرًا، وَقَرَأَ: ﴿حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ أُمَّهَاتُكُمْ وَبَنَاتُكُمْ وَأَخَوَاتُكُمْ﴾ الْآيَةَ.

وَحَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَعِيدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أَحْمَدَ، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ رَجَاءٍ عَنْ عُمَير مَوْلَى ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: يَحْرُمُ مِنَ النَّسَبِ سَبْعٌ وَمِنَ الصِّهْرِ سَبْعٌ، ثُمَّ قَرَأَ: ﴿حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ أُمَّهَاتُكُمْ وَبَنَاتُكُمْ وَأَخَوَاتُكُمْ وَعَمَّاتُكُمْ وَخَالاتُكُمْ وَبَنَاتُ الأخِ وَبَنَاتُ الأخْتِ﴾ فَهُنَّ النَّسَبُ.

وَقَدِ اسْتَدَلَّ جُمْهُورُ الْعُلَمَاءِ عَلَى تَحْرِيمِ الْمَخْلُوقَةِ مِنْ مَاءِ الزَّانِي عَلَيْهِ بِعُمُومِ قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَبَنَاتُكُمْ﴾ ؛ فَإِنَّهَا بِنْتٌ فَتَدْخُلُ فِي الْعُمُومِ، كَمَا هُوَ مَذْهَبُ أَبِي حَنِيفَةَ، وَمَالِكٍ، وَأَحْمَدَ بْنِ حَنْبَلٍ. وَقَدْ حُكيَ عَنِ الشَّافِعِيِّ شَيْءٌ فِي إِبَاحَتِهَا؛ لِأَنَّهَا لَيْسَتْ بِنْتًا شَرْعِيَّةً، فَكَمَا لَمْ تَدْخُلْ فِي قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿يُوصِيكُمُ اللَّهُ فِي أَوْلادِكُمْ﴾ فَإِنَّهَا لَا تَرِثُ بِالْإِجْمَاعِ، فَكَذَلِكَ لَا تَدْخُلُ فِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ. وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَأُمَّهَاتُكُمُ اللاتِي أَرْضَعْنَكُمْ وَأَخَوَاتُكُمْ مِنَ الرَّضَاعَةِ﴾ أَيْ كَمَا تَحْرُمُ عَلَيْكَ أُمُّكَ الَّتِي وَلَدَتْكَ، كَذَلِكَ يَحْرُمُ عَلَيْكَ أُمُّكَ الَّتِي أَرْضَعَتْكَ؛ وَلِهَذَا رَوَى الْبُخَارِيُّ وَمُسْلِمٌ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ مِنْ حَدِيثِ مَالِكِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ حَزْمٍ، عَنْ عَمْرَةَ بِنْتِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ أُمِّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "إِنَّ الرَّضَاعَةَ تُحَرِّمُ مَا تُحَرِّمُ الْوِلَادَةُ"، وَفِي لَفْظٍ لِمُسْلِمٍ: "يَحْرُم مِنَ الرَّضَاعَةِ مَا يَحْرُم مِنَ النَّسَبِ" .

وَقَدْ قَالَ بَعْضُ الْفُقَهَاءِ: كَمَا يَحْرُمُ بِالنَّسَبِ يَحْرُمُ بِالرَّضَاعِ إِلَّا فِي أَرْبَعِ صُوَرٍ. وَقَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ: سِتِّ صُوَرٍ، هِيَ مَذْكُورَةٌ فِي كُتُبِ الْفُرُوعِ. وَالتَّحْقِيقُ أَنَّهُ لَا يُسْتَثْنَى شَيْءٌ مِنْ ذَلِكَ؛ لِأَنَّهُ يُوجَدُ مِثْلُ بَعْضِهَا فِي النَّسَبِ، وَبَعْضُهَا إِنَّمَا يَحْرُمُ مِنْ جِهَةِ الصِّهْرِ، فَلَا يَرِدُ عَلَى الْحَدِيثِ شَيْءٌ أَصْلًا الْبَتَّةَ، وَلِلَّهِ الْحَمْدُ.

ثُمَّ اخْتَلَفَ الْأَئِمَّةُ فِي عَدَدِ الرَّضَعَاتِ الْمُحَرِّمَةِ، فَذَهَبَ ذَاهِبُونَ إِلَى أَنَّهُ يُحَرِّمُ مُجَرَّدُ الرَّضَاعِ لِعُمُومِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ. وَهَذَا قَوْلُ مَالِكٍ، وَيُحْكَى عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، وَإِلَيْهِ ذَهَبَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ المُسَيَّب، وعُرْوَة بْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ، والزُّهْرِي.

وَقَالَ آخَرُونَ: لَا يُحَرِّمُ أَقَلُّ مِنْ ثَلَاثِ رَضَعَاتٍ لِمَا ثَبَتَ فِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "لَا تُحرِّم المصةُ وَالْمَصَّتَانِ" .

وَقَالَ قَتَادَةُ، عَنْ أَبِي الْخَلِيلِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ أُمِّ الْفَضْلِ قَالَتْ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم: "لَا تُحرم الرَّضْعَة وَلَا الرَّضْعَتَانِ، والمصَّة وَلَا الْمَصَّتَانِ"، وَفِي لَفْظٍ آخَرَ: "لَا تُحَرِّمُ الإمْلاجَة وَلَا الْإِمْلَاجَتَانِ" رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ .

وَمِمَّنْ ذَهَبَ إِلَى هَذَا الْقَوْلِ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ بْنُ حَنْبَلٍ، وَإِسْحَاقُ بن رَاهْوَيْهِ، وَأَبُو عُبَيْدٍ، وَأَبُو ثَوْرٍ. وَيُحْكَى عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، وَعَائِشَةَ، وَأُمِّ الْفَضْلِ، وَابْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ، وَسُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ يَسَارٍ، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، رَحِمَهُمُ اللَّهُ.

وَقَالَ آخَرُونَ: لَا يُحَرِّمُ أَقَلُّ مِنْ خَمْسِ رَضَعَاتٍ، لِمَا ثَبَتَ فِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ مِنْ طَرِيقِ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ، عَنْ عَمْرة عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهَا، قَالَتْ: كَانَ فِيمَا أَنْزَلَ [اللَّهُ] مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ: عَشْرُ رَضَعَاتٍ مَعْلُومَاتٍ يُحَرِّمْنَ. ثُمَّ نُسِخْنَ بِخَمْسٍ مَعْلُومَاتٍ، فَتُوُفِّيَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى لِلَّهِ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَهُنَّ فِيمَا يُقْرَأُ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ.

وَرَوَى عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ نَحْوَ ذَلِكَ .

وَفِي حَدِيثِ سَهْلة بِنْتِ سُهَيْلٍ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ أَمَرَهَا أَنْ تُرضِع مَوْلَى أَبِي حُذَيْفَةَ خَمْسَ رَضَعَاتٍ وَكَانَتْ عَائِشَةُ تَأْمُرُ مَنْ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَدْخُلَ عَلَيْهَا أَنْ يُرْضع خَمْسَ رَضَعَاتٍ. وَبِهَذَا قَالَ الشَّافِعِيُّ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ [تَعَالَى] وَأَصْحَابُهُ. ثُمَّ لِيُعْلَمَ أَنَّهُ لَا بُدَّ أَنْ تَكُونَ الرَّضَاعَةُ فِي سِنِّ الصِّغَرِ دُونَ الْحَوْلَيْنِ عَلَى قَوْلِ الْجُمْهُورِ. وَكَمَا قَدَّمْنَا الْكَلَامَ عَلَى هَذِهِ الْمَسْأَلَةِ فِي سُورَةِ الْبَقَرَةِ، عِنْدَ قَوْلِهِ: ﴿يُرْضِعْنَ أَوْلادَهُنَّ حَوْلَيْنِ كَامِلَيْنِ لِمَنْ أَرَادَ أَنْ يُتِمَّ الرَّضَاعَةَ﴾ [الْآيَةَ: ٢٣٣] .

وَاخْتَلَفُوا: هَلْ يُحَرِّمُ لَبَنُ الفَحْل، كَمَا هُوَ قَوْلُ جُمْهُورِ الْأَئِمَّةِ الْأَرْبَعَةِ وَغَيْرِهِمْ؟ وَإِنَّمَا يَخْتَصُّ الرَّضَاعُ بِالْأُمِّ فَقَطْ، وَلَا يَنْتَشِرُ إِلَى نَاحِيَةِ الْأَبِ كَمَا هُوَ لِبَعْضِ السَّلَفِ؟ عَلَى قَوْلَيْنِ، تَحْرِيرُ هَذَا كُلِّهِ فِي كِتَابِ "الْأَحْكَامُ الْكَبِيرُ ".

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَأُمَّهَاتُ نِسَائِكُمْ وَرَبَائِبُكُمُ اللاتِي فِي حُجُورِكُمْ مِنْ نِسَائِكُمُ اللاتِي دَخَلْتُمْ بِهِنَّ فَإِنْ لَمْ تَكُونُوا دَخَلْتُمْ بِهِنَّ فَلا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ﴾ أَمَّا أَمُّ الْمَرْأَةِ فَإِنَّهَا تَحْرُمُ بِمُجَرَّدِ الْعَقْدِ عَلَى ابْنَتِهَا، سَوَاءٌ دَخَلَ بِهَا أَوْ لَمْ يَدْخُلْ. وَأَمَّا الرَّبِيبَةُ وَهِيَ بِنْتُ الْمَرْأَةِ فَلَا تَحْرُمُ بِمُجَرَّدِ الْعَقْدِ عَلَى أُمِّهَا حَتَّى يَدْخُلَ بِهَا، فَإِنْ طَلَّقَ الْأُمَّ قَبْلَ الدُّخُولِ بِهَا جَازَ لَهُ أَنْ يَتَزَوَّجَ بِنْتَهَا، وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿وَرَبَائِبُكُمُ اللاتِي فِي حُجُورِكُمْ مِنْ نِسَائِكُمُ اللاتِي دَخَلْتُمْ بِهِنَّ فَإِنْ لَمْ تَكُونُوا دَخَلْتُمْ بِهِنَّ فَلا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ﴾ [أَيْ] فِي تَزْوِيجِهِنَّ، فَهَذَا خَاصٌّ بِالرَّبَائِبِ وَحْدَهُنَّ.

وَقَدْ فَهِمَ بعضُهم عَوْدَ الضَّمِيرِ إِلَى الْأُمَّهَاتِ [وَ] الرَّبَائِبِ فَقَالَ: لَا تَحْرُمُ وَاحِدَةٌ مِنَ الأم ولا الْبِنْتِ بِمُجَرَّدِ الْعَقْدِ عَلَى الْأُخْرَى حَتَّى يَدْخُلَ بِهَا؛ لِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَإِنْ لَمْ تَكُونُوا دَخَلْتُمْ بِهِنَّ فَلا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ﴾

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ بَشَّارٍ، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ وَعَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ خِلاس بْنِ عَمْرو، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، فِي رَجُلٍ تَزَوَّجَ امْرَأَةً فَطَلَّقَهَا قَبْلَ أَنْ يَدْخُلَ بِهَا، أَيَتَزَوَّجُ أُمَّهَا؟ قَالَ: هِيَ بِمَنْزِلَةِ الرَّبِيبَةِ.

وَحَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ بَشَّارٍ حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ قَالَ: إِذَا طَلَّقَ الرَّجُلُ امْرَأَتَهُ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَدْخُلَ بِهَا فَلَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَتَزَوَّجَ أُمَّهَا.

وَفِي رِوَايَةٍ عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ؛ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ: إِذَا مَاتَتْ عِنْدَهُ وَأَخَذَ مِيرَاثَهَا كُره أَنْ يَخْلُفَ عَلَى أُمِّهَا، فَإِذَا طَلَّقَهَا قَبْلَ أَنْ يَدْخُلَ بِهَا فَإِنْ شَاءَ فَعَلَ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ الْمُنْذِرِ: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْحَاقُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ حَفْصٍ، عَنْ مُسْلِمِ بْنِ عُوَيْمِرٍ الْأَجْدَعِ أَنَّ بَكْرَ بْنَ كِنَانَةَ أَخْبَرَهُ أَنَّ أَبَاهُ أَنْكَحَهُ امْرَأَةً بِالطَّائِفِ قَالَ: فَلَمْ أُجَامِعْهَا حَتَّى تُوُفِّيَ عَمي عَنْ أُمِّهَا، وَأُمُّهَا ذَاتُ مَالٍ كَثِيرٍ، فَقَالَ أَبِي: هَلْ لَكَ فِي أُمِّهَا؟ قَالَ: فَسَأَلْتُ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ وَأَخْبَرْتُهُ الْخَبَرَ فَقَالَ: انْكِحْ أُمَّهَا. قَالَ: فَسَأَلْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ فَقَالَ: لَا تَنْكِحْهَا. فَأَخْبَرْتُ أَبِي مَا قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَمَا قَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ، فَكَتَبَ إِلَى مُعَاوِيَةَ وَأَخْبَرَهُ فِي كِتَابِهِ بِمَا قَالَ ابْنُ عُمَر وَابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ فَكَتَبَ مُعَاوِيَةُ: إِنِّي لَا أُحِلُّ مَا حَرم اللَّهُ، وَلَا أُحَرِّمُ مَا أَحَلَّ [اللَّهُ] وَأَنْتَ وَذَاكَ وَالنِّسَاءُ سِوَاهَا كَثِيرٌ. فَلَمْ يَنْهَ وَلَمْ يَأْذَنْ لِي، فَانْصَرَفَ أَبِي عَنْ أُمِّهَا فَلَمْ يَنْكِحْهَا .

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ: أَخْبَرَنَا مَعْمَر، عَنْ سِمَاك بْنِ الْفَضْلِ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ قَالَ: الرَّبِيبَةُ وَالْأُمُّ سَوَاءٌ، لَا بَأْسَ بِهَا إِذَا لَمْ يُدْخَلْ بِالْمَرْأَةِ. وَفِي إِسْنَادِهِ رَجُلٌ مُبْهَمٌ لَمْ يُسَمَّ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ أَخْبَرَنِي عِكْرِمَةُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ أَنَّ مُجَاهِدًا قَالَ لَهُ: ﴿وَأُمَّهَاتُ نِسَائِكُمْ وَرَبَائِبُكُمُ اللاتِي فِي حُجُورِكُمْ﴾ أَرَادَ بِهِمَا الدُّخُولَ جَمِيعًا فَهَذَا الْقَوْلُ مَرْوِيٌّ كَمَا تَرَى عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، وَزَيْدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، وَعَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، وَابْنِ جُبَيْرٍ وَابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، وَقَدْ تَوَقَّفَ فِيهِ مُعَاوِيَةُ، وَذَهَبَ إِلَيْهِ مِنَ الشَّافِعِيَّةِ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الصَّابُونِيِّ، فِيمَا نَقَلَهُ الرَّافِعِيُّ عَنِ الْعَبَّادِيِّ. [وَقَدْ خَالَفَهُ جُمْهُورُ الْعُلَمَاءِ مِنَ السَّلَفِ وَالْخَلَفِ، فَرَأَوْا أَنَّ الرَّبِيبَةَ لَا تَحْرُمُ بِمُجَرَّدِ الْعَقْدِ عَلَى الْأُمِّ، وَأَنَّهَا لَا تَحْرُمُ إِلَّا بِالدُّخُولِ بِالْأُمِّ، بِخِلَافِ الْأُمِّ فَإِنَّهَا تَحْرُمُ بِمُجَرَّدِ الْعَقْدِ عَلَى الرَّبِيبَةِ] .

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ هَارُونَ بْنِ عَزْرة حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ إِذَا طَلَّقَ الرَّجُلُ امْرَأَةً قَبْلَ أَنْ يَدْخُلَ بِهَا أَوْ مَاتَتْ لَمْ تَحِلَّ لَهُ أُمُّهَا، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: إِنَّهَا مُبْهَمَةٌ، فكرهها.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ورُويَ عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، وَعِمْرَانَ بْنِ حُصَين، وَمَسْرُوقٍ، وَطَاوُسٍ، وَعِكْرِمَةَ، وَعَطَاءٍ، وَالْحَسَنِ، وَمَكْحُولٍ، وَابْنِ سِيرِينَ، وَقَتَادَةَ، وَالزُّهْرِيِّ نَحْوُ ذَلِكَ. وَهَذَا مَذْهَبُ الْأَئِمَّةِ الْأَرْبَعَةِ وَالْفُقَهَاءِ السَّبْعَةِ، وَجُمْهُورُ الْفُقَهَاءِ قَدِيمًا وَحَدِيثًا، وَلِلَّهِ الْحَمْدُ وَالْمِنَّةُ.

قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: وَالصَّوَابُ، أَعْنِي قَوْلَ مَنْ قَالَ: "الْأُمُّ مِنَ الْمُبْهَمَاتِ"؛ لِأَنَّ اللَّهَ لَمْ يَشْرُطْ مَعَهُنَّ الدُّخُولَ كَمَا شَرَطَ ذَلِكَ مَعَ أُمَّهَاتِ الرَّبَائِبِ، مَعَ أَنَّ ذَلِكَ أَيْضًا إِجْمَاعٌ مِنَ الْحُجَّةِ الَّتِي لَا يَجُوزُ خِلَافُهَا فِيمَا جَاءَتْ بِهِ مُتَّفِقَةً عَلَيْهِ. وَقَدْ رُوِيَ بِذَلِكَ أَيْضًا عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ خَبَرٌ، غَيْرَ أنَّ فِي إِسْنَادِهِ نَظَرًا، وَهُوَ مَا حَدَّثَنِي بِهِ الْمُثَنَّى، حَدَّثَنَا حِبَّانُ بْنُ مُوسَى، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ، أَخْبَرَنَا الْمُثَنَّى بْنُ الصَّبَّاحِ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جده عن النبي صلىالله عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: إِذَا نَكَحَ الرَّجُلُ الْمَرْأَةَ فَلَا يَحِلُّ لَهُ أَنْ يَتَزَوَّجَ أُمَّهَا، دُخِلَ بِالْبِنْتِ أَوْ لَمْ يُدْخَلَ، وَإِذَا تَزَوَّجَ الْأُمَّ فَلَمْ يَدْخُلْ بِهَا ثُمَّ طَلَّقَهَا، فَإِنْ شَاءَ تَزَوَّجَ الِابْنَةَ .

ثُمَّ قَالَ: وَهَذَا الْخَبَرُ، وَإِنْ كَانَ فِي إِسْنَادِهِ مَا فِيهِ، فَإِنَّ فِي إِجْمَاعِ الْحُجَّةِ عَلَى صِحَّةِ الْقَوْلِ بِهِ مُسْتَغْنى عَنْ الِاسْتِشْهَادِ عَلَى صِحَّتِهِ بِغَيْرِهِ.

وَأَمَّا قَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَرَبَائِبُكُمُ اللاتِي فِي حُجُورِكُمْ﴾ فَجُمْهُورُ الْأَئِمَّةِ عَلَى أَنَّ الرَّبِيبَةَ حَرَامٌ سَوَاءٌ كَانَتْ فِي حِجْرِ الرَّجُلِ أَوْ لَمْ تَكُنْ فِي حِجْرِهِ، قَالُوا: وَهَذَا الْخِطَابُ خَرَجَ مَخْرَجَ الْغَالِبِ، فَلَا مَفْهُومَ لَهُ كَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَلا تُكْرِهُوا فَتَيَاتِكُمْ عَلَى الْبِغَاءِ إِنْ أَرَدْنَ تَحَصُّنًا﴾ [النُّورِ: ٣٣]

وَفِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ أَنَّ أُمَّ حَبيبة قَالَتْ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، انْكِحْ أُخْتِي بِنْتَ أَبِي سُفْيَانَ -وَفِي لَفْظٍ لِمُسْلِمٍ: عَزَّةَ بِنْتَ أَبِي سُفْيَانَ-قَالَ: "أَوْ تُحِبِّينَ ذَلِكَ؟ " قَالَتْ: نَعَمْ، لَسْتُ لَكَ بمُخْليَة، وَأَحَبُّ مَنْ شَارَكَنِي فِي خَيْرٍ أُخْتِي. قَالَ: "فَإِنَّ ذَلِكَ لَا يَحل لِي". قَالَتْ: فَإِنَّا نُحَدثُ أَنَّكَ تُرِيدُ أَنْ تَنْكِحَ بِنْتَ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ. قَالَ بنْتَ أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ؟ " قَالَتْ نَعَمْ. قَالَ: إِنَّهَا لَوْ لَمْ تَكُنْ رَبِيبَتِي فِي حِجْرِي مَا حَلَّتْ لِي، إِنَّهَا لَبِنْتُ أَخِي مِنَ الرَّضَاعَةِ، أَرْضَعَتْنِي وَأَبَا سَلَمَةَ ثُوَيْبَة فَلَا تَعْرضْن عَلَيَّ بَنَاتِكُنَّ وَلَا أَخَوَاتِكُنَّ". وَفِي رِوَايَةٍ لِلْبُخَارِيِّ: "إِنِّي لَوْ لَمْ أَتَزَوَّجْ أُمَّ سَلَمَةَ مَا حَلَّتْ لِي" .

فَجَعَلَ الْمَنَاطَ فِي التَّحْرِيمِ مُجَرَّدُ تَزْوِيجِهِ أُمَّ سَلَمَةَ وَحَكَمَ بِالتَّحْرِيمِ لِذَلِكَ، وَهَذَا هُوَ مَذْهَبُ الْأَئِمَّةِ الْأَرْبَعَةِ وَالْفُقَهَاءِ السَّبْعَةِ وَجُمْهُورِ الْخَلَفِ وَالسَّلَفِ. وَقَدْ قِيلَ بِأَنَّهُ لَا تَحْرُمُ الرَّبِيبَةُ إِلَّا إِذَا كَانَتْ فِي حِجْرِ الرَّجُلِ، فَإِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ كَذَلِكَ فَلَا تَحْرُمُ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو زُرْعة، حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ مُوسَى، أَنْبَأَنَا هِشَامٌ -يَعْنِي ابْنَ يُوسُفَ-عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، حَدَّثَنِي إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ رِفَاعَةَ، أَخْبَرَنِي مَالِكُ بْنُ أَوْسِ بْنِ الْحَدَثَانِ قَالَ: كَانَتْ عِنْدِي امْرَأَةٌ فَتُوُفِّيَتْ، وَقَدْ وَلَدَتْ لِي، فوجِدْت عَلَيْهَا، فَلَقِيَنِي عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ فَقَالَ: مَالَكَ؟ فَقُلْتُ: تُوُفِّيَتِ الْمَرْأَةُ. فَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ: لَهَا ابْنَةٌ؟ قُلْتُ: نَعَمْ، وَهِيَ بِالطَّائِفِ. قَالَ: كَانَتْ فِي حِجْرِكَ؟ قُلْتُ: لَا هِيَ بِالطَّائِفِ قَالَ: فَانْكِحْهَا. قُلْتُ: فَأَيْنَ قَوْلُ اللَّهِ [عَزَّ وَجَلَّ] ﴿وَرَبَائِبُكُمُ اللاتِي فِي حُجُورِكُمْ﴾ قَالَ: إِنَّهَا لَمْ تَكُنْ فِي حجْرك، إِنَّمَا ذَلِكَ إِذَا كَانَتْ فِي حِجْرِكَ.

هَذَا إِسْنَادٌ قَوِيٌّ ثَابِتٌ إِلَى عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ، عَلَى شَرْطِ مُسْلِمٍ، وَهُوَ قَوْلٌ غَرِيبٌ جِدًّا، وَإِلَى هَذَا ذَهَبَ دَاوُدُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ الظَّاهِرِيُّ وَأَصْحَابُهُ. وَحَكَاهُ أَبُو الْقَاسِمِ الرَّافِعِيُّ عَنْ مَالِكٍ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ، وَاخْتَارَهُ ابْنُ حَزْمٍ، وَحَكَى لِي شَيْخُنَا الْحَافِظُ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الذَّهَبِيُّ أَنَّهُ عَرَض هَذَا عَلَى الشَّيْخِ الْإِمَامِ تَقِيِّ الدِّينِ ابْنِ تَيْمِيَةَ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ، فَاسْتَشْكَلَهُ، وَتَوَقَّفَ فِي ذَلِكَ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ الْمُنْذِرِ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، حَدَّثَنَا الْأَثْرَمُ، عَنْ أَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿اللاتِي فِي حُجُورِكُم﴾ قَالَ: فِي بُيُوتِكُمْ.

وَأَمَّا الرَّبِيبَةُ فِي مِلْكِ الْيَمِينِ فَقَدْ قَالَ الْإِمَامُ مَالِكُ بْنُ أَنَسٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ: أَنَّ عُمَرَ بْنَ الْخَطَّابِ سُئلَ عَنِ الْمَرْأَةِ وَبِنْتِهَا مِنْ مِلْكِ الْيَمِينِ تُوطَأُ إِحْدَاهُمَا بَعْدَ الْأُخْرَى؟ فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: مَا أُحِبُّ أَنْ أَخْبُرَهُمَا جَمِيعًا. يُرِيدُ أَنْ أطَأهُمَا جَمِيعًا بِمِلْكِ يَمِينِي. وَهَذَا مُنْقَطِعٌ.

وَقَالَ سُنَيد بْنُ دَاوُدَ فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ طَارِقِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ قَيْسٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: أَيَقَعُ الرَّجُلُ عَلَى امْرَأَةٍ وَابْنَتِهَا مَمْلُوكِينَ لَهُ؟ فَقَالَ: أَحَلَّتْهُمَا آيَةٌ وَحَرَّمَتْهُمَا آيَةٌ، وَلَمْ أَكُنْ لِأَفْعَلَهُ.

قَالَ الشَّيْخُ أَبُو عُمَر بْنُ عَبْدِ الْبَرِّ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ: لَا خِلَافَ بَيْنِ الْعُلَمَاءِ أنَّه لَا يَحِلُّ لِأَحَدٍ أَنْ يَطَأَ امْرَأَةً وَابْنَتَهَا مِنْ مِلْكِ الْيَمِينِ، لِأَنَّ اللَّهَ حَرَّمَ ذَلِكَ فِي النِّكَاحِ، قَالَ: ﴿وَأُمَّهَاتُ نِسَائِكُمْ وَرَبَائِبُكُمُ اللاتِي فِي حُجُورِكُمْ مِنْ نِسَائِكُمُ﴾ وَمِلْكُ الْيَمِينِ هُمْ تَبَعٌ لِلنِّكَاحِ، إِلَّا مَا رُوِيَ عَنْ عُمَر وَابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، وَلَيْسَ عَلَى ذَلِكَ أَحَدٌ مِنْ أَئِمَّةِ الْفَتْوَى وَلَا مَنْ تَبِعَهُمْ. وَرَوَى هِشَامٌ عَنْ قَتَادَةَ: بِنْتُ الرَّبِيبَةِ وَبِنْتُ ابْنَتِهَا لَا تَصْلُحُ وَإِنْ كَانَتْ أَسْفَلَ بِبُطُونٍ كَثِيرَةٍ. وَكَذَا قَالَ قَتَادَةُ عَنْ أَبِي الْعَالِيَةِ.

وَمَعْنَى قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿اللاتِي دَخَلْتُمْ بِهِنَّ﴾ أَيْ: نَكَحْتُمُوهُنَّ. قَالَهُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَغَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جُرَيْجٍ عَنْ عَطَاءٍ: هُوَ أَنْ تُهْدَى إِلَيْهِ فَيَكْشِفُ وَيُفَتِّشُ وَيَجْلِسُ بَيْنَ رِجْلَيْهَا. قُلْتُ: أَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ فَعَلَ ذَلِكَ فِي بَيْتِ أَهْلِهَا. قَالَ: هُوَ سَوَاءٌ، وحسبه قد حرم ذلك عليه ابنتها.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: وَفِي إِجْمَاعِ الْجَمِيعِ عَلَى أَنَّ خَلْوَةَ الرَّجُلِ بِامْرَأَتِهِ لَا يُحرم ابْنَتَهَا عَلَيْهِ إِذَا طَلَّقَهَا قَبْلَ مَسِيسِهَا ومُبَاشرتها أَوْ قَبْلَ النَّظَرِ إِلَى فَرْجِهَا بِشَهْوَةٍ، مَا يَدُلُّ عَلَى أَنَّ مَعْنَى ذَلِكَ هُوَ الْوُصُولُ إِلَيْهَا بِالْجِمَاعِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَحَلائِلُ أَبْنَائِكُمُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ أَصْلابِكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: وحُرمت عَلَيْكُمْ زَوْجَاتُ أَبْنَائِكُمُ الَّذِينَ وَلَّدْتُمُوهُمْ مِنْ أَصْلَابِكُمْ، يُحْتَرَزُ بِذَلِكَ عَنِ الْأَدْعِيَاءِ الَّذِينَ كَانُوا يَتَبَنَونهم فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَلَمَّا قَضَى زَيْدٌ مِنْهَا وَطَرًا زَوَّجْنَاكَهَا لِكَيْ لَا يَكُونَ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ حَرَجٌ فِي أَزْوَاجِ أَدْعِيَائِهِمْ [إِذَا قَضَوْا مِنْهُنَّ وَطَرًا] ﴾ الْآيَةَ [الْأَحْزَابِ: ٣٧] .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جُرَيْج: سَأَلْتُ عَطَاءً عَنْ قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَحَلائِلُ أَبْنَائِكُمُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ أَصْلابِكُمْ﴾ قَالَ: كُنَّا نُحَدِّث، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ لَمَّا نَكَحَ امْرَأَةَ زَيْدٍ، قَالَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ بِمَكَّةَ فِي ذَلِكَ، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ [عَزَّ وَجَلَّ] ﴿وَحَلائِلُ أَبْنَائِكُمُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ أَصْلابِكُمْ﴾ وَنَزَلَتْ: ﴿وَمَا جَعَلَ أَدْعِيَاءَكُمْ أَبْنَاءَكُمْ﴾ [الْأَحْزَابِ: ٤] . وَنَزَلَتْ: ﴿مَا كَانَ مُحَمَّدٌ أَبَا أَحَدٍ مِنْ رِجَالِكُمْ﴾ [الْأَحْزَابِ: ٤٠] .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو زُرْعَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي بَكْرٍ الْمُقَدِّمِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا الْجُرْحُ بْنُ الْحَارِثِ، عَنِ الْأَشْعَثِ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ أَنَّ هَؤُلَاءِ الْآيَاتِ مُبْهَمَاتٍ: ﴿وَحَلائِلُ أَبْنَائِكُمُ﴾ ﴿أُمَّهَاتُ نِسَائِكُم﴾ ثُمَّ قَالَ: وَرُوِيَ عَنْ طَاوُسٍ وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ وَالزُّهْرِيِّ وَمَكْحُولٍ نَحْوُ ذَلِكَ.

قُلْتُ: مَعْنَى مُبْهَمَاتٍ: أَيْ عَامَّةٍ فِي الْمَدْخُولِ بِهَا وَغَيْرِ الْمَدْخُولِ، فَتَحْرُمُ بِمُجَرَّدِ الْعَقْدِ عَلَيْهَا، وَهَذَا مُتَّفَقٌ عَلَيْهِ. فَإِنْ قِيلَ: فَمِنْ أَيْنَ تَحْرُمُ امْرَأَةُ ابْنِهِ مِنَ الرَّضَاعَةِ، كَمَا هُوَ قَوْلُ الْجُمْهُورِ، وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَنْ يَحْكِيهِ إِجْمَاعًا وَلَيْسَ مِنْ صُلْبِهِ؟ فَالْجَوَابُ مِنْ قَوْلِهِ ﷺ: "يَحْرُم مِنَ الرَّضَاعِ مَا يَحْرُمُ مِنَ النَّسَبِ".

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَأَنْ تَجْمَعُوا بَيْنَ الأخْتَيْنِ إِلا مَا قَدْ سَلَفَ [إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا] ﴾ أَيْ: وَحُرِّمَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْجَمْعُ بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ مَعًا فِي التَّزْوِيجِ، وَكَذَا فِي مِلْكِ الْيَمِينِ إِلَّا مَا كَانَ مِنْكُمْ فِي جَاهِلِيَّتِكُمْ فَقَدْ عَفَوْنَا عَنْ ذَلِكَ وَغَفَرْنَاهُ. فَدَلَّ عَلَى أَنَّهُ لَا مَثْنَوِيَّةَ فِيمَا يُسْتَقْبَلُ وَلَا اسْتِثْنَاءَ فِيمَا سَلَفَ، كَمَا قَالَ: ﴿لَا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا الْمَوْتَ إِلا الْمَوْتَةَ الأولَى﴾ [الدُّخَانِ:٥٦] فَدَلَّ عَلَى أَنَّهُمْ لَا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا الْمَوْتَ أَبَدًا. وَقَدْ أَجْمَعَ الْعُلَمَاءُ مِنَ الصَّحَابَةِ وَالتَّابِعَيْنِ وَالْأَئِمَّةِ قَدِيمًا وَحَدِيثًا عَلَى أَنَّهُ يَحْرُمُ الْجَمْعُ بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ فِي النِّكَاحِ، وَمَنْ أَسْلَمَ وَتَحْتَهُ أُخْتَانِ خُيِّرَ، فَيُمْسِكُ إِحْدَاهُمَا وَيُطَلِّقُ الْأُخْرَى لَا مَحَالَةَ.

قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ بْنُ حَنْبَلٍ: حَدَّثَنَا مُوسَى بْنُ دَاوُدَ حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ لَهِيعة عَنْ أَبِي وهْب الجيْشاني عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ بْنِ فَيْرُوزَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ: أَسْلَمْتُ وَعِنْدِي امْرَأَتَانِ أُخْتَانِ، فأمَرني النَّبِيُّ ﷺ أَنْ أُطَلِّقَ إِحْدَاهُمَا .

ثُمَّ رَوَاهُ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ، وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ، وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ ابْنِ لَهِيعَةَ. وَأَخْرَجَهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ أَيْضًا مِنْ حَدِيثِ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي حَبِيبٍ، كِلَاهُمَا عَنْ أَبِي وَهْبٍ الجَيْشاني. قَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: وَاسْمُهُ دَيْلَمُ بْنُ الهُوشَع، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ بْنِ فَيْرُوزَ الدَّيْلَمِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، بِهِ وَفِي لَفْظٍ لِلتِّرْمِذِيِّ: فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "اخْتَرْ أَيَّتَهُمَا شِئْتَ". ثُمَّ قَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: هَذَا حَدِيثٌ حَسَنٌ .

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَاجَهْ أَيْضًا بِإِسْنَادٍ آخَرَ فَقَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ السَّلَامِ بْنُ حَرْبٍ، عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي فَرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي وَهْبٍ الْجَيْشَانِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي خِرَاشٍ الرُّعَيْني قَالَ: قَدِمْتُ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ وَعِنْدِي أُخْتَانِ تَزَوجْتُهما فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ، فَقَالَ: "إِذَا رَجَعْتَ فَطلقْ إِحْدَاهُمَا " .

قُلْتُ: فَيُحْتَمَلُ أَنَّ أَبَا خِرَاشٍ هَذَا هُوَ الضَّحَّاكُ بْنُ فَيْرُوزَ، وَيُحْتَمَلُ أَنْ يَكُونَ غَيْرُهُ، فَيَكُونُ أَبُو وَهْبٍ قَدْ رَوَاهُ عَنِ اثْنَيْنِ، عَنْ فَيْرُوزَ الدَّيْلَمِيِّ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ مَرْدويه: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ يَحْيَى بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى، حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى الْخَوْلَانِيُّ حَدَّثَنَا هَيْثَمُ بْنُ خَارِجَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي فَرْوة عَنْ رُزَيق بْنِ حَكِيمٍ، عَنْ كَثِيرِ بْنِ مُرَّةَ، عَنِ الدَّيْلَمِيِّ قَالَ: قُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنَّ تَحْتِي أُخْتَيْنِ؟ قَالَ: "طَلق أَيَّهُمَا شِئْتَ" .

فَالدَّيْلَمِيُّ الْمَذْكُورُ أَوَّلًا هُوَ الضَّحَّاكُ بْنُ فَيْرُوزَ الدَّيْلَمِيُّ [رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ] قَالَ أَبُو زُرْعَةَ الدِّمَشْقِيُّ: كَانَ يَصْحَبُ عَبْدَ الْمَلِكِ بْنَ مَرْوَانَ، وَالثَّانِي هُوَ أَبُو فَيْرُوزَ الدَّيْلَمِيُّ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، وَكَانَ مِنْ جُمْلَةِ الْأُمَرَاءِ بِالْيَمَنِ الَّذِينَ وُلُّوا قَتْلَ الْأَسْوَدِ الْعَنْسِيِّ الْمُتَنَبِّئِ لَعَنَهُ اللَّهُ.

وَأَمَّا الْجَمْعُ بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ فِي مِلْكِ الْيَمِينِ فَحَرَامٌ أَيْضًا لِعُمُومِ الْآيَةِ، وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ:

حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو زُرْعَة، حَدَّثَنَا مُوسَى بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي عِنَبَةَ -أَوْ عُتْبَةَ عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ: أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَجْمَعُ بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ، فَكَرِهَهُ، فَقَالَ لَهُ-يَعْنِي السَّائِلَ-: يَقُولُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: ﴿إِلا مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ﴾ فَقَالَ لَهُ ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ: وَبَعِيرُكَ مما ملكت يمينك.

وَهَذَا هُوَ الْمَشْهُورُ عَنِ الْجُمْهُورِ وَالْأَئِمَّةِ الْأَرْبَعَةِ وَغَيْرِهِمْ، وَإِنْ كَانَ بَعْضُ السَّلَفِ قَدْ تَوَقَّفَ فِي ذَلِكَ. قَالَ الْإِمَامُ مَالِكٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ، عَنْ قَبيصة بْنِ ذُؤيب: أَنَّ رَجُلًا سَأَلَ عُثْمَانَ بْنَ عَفَّانٍ عَنِ الْأُخْتَيْنِ فِي مِلْكِ الْيَمِينِ، هَلْ يُجْمَعُ بَيْنَهُمَا؟ فَقَالَ عُثْمَانُ: أَحَلَّتْهُمَا آيَةٌ وحَرمتهما آيَةٌ، وَمَا كُنْتُ لِأَصْنَعَ ذَلِكَ، فَخَرَجَ مِنْ عِنْدِهِ فَلَقِيَ رَجُلًا مِنْ أَصْحَابِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، فَسَأَلَهُ عَنْ ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ: لَوْ كَانَ لِي مِنَ الْأَمْرِ شَيْءٌ ثُمَّ وَجَدْتُ أَحَدًا فَعَلَ ذَلِكَ لَجَعَلْتُهُ نَكَالًا. قَالَ مَالِكٌ: قَالَ ابْنُ شِهَابٍ: أرَاه عَلِيَّ بْنَ أَبِي طَالِبٍ: قَالَ: وَبَلَغَنِي عَنِ الزُّبَيْرِ بْنِ الْعَوَّامِ مَثَلُ ذَلِكَ.

قَالَ الشَّيْخُ أَبُو عُمَرَ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْبَرِّ النَّمَري، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ، فِي كِتَابِهِ "الِاسْتِذْكَارِ": إِنَّمَا كَنَّى قَبِيصَةَ بْنَ ذُؤيب عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ، لِصُحْبَتِهِ عَبْدَ الْمَلِكِ بْنَ مَرْوَانَ، وَكَانُوا يَسْتَثْقِلُونَ ذِكْرَ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ أَبُو عُمَرَ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ: حَدَّثَنِي خَلَفُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ، قِرَاءَةً عَلَيْهِ: أَنَّ خَلَفَ بْنَ مُطَرِّفٍ حَدَّثَهُمْ: حَدَّثَنَا أَيُّوبُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ وَسَعِيدُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ وَمُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عُمَرَ بْنِ لُبَابَةَ قَالُوا: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو زَيْدٍ عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْمُقْرِيُّ عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ أَيُّوبَ الْغَافِقِيِّ، حَدَّثَنِي عَمِّي إِيَاسُ بْنُ عَامِرٍ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عَلِيَّ بْنَ أَبِي طَالِبٍ [رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ] فَقُلْتُ: إِنَّ لِي أُخْتَيْنِ مِمَّا مَلَكَتْ يَمِينِي، اتَّخَذْتُ إِحْدَاهُمَا سُرِّيَّةً فَوَلَدَتْ لِي أَوْلَادًا، ثُمَّ رَغِبْتُ فِي الْأُخْرَى، فَمَا أَصْنَعُ؟ فَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ: تَعْتِقُ الَّتِي كُنْتَ تطَأُ ثُمَّ تَطَأُ الْأُخْرَى. قُلْتُ: فَإِنَّ نَاسًا يَقُولُونَ: بَلْ تَزَوّجها ثُمَّ تَطَأُ الْأُخْرَى. فَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ: أَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ طَلَّقَهَا زَوْجُهَا أَوْ مَاتَ عَنْهَا أَلَيْسَ تَرْجِعُ إِلَيْكَ؟ لَأَنْ تَعْتِقَهَا أَسْلَمُ لَكَ. ثُمَّ أَخَذَ عَلِيٌّ بِيَدِي فَقَالَ لِي: إِنَّهُ يَحْرُمُ عَلَيْكَ مَا مَلَكَتْ يَمِينُكَ مَا يَحْرُمُ عَلَيْكَ فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ مِنَ الْحَرَائِرِ إِلَّا الْعَدَدَ -أَوْ قَالَ: إِلَّا الْأَرْبَعَ-ويَحْرُم عَلَيْكَ مِنَ الرَّضَاعِ مَا يَحْرُمُ عَلَيْكَ فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ مِنَ النَّسَبِ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ أَبُو عُمَرَ: هَذَا الْحَدِيثُ رِحْلَةٌ لَوْ لَمْ يُصِبِ الرَّجُلُ مِنْ أَقْصَى الْمَشْرِقِ أَوِ الْمَغْرِبِ إِلَى مَكَّةَ غَيْرَهُ لَمَا خَابَتْ رِحْلَتُهُ .

قُلْتُ: وَقَدْ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ نَحْوُ مَا تَقَدَّمَ عَنْ عُثْمَانَ، وَقَالَ أَبُو بَكْرِ بن مردويه:

حدثنا محمد بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْعَبَّاسِ، حَدَّثَنِي مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْمُبَارَكِ الْمُخَرِّمِيُّ حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ غَزْوان، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ عَمْرو بْنِ دِينَارٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: قَالَ لِي عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ: حَرَّمَتْهُمَا آيَةٌ وَأَحَلَّتْهُمَا آيَةٌ -يَعْنِي الْأُخْتَيْنِ-قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: يُحَرِّمْهُنَّ عَلَيَّ قَرَابَتِي مِنْهُنَّ، وَلَا يُحَرِّمْهُنَّ عَلَىَّ قَرَابَةُ بَعْضِهِنَّ مِنْ بَعْضٍ -يَعْنِي الْإِمَاءَ-وَكَانَتِ الْجَاهِلِيَّةُ يُحَرِّمُونَ مَا تُحَرَّمون إِلَّا امْرَأَةَ الْأَبِ وَالْجَمْعَ بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ، فَلَمَّا جاء الإسلام أنزل الله [عز وَجَلَّ] ﴿وَلا تَنْكِحُوا مَا نَكَحَ آبَاؤُكُمْ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ إِلا مَا قَدْ سَلَفَ﴾ ﴿وَأَنْ تَجْمَعُوا بَيْنَ الأخْتَيْنِ إِلا مَا قَدْ سَلَفَ﴾ يَعْنِي: فِي النِّكَاحِ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ أَبُو عُمَرَ: رَوَى الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ بْنُ حَنْبَلٍ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ: يَحْرُمُ مِنَ الْإِمَاءِ مَا يَحْرُمُ مِنَ الْحَرَائِرِ إِلَّا الْعَدَدَ. وَعَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ وَالشَّعْبِيِّ مَثَلُ ذَلِكَ.

قَالَ أَبُو عُمَرَ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ: وَقَدْ رُوِيَ مِثْلُ قَوْلِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ طَائِفَةٍ مِنَ السَّلَفِ، مِنْهُمُ: ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ، وَلَكِنَّهُمُ اخْتُلِفَ عَلَيْهِمْ، وَلَمْ يَلْتَفِتْ إِلَى ذَلِكَ أَحَدٌ مِنْ فُقَهَاءِ الْأَمْصَارِ وَالْحِجَازِ وَلَا بِالْعِرَاقِ وَلَا مَا وَرَاءَهُمَا مِنَ الْمَشْرِقِ وَلَا بِالشَّامِ وَلَا الْمَغْرِبِ، إِلَّا مَنْ شَذَّ عَنْ جَمَاعَتِهِمْ بِاتِّبَاعِ الظَّاهِرِ ونَفْي الْقِيَاسِ، وَقَدْ تَرَكَ مَنْ يَعْمَلُ ذَلِكَ مَا اجْتَمَعْنَا عَلَيْهِ، وَجَمَاعَةُ الْفُقَهَاءِ مُتَّفِقُونَ عَلَى أَنَّهُ لَا يَحِلُّ الْجَمْعُ بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ بِمِلْكِ الْيَمِينِ فِي الْوَطْءِ، كَمَا لَا يَحِلُّ ذَلِكَ فِي النِّكَاحِ. وَقَدْ أَجْمَعَ الْمُسْلِمُونَ عَلَى أَنَّ مَعْنَى قَوْلِهِ [تَعَالَى] ﴿حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ أُمَّهَاتُكُمْ وَبَنَاتُكُمْ وَأَخَوَاتُكُمْ [وَعَمَّاتُكُمْ وَخَالاتُكُمْ] ﴾ إِلَى آخِرِ الْآيَةِ: أَنَّ النِّكَاحَ وَمِلْكَ الْيَمِينِ فِي هَؤُلَاءِ كُلِّهِنَّ سَوَاءٌ، فَكَذَلِكَ يَجِبُ أَنْ يَكُونَ نَظَرًا وَقِيَاسًا الْجَمْعُ بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ وَأُمَّهَاتِ النِّسَاءِ وَالرَّبَائِبِ. وَكَذَلِكَ هُوَ عِنْدَ جُمْهُورِهِمْ، وَهُمُ الْحُجَّةُ الْمَحْجُوجُ بِهَا مَنْ خَالَفَهَا وَشَذَّ عَنْهَا، وَاللَّهُ الْمَحْمُودُ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ [تَعَالَى] ﴿وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ إِلا مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: وَحُرِّمَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْأَجْنَبِيَّاتُ الْمُحْصَنَاتُ وهي الْمُزَوَّجَاتُ ﴿إِلا مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ﴾ يَعْنِي: إِلَّا مَا مَلَكْتُمُوهُنَّ بِالسَّبْيِ، فَإِنَّهُ يَحِلُّ لَكُمْ وَطْؤُهُنَّ إِذَا اسْتَبْرَأْتُمُوهُنَّ، فَإِنَّ الْآيَةَ نَزَلَتْ فِي ذَلِكَ.

قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، أَخْبَرَنَا سُفْيَانُ -هُوَ الثَّوْرِيُّ-عَنْ عُثْمَانَ البَتِّي، عَنْ أَبِي الْخَلِيلِ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ قَالَ: أَصَبْنَا نِسَاءً مِنْ سَبْيِ أَوْطَاسَ، وَلَهُنَّ أَزْوَاجٌ، فَكَرِهْنَا أَنْ نَقَعَ عَلَيْهِنَّ وَلَهُنَّ أَزْوَاجٌ، فَسَأَلْنَا النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَسَلَّمَ، فَنَزَلَتْ هذه الآية: ﴿وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ إِلا مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ﴾ [قَالَ] فَاسْتَحْلَلْنَا فُرُوجَهُنَّ.

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مَنِيعٍ، عَنْ هُشَيم، وَرَوَاهُ النَّسَائِيُّ مِنْ حَدِيثِ سُفْيَانَ الثَّوْرِيِّ وَشُعْبَةَ بْنِ الْحَجَّاجِ، ثَلَاثَتُهُمْ عَنْ عُثْمَانَ الْبَتِّيِّ، وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ مِنْ حَدِيثِ أَشْعَثَ بْنِ سَوَّارِيِّ عَنْ عُثْمَانَ الْبَتِّيِّ، وَرَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ فِي صَحِيحِهِ مِنْ حَدِيثِ شُعْبَةَ عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، كِلَاهُمَا عَنْ أَبِي الْخَلِيلِ صَالِحِ بْنِ أَبِي مَرْيَمَ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ، فَذَكَرَهُ، وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ عَنْ أَبِي الخليل، عن أبي سعيد، به .

وَقَدْ رُوِيَ مِنْ وَجْهٍ آخَرَ عَنْ أَبِي الْخَلِيلِ، عَنْ أَبِي عَلْقَمَةَ الْهَاشِمِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ:

حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي عَدِيّ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْخَلِيلِ، عَنْ أَبِي عَلْقَمَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ؛ أَنَّ أَصْحَابَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ أَصَابُوا سَبَايَا يَوْمَ أَوَطَاسَ، لَهُنَّ أَزْوَاجٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الشِّرْكِ، فَكَأَنَّ أُنَاسًا مِنْ أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم كَفُّوا وَتَأَثَّمُوا مِنْ غَشَيَانِهِنَّ قَالَ: فَنَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ فِي ذَلِكَ: ﴿وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ إِلا مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ﴾

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ وَأَبُو دَاوُدَ وَالنَّسَائِيُّ مِنْ حَدِيثِ سَعِيدِ بْنِ أَبِي عَرُوبة -زَادَ مُسْلِمٌ: وَشُعْبَةُ-وَرَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ مِنْ حَدِيثِ هَمَّامِ بْنِ يَحْيَى، ثَلَاثَتُهُمْ عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، بِإِسْنَادِهِ نَحْوَهُ. وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: هَذَا حَدِيثٌ حَسَنٌ، وَلَا أَعْلَمُ أَنَّ أَحَدًا ذَكَرَ أَبَا عَلْقَمَةَ فِي هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ إِلَّا مَا ذَكَرَ هَمَّامٌ عَنْ قَتَادَةَ. كَذَا قَالَ. وَقَدْ تَابَعَهُ سَعِيدٌ وَشُعْبَةُ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ .

وَقَدْ رَوَى الطَّبَرَانِيُّ مِنْ طَرِيقِ الضَّحَّاكِ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: أَنَّهَا نَزَلَتْ فِي سَبَايَا خَيْبَرَ، وَذَكَرَ مِثْلَ حَدِيثِ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ، وَقَدْ ذَهَبَ جَمَاعَةٌ مِنَ السَّلَفِ إِلَى أَنَّ بَيْعَ الْأَمَةِ يَكُونُ طَلَاقًا لَهَا مِنْ زَوْجِهَا، أَخْذًا بِعُمُومِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ. قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُثَنَّى، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ: أَنَّهُ سُئل عَنِ الْأَمَةِ تُبَاعُ وَلَهَا زَوْجٌ؟ قَالَ: كَانَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ يَقُولُ: بَيْعُهَا طَلَاقُهَا، وَيَتْلُو هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ ﴿وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ إِلا مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ﴾

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ سُفْيَانُ عَنْ منصور، ومغيرة والأعمشن عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ: بَيْعُهَا طَلَاقُهَا. وَهُوَ مُنْقَطِعٌ.

وَقَالَ سُفْيَانُ الثَّوْرِيُّ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلابة، عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ: إِذَا بِيعَتِ الْأَمَةُ وَلَهَا زَوْجٌ فَسَيِّدُهَا أَحَقُّ بِبُضْعِهَا.

وَرَوَاهُ سَعِيدٌ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ قَالَ: إِنَّ أُبَيَّ بْنَ كَعْبٍ، وَجَابِرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، وَابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ قَالُوا: بَيْعُهَا طَلَاقُهَا.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنِي يَعْقُوبُ، [حَدَّثَنَا] ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: طَلَاقُ الْأَمَةِ سِتٌّ بَيْعُهَا طَلَاقُهَا، وَعِتْقُهَا طَلَاقُهَا، وَهِبَتُهَا طَلَاقُهَا، وَبَرَاءَتُهَا طَلَاقُهَا، وَطَلَاقُ زَوْجِهَا طَلَاقُهَا.

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ: أَخْبَرَنَا مَعْمَر، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَالْمُحْصَناَتُ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ﴾ قَالَ: هُن ذَوَاتُ الْأَزْوَاجِ، حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ نِكَاحَهُنَّ إِلَّا مَا مَلَكَتْ يَمِينُكَ فَبَيْعُهَا طَلَاقُهَا وقال معمر: وقال الحسن مثل ذلك.

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ سَعِيدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَرُوبة، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ إِلا مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ﴾ قَالَ: إِذَا كَانَ لَهَا زَوْجٌ فَبَيْعُهَا طَلَاقُهَا.

وَقَالَ عَوْفٌ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ: بَيْعُ الْأَمَةِ طَلَاقُهَا وبيعُه طلاقُها.

فَهَذَا قَوْلُ هَؤُلَاءِ مِنَ السَّلَفِ [رَحِمَهُمُ اللَّهُ] وَقَدْ خَالَفَهُمُ الْجُمْهُورُ قَدِيمًا وَحَدِيثًا، فَرَأَوْا أَنَّ بَيْعَ الْأَمَةِ لَيْسَ طَلَاقُهَا ؛ لِأَنَّ الْمُشْتَرِيَ نَائِبٌ عَنِ الْبَائِعِ، وَالْبَائِعَ كَانَ قَدْ أَخْرَجَ عَنْ مِلْكِهِ هَذِهِ الْمَنْفَعَةَ وَبَاعَهَا مَسْلُوبَةً عَنْهَا، وَاعْتَمَدُوا فِي ذَلِكَ عَلَى حَدِيثِ بَرِيرَةَ الْمُخَرَّجِ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ وَغَيْرِهِمَا؛ فَإِنَّ عَائِشَةَ أُمَّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ اشْتَرَتْهَا وَنَجَّزَتْ عِتْقَهَا، وَلَمْ يَنْفَسِخْ نِكَاحُهَا مِنْ زَوْجِهَا مُغِيثٍ، بَلْ خَيَّرَهَا النَّبِيُّ ﷺ بَيْنَ الْفَسْخِ وَالْبَقَاءِ، فَاخْتَارَتِ الْفَسْخَ، وَقِصَّتُهَا مَشْهُورَةٌ، فَلَوْ كَانَ بَيْعُ الْأَمَةِ طَلَاقُهَا -كَمَا قَالَ هَؤُلَاءِ لَمَا خَيَّرَهَا النَّبِيُّ ﷺ، فَلَمَّا خَيَّرَهَا دَلَّ عَلَى بَقَاءِ النِّكَاحِ، وَأَنَّ الْمُرَادَ مِنَ الْآيَةِ الْمَسْبِيَّاتُ فَقَطْ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.
