وَقَدْ قِيلَ: الْمُرَادُ بِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ﴾ يَعْنِي: الْعَفَائِفَ حَرَامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ حَتَّى تَمْلِكُوا عِصْمَتَهُنَّ بِنِكَاحٍ وَشُهُودٍ وَمُهُورٍ وَوَلِيٍّ وَاحِدَةً أَوِ اثْنَتَيْنِ أَوْ ثَلَاثًا أَوْ أَرْبَعًا. حَكَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي الْعَالِيَةِ وَطَاوُسٍ وَغَيْرُهُمَا. وَقَالَ عُمَر وَعُبَيْدَةُ: ﴿وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ﴾ مَا عَدَا الْأَرْبَعِ حَرَامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ إِلَّا مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿كِتَابَ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: هَذَا التَّحْرِيمُ كِتَابٌ كَتَبَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمْ، فَالْزَمُوا كِتَابَهُ، وَلَا تَخْرُجُوا عَنْ حُدُودِهِ، وَالْزَمُوا شَرْعَهُ وَمَا فَرَضَهُ.

وَقَدْ قَالَ عُبَيْدَةُ وَعَطَاءٌ وَالسُّدِّيُّ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿كِتَابَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكُم﴾ يَعْنِي الْأَرْبَعَ. وَقَالَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ: ﴿كِتَابَ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ﴾ يَعْنِي: مَا حَرَّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَأُحِلَّ لَكُم مَّا وَرَاءَ ذَلِكُمْ﴾ أي: ما عدا من ذكرن مِنَ الْمَحَارِمِ هُنَّ لَكُمْ حَلَالٌ، قَالَهُ عَطَاءٌ وَغَيْرُهُ. وَقَالَ عُبَيْدَةُ وَالسُّدِّيُّ: ﴿وَأُحِلَّ لَكُم مَّا وَرَاءَ ذَلِكُمْ﴾ مَا دُونَ الْأَرْبَعِ، وَهَذَا بَعِيدٌ، وَالصَّحِيحُ قَوْلُ عَطَاءٍ كَمَا تَقَدَّمَ. وَقَالَ قَتَادَةُ ﴿وَأُحِلَّ لَكُم مَّا وَرَاءَ ذَلِكُمْ﴾ يَعْنِي: مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ.

وَهَذِهِ الْآيَةُ هِيَ الَّتِي احْتَجَّ بِهَا مَنِ احْتَجَّ عَلَى تَحْلِيلِ الْجَمْعِ بَيْنَ الْأُخْتَيْنِ، وَقَوْلِ مَنْ قَالَ: أَحَلَّتْهُمَا آيَةٌ وَحَرَّمَتْهُمَا آيَةٌ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿أَنْ تَبْتَغُوا بِأَمْوَالِكُمْ مُحْصِنِينَ غَيْرَ مُسَافِحِينَ﴾ أَيْ: تَحَصَّلُوا بِأَمْوَالِكُمْ مِنَ الزَّوْجَاتِ إِلَى أَرْبَعٍ أَوِ السَّرَارِي مَا شِئْتُمْ بِالطَّرِيقِ الشَّرْعِيِّ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿مُحْصِنِينَ غَيْرَ مُسَافِحِينَ﴾

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَمَا اسْتَمْتَعْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْهُنَّ فَآتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ فَرِيضَةً﴾ أَيْ: كَمَا تَسْتَمْتِعُونَ بِهِنَّ فَآتَوْهُنَّ مُهُورَهُنَّ فِي مُقَابَلَةِ ذَلِكَ، كَقَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَكَيْفَ تَأْخُذُونَهُ وَقَدْ أَفْضَى بَعْضُكُمْ إِلَى بَعْضٍ﴾ [النِّسَاءِ: ٢١] وَكَقَوْلِهِ ﴿وَآتُوا النِّسَاءَ صَدُقَاتِهِنَّ نِحْلَةً﴾ وكقوله [النساء:٤] ﴿وَلا يَحِلُّ لَكُمْ أَن تَأْخُذُوا مِمَّا آتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ شَيْئًا﴾ [البقرة: ٢٢٩] وَقَدِ اسْتُدِلَّ بِعُمُومِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ عَلَى نِكَاحِ الْمُتْعَةِ، وَلَا شَكَّ أَنَّهُ كَانَ مَشْرُوعًا فِي ابْتِدَاءِ الْإِسْلَامِ، ثُمَّ نُسِخَ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ. وَقَدْ ذَهَبَ الشَّافِعِيُّ وَطَائِفَةٌ مِنَ الْعُلَمَاءِ إِلَى أَنَّهُ أُبِيحَ ثُمَّ نُسِخَ، ثُمَّ أُبِيحَ ثُمَّ نُسِخَ، مَرَّتَيْنِ. وَقَالَ آخَرُونَ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ، وَقَالَ آخَرُونَ: إِنَّمَا أُبِيحَ مَرَّةً، ثُمَّ نُسِخَ وَلَمْ يُبَحْ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ.

وَقَدْ رُويَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ وَطَائِفَةٍ مِنَ الصَّحَابَةِ القولُ بِإِبَاحَتِهَا لِلضَّرُورَةِ، وَهُوَ رِوَايَةٌ عَنِ الْإِمَامِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ حَنْبَلٍ، رَحِمَهُمُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى. وَكَانَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ، وَأُبَيُّ بْنُ كَعْبٍ، وَسَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْر، والسُّدِّي يَقْرَءُونَ: "فَمَا اسْتَمْتَعْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْهُنَّ إِلَى أَجَلٍ مُسَمَّى فَآتَوْهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ فَرِيضَةً". وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ: نَزَلَتْ فِي نِكَاحِ الْمُتْعَةِ، وَلَكِنَّ الْجُمْهُورَ عَلَى خِلَافِ ذَلِكَ، وَالْعُمْدَةُ مَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ، عَنْ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ [رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ] قَالَ: نَهَى النَّبِيُّ ﷺ عَنْ نِكَاحِ الْمُتْعَةِ وَعَنْ لُحُومِ الْحُمُرِ الْأَهْلِيَّةِ يَوْمَ خَيْبَرَ وَلِهَذَا الْحَدِيثِ أَلْفَاظٌ مُقَرَّرَةٌ هِيَ فِي كِتَابِ "الْأَحْكَامِ".

وَفِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ بْنِ سَبْرَة بْنِ مَعْبَدٍ الْجُهَنِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ: أَنَّهُ غَزَا مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَتْحَ مَكَّةَ، فَقَالَ: "يَأَيُّهَا النَّاسُ، إِنِّي كُنْتُ أَذِنْتُ لَكُمْ فِي الِاسْتِمْتَاعِ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ، وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ قَدْ حَرم ذَلِكَ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ، فَمَنْ كَانَ عِنْدَهُ مِنْهُنَّ شَيْءٌ فَلْيُخَلِّ سَبِيلَهُ، وَلَا تَأْخُذُوا مِمَّا آتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ شَيْئًا" وَفِي رِوَايَةٍ لِمُسْلِمٍ فِي حَجَّةِ الْوَدَاعِ وَلَهُ أَلْفَاظٌ مَوْضِعُهَا كِتَابُ "الْأَحْكَامُ".

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِيمَا تَرَاضَيْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ الْفَرِيضَةِ﴾ مَنْ حَمَلَ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ عَلَى نِكَاحِ الْمُتْعَةِ إِلَى أَجَلٍ مُسَمَّى قَالَ: فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذَا انْقَضَى الْأَجَلُ أَنْ تَرَاضَوْا عَلَى زِيَادَةٍ بِهِ وَزِيَادَةٍ لِلْجُعْلِ .

قَالَ السُّدِّيُّ: إِنْ شَاءَ أَرْضَاهَا مِنْ بَعْدِ الْفَرِيضَةِ الْأُولَى -يَعْنِي الْأَجْرَ الَّذِي أَعْطَاهَا عَلَى تَمَتُّعِهِ بِهَا-قَبْلَ انْقِضَاءِ الْأَجَلِ بَيْنَهُمَا فَقَالَ: أَتَمَتَّعُ مِنْكِ أَيْضًا بِكَذَا وَكَذَا، فَازْدَادَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَسْتَبْرِئَ رَحِمَهَا يَوْمَ تَنْقَضِي الْمُدَّةُ، وَهُوَ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِيمَا تَرَاضَيْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ الْفَرِيضَةِ﴾ .

قَالَ السُّدِّيُّ: إِذَا انْقَضَتِ الْمُدَّةُ فَلَيْسَ لَهُ عَلَيْهَا سَبِيلٌ، وَهِيَ مِنْهُ بَرِيئَةٌ، وَعَلَيْهَا أَنْ تَسْتَبْرِئَ مَا فِي رَحِمِهَا، وَلَيْسَ بَيْنَهُمَا مِيرَاثٌ، فَلَا يَرِثُ وَاحِدٌ مِنْهُمَا صَاحِبَهُ.

وَمَنْ قَالَ بِالْقَوْلِ الْأَوَّلِ جَعَلَ مَعْنَاهُ كَقَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَآتُوا النِّسَاءَ صَدُقَاتِهِنَّ نِحْلَةً [فَإِنْ طِبْنَ لَكُمْ عَنْ شَيْءٍ مِنْهُ نَفْسًا فَكُلُوهُ هَنِيئًا مَرِيئًا] ﴾ [النِّسَاءِ: ٤] أَيْ: إِذَا فَرَضْتَ لَهَا صَدَاقًا فَأَبْرَأَتْكَ مِنْهُ، أَوْ عَنْ شَيْءٍ مِنْهُ فَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكَ وَلَا عَلَيْهَا فِي ذَلِكَ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى، حَدَّثَنَا الْمُعْتَمِرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ قال: زعم الْحَضْرَمِيُّ أَنَّ رِجَالًا كَانُوا يَفْرِضُونَ الْمَهْرَ، ثُمَّ عَسَى أَنْ يُدْرِكَ أَحَدُهُمُ الْعُسْرَةُ، فَقَالَ: ﴿وَلا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ﴾ أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ ﴿فِيمَا تَرَاضَيْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ الْفَرَيضَةِ﴾ يَعْنِي: إِنْ وَضَعَتْ لَكَ مِنْهُ شَيْئًا فَهُوَ لَكَ سَائِغٌ، وَاخْتَارَ هَذَا الْقَوْلَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، وَقَالَ [عَلِيُّ] بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿وَلا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِيمَا تَرَاضَيْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ الْفَرِيضَةِ﴾ وَالتَّرَاضِي أَنْ يُوَفيها صَدَاقَهَا ثُمَّ يُخَيِّرَهَا، وَيَعْنِي فِي الْمُقَامِ أَوِ الْفِرَاقِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿إِنَّ اللهَ كَانَ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا﴾ مُنَاسِبُ ذِكْرِ هَذَيْنَ الْوَصْفَيْنِ بَعْدَ شَرْعِ هَذِهِ الْمُحْرِمَاتِ [الْعَظِيمَةِ] .

---

## 4:25 — verse 25

### Translation

He, [exalted is He], says: whoever does not find {tawl}, meaning capacity and means, {to marry believing free women}, meaning free women.

Ibn Wahb said: Abd al-Jabbar informed me, from Rabi'ah, concerning {And whoever among you does not have the means to marry believing free women}, that Rabi'ah said: al-tawl means inclination, he marries the slave woman if his inclination is toward her. Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim narrated this.

Then he began to condemn this view and refute it: {then from those your right hands possess, from your believing slave girls}, meaning: marry from among the believing slave women whom the believers possess. This is why He said {from your believing slave girls}. Ibn Abbas and others said: let him marry from the slave women of the believers. Al-Suddi and Muqatil ibn Hayyan said likewise.

Then He interjected with His statement {and Allah is most knowing of your faith, you are all from one another}, meaning: He is the One who knows the realities and inner truths of matters, while you, O people, only have access to the outward aspect of matters.

Then He said {so marry them with the permission of their guardians}, indicating that the master is the guardian of his slave woman, she is not to be married except with his permission. Likewise he is the guardian of his male slave, his male slave has no right to marry except with his permission, as it has come in the hadith: "Whichever slave marries without the permission of his masters is a fornicator (aahir)," meaning an adulterer.

If the owner of the slave woman is a woman, then she should have her married by whoever may act as marriage guardian for a woman, with her permission, because of what has come in the hadith: "A woman shall not arrange the marriage of a woman, nor shall a woman arrange her own marriage, for the adulteress is the one who marries herself off."

His statement {and give them their due wages in a fitting manner} means: pay their dowries in a fitting manner, that is, with willing hearts on your part, and do not withhold any of it out of contempt for them because they are owned slave women.

His statement {muhsanat} means: chaste from fornication, not engaging in it. This is why He said {not committing illicit sexual acts openly (ghayr musafihat)}, referring to fornicators who do not refuse anyone who seeks illicit relations with them.

His statement {nor those who take lovers} : Ibn Abbas said: al-musafihat are the open fornicators, meaning the fornicators who do not refuse anyone who seeks illicit relations with them. (And "those who take akhdan") means: intimate companions/lovers.

The same has been reported from Abu Hurayrah, Mujahid, al-Sha'bi, al-Dahhak, Ata al-Khurasani, Yahya ibn Abi Kathir, Muqatil ibn Hayyan, and al-Suddi, who all said: intimate companions. Al-Hasan al-Basri said: it means a close friend. Al-Dahhak also said regarding {nor those who take lovers}: a woman who has a single lover with whom intimacy has occurred and which she acknowledges. Allah forbade that, meaning He forbade marrying her while she remains in that state.

His statement {then when they are married, if they commit an illicit act, upon them is half of what is upon free women in punishment}: the reciters differed regarding {uhsina}. Some of them read it with damma on the hamza and kasra on the sad, constructed as a passive verb with the doer unnamed. It was also read with fatha on both the hamza and the sad, as an intransitive verb. It was then said that the meaning of the two readings is the same. They differed over it into two views:

The first: that what is meant by ihsan here is Islam. This has been reported from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, Ibn Umar, Anas, al-Aswad ibn Yazid, Zirr ibn Hubaysh, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, Ata, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, al-Sha'bi, and al-Suddi. Al-Zuhri reported something similar from Umar ibn al-Khattab, though this is disconnected (munqati'). This is the view that al-Shafi'i, [may Allah have mercy on him], stated explicitly in the report of al-Rabi', where he said: we only said this by way of evidence from the sunnah and the consensus of most scholars.

Ibn Abi Hatim narrated on this matter a raised (marfu') hadith, saying: Ali ibn al-Husayn ibn al-Junayd told us, Ahmad ibn Abd al-Rahman ibn Abdullah [al-Dimashqi] told us, my father told us, from his father, from Abu Hamzah, from Jabir, from a man, from Abu Abd al-Rahman, from Ali, who said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, regarding {then when they are married}: "Their ihsan is their Islam and their chastity." And it was said that what is meant here is marriage. He said: and Ali said: flog them.

[Then] Ibn Abi Hatim said: this is a munkar (rejected) hadith.

I say: its chain contains weakness, and among the narrators there is one unnamed, and the like of this cannot serve as proof.

Al-Qasim and Salim said: their ihsan is their Islam and their chastity.

It was also said that what is meant here is marriage. This is the view of Ibn Abbas, Mujahid, Ikrimah, Tawus, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, al-Hasan, Qatadah, and others. Abu Ali al-Tabari transmitted it in his book al-Idah from al-Shafi'i, as reported by Abu al-Hakam ibn Abd al-Hakam from him. Layth ibn Abi Sulaym narrated from Mujahid that he said: the ihsan of a slave woman is that a free man marries her, and the ihsan of a male slave is that he marries a free woman. Ibn Abi Talhah reported similarly from Ibn Abbas. Ibn Jarir narrated both of these in his tafsir, and Ibn Abi Hatim mentioned it from al-Sha'bi and al-Nakha'i.

It was also said that the meanings of the two readings differ: whoever reads {uhsinna} with damma on the hamza means marriage, and whoever reads "ahsanna" with fatha means Islam. The Imam Abu Ja'far Ibn Jarir chose this in his tafsir, established it, and defended it.

What is most apparent, and Allah knows best, is that what is meant by ihsan here is marriage, because the context of the verse indicates this, since the Exalted says: {And whoever among you does not have the means to marry believing free women, then from those whom your right hands possess, from your believing slave girls}. Allah knows best. The context of the noble verse throughout concerns believing slave girls, so it becomes necessary that what is meant by His statement {then when they are married} is: when they marry, as Ibn Abbas and those who followed him explained it.

On either of the two views there is a difficulty regarding the position of the majority. This is because they say: if a slave woman commits fornication, she receives fifty lashes, whether she is Muslim or non-Muslim, married or unmarried, even though the implication (mafhum) of the verse requires that there be no fixed punishment (hadd) for a slave woman who fornicates and is not muhsanah (married/chaste). Their responses to this differ. As for the majority, they said: there is no doubt that the explicit statement (mantuq) takes precedence over the implication (mafhum). General hadiths have come regarding the establishment of the hadd punishment upon slave women, so we give them precedence over the implication of the verse. Among these is what Muslim narrated in his Sahih, from Ali, may Allah be pleased with him, that he delivered a sermon and said: O people, establish the hadd punishment upon your slaves, whether married or unmarried among them, for a slave woman belonging to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ fornicated, and he ordered me to flog her. I found that she had recently given birth, so I feared that if I flogged her I would kill her. I mentioned this to the Prophet ﷺ, and he said: "You have done well, leave her until she recovers."

And according to Abdullah ibn Ahmad, from other than his father: "then when she recovers herself, apply her hadd of fifty."

And from Abu Hurayrah, who said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "If the slave woman of one of you commits fornication and her fornication becomes clear, let him flog her the hadd punishment and not reproach her for it. Then if she fornicates a second time, let him flog her the hadd punishment and not reproach her for it. Then if she fornicates a third time and her fornication becomes clear, let him sell her, even if for a rope of hair." And in the version of Muslim: "if she fornicates three times, let him sell her at the fourth."

Malik said, from Yahya ibn Sa'id, from Sulayman ibn Yasar, from Abdullah ibn Ayyash ibn Abi Rabi'ah al-Makhzumi, who said: Umar ibn al-Khattab ordered me, along with young men of Quraysh, and we flogged the slave girls of the leadership fifty lashes each for fornication.

The second response: the response of those who held that if a slave woman fornicates and is not muhsanah, there is no fixed hadd punishment upon her, and she is only struck by way of discipline. This is what has been related from Abdullah ibn Abbas, may Allah be pleased with him, and Tawus, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, Abu Ubayd al-Qasim ibn Sallam, and Dawud ibn Ali al-Zahiri in one report from him held this view. Their basis is the implication of the verse, which is among the implications of a conditional clause, and this is a valid proof according to most of them, so it takes precedence over the general (amm) evidence in their view. And the hadith of Abu Hurayrah and Zayd ibn Khalid, may Allah be pleased with them both, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was asked about the slave woman when she fornicates and is not muhsanah. He said: "If she fornicates, apply the hadd to her, then if she fornicates again, flog her, then sell her even if for a braided rope." Ibn Shihab said: I do not know whether this was after the third or the fourth time.

Both [Bukhari and Muslim] narrated it in their Sahihs, and in Muslim's version, Ibn Shihab said: al-dafir is the rope.

They said: no specific number was fixed in this hadith, as was fixed for the muhsanah woman at half of what is upon free women in punishment. So it became necessary to reconcile the verse and the hadith on this basis. And Allah knows best.

What is even more explicit than that is what Sa'id ibn Mansur narrated, from Sufyan, from Mis'ar, from 'Amr ibn Murrah, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "There is no hadd (prescribed punishment) upon a female slave until she is muhsana (in a state of protection through marriage), or until she is married. When she is made muhsana through a husband, upon her is half of what is upon free women."

Ibn Khuzaymah narrated it from 'Abdullah ibn 'Imran al-'Abidi, from Sufyan, with the same chain, as a raised report (marfu'). He said: "Raising it to the Prophet ﷺ is an error; it is rather from the statement of Ibn 'Abbas himself." Al-Bayhaqi likewise narrated it from the hadith of 'Abdullah ibn 'Imran, and said the same as what Ibn Khuzaymah said.

They said: The hadith of 'Ali and 'Umar (may Allah be pleased with them both) are rulings on specific cases (qadaya a'yan), while the hadith of Abu Hurayrah concerning him admits of several responses:

The first: that it is to be understood as referring to the married female slave, so as to reconcile it with this hadith.

The second: that the word "hadd" in his statement, "let him flog her the hadd," is a word inserted by some of the narrators, as indicated by the third response, which is:

That this hadith comes from two Companions, while that one comes only from the narration of Abu Hurayrah alone, and what is narrated by two takes precedence over what is narrated by one. Moreover, al-Nasa'i narrated it with a chain meeting the standard of Muslim, from the hadith of 'Abbad ibn Tamim, from his uncle, who had been present at Badr, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "When the female slave commits fornication, flog her; then if she commits fornication again, flog her; then if she commits fornication again, flog her; then if she commits fornication a fourth time, sell her, even if for a braided rope."

The fourth: it is not far-fetched that some of the narrators applied the term "hadd" loosely to flogging in the hadith, because since flogging is what occurred, he believed it to be a hadd, or else he applied the term "hadd" loosely to disciplinary punishment (ta'dib), just as the term "hadd" was applied loosely to striking a sick person who had committed fornication with a bundle of palm branches containing a hundred twigs, and to the flogging of one hundred lashes given to a man who committed fornication with his wife's female slave when she had given him permission regarding her. That is rather considered ta'zir (discretionary punishment) and disciplinary correction according to those who hold this view, such as Imam Ahmad and others among the Salaf. The true hadd is rather the flogging of the unmarried person with one hundred lashes, and the stoning of the previously married person or the one who commits sodomy. And Allah knows best.

Ibn Jarir narrated in his tafsir: [chain] Ibn al-Muthanna told us, Muhammad ibn Ja'far told us, Shu'bah told us, from 'Amr ibn Murrah, that he heard Sa'id ibn Jubayr say: "The female slave is not flogged if she commits fornication as long as she has not married."

This is a sound chain going back to him, but it is a strange position if he meant that she is not flogged at all, not even as disciplinary punishment, as though he took the apparent meaning (mafhum) of the verse and the hadith had not reached him. But if he meant that she is not flogged as a hadd, without denying that she may be flogged as disciplinary correction, then his statement agrees with that of Ibn 'Abbas and those who followed him in this. And Allah knows best.

The third response: that the verse indicates that the muhsana female slave is given half the hadd of the free woman. As for before ihsan (the state of being muhsana), the general texts of the Book and the Sunnah encompass her within the ruling of one hundred lashes, as in His statement, the Exalted: {The fornicatress and the fornicator, flog each one of them with a hundred lashes} [al-Nur: 2], and as in the hadith of 'Ubadah ibn al-Samit: "Take it from me, take it from me. Allah has appointed for them a way: the unmarried person with the unmarried person receives one hundred lashes and banishment for a year, and the previously married person receives one hundred lashes and stoning with stones." This hadith is in Sahih Muslim and other collections of hadith.

This position is well known from Dawud ibn 'Ali al-Zahiri, and it is exceedingly weak, because if Allah the Exalted had commanded that the muhsana female slave be flogged with half of what is upon the free woman as punishment, namely fifty lashes, how could her ruling before ihsan be more severe than after ihsan? The principle of the Shari'ah in such matters is the opposite of what he said. Furthermore, the Lawgiver, peace be upon him, was asked by his Companions about the female slave who commits fornication without being muhsana, and he said, "Flog her," without saying "one hundred." If her ruling were as Dawud claimed, it would have been necessary to clarify that for them, because they only asked about this due to the lack of clarification regarding the ruling of the hundred lashes after ihsan for female slaves. Otherwise, what would be the benefit of their saying "and she was not muhsana," if there were no difference between the two cases, had the verse not been revealed? Rather, once they knew the ruling for one of the two cases, they asked about the ruling for the other case, and he clarified it for them. This is just as is established in the two Sahihs, that when they asked him about sending prayers (salat) upon him, he mentioned it to them, then said: "As for the salam (greeting), you already know it." And in another wording: when Allah revealed His statement, {O you who believe, send prayers upon him and greet him with peace} [al-Ahzab: 56], they said: "This salam upon you, we already know how to render it, but how do we send prayers upon you?" And he mentioned the hadith. This question is similar to that one.

The fourth response, regarding the apparent meaning (mafhum) of the verse: the response of Abu Thawr. His position is even stranger than that of Dawud in several respects. He says that when she becomes muhsana, upon her is half of what is upon the married muhsanat, which is stoning. But stoning cannot be halved, so it would follow that the muhsana female slave must be stoned if she commits fornication, while before ihsan she must be flogged fifty lashes. He erred in understanding the verse and opposed the majority in the ruling. Indeed, Abu 'Abdullah al-Shafi'i, may Allah have mercy on him, said: "The Muslims have not differed that there is no stoning upon a slave for fornication." This is because the verse indicates that upon them is half of what is upon the muhsanat of punishment, and the definite article in "al-muhsanat" refers to something already mentioned, namely the muhsanat referred to at the beginning of the verse: {And whoever among you cannot afford to marry the muhsanat}, meaning by them free women only, without reference to the marriage of anyone else. And His statement, {half of what is upon the muhsanat of punishment}, indicates that what is meant is the punishment that can be halved, which is flogging, not stoning. And Allah knows best.

Furthermore, Imam Ahmad narrated a hadith that explicitly refutes the position of Abu Thawr, from the narration of al-Hasan ibn Sa'd, from his father, that Safiyyah had committed fornication with a man from the Humus, and bore a son, whom the fornicator claimed as his. The two disputed the matter before 'Uthman ibn 'Affan, who referred them to 'Ali ibn Abi Talib. 'Ali said: "I will judge between them according to the judgment of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ: 'The child belongs to the marriage bed, and for the fornicator is the stone (i.e., disappointment).'" And he flogged each of them fifty lashes.

It has also been said: rather, what is meant by the apparent implication (mafhum) is drawing attention from the higher case to the lower one, meaning that female slaves receive half the hadd of free women in flogging even if they are muhsanat, and that there is no stoning upon them at all, neither before marriage nor after it. Rather, flogging applies to them in both cases, based on the Sunnah. This was said by the author of al-Ifsah, quoting al-Shafi'i, in what Ibn 'Abd al-Hakam narrated from him. Al-Bayhaqi mentioned it in the Kitab al-Sunan wal-Athar. But this is far removed from the wording of the verse, because we derive the halving of the hadd from the verse and nowhere else, so how can halving be understood from it with respect to something other than what it addresses? He also said: rather, what is meant is that in the state of ihsan, only the imam may carry out the hadd upon her, and it is not permissible for her master to carry out the hadd upon her in that state, this being one position within the school of Imam Ahmad, may Allah have mercy on him. As for before ihsan, the master may do so, and the hadd in both cases is half the hadd of the free woman. This too is far-fetched, because there is nothing in the wording of the verse indicating it.

Were it not for this verse, we would not know what the ruling of the imam is regarding halving, and it would be necessary for them to fall under the general ruling of the verse requiring the hadd to be completed to one hundred lashes, or that they be stoned, as established by the evidence for it. It has already been mentioned from 'Ali that he said: "O people, carry out the hadd upon your slaves, whether muhsan or not muhsan." The general wording of the previously mentioned hadiths makes no distinction between the married female slave and others, based on the hadith of Abu Hurayrah which the majority cited as evidence: "When the female slave of one of you commits fornication and her fornication becomes clear, let him flog her the hadd, and let him not reproach her for it."

Summary regarding the verse: there are several positions as to what happens when she commits fornication. The first: that she is flogged fifty lashes, both before and after ihsan. As for whether she is banished, there are three positions on this:

The first: that she is banished. The second: that she is not banished at all (this is the position of 'Ali and the jurists of Madinah). The third: that she is banished for half a year, being half the banishment period of the free woman.

This disagreement is within the Shafi'i school. As for Abu Hanifah, in his view banishment is a ta'zir (discretionary punishment) and not part of the completion of the hadd (fixed legal penalty), rather it is a matter for the ruler's discretion: if he wishes he may impose it and if he wishes he may leave it, in respect of both men and women. In Malik's view, banishment applies only to men, while for women it does not, because that would contradict the preservation of their honor. [No report has come regarding banishment for either men or women; yes, there is the hadith of Ubadah and the hadith of Abu Hurayrah] that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ ruled, concerning one who committed zina and was not muhsan (previously married), that he be banished for a year and that the hadd be carried out upon him. Al-Bukhari related this. [All] of this is specified by the underlying meaning, which is that the purpose of banishment is preservation, and this is absent in the case of banishing women. And Allah knows best.

The second view: that the female slave, if she commits zina, is flogged fifty lashes after having attained ihsan (married status), and is struck [before that] as a disciplinary measure not limited to a fixed number. What Ibn Jarir related earlier from Sa'id ibn Jubayr has already been mentioned: that she is not struck before ihsan. If he meant to negate that entirely, this would be a position arrived at through interpretation; otherwise it is like the second view.

The other view: that she is flogged one hundred lashes before ihsan and fifty after it, as is well known from Dawud, and [this is] the weakest of the views: that she is flogged fifty lashes before ihsan and is stoned after it. This is the view of Abu Thawr, and it is also weak. Allah, glorified and exalted, knows best what is correct.

His saying: {That is for whoever among you fears hardship} means: marrying female slaves is permitted, with the conditions previously mentioned, only for one who fears for himself falling into zina, for whom abstaining from intercourse is difficult, and who suffers hardship because of all of that. In that case he may marry the female slave. But if he refrains from marrying the female slave and struggles against himself to abstain from zina, that is better for him, because if he marries her his children will be born as slaves belonging to her master, unless the husband is an Arab, in which case his children from her would not be slaves, according to an old view of al-Shafi'i. This is why He said: {But that you be patient is better for you, and Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.}

From this noble verse the majority of scholars derived that permission to marry female slaves requires both the absence of means (tawl) to marry free women and the fear of hardship, because of the corrupting effect on the children's status found in marrying them, and because of the lowering of oneself involved in turning away from free women toward them. Abu Hanifah and his companions differed from the majority regarding the requirement of both these conditions. They said: as long as a man is not married to a free woman, it is permissible for him to marry a believing female slave, or even a female slave from the People of the Book, regardless of whether he has the means to marry a free woman or not, and regardless of whether he fears hardship or not. Their basis for this position is [the generality] of His statement, Exalted is He: {and chaste women from among those who were given the Scripture before you} [al-Ma'idah: 5], meaning the chaste ones, which encompasses both free women and female slaves, and this verse is general. This too clearly indicates what the majority have said. And Allah knows best.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ [تَعَالَى] وَمَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ ﴿طَوْلا﴾ أَيْ: سَعَةً وَقُدْرَةً ﴿أَنْ يَنْكِحَ الْمُحْصَنَاتِ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ﴾ أَيِ الْحَرَائِرَ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ وَهْب: أَخْبَرَنِي عَبْدُ الْجَبَّارِ، عَنْ رَبِيعَةَ: ﴿وَمَنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ مِنْكُمْ طَوْلا أَنْ يَنْكِحَ الْمُحْصَنَاتِ﴾ قَالَ رَبِيعَةُ الطوْل الْهَوَى، يَنْكِحَ الْأَمَةَ إِذَا كَانَ هَوَاهُ فِيهَا. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ وَابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ.

ثُمَّ شَرَعَ يُشَنِّعُ عَلَى هَذَا الْقَوْلِ ويَرُدّه ﴿فَمِنْ مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ مِنْ فَتَيَاتِكُمُ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ﴾ أَيْ: فَتَزَوَّجُوا مِنَ الْإِمَاءِ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ اللَّاتِي يَمْلِكُهُنَّ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ، وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿مِنْ فَتَيَاتِكُمُ الْمُؤْمِنَات﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَغَيْرُهُ: فَلْيَنْكِحْ مِنْ إِمَاءِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ، وَكَذَا قَالَ السُّدِّيُّ وَمُقَاتِلُ بْنُ حَيَّان.

ثُمَّ اعْتَرَضَ بِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِإِيمَانِكُمْ بَعْضُكُمْ مِنْ بَعْضٍ﴾ أَيْ: هُوَ الْعَالِمُ بِحَقَائِقِ الْأُمُورِ وَسَرَائِرِهَا، وَإِنَّمَا لَكُمْ أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ الظَّاهِرُ مِنَ الْأُمُورِ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿فَانْكِحُوهُنَّ بِإِذْنِ أَهْلِهِنَّ﴾ فدلَّ عَلَى أَنَّ السَّيِّدَ هُوَ وَلِيُّ أَمَتِهِ لَا تُزَوَّجُ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ، وَكَذَلِكَ هُوَ وَلِيُّ عَبْدِهِ، لَيْسَ لِعَبْدِهِ أَنْ يَتَزَوَّجَ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ، كَمَا جَاءَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ: "أَيُّمَا عَبْدٍ تَزَوّج بِغَيْرِ إِذَنْ مَوَاليه فَهُوَ عَاهِر" أي زان.

فَإِنْ كَانَ مَالِكُ الْأَمَةِ امْرَأَةٌ زَوَّجَهَا مَنْ يُزَوِّجُ الْمَرْأَةَ بِإِذْنِهَا؛ لِمَا جَاءَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ: "لَا تُزَوِّجُ المرأةُ [المرأةَ، وَلَا المرأةُ نَفْسَهَا] فَإِنَّ الزَّانِيَةَ هِيَ الَّتِي تُزَوِّجُ نَفْسَهَا" .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَآتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ﴾ أَيْ: وَادْفَعُوا مُهُورَهُنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ، أَيْ: عَنْ طِيبِ نَفْسٍ مِنْكُمْ، وَلَا تَبْخَسُوا مِنْهُ شَيْئًا اسْتِهَانَةً بِهِنَّ؛ لِكَوْنِهِنَّ إِمَاءً مَمْلُوكَاتٍ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿مُحْصَنَاتٍ﴾ أَيْ: عَفَائِفَ عَنِ الزِّنَا لَا يَتَعَاطَيْنَهُ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿غَيْرَ مُسَافِحَاتٍ﴾ وَهُنَّ الزَّوَانِي اللَّاتِي لَا يَمْتَنِعْنَ مَنْ أَرَادَهُنَّ بِالْفَاحِشَةِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلَا مُتَّخِذَاتِ أَخْدَانٍ﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: الْمُسَافِحَاتُ، هُنَّ الزَّوَانِي الْمُعَالِنَاتُ يَعْنِي الزَّوَانِي اللَّاتِي لَا يَمْنَعْنَ أَحَدًا أَرَادَهُنَّ بِالْفَاحِشَةِ. (وَمُتَّخِذَاتُ أَخْدَانٍ) يَعْنِي: أَخِلَّاءَ.

وَكَذَا رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ وَالشَّعْبِيِّ، وَالضَّحَّاكِ، وَعَطَاءٍ الْخُرَاسَانِيِّ، وَيَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي كَثِيرٍ، وَمُقَاتِلِ بْنِ حَيَّانَ، وَالسُّدِّيِّ، قَالُوا: أَخِلَّاءَ. وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ الْبَصْرِيُّ: يَعْنِي: الصَّدِيقَ. وَقَالَ الضَّحَّاكُ أَيْضًا: ﴿وَلا مُتَّخِذَاتِ أَخْدَانٍ﴾ ذَاتُ الْخَلِيلِ الْوَاحِدِ [الْمَسِيسِ] الْمُقِرَّةُ بِهِ، نَهَى اللَّهُ عَنْ ذَلِكَ، يَعْنِي [عَنْ] تَزْوِيجِهَا مَا دَامَتْ كَذَلِكَ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَإِذَا أُحْصِنَّ فَإِنْ أَتَيْنَ بِفَاحِشَةٍ فَعَلَيْهِنَّ نِصْفُ مَا عَلَى الْمُحْصَنَاتِ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ﴾ اخْتَلَفَ القراءُ فِي ﴿أُحْصِنَّ﴾ فَقَرَأَهُ بَعْضُهُمْ بِضَمِّ الْهَمْزَةِ وَكَسْرِ الصَّادِ، مَبْنِيٌّ لِمَا لَمْ يُسَمَّ فَاعِلُهُ، وقُرئ بِفَتْحِ الْهَمْزَةِ وَالصَّادِ فِعْلٌ لَازِمٌ ثُمَّ قِيلَ: مَعْنَى الْقِرَاءَتَيْنِ وَاحِدٌ. وَاخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ عَلَى قَوْلَيْنِ:

أَحَدُهُمَا: أَنَّ الْمُرَادَ بِالْإِحْصَانِ هَاهُنَا الْإِسْلَامُ. رُوي ذَلِكَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، وَابْنِ عُمَرَ، وَأَنَسٍ، وَالْأَسْوَدِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، وزِرّ بْنِ حُبَيْش، وسعيد بن جُبَير، وعطاء، إبراهيم النَّخعي، وَالشَّعْبِيِّ، والسُّدِّي. وَرَوَى نَحْوَهُ الزُّهْرِيُّ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ، وَهُوَ مُنْقَطِعٌ. وَهَذَا هُوَ الْقَوْلُ الَّذِي نَصَّ عَلَيْهِ الشَّافِعِيُّ [رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى] فِي رِوَايَةِ الرَّبِيعِ، قَالَ: وَإِنَّمَا قُلْنَا [ذَلِكَ] اسْتِدْلَالًا بِالسُّنَّةِ وَإِجْمَاعِ أَكْثَرِ أَهْلِ الْعِلْمِ.

وَقَدْ رَوَى ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ فِي ذَلِكَ حَدِيثًا مَرْفُوعًا، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ الْجُنَيْدِ، حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ [الدِّمَشْقِيُّ] حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: ﴿فَإِذَا أُحْصِنَّ﴾ قَالَ: "إِحْصَانُهَا إِسْلَامُهَا وَعَفَافُهَا". وَقَالَ الْمُرَادُ بِهِ هَاهُنَا التَّزْوِيجُ، قَالَ: وَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ: اجلدوهن.

[ثُمَّ] قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: وَهُوَ حَدِيثٌ مُنْكَرٌ.

قُلْتُ: وَفِي إِسْنَادِهِ ضَعْفٌ، وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ لَمْ يُسَمَّ، و [مِثْلُهُ] لَا تَقُومُ بِهِ حُجَّةٌ .

وَقَالَ الْقَاسِمُ وَسَالِمٌ: إِحْصَانُهَا: إِسْلَامُهَا وَعَفَافُهَا.

وَقِيلَ: الْمُرَادُ بِهِ هَاهُنَا: التَّزْوِيجُ. وَهُوَ قولُ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، وعِكْرِمة، وَطَاوُسٍ، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَير، وَالْحَسَنِ، وَقَتَادَةَ وَغَيْرِهِمْ. وَنَقَلَهُ أَبُو عَلِيٍّ الطَّبَرِيُّ فِي كِتَابِهِ "الْإِيضَاحُ" عَنِ الشَّافِعِيِّ، فِيمَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو الْحَكَمِ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْهُ وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ لَيْث بْنُ أَبِي سُلَيْمٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: إِحْصَانُ الْأَمَةِ أَنْ يَنْكِحَهَا الْحُرُّ، وَإِحْصَانُ الْعَبْدِ أَنْ يَنْكِحَ الْحُرَّةَ. وَكَذَا رَوَى ابْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، رَوَاهُمَا ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ، وَذَكَرَهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ وَالنَّخَعِيِّ.

وَقِيلَ مَعْنَى الْقِرَاءَتَيْنِ مُتَبَايِنٌ فَمَنْ قَرَأَ ﴿أُحْصِنَّ﴾ بِضَمِّ الْهَمْزَةِ، فَمُرَادُهُ التَّزْوِيجُ، وَمَنْ قَرَأَ "أَحْصَنَّ" بِفَتْحِهَا، فَمُرَادُهُ الإسلام اختاره الإمام أبو جعفر ابن جَرِيرٍ فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ، وَقَرَّرَهُ وَنَصَرَهُ.

وَالْأَظْهَرُ -وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ-أَنَّ الْمُرَادَ بِالْإِحْصَانِ هَاهُنَا التَّزْوِيجُ؛ لِأَنَّ سِيَاقَ الْآيَةِ يَدُلُّ عَلَيْهِ، حَيْثُ يَقُولُ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ مِنْكُمْ طَوْلا أَنْ يَنْكِحَ الْمُحْصَنَاتِ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ فَمِنْ مَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ مِنْ فَتَيَاتِكُمْ﴾ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ. وَالْآيَةُ الْكَرِيمَةُ سِيَاقُهَا كُلُّهَا فِي الْفَتَيَاتِ الْمُؤْمِنَاتِ، فَتَعَيَّنَ أَنَّ الْمُرَادَ بِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَإِذَا أُحْصِنَّ﴾ أَيْ: تَزَوَّجْنَ، كَمَا فَسَّرَهُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ وَمِنْ تَبِعَهُ.

وَعَلَى كُلٍّ مِنَ الْقَوْلَيْنِ إِشْكَالٌ عَلَى مَذْهَبِ الْجُمْهُورِ؛ وَذَلِكَ أَنَّهُمْ يَقُولُونَ: إِنَّ الْأَمَةَ إِذَا زَنَتْ فَعَلَيْهَا خَمْسُونَ جِلْدَةً، سَوَاءٌ كَانَتْ مُسْلِمَةً أَوْ كَافِرَةً، مُزَوَّجَةً أَوْ بِكْرًا، مَعَ أَنَّ مَفْهُومَ الْآيَةِ يَقْتَضِي أَنَّهُ لَا حَدَّ عَلَى غَيْرِ الْمُحْصَنَةِ مِمَّنْ زَنَا مِنَ الْإِمَاءِ، وَقَدِ اخْتَلَفَتْ أَجْوِبَتُهُمْ عَنْ ذَلِكَ، فَأَمَّا الْجُمْهُورُ فَقَالُوا: لَا شَكَّ أَنَّ الْمَنْطُوقَ مُقَدَّمٌ عَلَى الْمَفْهُومِ. وَقَدْ وَرَدَتْ أَحَادِيثُ عَامَّةٌ فِي إِقَامَةِ الْحَدِّ عَلَى الْإِمَاءِ، فَقَدَّمْنَاهَا عَلَى مَفْهُومِ الْآيَةِ، فَمِنْ ذَلِكَ مَا رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ فِي صَحِيحِهِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، أَنَّهُ خَطَبَ فَقَالَ: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ، أَقِيمُوا عَلَى أرقَّائكم الْحَدَّ مَنْ أحْصَنَ مِنْهُمْ وَمَنْ لَمْ يُحْصَن، فَإنَّ أَمَةً لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ زَنَتْ فَأَمَرَنِي أَنْ أَجْلِدَهَا، فَإِذَا هِيَ حَدِيثَةُ عَهْدٍ بِنِفَاسٍ، فَخَشِيتُ إنْ جَلَدْتُهَا أَنْ أَقْتُلَهَا، فَذَكَرْتُ ذَلِكَ لِلنَّبِيِّ ﷺ، فَقَالَ: "أحْسَنْتَ، اتْرُكْهَا حَتَّى تَماثَل " .

وَعِنْدَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ، عَنْ غَيْرِ أَبِيهِ: "فَإِذَا تَعالتْ مِنْ نَفْسِها حدَّها خَمْسِينَ".

وَعَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: سمعتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى الله عليه وسلم يقول: "إذا زنت أمة أحدكم فتبين زناها، فليجلدها الْحَدَّ وَلَا يُثَرِّبْ عَلَيْهَا، ثُمَّ إِنْ زَنَتِ الثَّانِيَةَ فَلْيَجْلِدْهَا الْحَدَّ وَلَا يُثَرِّبْ عَلَيْهَا، ثُمَّ إن زنت الثالثة فتبين زِنَاهَا، فَلْيَبِعْهَا وَلَوْ بِحَبْل مَنْ شَعَر" وَلِمُسْلِمٍ إِذَا زَنتْ ثَلَاثًا فَلْيَبِعْهَا فِي الرَّابِعَةِ" .

وَقَالَ مَالِكٌ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ يَسار، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عيَّاش بْنِ أَبِي رَبِيعَةَ الْمَخْزُومِيِّ قَالَ: أمَرَني عُمَر بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ فِي فِتْيَةٍ مِنْ قُرَيْشٍ، فَجَلَدْنَا مِنْ وَلَائِدِ الْإِمَارَةِ خَمْسِينَ خَمْسِينَ فِي الزِّنَا.

الْجَوَابُ الثَّانِي: جَوَابُ مَنْ ذَهَبَ إِلَى أَنَّ الْأَمَةَ إِذَا زَنَتْ وَلَمْ تُحْصَنَ فَلَا حَدَّ عَلَيْهَا، وَإِنَّمَا تُضْرَبُ تَأْدِيبًا، وَهُوَ الْمَحْكِيُّ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، وَإِلَيْهِ ذَهَبَ طَاوُسٌ، وَسَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَير، وَأَبُو عُبَيد الْقَاسِمُ بْنُ سَلَّامٍ، وَدَاوُدُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ الظَّاهِرِيُّ فِي رِوَايَةٍ عَنْهُ. وعمْدتهُم مَفْهُومُ الْآيَةِ وَهُوَ مِنْ مَفَاهِيمِ الشَّرْطِ، وَهُوَ حُجَّةٌ عِنْدَ أَكْثَرِهِمْ فَهُوَ مُقَدَّمٌ عَلَى الْعُمُومِ عِنْدَهُمْ. وَحَدِيثُ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ وَزَيْدِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ سُئل عَنِ الْأَمَةِ إِذَا زَنَتْ وَلَمْ تحْصنْ؟ قَالَ: "إِنْ زَنَتْ فَحِدُّوهَا ثُمَّ إِنَّ زَنَتْ فَاجْلِدُوهَا ثُمَّ بِيعُوهَا وَلَوْ بِضَفِيرٍ" قَالَ ابْنُ شِهَابٍ: لَا أَدْرِي أَبَعْدَ الثَّالِثَةِ أَوِ الرَّابِعَةِ.

أَخْرَجَاهُ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ وَعِنْدَ مُسْلِمٍ: قَالَ ابْنُ شِهَابٍ: الضَّفِيرُ الْحَبْلُ.

قَالُوا: فَلَمْ يُؤَقَّت فِي هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ عَدَدٌ كَمَا وُقِّتَ فِي الْمُحْصَنَةِ بِنِصْفِ مَا عَلَى الْمُحْصَنَاتِ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ، فَوَجَبَ الْجَمْعُ بَيْنَ الْآيَةِ وَالْحَدِيثِ بِذَلِكَ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَأَصْرَحُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ مَا رَوَاهُ سَعِيدُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "لَيْسَ عَلَى أَمَةٍ حَدٌّ حَتَّى تُحْصَنَ -أَوْ حَتَّى تُزَوَّجَ-فَإِذَا أُحْصِنَتْ بِزَوْجٍ فَعَلَيْهَا نِصْفُ مَا عَلَى الْمُحْصَنَاتِ".

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ خُزَيْمَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عِمْرَانَ الْعَابِدِيِّ عَنْ سُفْيَانَ بِهِ مَرْفُوعًا. وَقَالَ: رَفْعُهُ خَطَأٌ، إِنَّمَا هُوَ مِنْ قَوْلِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ الْبَيْهَقِيُّ مِنْ حَدِيثِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عِمْرَانَ، وَقَالَ مِثْلَ مَا قَالَهُ ابْنُ خُزَيْمَةَ .

قَالُوا: وَحَدِيثُ عَلِيٍّ وَعُمَرَ [رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا] قَضَايَا أَعْيَانٍ، وَحَدِيثُ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ عَنْهُ أَجْوِبَةٌ:

أَحَدُهَا: أَنَّ ذَلِكَ مَحْمُولٌ عَلَى الْأَمَةِ الْمُزَوَّجَةِ جَمْعًا بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَ هَذَا الحديث.

الثَّانِي: أَنَّ لَفْظَ الْحَدِّ فِي قَوْلِهِ: فَلْيَجْلِدْهَا الْحَدَّ، لَفْظٌ مُقْحَمٌ مِنْ بَعْضِ الرُّوَاةِ، بِدَلِيلِ الْجَوَابِ الثَّالِثِ وَهُوَ:

أَنَّ هَذَا مِنْ حَدِيثِ صَحَابِيَّيْنِ وَذَلِكَ مِنْ رِوَايَةِ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ فَقَطْ، وَمَا كَانَ عَنِ اثْنَيْنِ فَهُوَ أَوْلَى بِالتَّقَدُّمِ مِنْ رِوَايَةِ وَاحِدٍ، وَأَيْضًا فَقَدْ رَوَاهُ النَّسَائِيُّ بِإِسْنَادٍ عَلَى شَرْطِ مُسْلِمٍ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ عَبَّاد بْنِ تَمِيمٍ، عَنْ عَمِّهِ -وَكَانَ قَدْ شَهِدَ بَدْرًا-أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "إِذَا زَنَتِ الْأَمَةُ فَاجْلِدُوهَا، ثُمَّ إِذَا زَنتِ فاجْلِدوها، ثُمَّ إِذَا زَنَتْ فَاجْلِدُوهَا، ثُمَّ إِذَا زَنَتْ فَبِيعُوهَا وَلَوْ بِضَفِيرٍ".

الرَّابِعُ: أَنَّهُ لَا يَبْعُدُ أَنَّ بَعْضَ الرُّوَاةِ أَطْلَقَ لَفْظَ الْحَدِّ فِي الْحَدِيثِ عَلَى الْجَلْدِ؛ لِأَنَّهُ لَمَّا كَانَ الْجَلْدُ اعْتَقَدَ أَنَّهُ حَدٌّ، أَوْ أَنَّهُ أَطْلَقَ لَفْظَةَ الْحَدِّ عَلَى التَّأْدِيبِ، كَمَا أَطْلَقَ الْحَدَّ عَلَى ضَرْبِ مَنْ زَنَى مِنَ الْمَرْضَى بُعثْكال نَخْلٍ فِيهِ مِائَةُ شِمْرَاخٍ، وَعَلَى جَلْدِ مَنْ زَنَى بِأَمَةِ امْرَأَتِهِ إِذَا أَذِنَتْ لَهُ فِيهَا مِائَةً، وَإِنَّمَا ذَلِكَ تَعْزِيرٌ وَتَأْدِيبٌ عِنْدَ مَنْ يَرَاهُ كَالْإِمَامِ أَحْمَدَ وَغَيْرِهِ مِنَ السَّلَفِ. وَإِنَّمَا الْحَدُّ الْحَقِيقِيُّ هُوَ جَلْدُ الْبِكْرِ مِائَةً، وَرَجْمُ الثَّيِّبِ أَوِ اللَّائِطِ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَقَدْ رَوَى ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ الْمُثَنَّى، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ؛ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ سَعِيدَ بْنَ جُبَيْرٍ يَقُولُ: لَا تُضْرَبُ الْأَمَةُ إِذَا زَنَتْ مَا لَمْ تَتَزَوَّجْ .

وَهَذَا إِسْنَادٌ صَحِيحٌ عَنْهُ، وَمَذْهَبٌ غَرِيبٌ إِنْ أَرَادَ أَنَّهَا لَا تُضْرَبُ أَصْلًا لَا حَدًّا، وَكَأَنَّهُ أَخَذَ بِمَفْهُومِ الْآيَةِ وَلَمْ يَبْلُغْهُ الْحَدِيثُ، وَإِنْ كَانَ أَرَادَ أَنَّهَا لَا تُضْرَبُ حَدًّا، وَلَا يَنْفِي ضَرْبَهَا تَأْدِيبًا، فَهُوَ كَقَوْلِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ وَمَنْ تَبِعَهُ فِي ذَلِكَ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

الْجَوَابُ الثَّالِثُ: أَنَّ الْآيَةَ دَلَّتْ عَلَى أَنَّ الْأَمَةَ الْمُحْصَنَةَ تُحَدُّ نِصْفَ حَدِّ الْحُرَّةِ، فَأَمَّا قَبْلَ الْإِحْصَانِ فَعُمُومَاتُ الْكِتَابِ وَالسُّنَّةِ شَامِلَةٌ لَهَا فِي جَلْدِهَا مِائَةً، كَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى ﴿الزَّانِيَةُ وَالزَّانِي فَاجْلِدُوا كُلَّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا مِائَةَ جَلْدَةٍ﴾ [النُّورِ:٢] وَكَحَدِيثِ عُبَادَةَ بْنِ الصَّامِتِ: "خُذوا عَنِّي، خُذُوا عَنِّي، قَدْ جَعلَ اللَّهُ لَهُنَّ سَبِيلا البِكر بالبِكْر جَلْدُ مِائَةٍ وتَغْرِيبُ عَامٍ، وَالثَّيِّبُ جَلْدُ مِائَةٍ ورَجْمُهَا بِالْحِجَارَةِ" وَالْحَدِيثُ فِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ وَغَيْرِ ذَلِكَ مِنَ الْأَحَادِيثِ.

وَهَذَا الْقَوْلُ هُوَ الْمَشْهُورُ عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ الظَّاهِرِيِّ، وَهُوَ فِي غَايَةِ الضَّعْفِ؛ لِأَنَّ اللَّهَ تَعَالَى إِذَا كَانَ أَمَرَ بِجِلْدِ الْمُحْصَنَةِ مِنَ الْإِمَاءِ بِنِصْفِ مَا عَلَى الْحُرَّةِ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ وَهُوَ خَمْسُونَ جَلْدَةً، فَكَيْفَ يَكُونُ حُكْمُهَا قَبْلَ الْإِحْصَانِ أَشَدَّ مِنْهُ بَعْدَ الْإِحْصَانِ. وَقَاعِدَةُ الشَّرِيعَةِ فِي ذَلِكَ عَكْسُ مَا قَالَ، وَهَذَا الشَّارِعُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ يَسْأَلُهُ أَصْحَابُهُ عَنِ الْأَمَةِ إِذَا زَنَتْ وَلَمْ تُحْصَنْ، فَقَالَ: "اجْلِدُوهَا" وَلَمْ يَقُلْ مِائَةً، فَلَوْ كَانَ حُكْمُهَا كَمَا قَالَ دَاوُدُ لَوَجَبَ بَيَانُ ذَلِكَ لَهُمْ؛ لِأَنَّهُمْ إِنَّمَا سَأَلُوا عَنْ ذَلِكَ لِعَدَمِ بَيَانِ حُكْمِ جَلْدِ الْمِائَةِ بَعْدَ الْإِحْصَانِ فِي الْإِمَاءِ، وَإِلَّا فَمَا الْفَائِدَةُ فِي قَوْلِهِمْ: "وَلَمْ تُحْصَنْ" لعدم الفرق بَيْنَهُمَا لَوْ لَمْ تَكُنِ الْآيَةُ نَزَلَتْ، لَكِنْ لَمَّا عَلِمُوا حُكْمَ أَحَدِ الْحُكْمَيْنِ سَأَلُوا عَنْ حُكْمِ الْحَالِ الْآخَرِ، فَبَيَّنَهُ لَهُمْ. كَمَا [ثَبَتَ] فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ أَنَّهُمْ لَمَّا سَأَلُوهُ عَنِ الصَّلَاةِ عَلَيْهِ، فَذَكَرَهَا لَهُمْ ثُمَّ قَالَ: "وَالسَّلَامُ مَا قَدْ عَلِمْتُمْ" وَفِي لَفْظٍ: لَمَّا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ قَوْلَهُ: ﴿يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا صَلُّوا عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيمًا﴾ [الْأَحْزَابِ:٥٦] قَالُوا: هَذَا السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ قَدْ عَرَّفْنَاهُ، فَكَيْفَ الصَّلَاةُ عَلَيْكَ؟ وَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ، وَهَكَذَا هَذَا السُّؤَالُ .

الْجَوَابُ الرَّابِعُ -عَنْ مَفْهُومِ الْآيَةِ-: جَوَابُ أَبِي ثَوْرٍ، فَإِنَّ مِنْ مَذْهَبِهِ مَا هُوَ أَغْرَبُ مِنْ قَوْلِ دَاوُدَ مِنْ وُجُوهٍ، ذَلِكَ أَنَّهُ يَقُولُ فَإِذَا أحْصن فَإِنَّ عَلَيْهِنَّ نِصْفُ مَا عَلَى الْمُحْصَنَاتِ الْمُزَوَّجَاتِ وَهُوَ الرَّجْمُ، وَهُوَ لَا يَتَنَاصَفُ فَيَجِبُ أَنْ تُرْجَمَ الْأَمَّةُ الْمُحَصَنَةُ إِذَا زَنَتْ، وَأَمَّا قَبْلَ الْإِحْصَانِ فَيَجِبُ جَلْدُهَا خَمْسِينَ. فَأَخْطَأَ فِي فَهْمِ الْآيَةِ وَخَالَفَ الْجُمْهُورَ فِي الْحُكْمِ، بَلْ قَدْ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الشَّافِعِيُّ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ: وَلَمْ يَخْتَلِفِ الْمُسْلِمُونَ فِي أَنْ لَا رَجْمَ عَلَى مَمْلُوكٍ فِي الزِّنَا؛ وَذَلِكَ لِأَنَّ الْآيَةَ دَلَّتْ عَلَى أَنَّ عَلَيْهِنَّ نِصْفُ مَا عَلَى الْمُحْصَنَاتِ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ، وَالْأَلِفُ وَاللَّامُ فِي الْمُحْصَنَاتِ لِلْعَهْدِ، وَهُنَّ الْمُحْصَنَاتُ الْمَذْكُورَاتُ فِي أَوَّلِ الْآيَةِ: ﴿وَمَنْ لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ مِنْكُمْ طَوْلا أَنْ يَنْكِحَ الْمُحْصَنَاتِ﴾ وَالْمُرَادُ بِهِنَّ الْحَرَائِرُ فَقَطْ، مِنْ غَيْرِ تَعَرُّضٍ لِتَزْوِيجِ غَيْرِهِ، وَقَوْلِهِ: ﴿نِصْفُ مَا عَلَى الْمُحْصَنَاتِ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ﴾ يَدُلُّ عَلَى أَنَّ الْمُرَادَ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ الَّذِي يُمْكِنُ تَنْصِيفُهُ وَهُوَ الْجَلْدُ لَا الرَّجْمُ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

ثُمَّ قَدْ رَوَى الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ [حَدِيثًا] نَصا فِي رَدِّ مَذْهَبِ أَبِي ثَوْرٍ مِنْ رِوَايَةِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ أَنَّ صَفِيَّةَ كَانَتْ قَدْ زَنَتْ بِرَجُلٍ مِنَ الْحُمْسِ، فَوَلَدَتْ غُلَامًا، فَادَّعَاهُ الزَّانِي، فَاخْتَصَمَا إِلَى عُثْمَانَ [بْنِ عَفَّانَ] فَرَفَعَهُمَا إِلَى عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ، فَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ: أَقْضِي فِيهِمَا بِقَضَاءِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "الْوَلَدُ للفِرَاش وللعَاهِر الحَجَر" وَجَلَدَهُمَا خَمْسِينَ خَمْسِينَ .

وَقِيلَ: بَلِ الْمُرَادُ مِنَ الْمَفْهُومِ التَّنْبِيهُ بِالْأَعْلَى عَلَى الْأَدْنَى، أَيْ: أَنَّ الْإِمَاءَ عَلَى النِّصْفِ مِنَ الْحَرَائِرِ فِي الْحَدِّ وَإِنْ كُنَّ مُحْصَنَاتٍ، وَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِنَّ رَجْمٌ أَصْلًا لَا قَبْلَ النِّكَاحِ وَلَا بَعْدَهُ، وَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْهِنَّ الْجَلْدُ فِي الْحَالَتَيْنِ بِالسُّنَّةِ. قَالَ ذَلِكَ صَاحِبُ الْإِفْصَاحِ عَنِ الشَّافِعِيِّ، فِيمَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ عَبْدِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْهُ. وَقَدْ ذَكَرَهُ الْبَيْهَقِيُّ فِي كِتَابِ السُّنَنِ وَالْآثَارِ، وَهُوَ بَعِيدٌ عَنْ لَفْظِ الْآيَةِ؛ لِأَنَّا إِنَّمَا اسْتَفَدْنَا تَنْصِيفَ الْحَدِّ مِنَ الْآيَةِ لَا مِنْ سِوَاهَا، فَكَيْفَ يُفْهَمُ مِنْهَا التَّنْصِيفُ فِيمَا عَدَاهَا، وَقَالَ: بَلْ أُرِيدُ بِأَنَّهَا فِي حَالِ الْإِحْصَانِ لَا يُقِيمُ الْحَدَّ عَلَيْهَا إِلَّا الْإِمَامُ، وَلَا يَجُوزُ لِسَيِّدِهَا إِقَامَةُ الْحَدِّ عَلَيْهَا وَالْحَالَةُ هَذِهِ -وَهُوَ قَوْلٌ فِي مَذْهَبِ الْإِمَامِ أَحْمَدَ رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ-فَأَمَّا قَبْلَ الْإِحْصَانِ فَلَهُ ذَلِكَ، وَالْحَدُّ فِي كِلَا الْمَوْضِعَيْنِ نِصْفُ حَدِّ الْحُرَّةِ. وَهَذَا أَيْضًا بَعِيدٌ؛ لِأَنَّهُ لَيْسَ فِي لَفْظِ الْآيَةِ مَا يدل عليه.

وَلَوْلَا هَذِهِ لَمْ نَدْرِ مَا حُكْمُ الْإِمَامِ فِي التَّنْصِيفِ، وَلَوَجَبَ دُخُولُهُنَّ فِي عُمُومِ الْآيَةِ فِي تَكْمِيلِ الْحَدِّ مِائَةً أَوْ رَجْمُهُنَّ، كَمَا أُثْبِتَ فِي الدَّلِيلِ عَلَيْهِ، وَقَدْ تَقَدَّمَ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ أَقِيمُوا عَلَى أَرِقَّائِكُمُ الْحَدَّ مَنْ أُحْصِنَ مِنْهُمْ وَمَنْ لَمْ يُحْصَنْ، وَعُمُومُ الْأَحَادِيثِ الْمُتَقَدِّمَةِ لَيْسَ فِيهَا تَفْصِيلٌ بَيْنَ الْمُزَوَّجَةِ وَغَيْرِهَا، لِحَدِيثِ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ الَّذِي احْتَجَّ بِهِ الْجُمْهُورُ: "إِذَا زَنَتْ أمةُ أحدِكم فَتَبَيَّنَ زِناهَا فَليجْلِدها الحدَّ وَلَا يُثَرِّبْ عَلَيْها".

مُلَخَّصُ الْآيَةِ: أَنَّهَا إِذَا زَنَتْ أَقْوَالٌ: أَحَدُهَا: أنها بجلد خَمْسِينَ قَبْلَ الْإِحْصَانِ وَبَعْدَهُ، وَهَلْ تُنْفَى؟ فِيهِ ثَلَاثَةُ أَقْوَالٍ:

[أَحَدُهَا] أَنَّهَا تُنْفَى عَنْهُ وَالثَّانِي: لَا تُنْفَى عَنْهُ مُطْلَقًا. [وَهُوَ قَوْلُ عَلِيٍّ وَفُقَهَاءِ الْمَدِينَةِ] وَالثَّالِثُ: أَنَّهَا تُنْفَى نِصْفَ سَنَةٍ وَهُوَ نَفْيُ نِصْفِ الْحُرَّةِ. وَهَذَا الْخِلَافُ فِي مَذْهَبِ الشَّافِعِيِّ، وَأَمَّا أَبُو حَنِيفَةَ فَعِنْدَهُ أَنَّ النَّفْيَ تَعْزِيرٌ لَيْسَ مِنْ تَمَامِ الْحَدِّ، وَإِنَّمَا هُوَ رَأْيُ الْإِمَامِ، إِنْ شَاءَ فَعَلَهُ وَإِنْ شَاءَ تَرَكَهُ فِي حَقِّ الرِّجَالِ وَالنِّسَاءِ، وَعِنْدَ مَالِكٍ أَنَّ النَّفْيَ إِنَّمَا هُوَ عَلَى الرِّجَالِ، وَأَمَّا النِّسَاءُ فَلَا ؛ لِأَنَّ ذَلِكَ مُضَادٌّ لِصِيَانَتِهِنَّ، [وَمَا وَرَدَ شَيْءٌ مِنَ النَّفْيِ فِي الرِّجَالِ وَلَا فِي النِّسَاءِ نَعَمْ حَدِيثُ عُبَادَة وَحَدِيثُ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ] أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَضَى فِيمَنْ زَنَى وَلَمْ يُحْصَنْ بِنَفْيِ عَامٍ وَبِإِقَامَةِ الْحَدِّ عَلَيْهِ، رَوَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ، وَ [كُلُّ] ذَلِكَ مَخْصُوصٌ بِالْمَعْنَى، وَهُوَ أَنَّ الْمَقْصُودَ مِنَ النَّفْيِ الصَّوْنُ وَذَلِكَ مَفْقُودٌ فِي نَفْيِ النِّسَاءِ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَالثَّانِي: أَنَّ الْأَمَةَ إِذَا زَنَتْ تُجلد خَمْسِينَ بَعْدَ الْإِحْصَانِ، وَتُضْرَبُ [قَبْلَهُ] تَأْدِيبًا غَيْرَ مَحْدُودٍ بِعَدَدٍ مَحْصُورٍ، وَقَدْ تَقَدَّمَ مَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ: أَنَّهَا لَا تُضْرَبُ قَبْلَ الْإِحْصَانِ، وَإِنْ أَرَادَ نَفْيَهُ فَيَكُونُ مَذْهَبًا بِالتَّأْوِيلِ وَإِلَّا فَهُوَ كَالْقَوْلِ الثَّانِي.

الْقَوْلُ الْآخِرُ: أَنَّهَا تُجْلَدُ قَبْلَ الْإِحْصَانِ مِائَةً وَبَعْدَهُ خَمْسِينَ، كَمَا هُوَ الْمَشْهُورُ عَنْ دَاوُدَ، وَ [هُوَ] أَضْعَفُ الْأَقْوَالِ: أَنَّهَا تُجْلَدُ قَبْلَ الْإِحْصَانِ خَمْسِينَ وَتُرْجَمَ بَعْدَهُ، وَهُوَ قَوْلُ أَبِي ثَوْرٍ، وَهُوَ ضَعِيفٌ أَيْضًا وَاللَّهُ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَى أَعْلَمُ بِالصَّوَابِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿ذَلِكَ لِمَنْ خَشِيَ الْعَنَتَ مِنْكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: إِنَّمَا يُبَاحُ نِكَاحُ الْإِمَاءِ بِالشُّرُوطِ الْمُتَقَدِّمَةِ لِمَنْ خَافَ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ الْوُقُوعَ فِي الزِّنَا، وَشَقَّ عَلَيْهِ الصَّبْرُ عَنِ الْجِمَاعِ، وَعَنَتَ بِسَبَبِ ذَلِكَ [كُلِّهِ، فَحِينَئِذٍ يَتَزَوَّجُ الْأَمَةَ، وَإِنْ تَرَكَ تَزَوُّجَ الْأَمَةِ] وَجَاهَدَ نَفْسَهُ فِي الْكَفِّ عَنِ الزِّنَا، فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ له؛ لأنه إذا تزوجها جَاءَ أَوْلَادُهُ أَرِقَّاءُ لِسَيِّدِهَا إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ الزَّوْجُ عَرَبِيًّا فَلَا تَكُونُ أَوْلَادُهُ مِنْهَا أَرِقَّاءَ فِي قَوْلٍ قَدِيمٍ لِلشَّافِعِيِّ، وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿وَأَنْ تَصْبِرُوا خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ وَاللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ﴾

وَمِنْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ الْكَرِيمَةِ اسْتَدَلَّ جمهورُ الْعُلَمَاءِ فِي جَوَازِ نِكَاحِ الْإِمَاءِ، عَلَى أَنَّهُ لَا بُدَّ مِنْ عَدَمِ الطَّوْل لِنِكَاحِ الْحَرَائِرِ وَمِنْ خَوْفِ الْعَنَتِ؛ لِمَا فِي نِكَاحِهِنَّ مِنْ مفْسَدة رِقِّ الْأَوْلَادِ، وَلِمَا فِيهِنَّ مِنَ الدَّنَاءَةِ فِي الْعُدُولِ عن الحرائر إليهن. وخالف الجمهورَ أبو حَنِيفَةَ وَأَصْحَابَهُ فِي اشْتِرَاطِ الْأَمْرَيْنِ، فَقَالُوا: مَتَى لَمْ يَكُنِ الرَّجُلُ مُزَوَّجًا بِحُرَّةِ جَازَ لَهُ نِكَاحُ الْأَمَةِ الْمُؤْمِنَةِ وَالْكِتَابِيَّةِ أَيْضًا، سَوَاءٌ كَانَ وَاجِدًا الطَّوْلَ لِحُرَّةٍ أَمْ لَا وَسَوَاءٌ خَافَ الْعَنَتَ أَمْ لَا وَعُمْدَتُهُمْ فِيمَا ذَهَبُوا إِلَيْهِ [عُمُومُ] قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَالْمُحْصَنَاتُ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتَابَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ﴾ [الْمَائِدَةِ: ٥] أَيِ: الْعَفَائِفُ، وَهُوَ يَعُمُّ الْحَرَائِرَ وَالْإِمَاءَ، وَهَذِهِ الْآيَةُ عَامَّةٌ، وَهَذِهِ أَيْضًا ظَاهِرَةٌ فِي الدَّلَالَةِ عَلَى مَا قَالَهُ الْجُمْهُورُ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

---

## 4:26-28 — verses 26-28 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Most High informs that He wishes to make clear to you, O believers, what He has made lawful for you and what He has forbidden you, from what has already been mentioned in this surah and elsewhere, {and to guide you to the ways of those who came before you}, meaning: their praiseworthy paths and the following of His laws which He loves and is pleased with, {and to turn to you in mercy}, that is, from sin and forbidden matters, {and Allah is All-Knowing, All-Wise}, that is, in His legislation, His decree, His actions, and His words.

As for His saying, {Allah wishes to turn to you in mercy, but those who follow their desires wish that you deviate a tremendous deviation}, meaning: the followers of the devils among the Jews, the Christians, and the fornicators wish {that you deviate}, that is, from the truth to falsehood, {a tremendous deviation. Allah wishes to lighten for you}, that is, in His laws, His commands, His prohibitions, and what He decrees for you. For this reason He permitted the marriage of slave women under their conditions, as Mujahid and others said: {mankind was created weak}, so lightening the burden suited him, due to his inherent weakness and the weakness of his resolve and determination.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] from the father of Ibn Tawus: {mankind was created weak}, that is, in the matter of women. And Waki' said: his reason departs in their presence.

And Musa the one who spoke with Allah (peace be upon him) said to our Prophet ﷺ on the night of the Isra, when he passed by him returning from the Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary, and said to him: "What has He enjoined upon you?" He said: "He commanded me with fifty prayers each day and night." Musa said to him: "Return to your Lord and ask Him to lighten it, for your community will not be able to bear that. I tested the people before you with what was less than that and they were unable to bear it, and your community is weaker in hearing, sight, and hearts." So he returned and ten were removed, then he returned to Musa, and it continued like that until five remained. [Allah, Mighty and Majestic, said: "They are five and they are fifty, the good deed multiplied tenfold."] The hadith continues.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يُخْبِرُ تَعَالَى أَنَّهُ يُريدُ أَنْ يُبَيِّنَ لَكُمْ -أَيُّهَا الْمُؤْمِنُونَ-مَا أَحَلَّ لَكُمْ وَحَرَّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ، مِمَّا تَقَدَّمَ ذِكْرُهُ فِي هَذِهِ السُّورَةِ وَغَيْرِهَا، ﴿وَيَهْدِيَكُمْ سُنَنَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ﴾ يَعْنِي: طَرَائِقَهُمُ الْحَمِيدَةَ وَاتِّبَاعَ شَرَائِعِهِ الَّتِي يُحِبُّهَا وَيَرْضَاهَا ﴿وَيَتُوبَ عَلَيْكُمْ﴾ أَيْ مِنَ الْإِثْمِ وَالْمَحَارِمِ، ﴿وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ﴾ أَيْ فِي شَرْعِهِ وَقَدَرِهِ وَأَفْعَالِهِ وَأَقْوَالِهِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿ [وَاللَّهُ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَتُوبَ عَلَيْكُمْ] وَيُرِيدُ الَّذِينَ يَتَّبِعُونَ الشَّهَوَاتِ أَنْ تَمِيلُوا مَيْلا عَظِيمًا﴾ أَيْ: يُريد أَتْبَاعُ الشَّيَاطِينِ مِنَ الْيَهُودِ وَالنَّصَارَى وَالزُّنَاةِ ﴿أَنْ تَمِيلُوا﴾ يَعْنِي: عَنِ الْحَقِّ إِلَى الْبَاطِلِ ﴿مَيْلا عَظِيمًا. يُرِيدُ اللَّهُ أَنْ يُخَفِّفَ عَنْكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: فِي شَرَائِعِهِ وَأَوَامِرِهِ وَنَوَاهِيهِ وَمَا يُقَدِّرُهُ لَكُمْ، وَلِهَذَا أَبَاحَ [نِكَاحَ] الْإِمَاءِ بِشُرُوطِهِ، كَمَا قَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ وَغَيْرُهُ: ﴿خُلِقَ الإنْسَانُ ضَعِيفًا﴾ فَنَاسَبَهُ التَّخْفِيفُ؛ لِضِعْفِهِ فِي نَفْسِهِ وَضِعْفِ عَزْمِهِ وَهِمَّتِهِ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ [الْأَحْمَسِيُّ] حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيع، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ، عَنِ أَبِيهِ: ﴿خُلِقَ الإنْسَانُ ضَعِيفًا﴾ أَيْ: فِي أَمْرِ النِّسَاءِ، وَقَالَ وَكِيعٌ: يَذْهَبُ عَقْلُهُ عِنْدَهُنَّ.

وَقَالَ مُوسَى الْكَلِيمُ عَلَيْهِ الصَّلَاةُ وَالسَّلَامُ لِنَبِيِّنَا صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ وَسَلَامُهُ عَلَيْهِ لَيْلَةَ الْإِسْرَاءِ حِينَ مَرَّ عَلَيْهِ رَاجِعًا مِنْ عِنْدِ سدْرة الْمُنْتَهَى، فَقَالَ لَهُ: مَاذَا فَرَضَ عليكم ؟ فقال: "أمرني بخمسين صَلَاةً فِي كُلِّ يَوْمٍ وَلَيْلَةٍ" فَقَالَ لَهُ: ارْجِعْ إِلَى رَبِّكَ فَاسْأَلْهُ التَّخْفِيفَ؛ فَإِنَّ أُمَّتَكَ لَا تُطِيقُ ذَلِكَ، فَإِنِّي قَدْ بَلَوْتُ النَّاسَ قَبْلَكَ عَلَى مَا هُوَ أَقَلَّ مِنْ ذَلِكَ فَعَجَزُوا، وَإِنَّ أُمَّتَكَ أَضْعَفُ أَسْمَاعًا وَأَبْصَارًا وَقُلُوبًا، فَرَجَعَ فَوَضَعَ عَشْرًا، ثُمَّ رَجَعَ إِلَى مُوسَى فَلَمْ يَزَلْ كَذَلِكَ حَتَّى بَقِيَتْ خَمْسًا [قَالَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: "هُنَّ خَمْسٌ وَهُنَّ خَمْسُونَ، الْحَسَنَةُ بِعَشْرِ أَمْثَالِهَا"] الْحَدِيثَ.

---

## 4:29-31 — verses 29-31 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Blessed and Exalted forbade His believing servants from consuming one another's wealth by falsehood, that is, by kinds of earnings that are not lawful in the Shariah, such as the various forms of riba (usury) and gambling, and whatever runs the same course among all the varieties of stratagems, even if in the outward form of the legal ruling they appear otherwise, in cases where God knows that the one engaging in them only intends to devise a stratagem toward riba. Ibn Jarir even said:

[chain] Ibn al-Muthanna, told to us by Abd al-Wahhab, told to us by Dawud, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas, concerning a man who buys a garment from another man and says: "If I am pleased with it I will take it, otherwise I will return it and return along with it a dirham," he said: this is what God, Mighty and Majestic, referred to when He said: {And do not consume your wealth among yourselves in falsehood}.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Ali ibn Harb al-Mawsili, told to us by Ibn Fudayl, from Dawud al-Awdi, from Amir, from Alqamah, from Abd Allah, [regarding {O you who believe, do not consume your wealth among yourselves in falsehood}] he said: it is a firmly established word (kalima muhkama) that has not been abrogated and will not be abrogated until the Day of Resurrection.

Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas: When God revealed {O you who believe, do not consume your wealth among yourselves in falsehood}, the Muslims said: God has forbidden us to consume our wealth among ourselves in falsehood, and food is the most valuable of possessions, so it is not lawful for any of us to eat at the house of another, so how much more so with other people! Then God revealed after that {There is no restriction upon the blind} [al-Nur: 61] and the rest of the verse. [Qatadah ibn Di'amah said likewise.]

Regarding His statement, {except that it be trade by mutual consent among you}: it has been read both "tijaratun" (nominative) and "tijaratan" (accusative), and this is an exception of the disjunctive type (istithna munqati'), as if He were saying: do not engage in the forbidden means of acquiring wealth, but as for lawful trade that occurs by mutual consent of the seller and the buyer, then do that and use it as your means of acquiring wealth. This is like His saying, exalted is He, {And do not kill the soul that God has forbidden, except by right} [al-An'am: 151], and like His saying {They will not taste death therein except the first death} [al-Dukhan: 56].

From this noble verse al-Shafi'i, may God have mercy on him, argued that a sale is not valid except by verbal acceptance (qabul), because it explicitly indicates mutual consent, unlike mere exchange of goods (mu'atah), which may not necessarily indicate consent. The majority, namely Malik, Abu Hanifah, Ahmad, and their companions, disagreed with him on this, holding that just as words indicate mutual consent, so too do actions in certain contexts definitively indicate it. So they held the sale by mu'atah to be valid absolutely, while some of them said: it is valid in matters of trivial value, and in whatever people commonly regard as a sale, and this is a cautious position taken by the verifying scholars of the school, and God knows best.

Mujahid said: {except that it be trade by mutual consent among you} means a sale, or a gift that one person gives to another. Ibn Jarir related this, then said:

[chain] Ibn Waki', told to us by my father, from al-Qasim, from Sulayman al-Ju'fi, from his father, from Maymun ibn Mihran, who said: the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Trade is by mutual consent, and the option (khiyar) is after the transaction, and it is not lawful for a Muslim to deceive another Muslim." This is a mursal (chain missing the Companion) hadith.

Part of the completeness of mutual consent is the establishment of the option of the session (khiyar al-majlis), as is established in the two Sahihs, that the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "The two parties to a sale each have the option as long as they have not separated." And in the wording of al-Bukhari: "When two men conduct a sale, each of them has the option as long as they have not separated."

Al-Shafi'i, Ahmad ibn Hanbal, their companions, and the majority of the early and later scholars held to the implication of this hadith. From this comes the legitimacy of the stipulated option (khiyar al-shart) after the contract, up to three days, [as is agreed upon among the scholars, extending even further than three days] according to what is needed to clarify the condition of the sold item, even up to a year in the case of real estate and the like, as is well known from Malik, may God have mercy on him. And they held the sale by mu'atah to be valid absolutely, and this is one position within the Shafi'i school, while some of them said: the sale by mu'atah is valid in matters of trivial value, in whatever people commonly regard as a sale, and this is the choice of a group among the companions of the school.

Regarding His statement, {and do not kill yourselves}: that is, by committing what God has forbidden and engaging in acts of disobedience to Him, and by consuming your wealth among yourselves in falsehood. {Indeed God is ever Merciful to you}, that is, in what He has commanded you and what He has forbidden you.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Hasan ibn Musa, told to us by Ibn Lahi'ah, told to us by Yazid ibn Abi Habib, from Imran ibn Abi Anas, from Abd al-Rahman ibn Jubayr, from Amr ibn al-As, may God be pleased with him, that he said, when the Prophet ﷺ sent him in the year of Dhat al-Salasil: I had a nocturnal emission on a very cold night, and I feared that if I performed a full ritual washing (ghusl) I would perish, so I performed tayammum (dry ablution) and then led my companions in the dawn prayer. He said: When I came to the Messenger of God ﷺ I mentioned that to him, and he said: "O Amr, did you lead your companions in prayer while in a state of major ritual impurity?" He said: I said, O Messenger of God, I had a nocturnal emission on a very cold night, and I feared that if I performed ghusl I would perish, so I remembered the saying of God, Mighty and Majestic, {And do not kill yourselves, indeed God is ever Merciful to you}, so I performed tayammum and then prayed. The Messenger of God ﷺ laughed and said nothing.

Abu Dawud related it likewise, from the hadith of Yahya ibn Ayyub, from Yazid ibn Abi Habib, with the same content. He also related it from Muhammad ibn Abi Salamah, from Ibn Wahb, from Ibn Lahi'ah and Umar ibn al-Harith, both of them from Yazid ibn Abi Habib, from Imran ibn Abi Anas, from Abd al-Rahman ibn Jubayr al-Misri, from Abu Qays, freedman of Amr ibn al-As, from him, mentioning something similar. This, and God knows best, is closer to being correct.

Abu Bakr ibn Mardawayh said: [chain] Abd al-Rahman ibn Muhammad ibn Hamid al-Balkhi, told to us by Muhammad ibn Salih ibn Sahl al-Balkhi, told to us by Ubayd Allah ibn Umar al-Qawariri, told to us by Yusuf ibn Khalid, told to us by Ziyad ibn Sa'd, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas: that Amr ibn al-As led the people in prayer while in a state of major ritual impurity, and when they came to the Messenger of God ﷺ they mentioned that to him, so he summoned him and asked him about it, and he said: O Messenger of God, I feared that the cold would kill me, and God, exalted is He, has said {And do not kill yourselves [indeed God is ever Merciful to you]}. He said: so the Messenger of God ﷺ remained silent about it.

Then Ibn Mardawayh cited, in connection with this noble verse, from the hadith of al-A'mash, from Abu Salih, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Whoever kills himself with a piece of iron, his piece of iron will be in his hand, and he will stab himself with it in his belly on the Day of Resurrection in the fire of Hell, abiding therein forever and ever. And whoever kills himself with poison, his poison will be in his hand, and he will sip it in the fire of Hell, abiding therein forever and ever. And whoever throws himself from a mountain and kills himself, he will be one who falls in the fire of Hell, abiding therein forever and ever."

This hadith is established in the two Sahihs, and likewise Abu al-Zinad related it, from al-A'raj, from Abu Hurayrah, from the Prophet ﷺ, with similar content. And from Abu Qilabah, from Thabit ibn al-Dahhak, may God be pleased with him, who said: the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Whoever kills himself with something will be punished with it on the Day of Resurrection." The group of hadith compilers included this in their books by way of Abu Qilabah. And in the two Sahihs, from the hadith of al-Hasan, from Jundab ibn Abd Allah al-Bajali, who said: the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "There was a man among those who came before you who had a wound, and he took a knife and slit his hand with it, and the bleeding did not stop until he died. God, Mighty and Majestic, said: My servant hastened his own end, I have forbidden Paradise to him."

For this reason God, exalted is He, said: {And whoever does that in transgression and injustice}, that is, whoever engages in what God has forbidden him, transgressing in it and being unjust in his engaging in it, that is, knowing its prohibition and daring to violate it, {then We shall cast him into a fire, and that is easy for God}. This is a severe threat and a firm warning, so let every sensible, discerning person beware of it, among those who lend their ear while bearing witness.

Regarding His statement, {If you avoid the grave sins which you are forbidden, We will remove from you your lesser sins [and admit you to a noble entrance]}, that is: if you avoid the grave sins which you have been forbidden, We will remove from you your lesser sins and admit you into Paradise. For this reason He said: {and admit you to a noble entrance}.

Al-Hafiz Abu Bakr al-Bazzar said: Mu'ammal ibn Hisham narrated to us, Isma'il ibn Ibrahim narrated to us, Khalid ibn Ayyub narrated to us, from Mu'awiyah ibn Qurrah, from Anas [raising it to the Prophet ﷺ]: "That which has reached us from our Lord, Mighty and Majestic, and for which we would not go forth giving up every family and wealth, is that He overlooks for us what falls short of the major sins. Allah [the Exalted] says: {If you avoid the major sins which you are forbidden, We will remove from you your lesser sins [and admit you to a noble entrance]}."

Hadiths related to this noble verse have come down to us, so let us mention what is convenient of them.

Imam Ahmad said: Hushaym narrated to us from Mughirah, from Abu Ma'shar, from Ibrahim, from Qarthaʿ al-Dabbi, from Salman al-Farisi, who said: The Prophet ﷺ said to me, "Do you know what the day of Jumu'ah is?" I said: It is the day on which Allah gathered your father. He said: "But I know what the day of Jumu'ah is. There is no man who purifies himself, performing his purification well, then comes to the Friday prayer and remains silent until the imam finishes his prayer, except that it becomes an expiation for him between that Friday and the following Friday, so long as the mortal sins (al-maqtalah) are avoided." Al-Bukhari also related something similar to this from Salman by another route.

Abu Ja'far ibn Jarir said: Al-Muthanna [ibn Ibrahim] narrated to me, Abu Salih narrated to us, al-Layth narrated to us, Khalid narrated to me, from Sa'id ibn Abi Hilal, from Nu'aym al-Mujmir, Suhayb the freedman of al-'Utwari informed me, that he heard Abu Hurayrah and Abu Sa'id saying: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ addressed us one day and said: "By the One in whose hand is my soul," three times, then he bowed his head, and every man among us bowed his head weeping, not knowing what he had sworn about. Then he raised his head and there was joy on his face, which was dearer to us than red camels. He [ﷺ] said: "There is no servant who prays the five prayers, fasts Ramadan, pays the zakat, and avoids the seven major sins, except that the gates of Paradise are opened for him, and then it is said to him: Enter in peace."

This is likewise how al-Nasa'i and al-Hakim in his Mustadrak related it, from the hadith of al-Layth ibn Sa'd. Al-Hakim also related it, as did Ibn Hibban in his Sahih, from the hadith of 'Abdullah ibn Wahb, from 'Amr ibn al-Harith, from Sa'id ibn Abi Hilal, with the same chain. Al-Hakim then said: Sound according to the criterion of the two Shaykhs (al-Bukhari and Muslim), though they did not include it.

Explanation of these seven:

This is established in the two Sahihs from the hadith of Sulayman ibn Bilal, from Thawr ibn Zayd, from Salim Abu al-Ghayth, from Abu Hurayrah, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Avoid the seven destructive sins." It was said: O Messenger of Allah, what are they? He said: "Associating partners with Allah, killing a soul which Allah has forbidden except by right, sorcery, consuming usury, consuming the property of an orphan, fleeing on the day of battle, and slandering chaste believing women who are unaware."

Another route on his authority: Ibn Abi Hatim said: My father narrated to us, Fahd ibn 'Awf narrated to us, Abu 'Awanah narrated to us, from 'Amr ibn Abi Salamah, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The major sins are seven: first among them is associating partners with Allah, then killing a soul without right, consuming usury, consuming the property of an orphan until he reaches maturity, fleeing from battle, accusing chaste women, and returning to bedouin life after emigration (hijrah)."

The mention of these seven as major sins does not negate what is beyond them, except according to one who holds the view of mafhum al-laqab (the implicit meaning derived from a proper designation), which is a weak position in the absence of a contextual indicator, especially when there is explicit evidence against such an implied meaning, as we shall present from the hadiths that include major sins other than these seven. Among these is what al-Hakim related in his Mustadrak, where he said:

Ahmad ibn Kamil the judge narrated to us by dictation, Abu Qilabah 'Abd al-Malik ibn Muhammad narrated to us, Mu'adh ibn Hani' narrated to us, Harb ibn Shaddad narrated to us, Yahya ibn Abi Kathir narrated to us, from 'Abd al-Hamid ibn Sinan, from 'Ubayd ibn 'Umayr, from his father, that is, 'Umayr ibn Qatadah, may Allah be pleased with him, who had companionship with the Prophet, that he narrated to him that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said during the Farewell Pilgrimage: "Behold, the allies of Allah who pray are those who establish the five prayers which have been prescribed for them, who fast Ramadan seeking its reward, regarding it as an obligation upon them, who give the zakat of their wealth seeking its reward, and who avoid the major sins which Allah has forbidden." Then a man asked him, saying: O Messenger of Allah, what are the major sins? He said: "They are nine: associating partners with Allah, killing a believing soul without right, fleeing on the day of battle, consuming the property of an orphan, consuming usury, slandering a chaste woman, disobedience to one's two Muslim parents, and treating the Sacred House, your qiblah, as permissible to violate, whether alive or dead." Then he said: "No man dies without having committed these major sins, while establishing the prayer and paying the zakat, except that he will be with the Prophet ﷺ in a dwelling whose doors are made of gold."

This is how al-Hakim related it at length. Abu Dawud and al-Tirmidhi also extracted it in abridged form from the hadith of Mu'adh ibn Hani', with the same chain. Ibn Abi Hatim likewise related it from his hadith in expanded form. Al-Hakim then said: Its narrators are all cited as evidence in the two Sahihs except 'Abd al-Hamid ibn Sinan.

I say: he is from the Hijaz and is not known except through this hadith. Ibn Hibban mentioned him in his book of trustworthy narrators (al-Thiqat), while al-Bukhari said: There is something questionable in his hadith.

Ibn Jarir related it from Sulayman ibn Thabit al-Jahdari, from Salm ibn Salam, from Ayyub ibn 'Utbah, from Yahya ibn Abi Kathir, from 'Ubayd ibn 'Umayr, from his father, and mentioned it, but he did not mention 'Abd al-Hamid ibn Sinan in the chain. Allah knows best.

Another hadith with a similar meaning to what preceded: Ibn Marduyah said: 'Abdullah ibn Ja'far narrated to us, Ahmad ibn Yunus narrated to us, Yahya ibn 'Abd al-Hamid narrated to us, 'Abd al-'Aziz ibn Muslim ibn al-Walid narrated to us, from al-Muttalib, from 'Abdullah ibn Hantab, from 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr, who said: The Prophet ﷺ ascended the pulpit and said: "I do not swear, I do not swear." Then he came down and said: "Rejoice, rejoice. Whoever prays the five prayers and avoids the seven major sins will be called from the gates of Paradise: Enter." 'Abd al-'Aziz said: I do not know him except to have said "in peace." Al-Muttalib said: I heard someone ask 'Abdullah ibn 'Amr: Did you hear the Messenger of Allah ﷺ mention them? He said: Yes: "disobedience to parents, associating partners with Allah, killing a soul, slandering chaste women, consuming the property of an orphan, fleeing from battle, and consuming usury."

Another hadith with a similar meaning: Abu Ja'far ibn Jarir said in the Tafsir: Ya'qub narrated to us, Ibn 'Ulayyah narrated to us, Ziyad ibn Mikhraq informed us, from Taysalah ibn Mayyas, who said: I was with the Najdat (a Kharijite faction), and I committed sins which I could only regard as major sins. I met Ibn 'Umar and said to him: I have committed sins which I can only regard as major sins. He said: What are they? I said: I did such and such. He said: That is not among the major sins. I said: And I did such and such. He said: That is not among the major sins, he said with something Taysalah did not name. He said: They are nine, and I will enumerate them for you: associating partners with Allah, killing a soul without right, fleeing from battle, slandering a chaste woman, consuming usury, consuming the property of an orphan unjustly, deviation (ilhad) in the Sacred Mosque, practicing sorcery, and causing one's parents to weep through disobedience. Ziyad said: Taysalah said, when he saw Ibn 'Umar: I became afraid. He said: Do you fear entering the Fire? I said: Yes. He said: And do you love to enter Paradise? I said: Yes. He said: Are your two parents alive? I said: I have my mother with me. He said: By Allah, if you soften your speech to her and feed her food, you will surely enter Paradise, so long as you avoid the sins that necessitate the Fire.

Another route: Ibn Jarir said: Sulayman ibn Thabit al-Jahdari al-Wasiti narrated to us, Salm ibn Salam narrated to us, Ayyub ibn 'Utbah narrated to us, from Taysalah ibn 'Ali al-Nahdi, who said: I came to Ibn 'Umar while he was in the shade of an arak tree on the Day of 'Arafah, pouring water over his head and face. I said: Tell me about the major sins. He said: They are nine. I said: What are they? He said: Associating partners with Allah, slandering a chaste woman, he said, I said: Before killing? He said: Yes, and even more so, and killing a believing soul, fleeing from battle, sorcery, consuming usury, consuming the property of an orphan, disobedience to one's two Muslim parents, and violating the Sacred House, your qiblah, whether alive or dead.

Thus he related it through these two chains as mawquf (halted report). It was also related by Ali ibn al-Ja'd, from Ayyub ibn Utbah, from Taysalah ibn Ali [al-Nahdi], who said: I came to Ibn Umar on the evening of Arafah, while he was under the shade of an arak tree, pouring water on his head, and I asked him about the major sins. He said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say, "They are seven." I said: What are they? He said: "Associating partners with Allah, accusing a chaste woman of unchastity," (he said: I said, before shedding blood? He said: Yes, and reluctantly so) "and killing a believing soul, fleeing from the battlefield, sorcery, consuming usury, consuming the property of an orphan, disobedience to one's parents, and deviation within the Sacred House, your qiblah, whether alive or dead."

Likewise it was related by al-Hasan ibn Musa al-Ashyab, from Ayyub ibn Utbah al-Yamani, though there is weakness in him. And Allah knows best.

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Zakariyya ibn Adi told us, Baqiyyah told us, from Bahir ibn Sa'd, from Khalid ibn Ma'dan, that Abu Ruhm al-Sama'i related to them, from Abu Ayyub, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "Whoever worships Allah, associating nothing with Him, establishes prayer, gives zakat, fasts Ramadan, and avoids the major sins, for him is Paradise" or "he enters Paradise." A man asked him: What are the major sins? He said: "Associating partners with Allah, killing a Muslim soul, and fleeing on the day of battle."

Ahmad also related it, as did al-Nasa'i, through other chains, from Baqiyyah.

Another hadith: The hafiz Abu Bakr ibn Mardawayh related in his tafsir, through the route of Sulayman ibn Dawud al-Yamani, who is weak, from al-Zuhri, from Abu Bakr ibn Muhammad ibn Amr ibn Hazm, from his father, from his grandfather, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ wrote a letter to the people of Yemen containing the obligatory duties, the sunan, and the blood-money rulings, and sent it with Amr ibn Hazm. He said: In the letter was written: "The greatest of the major sins before Allah on the Day of Resurrection are: associating partners with Allah, killing a believing soul without right, fleeing in the path of Allah on the day of battle, disobedience to one's parents, accusing a chaste woman of unchastity, learning sorcery, consuming usury, and consuming the property of an orphan."

Another hadith mentioning false testimony: Imam Ahmad said: Muhammad ibn Ja'far told us, Shu'bah told us, Ubaydullah ibn Abi Bakr told me, saying: I heard Anas ibn Malik say: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ mentioned the major sins, or was asked about the major sins, and said: "Associating partners with Allah, killing a soul, and disobedience to parents." And he said: "Shall I not inform you of the greatest of the major sins?" He said: "False speech," or "false testimony." Shu'bah said: I am most inclined to think he said "false testimony."

Both al-Bukhari and Muslim reported it from the hadith of Shu'bah as such. Ibn Mardawayh also related it through two other gharib (singular) chains from Anas, with similar wording.

Another hadith: Reported by the two Shaykhs (al-Bukhari and Muslim) from the hadith of Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Bakrah, from his father, who said: The Prophet ﷺ said, "Shall I not inform you of the greatest of the major sins?" We said: Yes indeed, O Messenger of Allah. He said: "Associating partners with Allah, and disobedience to parents." He had been reclining, then he sat up and said: "Indeed, and false testimony. Indeed, and false speech." He kept repeating it until we said: We wish he would stop.

Another hadith mentioning the killing of one's child, established in the two Sahihs, from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, who said: I said: O Messenger of Allah, which sin is greatest (in another narration: which is the greater)? He said: "That you set up a rival to Allah while it is He who created you." I said: Then which? He said: "That you kill your child for fear that he will eat with you." I said: Then which? He said: "That you commit adultery with your neighbor's wife." Then he recited: {And those who do not invoke another god besides Allah, [nor kill the soul which Allah has forbidden except by right, nor commit unlawful sexual intercourse; and whoever does that will meet a penalty]} up to His saying: {except one who repents} [al-Furqan: 68].

Another hadith mentioning the drinking of wine. Ibn Abi Hatim said: Yunus ibn Abd al-A'la informed us, Ibn Wahb informed us, Abu Sakhr told me: that a man related to him from Amrah ibn Hazm, that he heard Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As, while at al-Hijr in Mecca, being asked about wine. He said: By Allah, it would be a grave matter before Allah for an old man like myself to lie in this station about the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He then went and asked him, and returned saying: I asked him about wine, and he said: "It is the greatest of the major sins and the mother of indecencies. Whoever drinks wine abandons prayer and may fall upon his mother, his maternal aunt, and his paternal aunt." Gharib from this route.
