The Ansari said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ remained standing until I began to feel pity for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ for the length of his standing. When he finished, I said: O Messenger of Allah, this man kept you standing until I began to feel pity for you at the length of your standing. He said: "Did you indeed see him?" I said: yes. He said: "Do you know who he was?" I said: no. He said: "That was Gabriel. He kept exhorting me concerning the neighbor until I thought he would make him an heir." Then he said: "Had you greeted him with peace, he would have returned the greeting of peace to you."

The seventh hadith: Abd ibn Humayd said in his Musnad: [chain] Jabir ibn Abdullah said: a man came from the outlying districts while the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and Gabriel (peace be upon him) were performing prayer where the funeral prayer is performed. When he finished, the man said: O Messenger of Allah, who was this man I saw with you? He said: "Did you indeed see him?" He said: yes. He said: "You have indeed seen much good. This is Gabriel. He kept exhorting me concerning the neighbor until it seemed to me that he would make him an heir."

This is related solely by this route, and it corroborates the one before it.

The eighth hadith: Abu Bakr al-Bazzar said: [chain] Jabir ibn Abdullah said: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Neighbors are of three kinds: a neighbor who has one right, and he is the least of neighbors in right; a neighbor who has two rights; and a neighbor who has three rights, and he is the most excellent of neighbors in right. As for the one who has one right, he is a polytheist neighbor with no kinship tie, who has only the right of neighborliness. As for the one who has two rights, he is a Muslim neighbor, who has the right of Islam and the right of neighborliness. And as for the one who has three rights, he is a Muslim neighbor who is also a relative, who has the right of neighborliness, the right of Islam, and the right of kinship."

Al-Bazzar said: we do not know of anyone who related from Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Fudayl except Ibn Abi Fudayk.

The ninth hadith: Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Aisha asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, saying: I have two neighbors, so to which of them should I send a gift? He said: "To whichever of them has the nearer door to you." Bukhari related it from the hadith of Shu'bah, with this wording.

Concerning His saying: {and the companion at one's side}, al-Thawri said, from Jabir al-Ju'fi, from al-Sha'bi, from Ali and Ibn Mas'ud, they both said: it is the wife.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: something similar has been related from Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Layla, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, al-Hasan, and Sa'id ibn Jubayr in one of the reports.

Ibn Abbas, Mujahid, Ikrimah, and Qatadah said: it is the companion in travel. Sa'id ibn Jubayr said: it is the righteous companion. Zayd ibn Aslam said: he is your companion in sitting while at home, and your companion in travel.

As for {the wayfarer (ibn al-sabil)}, from Ibn Abbas and a group it is: the guest.

Mujahid, Abu Ja'far al-Baqir, al-Hasan, al-Dahhak, and Muqatil said: it is the one who passes by you as a traveler, journeying through.

This is more apparent, though if the one who said "the guest" meant the one passing by on the road, then the two are the same. The discussion of "sons of the road" (wayfarers) will come in Surat Bara'ah (al-Tawbah), and trust is in God, and reliance is upon Him.

His statement, {and those whom your right hands possess}, is an instruction concerning slaves, because the slave is weak in his capacity to act for himself and is a captive in the hands of people. For this reason it is established that the Messenger of God ﷺ kept instructing his nation during his death illness, saying: "Prayer, prayer, and those whom your right hands possess." He kept repeating it until his tongue could scarcely bring it forth.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] al-Miqdam ibn Ma'ikarib said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Whatever you feed yourself is charity for you, whatever you feed your child is charity for you, whatever you feed your wife is charity for you, and whatever you feed your servant is charity for you."

Al-Nasa'i also related it through the hadith of Baqiyyah, and its chain is sound, praise be to God.

It is reported from Abdullah ibn Amr that he said to his steward: "Have you given the slaves their provision?" He said: "No." He said: "Go and give it to them, for the Messenger of God ﷺ said: 'It is enough of a sin for a man that he withhold from those whose provision he controls.'" Related by Muslim.

From Abu Hurayrah, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "The slave is entitled to his food and clothing, and he should not be burdened with work beyond what he can bear." Also related by Muslim.

And from him, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "When your servant brings you your food, if you do not seat him with you, then give him a mouthful or two, or a bite or two, for he was the one who bore its heat and its preparation."

Both Bukhari and Muslim related it, and the wording of Muslim is: "let him seat him with him so he may eat; and if the food is scant, let him place in his hand a bite or two."

From Abu Dharr, may God be pleased with him, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "They are your brothers, your dependents, whom God has placed under your authority. So whoever has his brother under his authority, let him feed him from what he eats, and let him clothe him from what he wears. Do not burden them with what overwhelms them, and if you do burden them with it, then help them." Both Bukhari and Muslim related it.

His statement, {Indeed, God does not love whoever is boastful and self-glorifying}, means: conceited within himself, self-admiring, arrogant, boastful before people, seeing himself as better than them. So he is great in his own eyes, yet he is contemptible before God and hateful before people.

Mujahid said concerning His statement, {Indeed, God does not love whoever is boastful}, meaning: arrogant, {and self-glorifying}, meaning: he counts up what he has been given while not thanking God, Mighty and Majestic. That is, he boasts before people about the blessings God has given him, while being scant in his gratitude to God for them.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Abdullah ibn Waqid Abu Raja al-Harawi said: "You will not find one who treats those in his possession badly except that you find him boastful and self-glorifying," and he recited: {and those whom your right hands possess. Indeed, God does not love whoever is boastful and self-glorifying}. "Nor will you find one undutiful to his parents except that you find him tyrannical and wretched," and he recited: {and dutiful to my mother, and He has not made me a tyrant, wretched} [Maryam: 32].

Ibn Abi Hatim related the like of this from al-Awwam ibn Hawshab concerning "the boastful, self-glorifying one." He said:

[chain] Yazid ibn Abdullah ibn al-Shikhkhir said: Mutarrif said: "A hadith used to reach me from Abu Dharr which I longed to hear directly, so I met him and said: 'O Abu Dharr, it has reached me that you claim the Messenger of God ﷺ told you: "Indeed God loves three and detests three."' He said: 'Yes, and I do not think that I would lie about my beloved friend,' three times. I said: 'Who are the three whom God detests?' He said: 'The boastful, self-glorifying one, do you not find him among yourselves in the revealed Book of God?' Then he recited the verse: {Indeed, God does not love whoever is boastful and self-glorifying} [al-Nisa: 36]."

[chain] A man from Balhujaym said: "I said: O Messenger of God, advise me. He said: 'Beware of letting your garment trail, for the trailing of the garment is from vanity, and indeed God does not love vanity.'"

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَأْمُرُ تَبَارَكَ وَتَعَالَى بِعِبَادَتِهِ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ؛ فَإِنَّهُ هُوَ الْخَالِقُ الرَّازِقُ الْمُنْعِمُ الْمُتَفَضِّلُ عَلَى خَلْقِهِ فِي جَمِيعِ الْآنَاتِ وَالْحَالَاتِ، فَهُوَ الْمُسْتَحِقُّ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُوَحِّدُوهُ، وَلَا يُشْرِكُوا بِهِ شَيْئًا مِنْ مَخْلُوقَاتِهِ، كَمَا قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ لِمُعَاذٍ: "أتَدْرِي مَا حَقُّ اللَّهِ عَلَى الْعِبَادِ ؟ " قَالَ: اللَّهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَعْلَمُ. قَالَ: "أَنْ يَعْبدُوهُ ولا يُشْرِكُوا بِهِ شَيْئًا"، ثُمَّ قَالَ: "أتَدْري مَا حَقُّ العبادِ عَلَى اللهِ إِذَا فَعَلُوا ذَلِكَ؟ أَلَّا يُعَذِّبَهُم" ثُمَّ أَوْصَى بِالْإِحْسَانِ إِلَى الْوَالِدَيْنِ، فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ، سُبْحَانَهُ، جَعَلَهُمَا سَبَبًا لِخُرُوجِكَ مِنَ الْعَدَمِ إِلَى الْوُجُودِ، وَكَثِيرًا مَا يقرنُ اللَّهُ، سُبْحَانَهُ، بَيْنَ عِبَادَتِهِ وَالْإِحْسَانِ إِلَى الْوَالِدَيْنِ، كَقَوْلِهِ: ﴿أَنِ اشْكُرْ لِي وَلِوَالِدَيْكَ﴾ [لُقْمَانَ:١٤] وَكَقَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَقَضَى رَبُّكَ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوا إِلا إِيَّاهُ وَبِالْوَالِدَيْنِ إِحْسَانًا﴾ [الْإِسْرَاءِ: ٢٣] .

ثُمَّ عَطَفَ عَلَى الْإِحْسَانِ إِلَى الْوَالِدَيْنِ الْإِحْسَانَ إِلَى الْقَرَابَاتِ مِنَ الرِّجَالِ وَالنِّسَاءِ، كَمَا جَاءَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ: "الصَّدَقَةُ عَلَى المِسْكِينِ صَدَقَةٌ، وعَلَى ذِي الرَّحِم صَدَقَةٌ وصِلَةٌ" .

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿وَالْيَتَامَى﴾ وَذَلِكَ لِأَنَّهُمْ قَدْ فَقَدُوا مَنْ يَقُومُ بِمَصَالِحِهِمْ، وَمَنْ يُنْفِقُ عَلَيْهِمْ، فَأَمَرَ اللَّهُ بِالْإِحْسَانِ إِلَيْهِمْ وَالْحُنُوِّ عَلَيْهِمْ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿وَالْمَسَاكِينِ﴾ وَهُمُ الْمَحَاوِيجُ مِنْ ذَوِي الْحَاجَاتِ الَّذِينَ لَا يَجِدُونَ مَا يَقُومُ بِكِفَايَتِهِمْ، فَأَمَرَ اللَّهُ بِمُسَاعَدَتِهِمْ بِمَا تَتِمُّ بِهِ كِفَايَتُهُمْ وَتَزُولُ بِهِ ضَرُورَتُهُمْ. وَسَيَأْتِي الْكَلَامُ عَلَى الْفَقِيرِ وَالْمِسْكِينِ فِي سُورَةِ بَرَاءَةَ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَالْجَارِ ذِي الْقُرْبَى وَالْجَارِ الْجُنُبِ﴾ قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿وَالْجَارِ ذِي الْقُرْبَى﴾ يَعْنِي الَّذِي بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَهُ قَرَابَةٌ، ﴿وَالْجَارِ الْجُنُبِ﴾ الَّذِي لَيْسَ بَيْنَكَ وَبَيْنَهُ قُرَابَةٌ. وَكَذَا رُوِيَ عَنْ عِكْرِمةَ، ومُجَاهد، وَمَيْمُونِ بنِ مهْرانَ، وَالضَّحَّاكِ، وَزَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ، وَمُقَاتِلِ بْنِ حيَّان، وَقَتَادَةَ.

وَقَالَ أَبُو إِسْحَاقَ عَنْ نَوْف البِكَالِي فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَالْجَارِ ذِي الْقُرْبَى﴾ يَعْنِي الْمُسْلِمَ ﴿وَالْجَارِ الْجُنُبِ﴾ يَعْنِي الْيَهُودِيَّ وَالنَّصْرَانِيَّ رَوَاهُ ابنُ جَريرٍ، وابنُ أَبِي حَاتم.

وَقَالَ جَابِرٌ الْجُعْفِيّ، عن الشعبي، عن علي وابنِ مَسْعُودٍ: ﴿وَالْجَارِ ذِي الْقُرْبَى﴾ يَعْنِي الْمَرْأَةَ. وَقَالَ مُجَاهِد أَيْضًا فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَالْجَارِ الْجُنُبِ﴾ يَعْنِي الرَّفِيقَ فِي السَّفَرِ.

وَقَدْ وَرَدَتِ الْأَحَادِيثُ بِالْوَصَايَا بِالْجَارِ، فَنَذْكُرُ مِنْهَا مَا تَيَسَّرَ، وَاللَّهُ الْمُسْتَعَانُ:

الْحَدِيثُ الْأَوَّلُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ: أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ أَبَاهُ مُحَمَّدًا يُحَدِّثُ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "مَا زَالَ جِبرِيل يُوصِينِي بالْجَارِ حَتِّى ظَنَنْتُ أَنَّهُ سَيُوَرِثُه".

أَخْرَجَاهُ فِي الصَّحِيحِ مِنْ حَدِيثِ عُمَرَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، بِهِ .

الْحَدِيثُ الثَّانِي: قَالَ الإمامُ أحمدُ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ داودَ بنِ شَابُورٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَا زالَ جِبْرِيلُ يُوصِينِي بالْجَارِ حتى ظننْتُ أنَّه سَيُوَرِّثُهُ" .

وَرَوَى أَبُو دَاوُدَ وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ نَحْوَهُ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ سُفْيَانَ بْنِ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ بَشِيرِ أَبِي إسْمَاعيلَ -زَادَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: وَدَاوُدَ بْنِ شَابُورٍ -كِلَاهُمَا عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، بِهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: حَسَنٌ غَرِيبٌ مِنْ هَذَا الْوَجْهِ وَقَدْ رُوي عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ عَنْ عائشةَ وَأَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ.

الْحَدِيثُ الثَّالِثُ عَنْهُ: قَالَ أَحْمَدُ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ يَزِيد، أَخْبَرَنَا حَيْوةُ، أَخْبَرَنَا شَرْحَبِيلُ بنُ شُرَيكٍ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ أَبَا عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الحُبُلي يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بنِ الْعَاصِ، عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ أنه قَالَ: "خَيْرُ الأصْحَابِ عِندَ اللهِ خَيْرُهُم لِصَاحِبِهِ، وخَيْرُ الجِيرانِ عِنْدَ اللهِ خَيْرُهُمْ لِجَارِهِ".

وَرَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْمُبَارَكِ، عَنْ حَيْوَةَ بْنِ شُرَيْحٍ -بِهِ، وَقَالَ: [حَدِيثٌ] حَسَنٌ غَرِيبٌ .

الْحَدِيثُ الرَّابِعُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَبَايَةَ بْنِ رِفَاعَةَ عَنْ عُمَر قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "لَا يَشْبَعُ الرَّجُلُ دُونَ جَارِهِ". تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ أَحْمَدُ .

الْحَدِيثُ الْخَامِسُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ فُضَيْلِ بْنِ غَزْوان، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ الْأَنْصَارِيُّ، سَمِعْتُ أَبَا ظَبْية الكَلاعِيّ، سَمِعْتُ المقدادَ بْنَ الْأَسْوَدِ يَقُولُ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ لِأَصْحَابِهِ: ["مَا تَقُولُونَ فِي الزِّنَا؟ " قَالُوا: حَرَامٌ حَرَّمَهُ اللهُ ورسُولُه، فَهُوَ حَرَامٌ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ. فَقَالَ: رسولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ] لأنْ يَزني الرَّجُلُ بِعَشْرِ نِسْوَة، أَيْسَرُ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَن يزنيَ بامرَأَةِ جَارِهِ". قَالَ: مَا تَقُولُونَ فِي السَّرِقَة؟ قَالُوا: حَرَّمَهَا اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ فَهِيَ حَرَامٌ. قَالَ "لَأَنْ يَسْرِقَ الرَّجُلُ مِن عَشْرَةِ أَبْيَاتٍ، أَيْسَرُ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَنْ يسرِقَ مِنْ جَارِهِ".

تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ أَحْمَدُ وَلَهُ شَاهِدٌ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ مِنْ حَدِيثِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ: قُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أيُّ الذَّنْب أَعْظَمُ؟ قَالَ: "أَنْ تَجْعَلَ لِلَّهِ نِدًّا وهُوَ خَلَقَكَ". قُلْتُ: ثُمَّ أَيُّ؟ قَالَ: "أَنْ تَقْتُلَ وَلَدَكَ خَشْيَةَ أَن يُطْعَم مَعَكَ". قُلتُ: ثُمَّ أيُّ؟ قَالَ: "أَنْ تُزَاني حَليلةَ جَارِكَ" .

الْحَدِيثُ السَّادِسُ: قَالَ الإمامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ، أَخْبَرَنَا هِشَامُ، عَنْ حَفْصَةَ، عَنْ أبِي الْعَالية، عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ قَالَ: خَرَجْتُ مِنْ أَهْلِي أريدُ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ، فإذَا بِهِ قَائِمٌ وَرَجُلٌ مَعَهُ مُقْبِل عَليه، فَظَنَنْتُ أَنَّ لَهُمَا حَاجة -قَالَ الأنْصَارِيُّ: لَقَدْ قَامَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ حتى جَعَلْتُ أَرْثِي لِرَسُولِ اللهِ ﷺ مِنْ طُولِ الْقِيَامِ، فَلمَّا انْصَرفَ قُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، لَقَدْ قَامَ بِكَ هَذَا الرَّجُلُ حَتَّى جَعَلْتُ أَرْثِي لَك مِنْ طُولِ الْقِيَامِ. قَالَ: "وَلَقَدْ رَأَيتَه؟ " قُلتُ: نَعَمْ. قَالَ: "أَتَدْرِي مَن هُوَ؟ " قُلْتُ: لَا. قَال: "ذَاكَ جِبْرِيِلُ، مَا زَالَ يُوصِينِي بِالجِارِ حَتَّى ظَنَنْتُ أَنَّه سَيُورثُه. ثُمَّ قَالَ: أَمَا إِنَّك لَو سَلَّمْتَ عَلَيْهِ، رَدَّ عَلَيْكَ السَّلَامَ" .

الْحَدِيثُ السَّابِعُ: قَالَ عَبْدُ بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ فِي مَسْنَدِهِ: حَدَّثَنَا يَعْلَى بْنُ عُبَيْد، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ -يَعْنِي الْمدَنيّ-عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: جَاءَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْعَوَالِي وَرَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ وجِبْرِيلُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ يُصَلِّيانِ حَيْثُ يُصَلَّى عَلَى الْجَنائِز، فَلَمَّا انْصَرَفَ قَالَ الرَّجُلُ: يَا رسولَ اللَّهِ، مَنْ هَذَا الرَّجُلُ الَّذِي رَأَيْتُ مَعَكَ؟ قَالَ: "وَقَدْ رأيْتَه؟ " قَالَ: نَعَمْ. قَالَ: "لَقَدْ رأَيْتَ خَيْرًا كَثِيرًا، هَذَا جِبْرِيلُ مَا زَالَ يُوصِينِي بِالْجَارِ حَتَّى رُئِيت أَنَّه سَيُورثُه".

تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ مِنْ هَذَا الْوَجْهِ وَهُوَ شَاهِدٌ لِلَّذِي قَبْلَهُ.

الْحَدِيثُ الثَّامِنُ: قَالَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ الْبَزَّارُ: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ أَبُو الرَّبِيعِ الْحَارِثِيّ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْن أَبِي فُدَيْك، أَخْبَرَنِي عَبْدُ الرَّحمن بنُ الْفَضل عَنْ عَطَاء الخَراساني، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، عَنْ جَابِرِ بنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: قَالَ رسولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "الجِيرانُ ثَلاثَةٌ: جَارٌ لهُ حَقٌ وَاحِدٌ، وَهُوَ أَدْنَى الجيرانِ حَقًّا، وَجَارٌ لَهُ حقَّان، وجَارٌ لَهُ ثلاثةُ حُقُوقٍ، وَهُوَ أفضلُ الجيرانِ حَقًّا، فَأَمَّا الَّذِي لَهُ حَقٌّ وَاحِدٌ فَجَارٌ مُشْرِكٌ لَا رَحمَ لَهُ، لَهُ حَقُّ الجَوار. وأمَّا الَّذِي لَهُ حقانِ فَجَارٌ مُسْلِمٌ، لَهُ حَقُّ الْإِسْلَامِ وَحَقُّ الْجِوارِ، وأَمَّا الَّذِي لَهُ ثَلاثةُ حُقُوقٍ، فَجَارٌ مُسْلِمٌ ذُو رَحِمٍ لَهُ حَقُّ الْجِوَارِ وَحَقُّ الْإِسْلَامِ وحَقُّ الرحِمِ".

قَالَ البَزَّارُ: لَا نَعْلَمُ أَحَدًا رَوَى عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْفُضَيْل إِلَّا ابْنُ أَبِي فُدَيْك .

الْحَدِيثُ التَّاسِعُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، حدثنا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ أَبِي عِمْرَانَ، عنْ طَلْحَةَ بنِ عَبْد اللهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ؛ أَنَّهَا سَأَلَتْ رسولَ اللهِ ﷺ فَقَالَتْ: "إنَّ لِي جَارَيْنِ، فَإِلَى أيِّهِمَا أُهْدِي؟ قَالَ: "إِلَى أقْرَبِهِمَا مِنْك بَابًا".

وَرَوَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ مِنْ حَدِيثِ شُعْبَةَ، بِهِ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَالصَّاحِبِ بِالْجَنْبِ﴾ قَالَ الثوريُّ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ الْجُعْفِي، عَنِ الشَّعبي، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ وابنِ مسعودٍ قَالَا هِيَ الْمَرْأَةُ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: ورُويَ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ النَّخَعِيّ، وَالْحَسَنِ، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَير -فِي إِحْدَى الرِّوَايَاتِ-نحوُ ذَلِكَ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ ومجاهدٌ، وعِكْرِمَةُ، وقَتَادةُ: هُوَ الرَّفِيقُ فِي السَّفَرِ. وَقَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ: هُوَ الرَّفِيقُ الصَّالِحُ. وَقَالَ زَيْدُ بنُ أَسْلَمَ: هُوَ جَلِيسُكَ فِي الْحَضَرِ، وَرَفِيقُكَ فِي السَّفَرِ.

وَأَمَّا ﴿ابْنِ السَّبِيلِ﴾ فَعَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ وَجَمَاعَةٍ هُوَ: الضيف.

وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ، وَأَبُو جَعْفَرٍ الباقرُ، والحسنُ، والضحاكُ، ومقاتلُ: هُوَ الَّذِي يَمُرُّ عَلَيْكَ مُجْتَازًا فِي السَّفَرِ.

وَهَذَا أَظْهَرُ، وَإِنْ كَانَ مُرَادُ الْقَائِلِ بِالضَّيْفِ: الْمَارَّ فِي الطَّرِيقِ، فَهُمَا سَوَاءٌ. وَسَيَأْتِي الْكَلَامُ عَلَى أَبْنَاءِ السَّبِيلِ فِي سُورَةِ بَرَاءَةَ، وَبِاللَّهِ الثِّقَةُ وَعَلَيْهِ التُّكْلَانُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَمَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ﴾ وَصِيَّةٌ بِالْأَرِقَّاءِ؛ لِأَنَّ الرَّقِيقَ ضَعِيفُ الْحِيلَةِ أَسِيرٌ فِي أَيْدِي النَّاسِ، وَلِهَذَا ثَبَتَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ جَعَلَ يُوصِي أُمَّتَه فِي مرضِ الْمَوْتِ يَقُولُ: "الصلاةَ الصلاةَ وَمَا ملكتْ أيمانُكُم". فَجَعَلَ يُرَدِّدُها حَتَّى مَا يَفِيضُ بِهَا لِسَانُهُ .

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ أَبِي الْعَبَّاسِ، حَدَّثَنَا بَقِيّة، حَدَّثَنَا بَحِيرُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ خَالِدِ بْنِ مَعْدَان، عَنِ الْمِقْدَامِ بْنِ مَعْدِ يكَرِب قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَا أَطْعَمْتَ نَفْسَك فَهُوَ لَكَ صدقةٌ، وَمَا أطعمتَ وَلَدَكَ فَهُوَ لَكَ صَدَقَةٌ، وَمَا أَطْعَمْتَ زَوْجَتَكَ فَهُوَ لَكَ صَدَقَةٌ، ومَا أطعَمْتَ خَادِمَكَ فَهُوَ لَك صَدَقَهٌ".

وَرَوَاهُ النَّسَائِيُّ مِنْ حَدِيثِ بَقِيَّة، وَإِسْنَادُهُ صَحِيحٌ وَلِلَّهِ الْحَمْدُ.

وَعَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو أَنَّهُ قَالَ لِقَهْرَمَانَ لَهُ: هَلْ أَعْطَيْتَ الرَّقِيقَ قُوتَهم؟ قَالَ: لَا. قَالَ: فَانْطَلِقْ فَأَعْطِهِمْ؛ فَإِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "كَفَى بِالْمَرْءِ إِثْمًا أَنْ يَحْبِسَ عَمَّنْ يَمْلِكُ قُوتَهُمْ" رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ .

وَعَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: "لِلْمَمْلُوكِ طَعَامُهُ وكِسْوتُه، وَلَا يكلَّف مِنَ الْعَمَلِ إِلَّا مَا يُطيق". رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ أَيْضًا .

وَعَنْهُ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: "إِذَا أَتَى أَحَدَكُمْ خَادِمُهُ بِطَعَامِهِ، فَإِنْ لَمْ يُجْلِسْهُ مَعَهُ، فَلْيُنَاوِلْهُ لُقْمَةً أَوْ لُقْمَتَيْنِ أَوْ أكْلَةً أَوْ أكْلَتين، فَإِنَّهُ وَليَ حَرّه وَعِلَاجَهُ".

أَخْرَجَاهُ وَلَفْظُهُ لِلْبُخَارِيِّ وَلِمُسْلِمٍ فَلْيُقْعِدْهُ مَعَهُ فَلْيَأْكُلْ، فَإِنْ كَانَ الطَّعَامُ مَشْفُوها قَلِيلًا فَلْيضع فِي يَدِهِ أَكْلَةً أَوْ أَكْلَتَيْنِ".

وَعَنْ أَبِي ذَرٍّ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قَالَ: "هُمْ إِخْوَانُكُمْ خَوَلكم، جَعَلَهُمُ اللَّهُ تَحْتَ أَيْدِيكُمْ، فَمَنْ كَانَ أَخُوهُ تَحْتَ يَدِهِ فَلْيُطْعِمْهُ مِمَّا يَأْكُلُ، وَلْيُلْبِسْهُ مِمَّا يَلْبَسُ، وَلَا تُكَلِّفُوهُمْ مَا يَغْلِبُهُمْ، فَإِنْ كَلَّفْتُمُوهُمْ، فَأَعِينُوهُمْ". أَخْرَجَاهُ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ مَن كَانَ مُخْتَالا فَخُورًا﴾ أَيْ: مُخْتَالًا فِي نَفْسِهِ، مُعْجَبًا مُتَكَبِّرًا، فَخُورًا عَلَى النَّاسِ، يَرَى أَنَّهُ خَيْرٌ مِنْهُمْ، فَهُوَ فِي نَفْسِهِ كَبِيرٌ، وَهُوَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ حَقِيرٌ، وَعِنْدَ النَّاسِ بغيض.

قَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ مَنْ كَانَ مُخْتَالا﴾ يَعْنِي: مُتَكَبِّرًا ﴿فَخُورًا﴾ يَعْنِي: يَعُد مَا أُعْطِيَ، وَهُوَ لَا يَشْكُرُ اللَّهَ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ. يَعْنِي: يَفْخَرُ عَلَى النَّاسِ بِمَا أَعْطَاهُ اللَّهُ مِنْ نِعَمِهِ، وَهُوَ قَلِيلُ الشُّكْرِ لِلَّهِ عَلَى ذَلِكَ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنِي الْقَاسِمُ، حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَيْنُ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ كَثيرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ وَاقِدٍ أَبِي رَجَاءٍ الْهَرَوِيِّ قَالَ: لَا تَجِدُ سَيِّئَ الْمَلَكَةِ إِلَّا وَجَدَّتَهُ مُخْتَالًا فَخُورًا -وَتَلَا ﴿وَمَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ [إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ مَنْ كَانَ مُخْتَالا فَخُورًا] ﴾ وَلَا عَاقًّا إِلَّا وَجَدَّتَهُ جَبَّارًا شَقِيًّا -وَتَلَا ﴿وَبَرًّا بِوَالِدَتِي وَلَمْ يَجْعَلْنِي جَبَّارًا شَقِيًّا﴾ [مَرْيَمَ: ٣٢] .

وَرَوَى ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ، عَنِ الْعَوَّامِ بْنِ حَوْشَبٍ، مِثْلَهُ فِي الْمُخْتَالِ الْفَخُورِ. وَقَالَ:

حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو نُعَيْمٍ، حَدَّثَنَا الْأُسُودُ بْنُ شَيْبَان، حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الشِّخِّير قَالَ: قَالَ مُطَرِّف: كَانَ يَبْلُغُنِي عَنْ أَبِي ذَرٍّ حَدِيثٌ كُنْتُ أَشْتَهِي لِقَاءَهُ، فَلَقِيتُهُ فَقُلْتُ: يَا أَبَا ذَرٍّ، بَلَغَنِي أَنَّكَ تَزْعُمُ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ حَدَّثَكُمْ: "إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ ثَلَاثَةً ويُبْغض ثَلَاثَةً"؟ قَالَ: أَجَلْ، فَلَا إِخَالُنِي أَكْذِبُ عَلَى خَلِيلِي، ثَلَاثًا. قُلْتُ: مَنِ الثَّلَاثَةُ الَّذِينَ يُبْغِضُ اللَّهُ؟ قَالَ: الْمُخْتَالُ الْفَخُورُ، أَوَلَيْسَ تَجِدُونَهُ عِنْدَكُمْ فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ الْمُنَزَّلِ؟ ثُمَّ قَرَأَ الْآيَةَ: ﴿إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ مَنْ كَانَ مُخْتَالا فَخُورًا﴾ [النِّسَاءِ: ٣٦] .

وَحَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا مُوسَى بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، حَدَّثَنَا وُهَيْبُ عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي تَمِيمَةَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ بَلْهُجَيم قَالَ: قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أَوْصِنِي. قَالَ: "إِيَّاكَ وإسبالَ الْإِزَارِ، فَإِنَّ إِسْبَالَ الْإِزَارِ مِنَ المَخِيلة، وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ المَخِيلة" .

---

## 4:37-39 — verses 37-39 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Exalted says, condemning those who are miserly with their wealth, refusing to spend it on what Allah has commanded them, namely: kindness to parents, and good treatment of relatives, orphans, the poor, the neighbor who is a relative, the neighbor who is a stranger, the companion at one's side, the traveler, and those whom your right hands possess among slaves, while also failing to give Allah's due right in it, and moreover commanding people to be miserly as well. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "What disease is more diseased than miserliness?" And he said: "Beware of greed, for it destroyed those who came before you. It commanded them to sever ties, so they severed them, and it commanded them to commit immorality, so they committed it."

Regarding His statement: {And they conceal what Allah has given them of His bounty}, the miser is one who denies Allah's favor upon him; it does not appear on him nor become manifest, neither in his food, nor in his clothing, nor in his giving and spending, as the Exalted said: {Indeed, man is ungrateful to his Lord, and indeed, he is a witness to that} [al-Adiyat: 6-7], meaning through his condition and manner, {and indeed, he is intense in love of wealth} [al-Adiyat: 8]. And here He says: {And they conceal what Allah has given them of His bounty}, and for this reason He threatened them by saying: {and We have prepared for the disbelievers a humiliating punishment}. Kufr (disbelief) means concealment and covering. So the miser conceals Allah's favor upon him, hides it, and denies it, thus he is one who disbelieves in (kafir li) Allah's favors upon him.

In the hadith: "Indeed, when Allah bestows a favor upon a servant, He loves for its trace to appear upon him." And in the prophetic supplication: "And make us grateful for Your favor, praising You for it, accepting it, (also reported as: proclaiming it), and complete it upon us."

Some of the salaf interpreted this verse as referring to the miserliness of the Jews in revealing the knowledge they possessed concerning the description of the Prophet ﷺ, and their concealment of it; and for this reason He said: {and We have prepared for the disbelievers a humiliating punishment}. This was narrated by Ibn Ishaq, from Muhammad ibn Abi Muhammad, from Ikrimah or Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas. Mujahid and others said the same.

There is no doubt that the verse can bear this meaning, but the apparent sense is that the context concerns miserliness with wealth, though miserliness with knowledge is included in it with even greater reason. For the context of the discourse concerns spending on relatives and the weak, and likewise the verse that follows it, which is His statement: {And those who spend their wealth to be seen by people}. So He mentioned the withholding ones who are blameworthy, namely the misers, then He mentioned the spenders who show off, those who intend by their giving to be heard of and praised for generosity, not seeking thereby the Face of Allah.

In the hadith concerning the three who will be the first for whom the Fire is kindled, namely: the scholar, the fighter, and the spender, all of whom did their deeds for show: the possessor of wealth says: "I did not leave anything you love to be spent on without spending it in Your way." Allah says: "You have lied. Rather you wanted it to be said that you are generous, and it has already been said." That is: you have already taken your reward in this world, which is what you sought by your deed.

In the hadith, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to Adi: "Indeed your father sought something and he attained it."

In another hadith: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was asked about Abdullah ibn Jud'an, whether his spending and freeing of slaves would benefit him. He said: "No, for he never once in his lifetime said: 'My Lord, forgive me my sin on the Day of Judgment.'"

For this reason He said: {And they do not believe in Allah nor in the Last Day, [and whoever has Satan as a companion, then what an evil companion he is]}, meaning: it is Satan who drove them to this ugly deed and their turning away from performing obedience as it should be. For he tempted them, gave them false hope, kept company with them, and made ugly deeds appear good to them. {And whoever has Satan as a companion, then what an evil companion he is}. For this reason the poet said: "Do not ask about a man, but ask about his companion, for every companion follows the example of the one he keeps company with."

Then the Exalted says: {And what harm would it have done them if they had believed in Allah and the Last Day and spent from what Allah has provided them, [and Allah is ever Knowing of them]}, meaning: what would it have cost them had they followed the praiseworthy path, and turned away from ostentation toward sincerity and faith in Allah, and hope in His promise in the Hereafter for whoever does good, and spent from what Allah provided them in the ways that Allah loves and is pleased with?

Regarding His statement: {and Allah is ever Knowing of them}, meaning: He is Knowing of their sound and their corrupt intentions, and Knowing of who among them deserves success, so He grants him success and inspires him with right guidance, and prepares him for righteous deeds with which He will be pleased with him; and Knowing of who deserves abandonment and expulsion from His mighty divine presence, one who, having been expelled from His door, has indeed failed and been ruined in this world and the next. We seek refuge in Allah from that, by His abundant grace.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى ذَامًّا الَّذِينَ يَبْخَلُونَ بِأَمْوَالِهِمْ أَنْ يُنْفِقُوهَا فِيمَا أَمَرَهُمُ اللَّهُ بِهِ -مِنْ بِرِّ الْوَالِدَيْنِ، وَالْإِحْسَانِ إِلَى الْأَقَارِبِ وَالْيَتَامَى وَالْمَسَاكِينِ، وَالْجَارِ ذِي الْقُرْبَى، وَالْجَارِ الجُنُب، وَالصَّاحِبِ بِالْجَنْبِ، وَابْنِ السَّبِيلِ، وَمَا مَلَكَتْ أَيْمَانُكُمْ مِنَ الْأَرِقَّاءِ -وَلَا يَدْفَعُونَ حَقَّ اللَّهِ فِيهَا، وَيَأْمُرُونَ النَّاسَ بِالْبُخْلِ أَيْضًا. وَقَدْ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "وَأَيُّ دَاءٍ أَدْوَأ مِنَ الْبُخْلِ؟ ". وَقَالَ: "إِيَّاكُمْ والشّحَ، فَإِنَّهُ أَهْلَكَ مَنْ كَانَ قَبْلَكُمْ، أَمَرَهُمْ بالقطيعةِ فقطعوا، وأمرهم بالفجور فَفَجَرُوا" .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَيَكْتُمُونَ مَا آتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ﴾ فَالْبَخِيلُ جَحُود لِنِعْمَةِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ لَا تَظْهَرُ عَلَيْهِ وَلَا تَبِينُ، لَا فِي أَكْلِهِ وَلَا فِي مَلْبَسِهِ، وَلَا فِي إِعْطَائِهِ وَبَذْلِهِ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِنَّ الإنْسَاَن لِرَبِّهِ لَكَنُودٌ. وَإِنَّهُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ لَشَهِيدٌ﴾ [الْعَادِيَاتِ: ٦، ٧] أَيْ: بِحَالِهِ وَشَمَائِلِهِ، ﴿وَإِنَّهُ لِحُبِّ الْخَيْرِ لَشَدِيدٌ﴾ [الْعَادِيَاتِ: ٨] وَقَالَ هَاهُنَا: ﴿وَيَكْتُمُونَ مَا آتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ﴾ وَلِهَذَا توعَّدهم بِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَأَعْتَدْنَا لِلْكَافِرِينَ عَذَابًا مُهِينًا﴾ وَالْكَفْرُ هُوَ السَّتْرُ وَالتَّغْطِيَةُ، فَالْبَخِيلُ يَسْتُرُ نِعْمَةَ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ وَيَكْتُمُهَا وَيَجْحَدُهَا، فَهُوَ كَافِرٌ لِنِعَمِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ.

وَفِي الْحَدِيثِ: "إِنَّ اللَّهَ إِذَا أَنْعَمَ نِعْمَةً عَلَى عبدٍ أحبَّ أَنْ يَظْهَرَ أثرُها عَلَيْهِ" وَفِي الدُّعَاءِ النَّبَوِيِّ: "وَاجْعَلْنَا شَاكِرِينَ لِنِعْمَتِكَ، مُثِنِينَ بِهَا عَلَيْكَ قَابِلِيهَا -وَيُرْوَى: قَائِلِيهَا-وَأَتْمِمْهَا عَلَيْنَا" .

وَقَدْ حَمَلَ بعضُ السَّلَفِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ عَلَى بُخْلِ الْيَهُودِ بِإِظْهَارِ الْعِلْمِ الَّذِي عِنْدَهُمْ، مِنْ صِفَةِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ وَكِتْمَانِهِمْ ذَلِكَ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿وَأَعْتَدْنَا لِلْكَافِرِينَ عَذَابًا مُهِينًا﴾ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ أَوْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْر، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ. وَقَالَهُ مُجَاهِدٌ وَغَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ.

وَلَا شَكَّ أَنَّ الْآيَةَ مُحْتَمِلَةٌ لِذَلِكَ، وَالظَّاهِرُ أَنَّ السِّيَاقَ فِي الْبُخْلِ بِالْمَالِ، وَإِنْ كَانَ الْبُخْلُ بِالْعِلْمِ دَاخِلًا فِي ذَلِكَ بِطَرِيقِ الْأَوْلَى؛ فَإِنَّ سِيَاقَ الْكَلَامِ فِي الْإِنْفَاقِ عَلَى الْأَقَارِبِ وَالضُّعَفَاءِ، وَكَذَا الْآيَةُ الَّتِي بَعْدَهَا، وَهِيَ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿والَّذِينَ يُنْفِقُونَ أَمْوَالَهُمْ رِئَاءَ النَّاسِ﴾ فَذَكر الْمُمْسِكِينَ الْمَذْمُومِينَ وَهُمُ الْبُخَلَاءُ، ثُمَّ ذَكَرَ الْبَاذِلِينَ الْمُرَائِينَ الَّذِي يَقْصِدُونَ بِإِعْطَائِهِمُ السُّمْعَةَ وَأَنْ يُمدَحوا بِالْكَرَمِ، وَلَا يُرِيدُونَ بِذَلِكَ وَجْهَ اللَّهِ، وَفِي حديث الَّذِي فِيهِ الثَّلَاثَةُ الَّذِينَ هُمْ أَوَّلُ مَنْ تُسَجَّرُ بِهِمُ النَّارُ، وَهُمْ: الْعَالِمُ وَالْغَازِي وَالْمُنْفِقُ، وَالْمُرَاءُونَ بِأَعْمَالِهِمْ، يَقُولُ صَاحِبُ الْمَالِ: مَا تَرَكْتُ مِنْ شَيْءٍ تُحِبُّ أَنْ يُنْفَقَ فِيهِ إِلَّا أَنْفَقْتُ فِي سَبِيلِكَ. فَيَقُولُ اللَّهُ: كَذَبْتَ؛ إِنَّمَا أَرَدْتَ أَنْ يُقَالَ: جَوَادٌ فَقَدْ قِيلَ. أَيْ: فَقَدْ أَخَذْتَ جَزَاءَكَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَهُوَ الَّذِي أَرَدْتَ بِفِعْلِكَ.

وَفِي الْحَدِيثِ: أَنَّ رسولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ لِعَدِيّ: "إِنَّ أَبَاكَ رامَ أَمْرًا فَبَلَغَهُ".

وَفِي حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ: أن رسول الله صلى الله عليه وسلم سُئِلَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ جُدعان: هَلْ يَنْفَعُهُ إنفاقُه، وإعتاقُه؟ فَقَالَ: "لَا إِنَّهُ لَمْ يَقُلْ يَوْمًا مِنَ الدَّهْرِ: رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي خَطِيئَتِي يَوْمَ الدِّينِ".

وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿وَلا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَلا بِالْيَوْمِ الآخِرِ [وَمَنْ يَكُنِ الشَّيْطَانُ لَهُ قَرِينًا فَسَاءَ قَرِينًا] ﴾ أَيْ: إِنَّمَا حَمَلَهُمْ عَلَى صَنِيعِهِمْ هَذَا القبيحِ وَعُدُولِهِمْ عَنْ فِعْلِ الطَّاعَةِ عَلَى وَجْهِهَا الشيطانُ؛ فَإِنَّهُ سَوَّلَ لَهُمْ وَأَمْلَى لَهُمْ، وَقَارَنَهُمْ فَحَسَّنَ لَهُمُ الْقَبَائِحَ ﴿وَمَنْ يَكُنِ الشَّيْطَانُ لَهُ قَرِينًا فَسَاءَ قَرِينًا﴾ وَلِهَذَا قال الشاعر عَن المَرْء لَا تَسْأل وسَلْ عَنْ قَرينه ... فكلُّ قَرِينٍ بِالْمَقَارَنِ يَقْتَدي 

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَاذَا عَلَيْهِمْ لَوْ آمَنُوا بِاللهِ وَالْيَومِ الآخِرِ وَأَنْفَقُوا مِمَّا رَزَقَهُمُ اللهُ [وَكَانَ اللَّهُ بِهِمْ عَلِيمًا] ﴾ أَيْ: وَأَيُّ شَيْءٍ يَكرثُهم لَوْ سَلَكُوا الطَّرِيقَ الْحَمِيدَةَ، وعَدَلُوا عَنِ الرِّيَاءِ إِلَى الْإِخْلَاصِ وَالْإِيمَانِ بِاللَّهِ، وَرَجَاءِ مَوْعُودِهِ فِي الدَّارِ الْآخِرَةِ لِمَنْ أَحْسَنَ عَمَلًا وَأَنْفَقُوا مِمَّا رَزَقَهُمُ اللَّهُ فِي الْوُجُوهِ الَّتِي يُحِبُّهَا اللَّهُ وَيَرْضَاهَا.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَكَانَ اللَّهُ بِهِمْ عَلِيمًا﴾ أَيْ: وَهُوَ عَلِيمٌ بِنِيَّاتِهِمُ الصَّالِحَةِ وَالْفَاسِدَةِ، وَعَلِيمٌ بِمَنْ يَسْتَحِقُّ التَّوْفِيقَ مِنْهُمْ فَيُوَفِّقُهُ وَيُلْهِمُهُ رُشْدَهُ وَيُقَيِّضُهُ لِعَمَلٍ صَالِحٍ يَرْضَى بِهِ عَنْهُ، وَبِمَنْ يَسْتَحِقُّ الْخِذْلَانَ وَالطَّرْدَ عَنْ جَنَابِهِ الْأَعْظَمِ الْإِلَهِيِّ، الَّذِي مَنْ طُرِدَ عَنْ بَابِهِ فَقَدْ خَابَ وخَسِرَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ، عِيَاذًا بِاللَّهِ مِنْ ذَلِكَ [بِلُطْفِهِ الْجَزِيلِ] .

---

## 4:40-42 — verses 40-42 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Almighty informs that He does not wrong any servant of His on the Day of Resurrection by so much as the weight of a mustard seed or the weight of an atom. Rather, He gives it to him in full and multiplies it for him if it was a good deed, as the Almighty said: {And We place the scales of justice for the Day of Resurrection, so no soul will be wronged at all. Even if it be the weight of a mustard seed, We will bring it forth. And sufficient are We as reckoners} [al-Anbiya: 47]. And the Almighty said, informing about Luqman, that he said: {O my son, indeed if it should be the weight of a mustard seed and should be within a rock or in the heavens or in the earth, Allah will bring it forth. Indeed, Allah is subtle and aware} [Luqman: 16]. And the Almighty said: {That Day, the people will depart separated to be shown their deeds. So whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it, and whoever does an atom's weight of evil will see it}.

In the two Sahihs, from the hadith of Zayd ibn Aslam, from Ata' ibn Yasar, from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, in the long hadith of intercession, it contains: Allah, Mighty and Majestic, will say, "Go back, and whoever you find in whose heart there is the weight of a mustard seed of faith, bring him out of the Fire." And in another wording: "The very least, the very least, the very least, the weight of an atom of faith, bring him out of the Fire, and they will bring out a great multitude." Then Abu Sa'id said: "Recite, if you wish: {Indeed, Allah does not wrong anyone the weight of an atom. And if there is a good deed, He multiplies it and gives from Himself a great reward}."

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Abu Sa'id al-Ashajj, [chain] Isa ibn Yunus, from Harun ibn Antara, from Abdullah ibn al-Sa'ib, from Zadhan, who said: Abdullah ibn Mas'ud said: The male and female servant will be brought on the Day of Resurrection, and a caller will call out over the heads of the first and the last: "This is so-and-so, son of so-and-so. Whoever has a claim against him, let him come forward for his claim."

The woman will rejoice that she has a claim against her father, or her brother, or her husband. Then he recited: {So there will be no relationships among them that Day, nor will they ask about one another} [al-Mu'minun: 101]. Allah forgives what He wills of what is owed to Him, but He does not forgive anything of the rights owed to people. So the man is set up before the people and it is called out: "This is so-and-so, son of so-and-so. Whoever has a claim against him, let him come forward for his claim." He (the angel) will say: "Lord, the worldly goods have run out. From where shall I give them their rights?" He will say: "Take from his good deeds and give each claimant his due according to what he claims. And if he was a friend (wali) of Allah and there remains for him the weight of an atom of good deeds, Allah multiplies it for him until He admits him into Paradise by means of it." Then he recited to us: {Indeed, Allah does not wrong anyone the weight of an atom. And if there is a good deed, He multiplies it}. He said: "Enter Paradise." But if he was a wretched servant, the angel will say: "Lord, his good deeds have run out, and there remain many claimants." He will say: "Take from their bad deeds and add them to his bad deeds." Then a writ will be drawn up for him consigning him to the Fire.

Ibn Jarir narrated it by another route, from Zadhan, with something similar. Part of this report has a corroborating witness in the authentic hadith.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] my father, [chain] Abu Nu'aym, [chain] Fudayl, meaning Ibn Marzuq, from Atiyya al-Awfi: Abdullah ibn Umar told me, saying: This verse was revealed concerning the Bedouins: {Whoever comes with a good deed will have ten times its like} [al-An'am: 160]. A man said: "So what about the emigrants (muhajirin), O Abu Abd al-Rahman?" He said: "There is something better than that: {Indeed, Allah does not wrong anyone the weight of an atom. And if there is a good deed, He multiplies it and gives from Himself a great reward}."

And [chain] Abu Zur'a, [chain] Yahya ibn Abdullah ibn Bukayr, [chain] Abdullah ibn Lahi'a, [chain] Ata' ibn Dinar, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, concerning His saying {And if there is a good deed, He multiplies it}: as for the polytheist, the punishment is lightened for him on the Day of Resurrection, but he is never brought out of the Fire. Evidence for this has been drawn from the authentic hadith that al-Abbas said: "O Messenger of Allah, Abu Talib used to protect you and support you. Did you benefit him in any way?" He said: "Yes, he is in a shallow part of the Fire, and were it not for me he would be in the lowest depth of the Fire."

This may be specific to Abu Talib, apart from other disbelievers, as indicated by what Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi narrated in his Sunan: [chain] Imran, [chain] Qatada, from Anas, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Indeed, Allah does not wrong the believer of a good deed; he is rewarded for it with provision in this world and recompensed for it in the Hereafter. As for the disbeliever, he is fed for it in this world, and when the Day of Resurrection comes, he has no good deed left."

Abu Hurayra, Ikrima, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, al-Hasan, Qatada, and al-Dahhak said, concerning His saying {and gives from Himself a great reward}: meaning, Paradise.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Abd al-Samad, [chain] Sulayman, meaning Ibn al-Mughira, from Ali ibn Zayd, from Abu Uthman, who said: It reached me from Abu Hurayra that he said: "It has reached me that Allah the Almighty gives His believing servant, for a single good deed, a thousand thousand good deeds." He said: So it happened that I set out for Hajj or Umra, and I met him and said: "A report has reached me from you that you say: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'Indeed Allah gives His believing servant, for a good deed, a thousand thousand good deeds.'" Abu Hurayra said: "No, rather I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'Indeed Allah, Mighty and Majestic, gives him two thousand thousand good deeds.'" Then he recited {He multiplies it and gives from Himself a great reward}: so who can estimate its measure?

Imam Ahmad narrated it, saying: [chain] Yazid, [chain] Mubarak ibn Fadala, from Ali ibn Zayd, from Abu Uthman, who said: I came to Abu Hurayra and said to him: "It has reached me that you say a good deed is multiplied a thousand thousand times." He said: "What is so strange to you about that? By Allah, I heard," meaning the Prophet ﷺ, as my father said, "say: 'Indeed Allah multiplies a good deed two thousand thousand times.'"

Ali ibn Zayd's hadiths contain objectionable material (nakara), so Allah knows best.

As for His saying: {So how will it be when We bring a witness from every nation and We bring you as a witness against these people?} The Almighty is informing about the terror of the Day of Resurrection and the severity of its matter and situation: how will the state of affairs be on the Day of Resurrection, and when a witness is brought from every nation, meaning the prophets (peace be upon them)? As the Almighty said: {And the earth will shine with the light of its Lord, and the record will be placed, and the prophets and the witnesses will be brought, and it will be judged between them in truth, and they will not be wronged} [al-Zumar: 69]. And the Almighty said: {And the Day We will resurrect among every nation a witness against them from themselves, and We bring you as a witness against these people. And We have sent down to you the Book as clarification for all things, and as guidance and mercy and glad tidings for the Muslims} [al-Nahl: 89].

Al-Bukhari said: [chain] Muhammad ibn Yusuf, [chain] Sufyan, from al-A'mash, from Ibrahim, from Ubayda, from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, who said: The Prophet ﷺ said to me, "Recite to me." I said, "O Messenger of Allah, shall I recite to you when it was sent down upon you?" He said, "Yes, I love to hear it from someone other than myself." So I recited Surat al-Nisa until I came to this verse: {So how will it be when We bring a witness from every nation and We bring you as a witness against these people?} He said, "That is enough for you now." And then his eyes were flowing with tears.

Al-Bukhari and Muslim also narrated it from the hadith of al-A'mash, with the same content. It has been narrated through numerous chains from Ibn Mas'ud, so it is firmly established from him. Ahmad narrated it by the route of Abu Hayyan and Abu Razin, from him.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Abu Bakr ibn Abi al-Dunya, [chain] al-Salt ibn Mas'ud al-Jahdari, [chain] Fudayl ibn Sulayman, [chain] Yunus ibn Muhammad ibn Fadala al-Ansari, from his father, who said, and his father was among those who accompanied the Prophet ﷺ: that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to them among Banu Zafar, and he sat on the rock that is in Banu Zafar to this day, and with him were Ibn Mas'ud and Mu'adh ibn Jabal and some of his companions. The Prophet ﷺ ordered a reciter, and he recited, and he came to this verse: {So how will it be when We bring a witness from every nation and We bring you as a witness against these people?} The Messenger of Allah ﷺ wept until his jaws and sides trembled, and he said: "O Lord, this is that I have testified against those among whom I dwell, so how will it be for those whom I have not seen?"

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Abdullah ibn Muhammad al-Zuhri, [chain] Sufyan, from al-Mas'udi, from Ja'far ibn Amr ibn Hurayth, from his father, from Abdullah, that is, Ibn Mas'ud: {So how will it be when We bring a witness from every nation}. He said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "A witness against them for as long as I remain among them, and when You take me, You will be the watcher over them."

As for what Abu Abdullah al-Qurtubi mentioned in "al-Tadhkirah" where he said: "Chapter on what has come regarding the testimony of the Prophet ﷺ against his nation," he said: Ibn al-Mubarak told us, a man from the Ansar told us, from al-Minhal ibn Amr, who narrated that he heard Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab say: "There is no day but that the Prophet's ﷺ nation is presented to him morning and evening, so he recognizes them by their names and their deeds, and for that reason he bears witness against them. Allah, Exalted is He, says: {So how will it be when We bring from every nation a witness and bring you as a witness against these?}"

This is an athar (report), and it contains a break in the chain, for it includes an unnamed man who was not identified, and it is from the speech of Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, not raised to the Prophet ﷺ. Al-Qurtubi accepted it, and after citing it he said: "It has already been mentioned that deeds are presented to Allah every Monday and Thursday, and to the prophets and to parents on Friday." He said: "There is no contradiction, for it is possible that our Prophet is singled out for having deeds presented to him every day, and on Friday along with the prophets, peace be upon them."

Regarding His saying, {That Day those who disbelieved and disobeyed the Messenger will wish that the earth were leveled with them}, that is, that it would split open and swallow them, on account of what they will see of the horrors of the standing place, and the disgrace, exposure, and rebuke that will befall them. This is like His saying: {The Day when a man will see what his hands have sent forth, [and the disbeliever will say, "Oh, I wish I were dust!"]}. And His saying, {and they will not conceal from Allah any statement}, informs us about them that they will confess to everything they did, concealing nothing of it.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Sa'id ibn Jubayr said: A man came to Ibn Abbas and said: "I heard Allah, Mighty and Sublime, say," meaning informing about the polytheists on the Day of Resurrection, that they said: {By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists} [al-An'am: 23], and He said in another verse: {and they will not conceal from Allah any statement}. Ibn Abbas said: "As for His saying, {By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists}, when they saw that none would enter Paradise except the people of Islam, they said, 'Come, let us deny it,' so they said, {By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists}. So Allah sealed their mouths, and their hands and feet spoke, {and they will not conceal from Allah any statement}."

Abd al-Razzaq said: Ma'mar told us, from a man, from al-Minhal ibn Amr, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, who said: A man came to Ibn Abbas and said: "There are things in the Qur'an that seem inconsistent to me." He said: "What is it? Do you doubt the Qur'an?" He said: "It is not doubt, but rather an apparent inconsistency." He said: "Then bring forth what has seemed inconsistent to you of that." He said: "I hear Allah say, {Then their only argument will be that they say, 'By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists'} [al-An'am: 23], and He said, {and they will not conceal from Allah any statement}, yet they did conceal!"

Ibn Abbas said: "As for His saying, {Then their only argument will be that they say, 'By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists'}, when they saw on the Day of Resurrection that Allah forgives none but the people of Islam, and that He forgives sins but does not forgive associating partners with Him, and that no sin is too great for Him to forgive, the polytheists denied, and said: {By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists}, hoping to be forgiven. So Allah sealed their mouths, and their hands and their feet spoke of what they used to do. At that point: {those who disbelieved and disobeyed the Messenger will wish that the earth were leveled with them, and they will not conceal from Allah any statement}."

Juwaybir said, from al-Dahhak: Nafi' ibn al-Azraq came to Ibn Abbas and said: "O Ibn Abbas, what of the word of Allah: {That Day those who disbelieved and disobeyed the Messenger will wish that the earth were leveled with them, and they will not conceal from Allah any statement}, and His saying, {By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists}?"

Ibn Abbas said to him: "I think you got up from among your companions and said, 'Let me present to Ibn Abbas an ambiguous matter from the Qur'an.' So when you return to them, tell them that Allah will gather people on the Day of Resurrection in one plain. The polytheists will say, 'Allah accepts nothing from anyone except from one who affirmed His oneness,' so they will say, 'Let us go and lie.' Then He will question them, and they will say, {By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists}."

He said: "So their mouths will be sealed, and their limbs will be made to speak, and their limbs will bear witness against them that they were polytheists. At that point they will wish that the earth were leveled with them, {and they will not conceal from Allah any statement}." Narrated by Ibn Jarir.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يُخْبِرُ تَعَالَى أَنَّهُ لَا يَظْلِمُ عَبْدًا مِنْ عِبَادِهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ مِثْقَالَ حَبَّةِ خَرْدَلٍ وَلَا مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ، بَلْ يُوَفِّيهَا بِهِ وَيُضَاعِفُهَا لَهُ إِنْ كَانَتْ حَسَنَةً، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى ﴿وَنَضَعُ الْمَوَازِينَ الْقِسْطَ [لِيَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ فَلا تُظْلَمُ نَفْسٌ شَيْئًا وَإِنْ كَانَ مِثْقَالَ حَبَّةٍ مِنْ خَرْدَلٍ أَتَيْنَا بِهَا وَكَفَى بِنَا حَاسِبِينَ] ﴾ [الْأَنْبِيَاءِ: ٤٧] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى مُخْبِرًا عَنْ لُقْمَانَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: ﴿يَا بُنَيَّ إِنَّهَا إِنْ تَكُ مِثْقَالَ حَبَّةٍ مِنْ خَرْدَلٍ فَتَكُنْ فِي صَخْرَةٍ أَوْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ أَوْ فِي الأرْضِ يَأْتِ بِهَا اللَّهُ [إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَطِيفٌ خَبِيرٌ] ﴾ [لُقْمَانَ: ١٦] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿يَوْمَئِذٍ يَصْدُرُ النَّاسُ أَشْتَاتًا لِيُرَوْا أَعْمَالَهُمْ. فَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيْرًا يَرَهُ. وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ شَرًّا يَرَهُ﴾

وَفِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ، عَنْ عَطَاءِ بْنِ يَسَار، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الخُدْري، عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ فِي حَدِيثِ الشَّفَاعَةِ الطويلِ، وَفِيهِ: فَيَقُولُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: "ارْجِعُوا، فَمَن وَجَدْتُمْ فِي قَلْبِهِ مثقالَ حَبَّةِ خَرْدَلٍ مِنْ إِيمَانٍ، فَأَخْرِجُوهُ مِنَ النَّارِ". وَفِي لَفْظٍ: "أَدْنَى أَدْنَى أَدْنَى مِثْقَالِ ذَرَّةٍ مِنْ إِيمَانٍ فَأَخْرِجُوهُ مِنَ النَّارِ، فَيُخْرِجُونَ خَلْقًا كَثِيرًا" ثُمَّ يَقُولُ أَبُو سَعِيدٍ: اقْرَؤُوا إِنْ شِئْتُمْ: ﴿إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَظْلِمُ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ [وَإِنْ تَكُ حَسَنَةً يُضَاعِفْهَا وَيُؤْتِ مِنْ لَدُنْهُ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا] ﴾ .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَعِيدٍ الأشَجّ، حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُس، عَنْ هارونَ بْنِ عَنْتَرَةَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ، عَنْ زَاذَانَ قَالَ: قَالَ عبدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مَسْعُود: يُؤْتَى بِالْعَبْدِ والأمَة يومَ القيامةِ، فَيُنَادِي منادٍ على رءوس الْأَوَّلِينَ والآخِرين: هَذَا فلانُ بنُ فلانٍ، مَنْ كان له حق فليأت إلى حقه.

فتفرحُ المرأةُ أَنْ يَكُونَ لَهَا الْحَقُّ عَلَى أَبِيهَا أَوْ أَخِيهَا أَوْ زَوْجِهَا. ثُمَّ قَرَأَ: ﴿فَلا أَنْسَابَ بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ وَلا يَتَسَاءَلُونَ﴾ [الْمُؤْمِنُونَ:١٠١] فَيَغْفِرُ اللَّهُ مِنْ حَقِّهِ مَا يَشَاءُ، وَلَا يَغْفِرُ مِنْ حُقُوقِ النَّاسِ شَيْئًا، فينصَب لِلنَّاسِ فينادَي: هَذَا فلانُ بْنُ فلانٍ، مَنْ كَانَ لَهُ حَقٌّ فليأتِ إِلَى حَقِّهِ. فَيَقُولُ: رَبّ، فَنِيَت الدُّنْيَا، مِنْ أَيْنَ أُوتِيِهْم حقوقَهم؟ قَالَ: خُذُوا مِنْ أَعْمَالِهِ الصَّالِحَةِ، فَأَعْطُوا كلَ ذِي حَقِّ حَقَّهُ بِقَدْرِ طَلَبَتِهِ فَإِنْ كَانَ وَلِيًّا لِلَّهِ ففَضَلَ لَهُ مثقالُ ذَرَّةٍ، ضَاعَفَهَا اللَّهُ لَهُ حَتَّى يدخلَه بِهَا الْجَنَّةَ، ثُمَّ قَرَأَ عَلَيْنَا: ﴿إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَظْلِمُ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ وَإِنْ تَكُ حَسَنَةً يُضَاعِفْهَا﴾ قَالَ: ادْخُلِ الْجَنَّةَ؛ وَإِنْ كَانَ عَبْدًا شَقِيًّا قَالَ الْمَلَكُ: ربِّ فَنِيَتْ حَسَنَاتُهُ، وَبَقِيَ طَالِبُونَ كَثِيرٌ؟ فَيَقُولُ: خُذُوا مِنْ سَيِّئَاتِهِمْ فَأَضِيفُوهَا إِلَى سَيِّئَاتِهِ، ثُمَّ صُكُّوا لَهُ صَكًّا إِلَى النَّارِ.

وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ مِنْ وَجْهٍ آخَرَ، عَنْ زَاذَانَ -بِهِ نَحْوَهُ. وَلِبَعْضِ هَذَا الْأَثَرِ شَاهَدٌ فِي الْحَدِيثِ الصَّحِيحِ.

وقال ابن أبي حاتم: حدثنا أبي، حدثتا أَبُو نُعَيْمٍ، حَدَّثَنَا فُضَيلٌ -يَعْنِي ابْنَ مَرْزُوقٍ-عَنْ عطيَّة العَوْفي، حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُمَرَ قَالَ: نَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ فِي الْأَعْرَابِ: ﴿مَنْ جَاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ عَشْرُ أَمْثَالِهَا﴾ [الْأَنْعَامِ: ١٦٠] قَالَ رَجُلٌ: فَمَا لِلْمُهَاجِرِينَ يَا أَبَا عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ؟ قَالَ: مَا هُوَ أفضلُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ: ﴿إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَظْلِمُ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ وَإِنْ تَكُ حَسَنَةً يُضَاعِفْهَا وَيُؤْتِ مِنْ لَدُنْهُ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا﴾ .

وَحَدَّثَنَا أَبُو زُرْعَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ بُكَيْرٍ، حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ لَهِيعَةَ، حَدَّثَنِي عَطَاءُ بْنُ دِينَارٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ ﴿وَإِنْ تَكُ حَسَنَةً يُضَاعِفْهَا﴾ فَأَمَّا الْمُشْرِكُ فَيُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُ الْعَذَابُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ، وَلَا يُخْرَجُ مِنَ النَّارِ أَبَدًا. وَقَدِ اسْتُدِلَّ لَهُ بِالْحَدِيثِ الصَّحِيحِ أَنَّ الْعَبَّاسَ قَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنْ أَبَا طَالِبٍ كَانَ يَحُوطُكَ وَيَنْصُرُكَ فَهَلْ نَفَعْتَهُ بِشَيْءٍ؟ قَالَ: "نَعَمْ هُوَ فِي ضَحْضَاح مِنْ نَارٍ، وَلَوْلَا أنا لكان في الدَّرْك الأسفل مِنَ النَّارِ" .

وَقَدْ يَكُونُ هَذَا خَاصًّا بِأَبِي طَالِبٍ مِنْ دُونِ الْكَفَّارِ، بِدَلِيلِ مَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيالسِي فِي سُنَنِهِ حَدَّثَنَا عِمْرَانُ، حَدَّثَنَا قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَظْلِمُ الْمُؤْمِنَ حَسَنَةً، يُثَابُ عَلَيْهَا الرِّزْقَ فِي الدُّنْيَا ويُجْزَى بِهَا فِي الْآخِرَةِ، وَأَمَّا الْكَافِرُ فَيُطْعَمُ بِهَا فِي الدُّنْيَا، فَإِذَا كَانَ يَوْمُ الْقِيَامَةِ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ حَسَنَةٌ" .

وَقَالَ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ، وعِكْرِمَةُ، وسعيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ، والحسنُ وقتادةُ والضحاكُ، فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَيُؤْتِ مِنْ لَدُنْهُ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا﴾ يَعْنِي: الْجَنَّةَ.

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الصَّمَدِ، حَدَّثَنَا سُلَيْمانُ -يَعْنِي ابْنَ الْمُغَيْرَةِ-عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ قَالَ: بَلَغَنِي عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: بَلَغَنِي أَنَّ اللَّهَ تَعَالَى يُعْطِي عَبْدَهُ الْمُؤْمِنَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ الْوَاحِدَةِ أَلْفَ أَلْفِ حَسَنَةٍ. قَالَ: فقُضي أَنِّي انْطَلَقْتُ حَاجًّا أَوْ مُعْتَمِرًا، فَلَقِيتُهُ فقلت: بلغني عنك حَدِيثٌ أَنَّكَ تَقُولُ: سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَقُولُ: "إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُعْطَى عَبْدَهُ الْمُؤْمِنَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ أَلْفَ أَلْفَ حَسَنَةٍ" قَالَ أَبُو هُرَيْرَةَ: لَا بَلْ سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَقُولُ: "إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ يُعْطِيهِ أَلْفَيْ أَلْفِ حَسَنَةٍ" ثُمَّ تَلَا ﴿يُضَاعِفْهَا وَيُؤْتِ مِنْ لَدُنْهُ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا﴾ فَمِنْ يُقَدِّرُهُ قَدْرَهُ .

رَوَاهُ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ فَقَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ، حَدَّثَنَا مباركُ بْنُ فَضَالَة، عَنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ قَالَ: أَتَيْتُ أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ: بَلَغَنِي أَنَّكَ تَقُولُ: إِنَّ الْحَسَنَةَ تُضَاعَفُ أَلْفَ أَلْفِ حَسَنَةٍ؟ قَالَ: وَمَا أَعْجَبَكَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ؟ فَوَاللَّهِ لَقَدْ سَمِعْتُ -يَعْنِي النَّبِيَّ ﷺ-كَذَا قَالَ أَبِي -يَقُولُ: "إِنَّ اللَّهَ لِيُضَاعِفُ الْحَسَنَةَ أَلْفَيْ أَلْفِ حَسَنَةٍ" .

عَلِيُّ بْنُ زَيْدٍ فِي أَحَادِيثِهِ نَكَارَةٌ، فَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَكَيْفَ إِذَا جِئْنَا مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ بِشَهِيدٍ وَجِئْنَا بِكَ عَلَى هَؤُلاءِ شَهِيدًا﴾ يَقُولُ تَعَالَى -مُخْبِرًا عَنْ هَوْلِ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ وَشِدَّةِ أَمْرِهِ وَشَأْنِهِ: فَكَيْفَ يَكُونُ الْأَمْرُ وَالْحَالُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَحِينَ يَجِيءُ مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ بِشَهِيدٍ -يَعْنِي الْأَنْبِيَاءَ عَلَيْهِمُ السَّلَامُ؟ كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَأَشْرَقَتِ الأرْضُ بِنُورِ رَبِّهَا وَوُضِعَ الْكِتَابُ وَجِيءَ بِالنَّبِيِّينَ وَالشُّهَدَاءِ [وَقُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ بِالْحَقِّ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ] ﴾ [الزُّمَرِ: ٦٩] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَيَوْمَ نَبْعَثُ فِي كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ شَهِيدًا عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ أَنْفُسِهِمْ [وَجِئْنَا بِكَ شَهِيدًا عَلَى هَؤُلاءِ وَنزلْنَا عَلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ تِبْيَانًا لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةً وَبُشْرَى لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ] ﴾ [النَّحْلِ: ٨٩] .

قَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يُوسُفَ، حَدَّثَنَا سفيانُ، عَنِ الأعْمَشِ، عَنْ إبراهيمَ، عَنْ عُبَيْدَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ قَالَ: قَالَ لِي النَّبِيُّ ﷺ "اقْرَأْ عَلَيَّ" قُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، آقْرَأُ عَلَيْكَ وَعَلَيْكَ أُنزلَ؟ قَالَ: "نَعَمْ، إِنِّي أُحِبُّ أَنَّ أَسْمَعَهُ مِنْ غَيْرِي" فَقَرَأْتُ سُورَةَ النِّسَاءِ، حَتَّى أَتَيْتُ إِلَى هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: ﴿فَكَيْفَ إِذَا جِئْنَا مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ بِشَهِيدٍ وَجِئْنَا بِكَ عَلَى هَؤُلاءِ شَهِيدًا﴾ قَالَ: "حَسْبُكَ الْآنَ" فَإِذَا عَيْنَاهُ تَذْرِفَان.

وَرَوَاهُ هُوَ وَمُسْلِمٌ أَيْضًا مِنْ حَدِيثِ الْأَعْمَشِ، بِهِ وَقَدْ رُوي مِنْ طُرُقٍ مُتَعَدِّدَةٍ عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، فَهُوَ مَقْطُوعٌ بِهِ عَنْهُ. وَرَوَاهُ أَحْمَدُ مِنْ طَرِيقِ أَبِي حَيَّانَ، وَأَبِي رَزِين، عَنْهُ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ أَبِي الدُّنْيَا، حَدَّثَنَا الصَّلْتُ بنُ مَسْعُود الجَحْدَري، حَدَّثَنَا فُضَيْلُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، حَدَّثَنَا يونُس بنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ فضَالَة الْأَنْصَارِيُّ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ -وَكَانَ أَبِي مِمَّنْ صَحِبَ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ أَتَاهُمْ فِي بَنِي ظَفَر، فَجَلَسَ عَلَى الصَّخْرَةِ الَّتِي فِي بَنِي ظَفَرٍ الْيَوْمَ، وَمَعَهُ ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ وَمُعَاذُ بْنُ جَبَلٍ وَنَاسٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ، فَأَمْرَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ قَارِئًا فَقَرَأَ، فَأَتَى عَلَى هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: ﴿فَكَيْفَ إِذَا جِئْنَا مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ بِشَهِيدٍ وَجِئْنَا بِكَ عَلَى هَؤُلاءِ شَهِيدًا﴾ فَبَكَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ حتى اضْطَرَبَ لِحْيَاهُ وَجَنْبَاهُ، فَقَالَ: "يَا رَبُّ هَذَا شهدتُ عَلَى مَنْ أَنَا بَيْنَ ظَهْرَيْهِ، فَكَيْفَ بِمَنْ لَمْ أَرَهُ؟ " .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ الزُّهْرِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنِ الْمَسْعُودِيِّ، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ عَمْرِو بْنِ حُرَيْثٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ -هُوَ ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ- ﴿فَكَيْفَ إِذَا جِئْنَا مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ بِشَهِيدٍ﴾ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "شَهِيدٌ عَلَيْهِمْ مَا دُمْتُ فِيهِمْ، فَإِذَا تَوَفَّيْتَنِي كُنْتَ أَنْتَ الرَّقِيبَ عَلَيْهِمْ".

وَأَمَّا مَا ذَكَرَهُ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ القُرْطُبي فِي "التَّذْكِرَةِ" حَيْثُ قَالَ: بَابُ مَا جَاءَ فِي شَهَادَةِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ على أمته: قال: أخبرنا ابن المبارك، أخبرنارجل مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ، عَنِ المِنْهَال بنِ عمرٍو، حَدَّثَهُ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ سَعِيدَ بْنَ المُسَيَّبِ يَقُولُ: لَيْسَ مِنْ يَوْمٍ إِلَّا تُعْرَضُ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ ﷺ أُمَّتُهُ غُدْوة وعَشيّة، فَيَعْرِفُهُمْ بِأَسْمَائِهِمْ وَأَعْمَالِهِمْ، فَلِذَلِكَ يَشْهَدُ عَلَيْهِمْ، يَقُولُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَكَيْفَ إِذَا جِئْنَا مِنْ كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ بِشَهِيدٍ وَجِئْنَا بِكَ عَلَى هَؤُلاءِ شَهِيدًا﴾ فَإِنَّهُ أَثَرٌ، وَفِيهِ انْقِطَاعٌ، فَإِنَّ فِيهِ رَجُلًا مُبْهَمًا لَمْ يُسَمَّ، وَهُوَ مِنْ كَلَامِ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ لَمْ يَرْفَعْهُ. وَقَدْ قَبِلَهُ الْقُرْطُبِيُّ فَقَالَ بَعْدَ إِيرَادِهِ: [قَدْ تَقَدَّمَ] أَنَّ الْأَعْمَالَ تُعْرَضُ عَلَى اللَّهِ كُلَّ يَوْمِ اثْنَيْنِ وَخَمِيسٍ، وَعَلَى الْأَنْبِيَاءِ وَالْآبَاءِ وَالْأُمَّهَاتِ يَوْمَ الجُمُعة. قَالَ: وَلَا تَعَارُضَ، فَإِنَّهُ يُحْتَمَلُ أَنْ يُخَصَّ نَبِيُّنَا بِمَا يُعْرَضُ عَلَيْهِ كُلَّ يَوْمٍ، وَيَوْمَ الْجُمُعَةِ مَعَ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ، عَلَيْهِمُ السَّلَامُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ ﴿يَوْمَئِذٍ يَوَدُّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَعَصَوُا الرَّسُولَ لَوْ تُسَوَّى بِهِمُ الأرْضُ﴾ أَيْ: لَوِ انْشَقَّتْ وَبَلَعَتْهُمْ، مِمَّا يَرَوْنَ مِنْ أَهْوَالِ الْمَوْقِفِ، وَمَا يَحِلُّ بِهِمْ مِنَ الْخِزْيِ وَالْفَضِيحَةِ وَالتَّوْبِيخِ، كَقَوْلِهِ: ﴿يَوْمَ يَنْظُرُ الْمَرْءُ مَا قَدَّمَتْ يَدَاهُ [وَيَقُولُ الْكَافِرُ يَا لَيْتَنِي كُنْتُ تُرَابًا] ﴾ وَقَوْلُهُ ﴿وَلا يَكْتُمُونَ اللَّهَ حَدِيثًا﴾ أَخْبَرَ عَنْهُمْ بِأَنَّهُمْ يَعْتَرِفُونَ بِجَمِيعِ مَا فَعَلُوهُ، وَلَا يَكْتُمُونَ مِنْهُ شَيْئًا.

قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ حُمَيْد، حَدَّثَنَا حكَّام، حَدَّثَنَا عَمْرٌو، عَنْ مُطرِّف، عَنِ الْمِنْهِالِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْر قَالَ: أَتَى رَجُلٌ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ فَقَالَ: سمعتُ اللَّهَ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، يَقُولُ -يَعْنِي إِخْبَارًا عَنِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ أَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا-: ﴿وَاللَّهِ رَبِّنَا مَا كُنَّا مُشْرِكِينَ﴾ [الْأَنْعَامِ: ٢٣] وَقَالَ فِي الْآيَةِ الْأُخْرَى: ﴿وَلا يَكْتُمُونَ اللَّهَ حَدِيثًا﴾ فَقَالَ ابنُ الْعَبَّاسِ: أَمَّا قَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَاللَّهِ رَبِّنَا مَا كُنَّا مُشْرِكِينَ﴾ فَإِنَّهُمْ لَمَّا رَأَوْا أَنَّهُ لَا يَدْخُلُ الْجَنَّةَ إِلَّا أهلُ الْإِسْلَامِ قَالُوا: تَعَالَوْا فَلْنَجْحَدْ، فَقَالُوا: ﴿وَاللَّهِ رَبِّنَا مَا كُنَّا مُشْرِكِينَ﴾ فَخَتَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَى أَفْوَاهِهِمْ، وَتَكَلَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَأَرْجُلُهُمْ ﴿وَلا يَكْتُمُونَ اللَّهَ حَدِيثًا﴾

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ: أَخْبَرَنَا مَعْمَرٌ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ عَنِ المِنْهال بْنِ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْر قَالَ: جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فَقَالَ: أَشْيَاءٌ تَخْتَلِفُ عَلَيَّ فِي الْقُرْآنِ. قَالَ: مَا هُوَ؟ أَشُكُّ في القرآن؟ قال: ليس هُوَ بِالشَّكِّ. وَلَكِنِ اخْتِلَافٌ. قَالَ: فَهَاتِ مَا اخْتَلَفَ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ. قَالَ: أَسْمَعُ اللَّهَ يَقُولُ: ﴿ثُمَّ لَمْ تَكُنْ فِتْنَتُهُمْ إِلا أَنْ قَالُوا وَاللَّهِ رَبِّنَا مَا كُنَّا مُشْرِكِينَ﴾ [الْأَنْعَامِ: ٢٣] وَقَالَ ﴿وَلا يَكْتُمُونَ اللَّهَ حَدِيثًا﴾ ؛ فَقَدْ كَتَمُوا! فَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: أَمَّا قَوْلُهُ: ﴿ثُمَّ لَمْ تَكُنْ فِتْنَتُهُمْ إِلا أَنْ قَالُوا وَاللَّهِ رَبِّنَا مَا كُنَّا مُشْرِكِينَ﴾ فَإِنَّهُمْ لَمَّا رَأَوْا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ أَنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَغْفِرُ إِلَّا لِأَهْلِ الْإِسْلَامِ وَيَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ وَلَا يَغْفِرُ شِرْكًا، وَلَا يَتَعَاظَمُهُ ذَنْبٌ أَنْ يَغْفِرَهُ، جَحَدَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ، فَقَالُوا: ﴿وَاللَّهِ رَبِّنَا مَا كُنَّا مُشْرِكِينَ﴾ ؛ رَجَاءَ أَنْ يُغْفَرَ لَهُمْ. فَخَتَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَى أَفْوَاهِهِمْ، وَتَكَلَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَأَرْجُلُهُمْ بِمَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ، فَعِنْدَ ذَلِكَ: ﴿يَوَدُّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَعَصَوُا الرَّسُولَ لَوْ تُسَوَّى بِهِمُ الأرْضُ وَلا يَكْتُمُونَ اللَّهَ حَدِيثًا﴾

وَقَالَ جُوَيْبِرٌ عَنِ الضَّحَّاك: إِنَّ نافِعَ بْنَ الأزْرَقِ أَتَى ابنَ عَبَّاسٍ فَقَالَ: يَا ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ، قَوْلُ اللَّهِ: ﴿يَوْمَئِذٍ يَوَدُّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَعَصَوُا الرَّسُولَ لَوْ تُسَوَّى بِهِمُ الأرْضُ وَلا يَكْتُمُونَ اللَّهَ حَدِيثًا﴾ وَقَوْلُهُ ﴿وَاللَّهِ رَبِّنَا مَا كُنَّا مُشْرِكِينَ﴾ ؟ فَقَالَ لَهُ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: إِنِّي أَحْسَبُكَ قُمْتَ مِنْ عِنْدِ أَصْحَابِكَ فَقُلْتَ ألْقي عَلَى ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ مُتَشَابِهُ الْقُرْآنِ. فَإِذَا رَجَعْتَ إِلَيْهِمْ فَأَخْبِرْهُمْ أَنَّ اللَّهَ جَامِعُ النَّاسِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ فِي بَقِيعٍ وَاحِدٍ. فَيَقُولُ الْمُشْرِكُونَ: إِنِ اللَّهَ لَا يَقْبَلُ مِنْ أَحَدٍ شَيْئًا إِلَّا مِمَّنْ وَحَّدَهُ، فَيَقُولُونَ: تَعَالَوْا نَقُلْ فَيَسْأَلُهُمْ فَيَقُولُونَ: ﴿وَاللَّهِ رَبِّنَا مَا كُنَّا مُشْرِكِينَ﴾ قَالَ: فَيُخْتَم عَلَى أَفْوَاهِهِمْ، وتُسْتَنطق جَوَارِحُهُمْ، فَتَشْهَدُ عَلَيْهِمْ جوارحُهم أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا مُشْرِكِينَ. فَعِنْدَ ذَلِكَ تَمَنَّوْا لَوْ أَنَّ الأرضَ سُوِّيَتْ بِهِم ﴿وَلا يَكْتُمُونَ اللَّهَ حَدِيثًا﴾ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ.

---

## 4:43 — verse 43

### Translation

The Almighty is forbidding His believing servants from performing the prayer in a state of drunkenness, in which the one praying does not know what he is saying, and from approaching its location, which is the mosques, while in a state of major ritual impurity (janaba), except when merely passing through from one door to another without lingering. This was before the prohibition of wine, as indicated by the hadith we mentioned in Surat al-Baqarah at His saying {They ask you about wine and gambling [Say: In both of them is great sin]} (al-Baqarah: 219). The Messenger of Allah ﷺ recited it to Umar, and he said: "O Allah, give us a fully clarifying explanation concerning wine." Then when this verse came down, it was recited to him, and he said: "O Allah, give us a fully clarifying explanation concerning wine." So they would not drink wine at the times of the prayers. Then when His saying {O you who believe, wine, gambling, idols, and divining arrows are but filth from the work of Satan, so avoid it that you may succeed} down to His saying {will you not then desist?} (al-Ma'idah: 90-91) came down, Umar said: "We have desisted, we have desisted."

In the narration of Isra'il, from Abu Ishaq, from Amr (that is, Ibn Shurahbil), from Umar ibn al-Khattab, concerning the story of the prohibition of wine, he mentioned the hadith, and in it: "Then the verse in Surat al-Nisa' came down: {O you who believe, do not approach the prayer while you are drunk until you know what you are saying}. So the caller of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when the prayer was established, would call out: 'No drunk person should approach the prayer.'" This is the wording of Abu Dawud.

They have mentioned, regarding the occasion of the revelation of this verse, what Ibn Abi Hatim reported.

[chain] Sa'd said: "Four verses were revealed concerning me. A man from the Ansar prepared food and invited some people from the Muhajirun and some from the Ansar. We ate and drank until we became drunk, then we boasted to one another, and a man picked up the jawbone of a camel and struck Sa'd's nose with it, breaking it, and that was before wine was prohibited. So {O you who believe, do not approach the prayer while you are drunk} was revealed."

The hadith at length is found in Muslim from the narration of Shu'bah, and it was reported by the compilers of the Sunan except Ibn Majah, through various chains from Simak, containing it.

Another occasion: Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Ali ibn Abi Talib said: "Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf prepared food for us and invited us, and gave us wine to drink, so the wine took hold of us. Then the prayer time came, and they put forward so-and-so." He said: "So he recited: {Say, O disbelievers, I do not worship what you worship, and we worship what you worship}." He said: "So Allah the Almighty sent down {O you who believe, do not approach the prayer while you are drunk until you know what you are saying}."

Thus it was reported by Ibn Abi Hatim, and likewise it was reported by al-Tirmidhi from Abd ibn Humayd, from Abd al-Rahman al-Dashtaki, containing it, and he said: "Hasan sahih (good and authentic)."

Ibn Jarir also reported it, from Muhammad ibn Bashshar, from Abd al-Rahman ibn Mahdi, from Sufyan al-Thawri, from Ata' ibn al-Sa'ib, from Abu Abd al-Rahman, from Ali: that he, Abd al-Rahman, and another man drank wine, and Abd al-Rahman led them in prayer, and he recited {Say, O disbelievers} but confused its wording, so {do not approach the prayer while you are drunk} was revealed.

This was likewise reported by Abu Dawud and al-Nasa'i, from the hadith of al-Thawri, containing it. Ibn Jarir also reported it, from Ibn Humayd, from Jarir, from Ata', from Abu Abd Allah al-Salami, who said: "Ali was among a group of the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ in the house of Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf. They ate food, and he brought them wine, and they drank from it, and that was before wine was prohibited. Then the prayer time came, and they put Ali forward, and he recited to them {Say, O disbelievers}, but did not recite it as it should be recited. So Allah, mighty and majestic, sent down {O you who believe, do not approach the prayer while you are drunk}."

Then he said: [chain] that Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf prepared food and drink and invited a group of the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ, and he led them in the Maghrib prayer, and he recited: "Say, O disbelievers. I worship what you worship. And you are worshippers of what I worship. And I am a worshipper of what you worshipped. To you your religion, and to me my religion." So Allah, mighty and majestic, sent down this verse: {O you who believe, do not approach the prayer while you are drunk until you know what you are saying}.

Al-Awfi said, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, concerning His saying {O you who believe, do not approach the prayer while you are drunk [until you know what you are saying]}: that this was because men used to come to the prayer while drunk, before wine was prohibited, so Allah said {do not approach the prayer while you are drunk}. Reported by Ibn Jarir. Abu Razin and Mujahid said likewise. Abd al-Razzaq said, from Ma'mar, from Qatadah: "They used to avoid drunkenness at the time when the prayers were due, then this was abrogated by the prohibition of wine."

Al-Dahhak said concerning His saying {O you who believe, do not approach the prayer while you are drunk}: "He did not mean by it the drunkenness of wine, but rather he meant the drowsiness of sleep." Reported by Ibn Jarir and Ibn Abi Hatim.

Then Ibn Jarir said: "The correct view is that what is meant is drunkenness from drink." He said: "The prohibition was not directed at the drunk person who does not understand the address, because that one is in the ruling of the insane person. Rather, the one addressed by the prohibition is the one who is tipsy but still understands what is required of him."

This is the substance of what he said. It has been mentioned by more than one of the scholars of legal theory (usuliyyun), namely that the address is directed at one who understands speech, not at the drunk person who does not know what is being said to him, for understanding is a condition of legal responsibility (taklif). It is also possible that what is meant is an implicit prohibition of drunkenness altogether, since they were commanded to pray at the five times of night and day, so the wine drinker would not be able to perform the prayer at its times constantly, and Allah knows best. On this understanding it would be like His saying {O you who believe, fear Allah as He should be feared, and do not die except as Muslims} (Al Imran: 102), which is a command to them to prepare for death upon Islam and to persist in obedience for that purpose.

As for His saying {until you know what you are saying}, this is the best that can be said in defining the drunk person: he is the one who does not know what he is saying. The one intoxicated has confusion in his recitation and lacks reflection and humility (khushu') in it. Imam Ahmad said:

[chain] Anas said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "If one of you becomes drowsy while praying, let him withdraw and complete it once he knows what he is saying." Al-Bukhari alone reported it, not Muslim, and he and al-Nasa'i reported it from the hadith of Ayyub, containing it. In some wordings of the hadith: "For perhaps he goes to seek forgiveness and instead curses himself."

As for His saying {nor in a state of major impurity, except when passing through, until you have washed}, Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Ibn Abbas said concerning His saying {nor in a state of major impurity, except when passing through, until you have washed}: "Do not enter the mosque while you are in a state of major impurity, except as ones passing through," meaning: "he passes through it without sitting down." Then he said: it has been narrated similarly from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, Anas, Abu Ubaydah, Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, Abu al-Duha, Ata', Mujahid, Masruq, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, Zayd ibn Aslam, Abu Malik, Amr ibn Dinar, al-Hakam ibn Utaybah, Ikrimah, al-Hasan al-Basri, Yahya ibn Sa'id al-Ansari, Ibn Shihab, and Qatadah, to similar effect.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Yazid ibn Abi Habib said, concerning the word of Allah, mighty and majestic, {nor in a state of major impurity, except when passing through}: that there were men among the Ansar whose doors opened onto the mosque, and major impurity would befall them while they had no water, so they would go out to fetch water and find no path except through the mosque. So Allah sent down {nor in a state of major impurity, except when passing through}.

Bearing witness to the soundness of what Yazid ibn Abi Habib, may God have mercy on him, said, is what is established in Sahih al-Bukhari: that the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "Close every small door in the mosque except the door of Abu Bakr."

This he said near the end of his life ﷺ, knowing that Abu Bakr, may God be pleased with him, would take charge of the affair after him, and would need to enter the mosque frequently for important matters concerning what is beneficial for the Muslims. So he ordered the closing of the doors opening onto the mosque except his door, may God be pleased with him. Whoever narrates "except the door of Ali," as occurs in some of the Sunan collections, has erred, and the sound version is what is established in the Sahih.

From this verse, many of the imams have argued that it is forbidden for one in a state of janabah (major ritual impurity) to remain in the mosque, though passing through is permitted for him. The same applies, in meaning, to the menstruating woman and the woman in postpartum bleeding. Except that some of them said: their passing through is prohibited due to the possibility of soiling the mosque. And some of them said: if each of the two is confident of not soiling it while passing through, then passing through is permitted for them, otherwise not.

It is established in Sahih Muslim, from Aisha, may God be pleased with her, that she said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said to me: "Hand me the khumrah (small prayer mat) from the mosque." I said: I am menstruating. He said: "Your menstruation is not in your hand." A similar report comes from Abu Hurayrah. In this there is evidence for the permissibility of a menstruating woman passing through the mosque, and the woman in postpartum bleeding is the same in meaning, and God knows best.

Abu Dawud narrated, from the hadith of Aflat ibn Khalifah al-Amiri, from Jasrah bint Dajajah, from Aisha, may God be pleased with her, that she said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "I do not permit the mosque to a menstruating woman nor to one in janabah." Abu Muslim al-Khattabi said: A group of scholars have graded this hadith weak, saying: Aflat is majhul (unknown). However, Ibn Majah narrated it from the hadith of Abu al-Khattab al-Hajari, from Mahduj al-Dhuhali, from Jasrah, from Umm Salamah, from the Prophet ﷺ, with the same content. Abu Zur'ah al-Razi said: They say: Jasrah from Umm Salamah, but the sound version is Jasrah from Aisha.

As for what Abu Isa al-Tirmidhi narrated, from the hadith of Salim ibn Abi Hafsah, from Atiyyah, from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "O Ali, it is not permissible for anyone to be in a state of janabah in this mosque except me and you." This is a weak hadith that is not established, for this Salim is matruk (discarded as a narrator), and his teacher Atiyyah is weak, and God knows best.

Another view on the meaning of the verse: Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] al-Mundhir ibn Shadhan told us, Ubaydullah ibn Musa told us, Ibn Abi Layla informed me, from al-Minhal, from Zirr ibn Hubaysh, from Ali, regarding {nor while in a state of janabah, except as travelers passing through, until you have washed}, he said: He should not approach the prayer, except if he is a traveler afflicted by janabah and cannot find water, in which case he prays until he finds water.

Then he narrated it by another route, from al-Minhal ibn Amr, from Zirr, from Ali ibn Abi Talib, mentioning the same. He said: It is also narrated from Ibn Abbas in one of the reports, and from Sa'id ibn Jubayr and al-Dahhak, similarly.

Ibn Jarir narrated, from the hadith of Waki', from Ibn Abi Layla, from al-Minhal, from Abbad ibn Abdullah or from Zirr ibn Hubaysh, from Ali, mentioning the same. He also narrated it via al-Awfi and Abu Mijlaz, from Ibn Abbas, mentioning the same. And he narrated it from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, and from Mujahid, al-Hasan ibn Muslim, al-Hakam ibn Utaybah, Zayd ibn Aslam and his son Abd al-Rahman, similarly. It is also narrated via Ibn Jurayj, from Abdullah ibn Kathir, who said: We used to hear that it referred to travel.

This view is supported by the hadith narrated by Imam Ahmad and the compilers of the Sunan, from the hadith of Abu Qilabah, from Amr ibn Bujdan, from Abu Dharr, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said: "The clean earth is a purifier for the Muslim, even if you do not find water for ten years. But when you find water, then apply it to your skin, for that is better."

Then Ibn Jarir said, after recounting the two views: The preferable view is that of those who said: {nor while in a state of janabah, except as travelers passing through} means: except one crossing a path within it. This is because He has already clarified the ruling for the traveler who lacks water while in a state of janabah, in His saying: {And if you are ill or on a journey, or one of you comes from relieving himself, or you have touched women, and you find no water, then seek clean earth} [al-Ma'idah: 6] to the end of the verse. Thus it is known thereby that His saying, {nor while in a state of janabah, except as travelers passing through, until you have washed}, if it were meant to refer to the traveler, there would be no comprehensible meaning in repeating its mention in His saying {And if you are ill or on a journey}, since its ruling had already passed before that. If that is so, then the interpretation of the verse is: O you who believe, do not approach the places of prayer for prayer while performing prayer in them while you are intoxicated, until you know what you are saying, nor approach them while in janabah until you have washed, except those passing through on a way. He said: "One passing through" (abir sabil) means one who crosses and traverses. From this it is said: "I crossed this road, I cross it, crossing and traversal." From this it is said: "So-and-so crossed the river" when he traversed and passed beyond it. And from this the she-camel strong for journeys is called "abr asfar" and "abr asfar," due to her strength in traversing journeys.

This view which he supported is the view of the majority, and it is the apparent meaning of the verse. It is as though God, exalted is He, forbade undertaking the prayer in a deficient state that contradicts its purpose, and forbade entering its place in a deficient state, namely janabah, which distances one both from the prayer and from its place, and God knows best.

His saying, {until you have washed}, is evidence for what the three imams, Abu Hanifah, Malik, and al-Shafi'i, held: that it is forbidden for one in janabah to remain in the mosque until he washes or performs tayammum (dry ablution), if he lacks water or is unable to use it for some reason. Imam Ahmad held that once the person in janabah performs wudu (ablution), it becomes permissible for him to remain in the mosque, based on what he and Sa'id ibn Mansur narrated in his Sunan with a sound chain: that the Companions used to do that. Sa'id ibn Mansur said:

[chain] Abd al-Aziz ibn Muhammad, that is al-Darawardi, told us, from Hisham ibn Sa'd, from Zayd ibn Aslam, from Ata ibn Yasar, who said: I saw men among the Companions of the Messenger of God ﷺ sitting in the mosque while in a state of janabah, when they had performed the ablution as for prayer. This is a sound chain according to the criterion of Muslim, and God knows best.

His saying, {And if you are ill or on a journey, or one of you comes from relieving himself, or you have touched women, and you find no water, then seek clean earth}: as for the illness that permits tayammum, it is that in which one fears, from using water, the loss of a limb, its disfigurement, or the prolonging of recovery. Some scholars permitted tayammum merely due to illness, based on the general wording of the verse. Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] my father told us, Abu Ghassan Malik ibn Isma'il told us, Qays told us, from Khusayf, from Mujahid, regarding His saying {And if you are ill}, he said: It was revealed concerning a man from the Ansar who was ill and unable to stand to perform wudu, and he had no servant to assist him. So he came to the Messenger of God ﷺ and mentioned that to him, and God revealed this verse.

This is mursal (a report with a missing link at the Companion level). As for travel, it is well known, and there is no difference in it between long and short journeys.

His saying, {or one of you comes from relieving himself}: al-gha'it is the low-lying place on the ground; it is used here as a euphemism for relieving oneself, which is the minor ritual impurity (hadath).

As for His saying, {or you have touched women}, it is read both as "lamastum" and "lamastum" (with variant vocalization), and the exegetes and the imams differed as to its meaning, on two views:

The first: that this is a euphemism for sexual intercourse, based on His saying: {And if you divorce them before you have touched them, and you have already specified for them a portion, then half of what you specified} [al-Baqarah: 237], and His saying, exalted is He: {O you who believe, when you marry believing women and then divorce them before you have touched them, then you have no waiting period to count against them} [al-Ahzab: 49].

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Abu Sa'id al-Ashajj told us, Waki' told us, from Sufyan, from Abu Ishaq, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas, regarding His saying {or lamastum women}, he said: it means sexual intercourse. And it is narrated similarly from Ali, Ubayy ibn Ka'b, Mujahid, Tawus, al-Hasan, Ubayd ibn Umayr, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, al-Sha'bi, Qatadah, and Muqatil ibn Hayyan.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Humayd ibn Mas'adah told me, Yazid ibn Zurayi' told us, Shu'bah told us, from Abu Bishr, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, who said: They mentioned "touching," and some people among the mawali (non-Arab clients) said: It does not mean sexual intercourse.

Some people among the Arabs said: al-lams (touching) means intercourse. He said: I came to Ibn Abbas and said to him: people among the non-Arab clients and the Arabs have differed concerning al-lams. The non-Arab clients said it is not intercourse, while the Arabs said it is intercourse. He said: which of the two groups were you with? I said: I was with the non-Arab clients. He said: the group of the non-Arab clients has been overcome. Indeed al-lams, al-mass, and al-mubasharah (touching, contact, and direct bodily contact) all mean intercourse, but Allah refers by euphemism to whatever He wills with whatever expression He wills.

Then he related it from Ibn Bashshar, from Ghundar, from Shu'bah, with similar wording. Then he related it through other channels from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, similarly.

Similarly he said: Ya'qub told me, Hushaym told us, he said: Abu Bishr told us, Sa'id ibn Jubayr informed us, from Ibn Abbas, who said: al-lams, al-mass, and al-mubasharah mean intercourse, but Allah refers by euphemism to whatever He wills.
