Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather, said: "I once sat, my brother and I, in a gathering that I would not exchange for red camels. My brother and I came forward and found a group of elders among the Companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ at one of his doors. We disliked to separate them, so we sat off to the side. Then they mentioned a verse of the Qur'an and disputed over it until their voices rose. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ came out angry, his face reddened, throwing dust at them and saying: 'Gently, O people! It was by this that the nations before you were destroyed, by their disputing about their prophets and striking some of the Books against others. The Qur'an was not revealed so that some of it would contradict some other part, rather some of it confirms other parts. So whatever you know of it, act upon it, and whatever you do not know of it, refer it back to the one who knows it.'"

It was likewise narrated also from Abu Mu'awiyah, from Dawud ibn Abi Hind, from Amr ibn Shu'ayb, from his father, from his grandfather, who said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ came out one day while the people were speaking about divine decree (al-qadar), and it was as though pomegranate seeds had been burst upon his face from anger. He said to them: 'What is wrong with you, striking the Book of Allah against itself? It was by this that those before you were destroyed.'" He said: "I have never envied myself for a gathering in which the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was present that I did not attend, as much as I envied myself over not having attended that gathering."

Ibn Majah also narrated it from the hadith of Dawud ibn Abi Hind, with similar wording.

Ahmad said: [chain] Abu Imran al-Jawni said: Abdullah ibn Rabah wrote to me, narrating from Abdullah ibn Amr, who said: "I went at midday to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ one day, and while we were sitting, two men differed over a verse, and their voices rose. He said: 'The nations before you were only destroyed by their disputing over the Book.'" Muslim and al-Nasa'i narrated it from the hadith of Hammad ibn Zayd, with the same chain.

As for His saying: {And when a matter of security or fear comes to them, they spread it about}, this is a condemnation of those who rush to matters before verifying them, reporting them, spreading them, and publicizing them, when they may not even be true.

Muslim said in the introduction to his Sahih: [chain] Abu Hurayrah, from the Prophet ﷺ, said: "It is enough falsehood for a man that he report everything he hears." Abu Dawud likewise narrated it in the Book of Manners (al-Adab) of his Sunan, from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn ibn Ishkab, from Ali ibn Hafs, from Shu'bah, with a connected chain. Muslim also narrated it from the hadith of Mu'adh ibn Hisham al-Anbari and Abd al-Rahman ibn Mahdi. Abu Dawud also reported it from the hadith of Hafs ibn Umar al-Namiri, all three from Shu'bah, from Khubayb, from Hafs ibn Asim, with the same wording, as a mursal (chain missing a link at the Companion level) report.

In the two Sahihs, from al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah: that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade "qila wa qal" (idle hearsay), meaning excessive reporting of what people say without verification, reflection, or clarification.

In the Sunan of Abu Dawud, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "What a wretched mount for a man is 'they claim.'"

In the Sahih: "Whoever narrates a report while believing it to be false is one of the two liars." Here should be mentioned the agreed-upon hadith of Umar ibn al-Khattab, when it reached him that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ had divorced his wives. He came from his house until he entered the mosque and found the people saying this. He could not be patient until he sought permission to enter upon the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and asked him directly: "Have you divorced your wives?" He said: "No." So I said, "Allah is greatest." And he mentioned the hadith at length.

In Muslim's version: "So I said: 'Have you divorced them?' He said: 'No.' So I stood at the door of the mosque and called out at the top of my voice: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ has not divorced his wives.'" And this verse was revealed: {And when a matter of security or fear comes to them, they spread it about, but if they had referred it to the Messenger or to those in authority among them, those among them who can draw the correct conclusions would have known it}. "I was the one," he said, "who drew that conclusion (istanbata) about that matter."

The meaning of His saying {yastanbitunahu} is: they extract it and derive knowledge of it from its sources. It is said, "the man istanbata (drew out) the spring," when he dug it and brought its water out from its depths.

As for the meaning of His saying {you would have followed Satan, except for a few}: Ali ibn Abi Talhah reported from Ibn Abbas that it means the believers.

Abd al-Razzaq reported from Ma'mar, from Qatadah: {you would have followed Satan, except for a few} meaning all of you. Those who supported this view cited the words of al-Tirimmah ibn Hakim in praise of Yazid ibn al-Muhallab:

"Lofty of nose, abundant of hand in generosity, few in faults and blemishes."

Meaning: he has no faults, and no blemish is found in him.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى آمِرًا عِبَادَهُ بِتَدَبُّرِ الْقُرْآنِ، وَنَاهِيًا لَهُمْ عَنِ الْإِعْرَاضِ عَنْهُ، وَعَنْ تَفَهُّمِ مَعَانِيهِ الْمُحْكَمَةِ وَأَلْفَاظِهِ الْبَلِيغَةِ، وَمُخْبِرًا لَهُمْ أَنَّهُ لَا اخْتِلَافَ فِيهِ وَلَا اضْطِرَابَ، وَلَا تَضَادَّ وَلَا تَعَارُضَ؛ لِأَنَّهُ تَنْزِيلٌ مِنْ حَكِيمٍ حَمِيدٍ، فَهُوَ حَقٌّ مِنْ حَقٍّ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿أَفَلا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ الْقُرْآنَ [أَمْ عَلى قُلُوبٍ أَقْفَالُهَا] ﴾ [مُحَمَّدٍ: ٢٤] ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿وَلَوْ كَانَ مِنْ عِنْدِ غَيْرِ اللَّهِ﴾ أَيْ: لَوْ كَانَ مُفْتَعَلًا مُخْتَلَقًا، كَمَا يَقُولُهُ مَنْ يَقُولُهُ مِنْ جَهَلَةِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ وَالْمُنَافِقِينَ فِي بَوَاطِنِهِمْ ﴿لَوَجَدُوا فِيهِ اخْتِلافًا كَثِيرًا﴾ أَيْ: اضْطِرَابًا وَتَضَادًّا كَثِيرًا. أَيْ: وَهَذَا سَالِمٌ مِنَ الِاخْتِلَافِ، فَهُوَ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللَّهِ. كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى مخبرا عن الراسخين في العلم حَيْثُ قَالُوا: ﴿آمَنَّا بِهِ كُلٌّ مِنْ عِنْدِ رَبِّنَا﴾ [آلِ عِمْرَانَ:٧] أَيْ: مُحْكَمُهُ وَمُتَشَابِهُهُ حَقٌّ؛ فَلِهَذَا رَدُّوا الْمُتَشَابِهَ إِلَى الْمُحْكَمِ فَاهْتَدَوْا، وَالَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ زَيْغٌ رَدُّوا الْمُحْكَمَ إِلَى الْمُتَشَابِهِ فغووا؛ وَلِهَذَا مَدَحَ تَعَالَى الرَّاسِخِينَ وَذَمَّ الزَّائِغِينَ.

قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا أَنَسُ بْنُ عِيَاضٍ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو حَازِمٍ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ قَالَ: لَقَدْ جَلَسْتُ أَنَا وَأَخِي مَجْلِسًا مَا أُحِبُّ أَنَّ لِي بِهِ حُمر النَّعم، أَقْبَلْتُ أَنَا وَأَخِي وَإِذَا مَشْيَخَةٌ مِنْ صَحَابَةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ عَلَى بَابٍ مِنْ أَبْوَابِهِ، فَكَرِهْنَا أَنْ نُفَرِّقَ بَيْنَهُمْ، فَجَلَسْنَا حَجْرَة، إِذْ ذَكَرُوا آيَةً مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ، فَتَمَارَوْا فِيهَا حَتَّى ارْتَفَعَتْ أَصْوَاتُهُمْ، فَخَرَجَ رسولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ مُغْضَبًا حَتَّى احْمَرَّ وَجْهُهُ، يَرْمِيهِمْ بِالتُّرَابِ، وَيَقُولُ: "مَهْلًا يَا قَوْمُ، بِهَذَا أُهْلِكَتِ الْأُمَمُ مَنْ قَبْلِكُمْ بِاخْتِلَافِهِمْ عَلَى أَنْبِيَائِهِمْ، وَضَرْبِهِمُ الْكُتُبَ بَعْضَهَا ببعض، إن القرآن لم ينزل يكذب بَعْضُهُ بَعْضًا، بَلْ يُصَدِّقُ بَعْضُهُ بَعْضًا، فَمَا عَرَفْتُمْ مِنْهُ فَاعْمَلُوا بِهِ، وَمَا جَهِلْتُمْ مِنْهُ فردوه إِلَى عالمِه" .

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَيْضًا عَنْ أَبِي مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ أَبِي هِنْدٍ، عَنْ عَمْرو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جده قَالَ: خَرَجَ رسولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ، وَالنَّاسُ يَتَكَلَّمُونَ فِي الْقَدَرِ، فَكَأَنَّمَا يُفْقَأ فِي وَجْهِهِ حَبُّ الرُّمان مِنَ الْغَضَبِ، فَقَالَ لَهُمْ: "مَا لَكُمْ تَضْرِبُونَ كِتَابَ اللَّهِ بَعْضَهُ بِبَعْضٍ؟ بِهَذَا هَلَكَ مَنْ كَانَ قَبْلَكُمْ". قَالَ: فَمَا غَبَطْتُ نَفْسِي بِمَجْلِسٍ فِيهِ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ ولم أَشْهَدْهُ مَا غَبَطْتُ نَفْسِي بِذَلِكَ الْمَجْلِسِ، أَنِّي لَمْ أَشْهَدْهُ.

وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَاجَهْ مِنْ حَدِيثِ دَاوُدَ بْنِ أَبِي هِنْدٍ، بِهِ نَحْوَهُ .

وَقَالَ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي عمْران الجَوْني قَالَ: كَتَبَ إِلَيَّ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ رَبَاح، يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو قَالَ: هَجَّرتُ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَوْمًا، فَإِنَّا لَجُلُوسٌ إِذِ اخْتَلَفَ اثْنَانِ فِي آيَةٍ، فَارْتَفَعَتْ أَصْوَاتُهُمَا فَقَالَ: "إِنَّمَا هَلَكَتِ الْأُمَمُ قَبْلَكُمْ بِاخْتِلَافِهِمْ فِي الْكِتَابِ" وَرَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ وَالنَّسَائِيُّ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ حَمَّادِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، بِهِ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَإِذَا جَاءَهُمْ أَمْرٌ مِنَ الأمْنِ أَوِ الْخَوْفِ أَذَاعُوا بِهِ﴾ إِنْكَارٌ عَلَى مَنْ يُبَادِرُ إِلَى الْأُمُورِ قَبْلَ تَحَقُّقِهَا، فَيُخْبِرُ بِهَا وَيُفْشِيهَا وَيَنْشُرُهَا، وَقَدْ لَا يَكُونُ لَهَا صِحَّةٌ.

وَقَدْ قَالَ مُسْلِمٌ فِي "مُقَدِّمَةِ صَحِيحِهِ" حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ حَفْصٍ، حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ خُبَيْبِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ حَفْصِ بْنِ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ قال: "كفى بالمرء كذبا أَنْ يُحدِّث بِكُلِّ مَا سَمِعَ" وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ فِي كِتَابِ "الْأَدَبِ" مِنْ سُنَنِهِ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ إِشْكَابٍ، عَنْ علي بن حفص، عن شعبة مسندًا ورواه مسلم أيضا من حديث مُعَاذِ بْنِ هِشَامٍ الْعَنْبَرِيِّ، وَعَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ مَهْدِيٍّ. وَأَخْرَجَهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ أَيْضًا مِنْ حَدِيثِ حَفْصِ بْنِ عُمَرَ النَّمِرِيِّ، ثَلَاثَتُهُمْ عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ خُبَيب عَنْ حَفْصِ بْنِ عَاصِمٍ، بِهِ مُرْسَلًا .

وَفِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ عَنِ الْمُغِيرَةِ بْنِ شُعْبَةَ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى الله عليه وسلم نَهَى عَنْ قِيلَ وَقَالَ أَيْ: الَّذِي يُكْثِرُ مِنَ الْحَدِيثِ عَمَّا يَقُولُ النَّاسُ مِنْ غَيْرِ تَثبُّت، وَلَا تَدبُّر، وَلَا تبَيُّن .

وَفِي سُنَنِ أَبِي دَاوُدَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "بِئْسَ مَطِيَّة الرَّجُلِ زَعَمُوا عَلَيْهِ".

وَفِي الصَّحِيحِ: "مَنْ حَدَّث بِحَدِيثٍ وَهُوَ يَرَى أَنَّهُ كَذِبٌ فَهُوَ أَحَدُ الكاذبَيْن". وَيُذْكَرُ هَاهُنَا حَدِيثُ عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ الْمُتَّفَقُ عَلَيْهِ، حِينَ بَلَغَهُ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ طَلَّق نِسَاءَهُ، فَجَاءَهُ مِنْ مَنْزِلِهِ حَتَّى دَخَلَ الْمَسْجِدَ فَوَجَدَ النَّاسَ يَقُولُونَ ذَلِكَ، فَلَمْ يَصْبِرْ حَتَّى اسْتَأْذَنَ عَلِيَّ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَاسْتَفْهَمَهُ: أَطَلَّقْتَ نِسَاءَكَ؟ قَالَ: "لَا". فَقُلْتُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ. وَذَكَرَ الْحَدِيثَ بِطُولِهِ.

وَعِنْدَ مُسْلِمٍ: فَقُلْتُ: أَطَلَّقْتَهُنَّ؟ فَقَالَ: "لَا" فَقُمْتُ عَلَى بَابِ الْمَسْجِدِ فَنَادَيْتُ بِأَعْلَى صَوْتِي: لَمْ يُطَلِّقْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ نِسَاءَهُ. وَنَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ: ﴿وَإِذَا جَاءَهُمْ أَمْرٌ مِنَ الأمْنِ أَوِ الْخَوْفِ أَذَاعُوا بِهِ وَلَوْ رَدُّوهُ إِلَى الرَّسُولِ وَإِلَى أُولِي الأمْرِ مِنْهُمْ لَعَلِمَهُ الَّذِينَ يَسْتَنْبِطُونَهُ مِنْهُمْ﴾ فَكُنْتُ أَنَا اسْتَنْبَطْتُ ذَلِكَ الْأَمْرَ.

وَمَعْنَى قَوْلِهِ: (يَسْتَنْبِطُونَهُ) أَيْ: يَسْتَخْرِجُونَهُ وَيَسْتَعْلِمُونَهُ مِنْ مَعَادِنِهِ، يُقَالُ: اسْتَنْبَطَ الرَّجُلُ الْعَيْنَ، إِذَا حَفَرَهَا وَاسْتَخْرَجَهَا مِنْ قُعُورِهَا .

وَمَعْنَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿لاتَّبَعْتُمُ الشَّيْطَانَ إِلا قَلِيلا﴾ قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: يَعْنِي الْمُؤْمِنِينَ.

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ: ﴿لاتَّبَعْتُمُ الشَّيْطَانَ إِلا قَلِيلا﴾ يَعْنِي: كُلَّكُمْ. وَاسْتَشْهَدَ مَنْ نَصَرَ هَذَا الْقَوْلَ. بِقَوْلِ الطِّرْمَاحِ بْنِ حَكِيمٍ، فِي مَدْحِ يَزِيدَ بْنِ المُهَلَّب:

أشَمَّ كَثِيرَ يَدى النَّوَالِ قَلِيلَ المَثَالب والقَادحة 

يَعْنِي: لَا مَثَالِبَ لَهُ، وَلَا قَادِحَةَ فِيهِ.

---

## 4:84-87 — verses 84-87 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Exalted commands His servant and messenger Muhammad ﷺ to personally undertake the fighting, and whoever holds back from it, no blame falls on him because of it. Hence He says: {You are charged with none but yourself}.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: my father narrated to us, Muhammad ibn Amr ibn Nubayh narrated to us, Hakkam narrated to us, al-Jarrah al-Kindi narrated to us, from Abu Ishaq, who said: I asked al-Bara' ibn Azib about a man who encounters a hundred of the enemy and fights them, would he be among those about whom Allah says: {and do not cast yourselves into destruction} [al-Baqarah: 195]? He said: Allah the Exalted has said to His Prophet ﷺ: {So fight in the way of Allah; you are charged with none but yourself, and urge on the believers}.

Imam Ahmad narrated it, from Sulayman ibn Dawud, from Abu Bakr ibn Ayyash, from Abu Ishaq, who said: I said to al-Bara': the man who charges against the polytheists, is he among those who cast themselves into destruction? He said: No, because Allah sent His Messenger ﷺ and said: {So fight in the way of Allah; you are charged with none but yourself}. That verse [about casting oneself into destruction] concerns spending only.

Ibn Marduyah likewise narrated it, by way of Abu Bakr ibn Ayyash and Ali ibn Salih, from Abu Ishaq, from al-Bara', with the same content.

Then Ibn Marduyah said: Sulayman ibn Ahmad narrated to us, Ahmad ibn al-Nadr al-'Askari narrated to us, Muslim ibn Abd al-Rahman al-Jarmi narrated to us, Muhammad ibn Himyar narrated to us, Sufyan al-Thawri narrated to us, from Abu Ishaq, from al-Bara', who said: When it was revealed to the Prophet ﷺ: {So fight in the way of Allah; you are charged with none but yourself, and urge on the believers, [perhaps Allah will restrain the might of those who disbelieve]}, he said to his companions: "My Lord has commanded me to fight, so fight." This is a gharib (singular) report.

His saying: {and urge on the believers}, that is, urge them to fight, encourage them toward it, and embolden them at the time, just as the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to them on the day of Badr, while straightening the ranks: "Rise up to a Garden as wide as the heavens and the earth."

Many hadiths have come conveying encouragement toward this. Among them is what al-Bukhari narrated from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever believes in Allah and His Messenger, establishes the prayer, gives zakat, and fasts Ramadan, it is a right upon Allah that He admit him into the Garden, whether he emigrated in the way of Allah or remained in the land where he was born." They said: O Messenger of Allah, shall we not give the people glad tidings of this? He said: "In the Garden there are a hundred levels which Allah has prepared for those who strive in His way. Between every two levels is a distance like that between the heaven and the earth. So when you ask Allah, ask Him for al-Firdaws, for it is the middle of the Garden and its highest part, and above it is the Throne of the Merciful, and from it the rivers of the Garden burst forth."

Something similar has been narrated from the hadiths of Mu'adh, Abu al-Darda', and 'Ubadah.

From Abu Sa'id al-Khudri: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "O Abu Sa'id, whoever is content with Allah as Lord, with Islam as religion, and with Muhammad as prophet, the Garden becomes obligatory for him." He said: Abu Sa'id marveled at this and said: Repeat it for me, O Messenger of Allah. So he did. Then the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "And there is another matter by which Allah raises the servant a hundred levels in the Garden, the distance between every two levels being like that between the heaven and the earth." He said: What is it, O Messenger of Allah? He said: "Jihad in the way of Allah." Muslim narrated it.

His saying: {perhaps Allah will restrain the might of those who disbelieve}, that is, through your urging them on to fight, their resolve is roused to confront the enemies decisively, to defend the domain of Islam and its people, and to stand against them and endure steadfastly.

His saying: {and Allah is stronger in might and stronger in punishment}, that is, He is able against them in this world and the next, as He the Exalted says: {That, and had Allah willed, He would have taken vengeance upon them Himself, but [He ordained it] that He might test some of you by means of others. [And those who are slain in the way of Allah, He will never let their deeds be lost]} [Muhammad: 4].

His saying: {Whoever intercedes with a good intercession will have a share of it}, that is, whoever exerts himself in a matter and good results from it will have a share of that. {And whoever intercedes with an evil intercession will have a portion of it}, that is, he will bear a burden from that matter which resulted from his effort and intention, just as it is established in the sound hadith that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Intercede, and you will be rewarded, and Allah will decree upon the tongue of His Prophet what He wills."

Mujahid ibn Jabr said: This verse was revealed concerning people's interceding for one another.

Al-Hasan al-Basri said: Allah the Exalted said: {Whoever intercedes}, and He did not say "whoever is interceded for."

His saying: {and Allah has power (muqit) over all things}: Ibn Abbas, Ata', Atiyyah, Qatadah, and Matar al-Warraq said: muqit means guardian and preserver. Mujahid said: witness. And in another report from him: reckoner. Sa'id ibn Jubayr, al-Suddi, and Ibn Zayd said: powerful. Abdullah ibn Kathir said: al-Muqit is the ever-sustaining one. Al-Dahhak said: al-Muqit is the provider of sustenance.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: my father narrated to us, Abd al-Rahim ibn Mutarrif narrated to us, Isa ibn Yunus narrated to us, from Isma'il, from a man, from Abdullah ibn Rawahah, whom a man asked about Allah's saying: {and Allah has power over all things}. He said: He provides for every human being according to the measure of his deeds.

His saying: {And when you are greeted with a greeting, greet with one better than it, or return it}, that is, if a Muslim greets you with salam, return to him something better than what he greeted you with, or return to him the like of what he greeted you with. So the increase is recommended, and the matching is obligatory.

Ibn Jarir said: Musa ibn Sahl al-Ramli narrated to me, Abdullah ibn al-Sari al-Antaki narrated to us, Hisham ibn Lahiq narrated to us, from Asim al-Ahwal, from Abu Uthman al-Nahdi, from Salman al-Farisi, who said: A man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: "Peace be upon you, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "And upon you be peace and the mercy of Allah." Then another came and said: "Peace be upon you, O Messenger of Allah, and the mercy of Allah." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to him: "And upon you be peace and the mercy of Allah and His blessings." Then another came and said: "Peace be upon you, O Messenger of Allah, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings." He said to him: "And upon you." The man said: O Prophet of Allah, by my father and mother, so-and-so and so-and-so came to you and greeted you, and you returned to them more than what you returned to me. He said: "You left nothing for us to add. Allah the Exalted says: {And when you are greeted with a greeting, greet with one better than it, or return it}, so we returned it to you [in full, having nothing left to add]."

Ibn Abi Hatim likewise narrated it in a suspended (mu'allaq) form, saying: it was mentioned from Ahmad ibn al-Hasan al-Tirmidhi, Abdullah ibn al-Sari, Abu Muhammad al-Antaki, narrated to us, Abu al-Hasan said: he was a righteous man, Hisham ibn Lahiq narrated to us, and he mentioned it with its chain, the same.

Abu Bakr ibn Marduyah narrated it: Abd al-Baqi ibn Qani' narrated to us, Abdullah ibn Ahmad ibn Hanbal narrated to us, my father narrated to us, Hisham ibn Lahiq Abu Uthman narrated to us, and he mentioned it the same way. I did not find it in the Musnad, and Allah knows best.

In this hadith there is an indication that there is no addition to the greeting beyond this form: "Peace be upon you, and the mercy of Allah, and His blessings," for if more than that had been legislated, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ would have added it.

Imam Ahmad said: Muhammad ibn Kathir, brother of Sulayman ibn Kathir, narrated to us, Ja'far ibn Sulayman narrated to us, from Awf, from Abu Raja' al-Utaridi, from Imran ibn Husayn: a man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said, "Peace be upon you," so he returned the greeting to him, then the man sat down, and the Prophet said: "Ten." Then another came and said: "Peace be upon you, and the mercy of Allah." He returned the greeting to him, then the man sat down, and he said: "Twenty." Then another came and said: "Peace be upon you, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings." He returned the greeting to him, then the man sat down, and he said: "Thirty."

Abu Dawud likewise narrated it from Muhammad ibn Kathir, and al-Tirmidhi, al-Nasa'i, and al-Bazzar extracted it from his hadith. Al-Tirmidhi then said: hasan gharib (sound but singular) from this route, and on this subject there are reports from Abu Sa'id, Ali, and Sahl ibn Hunayf, may Allah be pleased with them.

Al-Bazzar said: This has been narrated from the Prophet ﷺ by several routes, and this is the best of them in terms of chain. Ibn Abi Hatim said: Ali ibn Harb al-Mawsili narrated to us, Humayd ibn Abd al-Rahman al-Ru'asi narrated to us, from al-Hasan ibn Salih, from Simak, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas, who said: Whoever among Allah's creation greets you with salam, return the greeting to him, even if he is a Magian, because Allah says: {greet with one better than it, or return it}.

Qatadah said: {greet with one better than it}, meaning for Muslims, {or return it}, meaning for the people of the covenant (non-Muslim subjects, dhimmis).

This application requires reconsideration. Rather, as stated previously in the hadith, what is meant is that one should return a greeting better than what one was greeted with, and if the Muslim reaches the utmost form legislated for the greeting, then the like of what he said should be returned to him. As for the people of the covenant, they are not to be greeted first, nor are they to be given more than their due; rather, one responds to them as established in the two Sahihs, from Ibn Umar, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "When the Jews greet you, one of them only says 'al-sam upon you' [death upon you], so say: 'and upon you.'"

In Sahih Muslim, from Abu Hurayrah, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Do not initiate the greeting with the Jews and the Christians, and when you meet them on a road, force them to its narrowest part."

Sufyan al-Thawri said, from a man, from al-Hasan al-Basri, who said: The greeting is voluntary, and the response to it is obligatory.

This statement of his is the view of the scholars unanimously: that the response is obligatory upon whoever is greeted, and he sins if he does not do it, because he would have opposed Allah's command in His saying: {greet with one better than it, or return it}, as has come in the hadith which he narrated.

His saying: {Allah, there is no god but He}, is a declaration of His oneness and His exclusive possession of divinity over all creation. It contains within it an oath, on account of His saying: {He will surely gather you together for the Day of Resurrection, about which there is no doubt}. This lam (letter) is a preparatory lam for the oath. So His saying: {Allah, there is no god but He} is both a statement of fact and an oath that He will gather the first and the last on a single plain, and repay every doer according to his deed.

His saying, the Exalted: {And who is more truthful than Allah in speech}, that is, no one is more truthful than He in His speech and His report, and His promise and His threat, so there is no god but He, and no lord besides Him.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَأْمُرُ تَعَالَى عَبْدَهُ وَرَسُولَهُ مُحَمَّدًا ﷺ أَنْ يُبَاشِرَ الْقِتَالَ بِنَفْسِهِ، وَمَنْ نَكَلَ عَلَيْهِ فَلَا عَلَيْهِ مِنْهُ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿لَا تُكَلَّفُ إِلا نَفْسَكَ﴾

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَمْرِو بْنِ نُبَيْح، حَدَّثَنَا حَكَّام، حَدَّثَنَا الْجَرَّاحُ الْكِنْدِيُّ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْبَرَاءَ بْنَ عَازِبٍ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَلْقَى مائة من العدو، فَيُقَاتِلُ، أَيَكُونُ مِمَّنْ يَقُولُ اللَّهُ: ﴿وَلا تُلْقُوا بِأَيْدِيكُمْ إِلَى التَّهْلُكَةِ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ١٩٥] قَالَ: قَدْ قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى لِنَبِيِّهِ ﷺ ﴿فَقَاتِلْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ لَا تُكَلَّفُ إِلا نَفْسَكَ وَحَرِّضِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ﴾

وَرَوَاهُ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ دَاوُدَ، عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ عيَّاش، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِلْبَرَاءِ: الرَّجُلُ يَحْمِلُ عَلَى الْمُشْرِكِينَ أَهْوَ مِمَّنْ أَلْقَى بِيَدِهِ إِلَى التَّهْلُكَةِ؟ قَالَ: لَا؛ لِأَنَّ اللَّهَ بَعَثَ رَسُولَهُ ﷺ وَقَالَ: ﴿فَقَاتِلْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ لَا تُكَلَّفُ إِلا نَفْسَكَ﴾ إِنَّمَا ذَلِكَ فِي النَّفَقَةِ.

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مردُويه، مِنْ طَرِيقِ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ عَيَّاشٍ، وَعَلِيِّ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْبَرَاءِ بِهِ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ ابْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ: حَدَّثَنَا سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ، حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ النَّضْرِ الْعَسْكَرِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا مُسْلِمُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الْجَرْمِيّ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ حِمْيَر، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ الثَّوْرِيُّ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْبَرَاءِ قَالَ: لَمَّا نَزَلَتْ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ ﷺ: ﴿فَقَاتِلْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ لَا تُكَلَّفُ إِلَّا نَفْسَكَ وَحَرِّضِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ [عَسَى اللَّهُ أَنْ يَكُفَّ بَأْسَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا] ﴾ الْآيَةَ، قَالَ لِأَصْحَابِهِ: "قَدْ أَمَرَنِي رَبِّي بِالْقِتَالِ فَقَاتِلُوا" حَدِيثٌ غَرِيبٌ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَحَرِّضِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ﴾ أَيْ: عَلَى الْقِتَالِ وَرَغِّبْهُمْ فِيهِ وَشَجِّعْهُمْ عِنْدَهُ كَمَا قَالَ لَهُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ يوم بدر، وَهُوَ يُسَوِّي الصُّفُوفَ: "قُومُوا إِلَى جَنَّةٍ عَرْضُهَا السماوات والأرض".

وَقَدْ وَرَدَتْ أحاديثُ كَثِيرَةٌ فِي التَّرْغِيبِ فِي ذَلِكَ، فَمِنْ ذَلِكَ مَا رَوَاهُ الْبُخَارِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَنْ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَأَقَامَ الصَّلَاةَ، وَآتَى الزَّكَاةَ، وَصَامَ رَمَضَانَ، كَانَ حَقًّا عَلَى اللَّهِ أَنْ يُدْخِلَهُ الْجَنَّةَ، هَاجَرَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ أَوْ جَلَسَ فِي أَرْضِهِ الَّتِي وُلِدَ فِيهَا" قَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، أَفَلَا نُبَشِّرُ الناسَ بِذَلِكَ؟ فَقَالَ: "إِنَّ فِي الْجَنَّةِ مائةَ دَرَجَةٍ، أعدَّها اللَّهُ لِلْمُجَاهِدِينَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ، بَيْنَ كُلِّ دَرَجَتَيْنِ كَمَا بَيْنَ السَّمَاءِ وَالْأَرْضِ، فَإِذَا سَأَلْتُمُ اللَّهَ فَاسْأَلُوهُ الْفِرْدَوْسَ فَإِنَّهُ أَوْسَطُ الْجَنَّةِ. وَأَعْلَى الْجَنَّةِ، وَفَوْقَهُ عَرْشُ الرَّحْمَنِ، وَمِنْهُ تُفَجَّر أَنْهَارُ الْجَنَّةِ" .

ورُوي مِنْ حَدِيثِ مُعَاذٍ وَأَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ وعُبادة نَحْوُ ذَلِكَ.

وَعَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الخدْري أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "يَا أَبَا سعيد، من رضي بالله ربا، وبالإسلام دِينًا، وَبِمُحَمَّدٍ نَبِيًّا، وَجَبَتْ لَهُ الْجَنَّةُ" قَالَ: فَعَجِبَ لَهَا أَبُو سَعِيدٍ فَقَالَ: أَعِدْهَا عليَّ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ. فَفَعَلَ. ثُمَّ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "وَأُخْرَى يَرْفَعُ اللَّهُ بِهَا الْعَبْدَ مِائَةَ دَرَجَةٍ فِي الْجَنَّةِ، مَا بَيْنَ كُلِّ دَرَجَتَيْنِ كَمَا بَيْنَ السَّمَاءِ وَالْأَرْضِ" قَالَ: وَمَا هِيَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ؟ قَالَ: "الْجِهَادُ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ" رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿عَسَى اللَّهُ أَنْ يَكُفَّ بَأْسَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا﴾ أَيْ: بِتَحْرِيضِكَ إِيَّاهُمْ عَلَى الْقِتَالِ تَنْبَعِثُ هِمَمُهُمْ عَلَى مُنَاجَزَةِ الْأَعْدَاءِ، وَمُدَافَعَتِهِمْ عَنْ حَوْزَةِ الْإِسْلَامِ وَأَهْلِهِ، وَمُقَاوَمَتِهِمْ وَمُصَابِرَتِهِمْ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَاللَّهُ أَشَدُّ بَأْسًا وَأَشَدُّ تَنْكِيلا﴾ أَيْ: هُوَ قَادِرٌ عَلَيْهِمْ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ، كَمَا قَالَ [تَعَالَى] ﴿ذَلِكَ وَلَوْ يَشَاءُ اللَّهُ لانْتَصَرَ مَنْهُمْ وَلَكِنْ لِيَبْلُوَ بَعْضَكُمْ بِبَعْضٍ [وَالَّذِينَ قُتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ فَلَنْ يُضِلَّ أَعْمَالَهُمْ] ﴾ [مُحَمَّدٍ: ٤] .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿مَنْ يَشْفَعْ شَفَاعَةً حَسَنَةً يَكُنْ لَهُ نَصِيبٌ مِنْهَا﴾ أَيْ: مَنْ سَعَى فِي أَمْرٍ، فَتَرَتَّبَ عَلَيْهِ خَيْرٌ، كَانَ لَهُ نَصِيبٌ مِنْ ذَلِكَ ﴿وَمَنْ يَشْفَعْ شَفَاعَةً سَيِّئَةً يَكُنْ لَهُ كِفْلٌ مِنْهَا﴾ أَيْ: يَكُونُ عَلَيْهِ وِزْرٌ مِنْ ذَلِكَ الْأَمْرِ الَّذِي تَرَتَّبَ عَلَى سَعْيِهِ وَنِيَّتِهِ، كَمَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحِ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "اشْفَعُوا تُؤْجَرُوا وَيَقْضِي اللَّهُ عَلَى لِسَانِ نَبِيِّهِ مَا شَاءَ".

وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدُ بْنُ جَبْر: نَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ فِي شَفَاعَاتِ النَّاسِ بَعْضِهِمْ لِبَعْضٍ.

وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ الْبَصْرِيُّ: قَالَ اللَّهُ تعالى: ﴿مَنْ يَشْفَعْ﴾ ولم يقل: مَنْ يُشَفَّع.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُقِيتًا﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ، وَعَطَاءٌ، وَعَطِيَّةُ، وَقَتَادَةُ، وَمَطَرٌ الْوَرَّاقُ: ﴿مُقِيتًا﴾ أَيْ: حَفِيظًا. وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ: شَهِيدًا. وَفِي رِوَايَةٍ عَنْهُ: حَسِيبًا. وَقَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ، وَالسُّدِّيُّ، وَابْنُ زَيْدٍ: قَدِيرًا. وَقَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ كَثِيرٍ: الْمُقِيتُ: الْوَاصِبُ وَقَالَ الضَّحَّاكُ: الْمَقِيتُ: الرَّزَّاقُ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ بْنُ مُطَرِّفٍ، حَدَّثَنَا عِيسَى بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ رَوَاحَةَ، وَسَأَلَهُ رَجُلٌ عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ: ﴿وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُقِيتًا﴾ قَالَ: يُقيت كُلَّ إِنْسَانٍ عَلَى قَدْرِ عَمَلِهِ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَإِذَا حُيِّيتُمْ بِتَحِيَّةٍ فَحَيُّوا بِأَحْسَنَ مِنْهَا أَوْ رُدُّوهَا﴾ أَيْ: إِذَا سَلَّمَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمُسْلِمُ، فَرَدُّوا عَلَيْهِ أَفْضَلَ مِمَّا سَلَّمَ، أَوْ رَدُّوا عَلَيْهِ بِمِثْلِ مَا سَلَّمَ [بِهِ] فَالزِّيَادَةُ مَنْدُوبَةٌ، وَالْمُمَاثَلَةُ مَفْرُوضَةٌ.

قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنِي مُوسَى بْنُ سَهْلٍ الرَّمْلِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ السَّري الْأَنْطَاكِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامُ بْنُ لَاحِقٍ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ الْأَحْوَلِ، عَنْ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ النَّهْدي، عَنْ سَلْمَانَ الْفَارِسِيِّ قَالَ: جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ﷺ فَقَالَ: السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ. فَقَالَ: "وَعَلَيْكَ السَّلَامُ وَرَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ". ثُمَّ أَتَى آخر فَقَالَ: السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَرَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ. فَقَالَ لَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "وَعَلَيْكَ السَّلَامُ وَرَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ وَبَرَكَاتُهُ". ثُمَّ جَاءَ آخَرُ فَقَالَ: السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَرَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ وَبَرَكَاتُهُ فَقَالَ لَهُ: "وَعَلَيْكَ" فَقَالَ لَهُ الرَّجُلُ: يَا نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ، بِأَبِي أَنْتَ وَأُمِّي، أَتَاكَ فُلَانٌ وَفُلَانٌ فَسَلَّمَا عَلَيْكَ فَرَدَدْتَ عَلَيْهِمَا أَكْثَرَ مِمَّا رَدَدْتَ عَلَيَّ. فَقَالَ: "إِنَّكَ لَمْ تَدَعْ لَنَا شَيْئًا، قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَإِذَا حُيِّيتُمْ بِتَحِيَّةٍ فَحَيُّوا بِأَحْسَنَ مِنْهَا أَوْ رُدُّوهَا﴾ فَرَدَدْنَاهَا عَلَيْكَ".

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ مُعَلَّقًا فَقَالَ: ذُكِرَ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ التِّرْمِذِيِّ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ السَّرِيِّ -أَبُو مُحَمَّدٍ الْأَنْطَاكِيُّ -قَالَ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ: وَكَانَ رَجُلًا صَالِحًا -حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامُ بْنُ لَاحِقٍ، فَذَكَرَ بِإِسْنَادِهِ مِثْلَهُ.

وَرَوَاهُ أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْبَاقِي بْنُ قَانِعٍ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ حَنْبَلٍ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامُ بْنُ لَاحِقٍ أَبُو عُثْمَانَ، فَذَكَرَهُ بِمِثْلِهِ، وَلَمْ أَرَهُ فِي الْمُسْنَدِ وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَفِي هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ دَلَالَةٌ عَلَى أَنَّهُ لَا زِيَادَةَ فِي السَّلَامِ عَلَى هَذِهِ الصِّفَةِ: السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ وَبَرَكَاتُهُ، إِذْ لَوْ شُرِعَ أَكْثَرُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ، لَزَادَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ.

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ كَثِيرٍ -أَخُو سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ كَثِيرٍ -حَدَّثَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ عَوْفٍ، عَنْ أَبِي رَجَاءٍ العُطَاردي، عَنْ عِمْرَانَ بْنِ حُصَين؛ أَنَّ رَجُلًا جَاءَ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ﷺ فَقَالَ: السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ فَرَدَّ عَلَيْهِ ثُمَّ جَلَسَ، فَقَالَ: "عَشْرٌ". ثُمَّ جَاءَ آخَرُ فَقَالَ: "السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ. فَرَدَّ عَلَيْهِ، ثُمَّ جَلَسَ، فَقَالَ: "عِشْرُونَ". ثُمَّ جَاءَ آخَرُ فَقَالَ: السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَةُ اللَّهِ وَبَرَكَاتُهُ. فَرَدَّ عَلَيْهِ، ثُمَّ جَلَسَ، فَقَالَ: "ثَلَاثُونَ".

وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ كَثِيرٍ، وَأَخْرَجَهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ وَالنَّسَائِيُّ وَالْبَزَّارُ مِنْ حَدِيثِهِ، ثُمَّ قَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: حَسَنٌ غَرِيبٌ مِنْ هَذَا الْوَجْهِ، وَفِي الْبَابِ عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ وَعَلِيٍّ وَسَهْلِ بْنِ حُنَيف [رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ] .

وَقَالَ البزَّار: قَدْ رُوِيَ هَذَا عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ مِنْ وُجُوهٍ، هَذَا أَحْسَنُهَا إِسْنَادًا وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ حَرْبٍ الْمَوْصِلِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرُّؤَاسِيُّ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ سِمَاك، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: مَنْ يُسَلِّمْ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ خَلْقِ اللَّهِ، فَارْدُدْ عَلَيْهِ وَإِنْ كَانَ مَجُوسِيًّا؛ ذَلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللَّهَ يَقُولُ: ﴿فَحَيُّوا بِأَحْسَنَ مِنْهَا أَوْ رُدُّوهَا﴾

وَقَالَ قَتَادَةُ: ﴿فَحَيُّوا بِأَحْسَنَ مِنْهَا﴾ يَعْنِي: لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ ﴿أَوْ رُدُّوهَا﴾ يَعْنِي: لِأَهْلِ الذِّمَّةِ.

وَهَذَا التَّنْزِيلُ فِيهِ نَظَرٌ، بَلْ كَمَا تَقَدَّمَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ مِنْ أَنَّ الْمُرَادَ أَنْ يَرُدَّ بِأَحْسَنِ مِمَّا حَيَّاهُ به، فإن بلغ الْمُسْلِمُ غَايَةَ مَا شُرِعَ فِي السَّلَامِ؛ رُدَّ عَلَيْهِ مِثْلُ مَا قَالَ، فَأَمَّا أَهْلُ الذِّمَّةِ فلا يُبْدؤون بِالسَّلَامِ وَلَا يُزَادُونَ، بَلْ يُرَدُّ عَلَيْهِمْ بِمَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى الله عليه وسلم قال: "إِذَا سَلَّمَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْيَهُودُ فَإِنَّمَا يَقُولُ أَحَدُهُمْ: السَّامُّ عَلَيْكَ فَقُلْ: وَعَلَيْكَ" .

وَفِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٌ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى الله عليه وسلم قال: "لا تبدؤوا الْيَهُودَ وَالنَّصَارَى بِالسَّلَامِ، وَإِذَا لَقِيتُمُوهُمْ فِي طَرِيقٍ فَاضْطَرُّوهُمْ إِلَى أَضْيَقِهِ" .

وَقَالَ سُفْيَانُ الثَّوْرِيُّ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ الْبَصْرِيِّ قَالَ: السَّلَامُ تَطَوُّعٌ، وَالرَّدُّ فَرِيضَةٌ.

وَهَذَا الَّذِي قَالَهُ هُوَ قَوْلُ الْعُلَمَاءِ قَاطِبَةً: أَنَّ الرَّدَّ وَاجِبٌ عَلَى مَنْ سُلِّمَ عَلَيْهِ، فَيَأْثَمُ إِنْ لَمْ يَفْعَلْ؛ لِأَنَّهُ خَالَفَ أَمْرَ اللَّهِ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَحَيُّوا بِأَحْسَنَ مِنْهَا أَوْ رُدُّوهَا﴾ وَقَدْ جَاءَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ الَّذِي رَوَاهُ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلا هُوَ﴾ إِخْبَارٌ بِتَوْحِيدِهِ وَتَفَرُّدِهِ بِالْإِلَهِيَّةِ لِجَمِيعِ الْمَخْلُوقَاتِ، وتضمَّن قَسَمًا، لِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿لَيَجْمَعَنَّكُمْ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ﴾ وَهَذِهِ اللَّامُ مُوَطِّئَةٌ لِلْقَسَمِ، فَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلا هُوَ﴾ خَبَرٌ وقَسَم أَنَّهُ سَيَجْمَعُ الْأَوَّلِينَ وَالْآخَرِينَ فِي صَعِيدٍ وَاحِدٍ فَيُجَازِي كُلَّ عَامِلٍ بِعَمَلِهِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَنْ أَصْدَقُ مِنَ اللَّهِ حَدِيثًا﴾ أَيْ: لَا أَحَدَ أَصْدَقُ مِنْهُ فِي حَدِيثِهِ وَخَبَرِهِ، وَوَعْدِهِ وَوَعِيدِهِ، فَلَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ، وَلَا رَبَّ سِوَاهُ.

---

## 4:88-91 — verses 88-91 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Exalted says, censuring the believers for their disagreement over the hypocrites, in which they held two opinions. There is also disagreement over the cause of that.

Imam Ahmad said: Bahz reported to us, Shu'bah reported to us, 'Adi ibn Thabit said: 'Abdullah ibn Yazid informed me, from Zayd ibn Thabit: that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ went out to Uhud, and some of those who had gone out with him turned back. So the companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ were divided into two groups concerning them: one group said, "Let us kill them," and another group said, "No." So Allah revealed: {What is the matter with you, that you are two parties concerning the hypocrites?} And the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "It (Madinah) is Tayyibah, and it expels evil just as fire expels the impurity of silver."

Both al-Bukhari and Muslim narrated it in the two Sahihs, from the hadith of Shu'bah.

Muhammad ibn Ishaq ibn Yasar mentioned, in the account of the Battle of Uhud, that 'Abdullah ibn Ubayy ibn Salul turned back that day with a third of the army, returning with three hundred men, while the Prophet ﷺ remained with seven hundred.

Al-'Awfi said, from Ibn 'Abbas: it was revealed concerning a group of people who were in Makkah, who had spoken words of Islam but were supporting the polytheists. They went out from Makkah seeking some errand of theirs, and said, "If we meet the companions of Muhammad, we will be safe from them." When the believers were informed that these people had left Makkah, one group of the believers said, "Ride out to these cowards and kill them, for they are helping your enemy against you." Another group of the believers said, "Glory be to Allah!" or words to that effect, "Would you kill people who have spoken the same words that you have spoken? Is it because they did not emigrate and did not leave their homes that their blood and wealth should be made lawful?" So they remained thus, two parties, while the Messenger, being among them, did not forbid either party from anything. So Allah revealed: {What is the matter with you, that you are two parties concerning the hypocrites?}

Ibn Abi Hatim narrated it. Something close to this has also been reported from Abu Salamah ibn 'Abd al-Rahman, 'Ikrimah, Mujahid, al-Dahhak, and others.

Zayd ibn Aslam said, from a son of Sa'd ibn Mu'adh: that it was revealed concerning the mutual argument of the Aws and the Khazraj over the matter of 'Abdullah ibn Ubayy, when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sought an excuse regarding him from the pulpit, in the affair of the slander (al-ifk).

This is gharib (a singular, isolated report), and other things have also been said.

His saying, {And Allah has thrust them back for what they earned}, means: turned them back and cast them into error.

Ibn 'Abbas said: {arkasahum} means, cast them down. Qatadah said: destroyed them. Al-Suddi said: led them astray.

His saying, {for what they earned}, means: because of their disobedience, their opposition to the Messenger, and their following of falsehood.

{Do you wish to guide one whom Allah has led astray? And for one whom Allah leads astray, you will never find a way}, meaning: there is no path for him to guidance, and no escape for him toward it.

Then He said: {They wish that you would disbelieve as they have disbelieved, so that you would be alike}, meaning: they wish error upon you so that you and they become equal in it. That is only because of the intensity of their enmity and hatred toward you. Therefore He said: {So do not take from among them any allies until they emigrate in the way of Allah. But if they turn away}, meaning: abandon emigration, as al-'Awfi said from Ibn 'Abbas. Al-Suddi said: display their disbelief openly. {then seize them and kill them wherever you find them, and do not take from among them any ally or helper}, meaning: do not ally with them nor seek their help against enemies as long as their condition remains so.

Then Allah, glorified is He, made an exception from among these and said: {except those who join a people between whom and you there is a treaty}, meaning: except those who take refuge with, and attach themselves to, a people between whom and you there is a truce or a covenant of protection; treat them according to the same ruling as that people. This is the view of al-Suddi, Ibn Zayd, and Ibn Jarir.

Ibn Abi Hatim narrated: my father reported to us, Abu Salamah reported to us, Hammad ibn Salamah reported to us, from 'Ali ibn Zayd ibn Jud'an, from al-Hasan: that Suraqah ibn Malik al-Mudliji told them, saying: When he, meaning the Prophet ﷺ, prevailed over the people of Badr and Uhud, and those around them accepted Islam, Suraqah said: it reached me that he intended to send Khalid ibn al-Walid against my people, the Banu Mudlij. So I came to him and said, "I appeal to you by the favor (you owe)." They said, "Be quiet!" But the Prophet ﷺ said, "Leave him. What do you want?" He said, "It has reached me that you intend to send someone against my people, and I want you to make peace with them. If your people embrace Islam, they will embrace it and enter into Islam, and if they do not embrace Islam, the hearts of your people will not harden against them." So the Messenger of Allah ﷺ took the hand of Khalid ibn al-Walid and said, "Go with him and do what he wants." So Khalid made peace with them on condition that they would not help anyone against the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and that if Quraysh embraced Islam, they would embrace it with them, [and whoever attached himself to them from among the people would be under the same covenant as theirs]. So Allah revealed: {They wish that you would disbelieve as they have disbelieved, so that you would be alike. So do not take from among them any allies}

Ibn Mardawayh also narrated it through the route of Hammad ibn Salamah, and said: so Allah revealed: {except those who join a people between whom and you there is a treaty}, so that whoever attached himself to them would be with them under their covenant. This is more fitting to the flow of the discourse.

In Sahih al-Bukhari, in the account of the treaty of al-Hudaybiyah: whoever wished to enter into the treaty and covenant of Quraysh could do so, and whoever wished to enter into the treaty and covenant of Muhammad and his companions could do so.

It has also been reported from Ibn 'Abbas that he said: this was abrogated by His saying: {Then when the sacred months have passed, kill the polytheists [wherever you find them]} [al-Tawbah: 5].

His saying, {Or come to you, their hearts restrained from} {fighting you or fighting their own people}, this refers to another group among those excepted from the command to fight them, namely those who come to the battle lines with their hearts constricted, meaning, straitened, disliking to fight you, yet also finding it not easy to fight their own people alongside you. Rather they are neither with you nor against you. {And if Allah had willed, He could have given them power over you, and they would have fought you}, meaning: it was out of His kindness toward you that He restrained them from you. {So if they withdraw from you and do not fight you and offer you peace}, meaning, a peaceful settlement, {then Allah has not made for you a way against them}, meaning: you have no right to kill them as long as their condition remains so. These are like the group who went out on the day of Badr from the Banu Hashim along with the polytheists, and were present at the fighting while being unwilling, such as al-'Abbas and others like him. For this reason the Prophet ﷺ forbade the killing of al-'Abbas that day, and referred to it in terms of his being taken captive.

His saying, {You will find others who wish to be safe from you and safe from their own people} {every time they are returned to temptation, they are plunged into it}, these, in outward appearance, are like those who preceded them, but their intention differs from the intention of the former. For these are hypocrites who show Islam outwardly to the Prophet ﷺ and his companions, so that they may thereby be safe among them regarding their blood, their wealth, and their offspring, while inwardly conciliating the disbelievers, worshipping along with them what they worship, so as to be safe among them as well. Inwardly, however, they are with the disbelievers, just as Allah the Exalted says: {And when they are alone with their devils, they say, "Indeed we are with you," [we were only mocking]} [al-Baqarah: 14]. And He says here: {Every time they are returned to temptation, they are plunged into it}, meaning: they become immersed in it.

Al-Suddi said: temptation (al-fitnah) here means idolatry. Ibn Jarir related from Mujahid: that it was revealed concerning a group of the people of Makkah, who used to come to the Prophet ﷺ and offer greetings out of ostentation, then would return to Quraysh and plunge back into idol worship, seeking thereby to be safe on both sides. So he was commanded to fight them if they did not withdraw and reform themselves. Therefore the Exalted says: {But if they do not withdraw from you and do not offer you peace and restrain their hands}, meaning: from fighting, {then seize them and kill them wherever you come upon them}, meaning: wherever you encounter them, {and those, We have made for you against them a clear authority}, meaning: manifest and evident.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى مُنْكِرًا عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فِي اخْتِلَافِهِمْ فِي الْمُنَافِقِينَ عَلَى قَوْلَيْنِ، وَاخْتُلِفَ فِي سَبَبِ ذَلِكَ، فقال الإمام أحمد: حَدَّثَنَا بَهْز، حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، قَالَ عَدِيُّ بْنُ ثَابِتٍ: أَخْبَرَنِي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ زَيْدِ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ خَرَجَ إِلَى أُحُد، فَرَجَعَ نَاسٌ خَرَجُوا مَعَهُ، فَكَانَ أَصْحَابُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ فِيهِمْ فِرْقَتَيْنِ: فِرْقَةٌ تَقُولُ: نَقْتُلُهُمْ. وَفِرْقَةٌ تَقُولُ: لَا فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿فَمَا لَكُمْ فِي الْمُنَافِقِينَ فِئَتَيْنِ﴾ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إِنَّهَا طَيْبة، وَإِنَّهَا تَنْفِي الخَبَث كَمَا تَنْفِي النَّارُ خَبَثَ الْفِضَّةِ".

أَخْرَجَاهُ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ شُعْبَةَ .

وَقَدْ ذَكَرَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ يَسار فِي وَقْعَةِ أُحُدٍ أَنَّ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ أُبَيِّ بْنِ سَلُولٍ رَجَعَ يَوْمَئِذٍ بِثُلُثِ الْجَيْشِ، رَجَعَ بِثَلَاثِمِائَةٍ وَبَقِيَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ فِي سَبْعِمِائَةٍ.

وَقَالَ الْعَوْفِيُّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: نَزَلَتْ فِي قَوْمٍ كَانُوا بِمَكَّةَ، قَدْ تَكَلَّمُوا بِالْإِسْلَامِ، كَانُوا يُظَاهِرُونَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ، فَخَرَجُوا مِنْ مَكَّةَ يَطْلُبُونَ حَاجَةً لَهُمْ، فَقَالُوا: إِنْ لَقِينَا أَصْحَابَ مُحَمَّدٍ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْنَا مِنْهُمْ بَأْسٌ، وَأَنَّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ لَمَّا أُخْبِرُوا أَنَّهُمْ قَدْ خَرَجُوا مِنْ مَكَّةَ، قَالَتْ فِئَةٌ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ: ارْكَبُوا إِلَى الْجُبَنَاءِ فَاقْتُلُوهُمْ، فَإِنَّهُمْ يُظَاهِرُونَ عَلَيْكُمْ عَدُوَّكُمْ. وَقَالَتْ فِئَةٌ أُخْرَى مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ! أَوْ كَمَا قَالُوا: أَتَقْتُلُونَ قَوْمًا قَدْ تَكَلَّمُوا بِمِثْلِ مَا تَكَلَّمْتُمْ بِهِ؟ أمِنْ أَجْلِ أَنَّهُمْ لَمْ يُهَاجِرُوا وَلَمْ يَتْرُكُوا دِيَارَهُمْ تُسْتَحَلُّ دِمَاؤُهُمْ وَأَمْوَالُهُمْ. فَكَانُوا كَذَلِكَ فِئَتَيْنِ، وَالرَّسُولُ عِنْدَهُمْ لَا يَنْهَى وَاحِدًا مِنَ الْفَرِيقَيْنِ عَنْ شَيْءٍ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿فَمَا لَكُمْ فِي الْمُنَافِقِينَ فِئَتَيْنِ﴾

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ، وَقَدْ رُوي عَنْ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ وَعِكْرِمَةَ وَمُجَاهِدٍ وَالضَّحَّاكِ وَغَيْرِهِمْ قَرِيبٌ مِنْ هَذَا.

وَقَالَ زَيْدُ بْنُ أَسْلَمَ، عَنِ ابنٍ لِسَعْدِ بْنِ مُعَاذٍ: أَنَّهَا نَزَلَتْ فِي تَقَاوُلِ الْأَوْسِ وَالْخَزْرَجِ فِي شَأْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أُبَيٍّ، حِينَ اسْتَعْذَرَ مِنْهُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ فِي قَضِيَّةِ الْإِفْكِ.

وَهَذَا غَرِيبٌ، وَقِيلَ غَيْرُ ذَلِكَ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَاللَّهُ أَرْكَسَهُمْ بِمَا كَسَبُوا﴾ أَيْ: رَدَّهُمْ وَأَوْقَعَهُمْ فِي الْخَطَأِ.

قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿أَرْكَسَهُمْ﴾ أَيْ: أَوْقَعَهُمْ. وَقَالَ قَتَادَةُ: أَهْلَكَهُمْ. وَقَالَ السُّدِّيُّ: أَضَلَّهُمْ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿بِمَا كَسَبُوا﴾ أَيْ: بِسَبَبِ عِصْيَانِهِمْ وَمُخَالَفَتِهِمُ الرَّسُولَ وَاتِّبَاعِهِمُ الْبَاطِلَ.

﴿أَتُرِيدُونَ أَنْ تَهْدُوا مَنْ أَضَلَّ اللَّهُ وَمَنْ يُضْلِلِ اللَّهُ فَلَنْ تَجِدَ لَهُ سَبِيلا﴾ أَيْ: لَا طَرِيقَ لَهُ إِلَى الْهُدَى وَلَا مَخْلَصَ لَهُ إِلَيْهِ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿وَدُّوا لَوْ تَكْفُرُونَ كَمَا كَفَرُوا فَتَكُونُونَ سَوَاءً﴾ أَيْ: هُمْ يَوَدُّونَ لَكُمُ الضَّلَالَةَ لِتَسْتَوُوا أَنْتُمْ وَإِيَّاهُمْ فِيهَا، وَمَا ذَاكَ إِلَّا لِشِدَّةِ عَدَاوَتِهِمْ وَبُغْضِهِمْ لَكُمْ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿فَلا تَتَّخِذُوا مِنْهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءَ حَتَّى يُهَاجِرُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا﴾ أَيْ: تَرَكُوا الْهِجْرَةَ، قَالَهُ الْعَوْفِيُّ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ. وَقَالَ السُّدِّيُّ: أَظْهَرُوا كُفْرَهُمْ ﴿فَخُذُوهُمْ وَاقْتُلُوهُمْ حَيْثُ وَجَدْتُمُوهُمْ وَلا تَتَّخِذُوا مِنْهُمْ وَلِيًّا وَلا نَصِيرًا﴾ أي: لا توالوهم وَلَا تَسْتَنْصِرُوا بِهِمْ عَلَى الْأَعْدَاءِ مَا دَامُوا كَذَلِكَ.

ثُمَّ اسْتَثْنَى اللَّهُ، سُبْحَانَهُ مِنْ هَؤُلَاءِ فَقَالَ: ﴿إِلا الَّذِينَ يَصِلُونَ إِلَى قَوْمٍ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ مِيثَاقٌ﴾ أي: إلا الذين لجؤوا وَتَحَيَّزُوا إِلَى قَوْمٍ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ مُهَادَنَةٌ أَوْ عَقْدُ ذِمَّةٍ، فَاجْعَلُوا حُكْمَهُمْ كَحُكْمِهِمْ. وَهَذَا قَوْلُ السُّدِّيِّ، وَابْنِ زَيْدٍ، وَابْنِ جَرِيرٍ.

وَقَدْ رَوَى ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَلَمَةَ حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ جُدْعان، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ: أَنَّ سُرَاقَةَ بْنَ مَالِكٍ الْمُدْلِجِيَّ حَدَّثَهُمْ قَالَ: لَمَّا ظهر -يعني النبي ﷺ -عَلَى أَهْلِ بَدْرٍ وأُحُد، وَأَسْلَمَ مَنْ حَوْلَهُمْ قَالَ سُرَاقَةُ: بَلَغَنِي أَنَّهُ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَبْعَثَ خَالِدَ بْنَ الْوَلِيدِ إِلَى قَوْمِي -بَنِي مُدْلج -فَأَتَيْتُهُ فَقُلْتُ: أَنْشُدُك النِّعْمَةَ. فَقَالُوا: صَهٍ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "دَعُوهُ، مَا تُرِيدُ؟ ". قَالَ: بَلَغَنِي أَنَّكَ تُرِيدُ أَنْ تَبْعَثَ إِلَى قَوْمِي، وَأَنَا أُرِيدُ أَنَّ تُوَادِعَهُمْ، فَإِنْ أَسْلَمَ قَوْمُكَ أَسْلَمُوا وَدَخَلُوا فِي الْإِسْلَامِ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يُسْلِمُوا لَمْ تَخْشُن قُلُوبُ قَوْمِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ. فَأَخَذَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ بِيَدِ خَالِدِ بْنِ الْوَلِيدِ فَقَالَ: "اذْهَبْ مَعَهُ فَافْعَلْ مَا يُرِيدُ". فَصَالَحَهُمْ خَالِدٌ عَلَى أَلَّا يُعِينُوا عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ وَإِنْ أَسْلَمَتْ قُرَيْشٌ أَسْلَمُوا مَعَهُمْ، [وَمَنْ وَصَلَ إِلَيْهِمْ مِنَ النَّاسِ كَانُوا عَلَى مِثْلِ عَهْدِهِمْ] فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿وَدُّوا لَوْ تَكْفُرُونَ كَمَا كَفَرُوا فَتَكُونُونَ سَوَاءً فَلا تَتَّخِذُوا مِنْهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءَ﴾

وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ مِنْ طَرِيقِ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، وَقَالَ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿إِلا الَّذِينَ يَصِلُونَ إِلَى قَوْمٍ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَهُمْ مِيثَاقٌ﴾ فكان من وَصَلَ إِلَيْهِمْ كَانُوا مَعَهُمْ عَلَى عَهْدِهِمْ وَهَذَا أَنْسَبُ لِسِيَاقِ الْكَلَامِ.

وَفِي صَحِيحِ الْبُخَارِيِّ فِي قِصَّةِ صُلْحِ الْحُدَيْبِيَةِ فَكَانَ مَنْ أَحَبَّ أَنْ يَدْخُلَ فِي صُلْحِ قُرَيْشٍ وَعَهْدِهِمْ، وَمَنْ أَحَبَّ أَنْ يَدْخُلَ فِي صُلْحِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَأَصْحَابِهِ وَعَهْدِهِمْ.

وَقَدْ رُوِيَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: نَسَخَهَا قَوْلُهُ: ﴿فَإِذَا انْسَلَخَ الأشْهُرُ الْحُرُمُ فَاقْتُلُوا الْمُشْرِكِينَ [حَيْثُ وَجَدْتُمُوهُمْ] ﴾ [التَّوْبَةِ: ٥] .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿أَوْ جَاءُوكُمْ حَصِرَتْ صُدُورُهُمْ [أَنْ يُقَاتِلُوكُمْ أَوْ يُقَاتِلُوا قَوْمَهُمْ] ﴾ الْآيَةَ، هَؤُلَاءِ قَوْمٌ آخَرُونَ مِنَ المُسْتَثنَين عَنِ الْأَمْرِ بِقِتَالِهِمْ، وَهُمُ الَّذِينَ يَجِيئُونَ إِلَى الْمَصَافِّ وَهُمْ حَصِرَةٌ صُدُورُهُمْ أَيْ: ضَيِّقَةٌ صُدُورُهُمْ مُبْغضين أَنْ يُقَاتِلُوكُمْ، وَلَا يَهُونُ عَلَيْهِمْ أَيْضًا أَنْ يُقَاتِلُوا قَوْمَهُمْ مَعَكُمْ، بَلْ هُمْ لَا لَكُمْ وَلَا عَلَيْكُمْ. ﴿وَلَوْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ لَسَلَّطَهُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ فَلَقَاتَلُوكُمْ﴾ أَيْ: مِنْ لُطْفِهِ بِكُمْ أَنْ كَفَّهُمْ عَنْكُمْ ﴿فَإِنِ اعْتَزَلُوكُمْ فَلَمْ يُقَاتِلُوكُمْ وَأَلْقَوْا إِلَيْكُمُ السَّلَمَ﴾ أَيِ: الْمُسَالَمَةَ ﴿فَمَا جَعَلَ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ سَبِيلا﴾ أَيْ: فَلَيْسَ لَكُمْ أَنْ تَقْتُلُوهُمْ، مَا دَامَتْ حَالُهُمْ كَذَلِكَ، وَهَؤُلَاءِ كَالْجَمَاعَةِ الَّذِينَ خَرَجُوا يَوْمَ بِدْرٍ مِنْ بَنِي هَاشِمٍ مَعَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ، فَحَضَرُوا الْقِتَالَ وَهُمْ كَارِهُونَ، كَالْعَبَّاسِ وَنَحْوِهِ، وَلِهَذَا نَهَى النَّبِيُّ ﷺ يومئذ عن قتل العباس وعبّر بأسره.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿سَتَجِدُونَ آخَرِينَ يُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يَأْمَنُوكُمْ وَيَأْمَنُوا قَوْمَهُمْ [كُلَّمَا رُدُّوا إِلَى الْفِتْنَةِ أُرْكِسُوا فِيهَا] ﴾ الْآيَةَ، هَؤُلَاءِ فِي الصُّورَةِ الظَّاهِرَةِ كَمَنْ تَقَدَّمَهُمْ، وَلَكِنْ نِيَّةُ هَؤُلَاءِ غَيْرُ نِيَّةِ أُولَئِكَ، فَإِنَّ هَؤُلَاءِ مُنَافِقُونَ يُظْهِرُونَ لِلنَّبِيِّ ﷺ وَلِأَصْحَابِهِ الْإِسْلَامَ؛ لِيَأْمَنُوا بِذَلِكَ عِنْدَهُمْ عَلَى دِمَائِهِمْ وَأَمْوَالِهِمْ وَذَرَارِيهِمْ وَيُصَانِعُونَ الْكَفَّارَ فِي الْبَاطِنِ، فَيَعْبُدُونَ مَعَهُمْ مَا يَعْبُدُونَ، لِيَأْمَنُوا بِذَلِكَ عِنْدَهُمْ، وَهُمْ فِي الْبَاطِنِ مَعَ أُولَئِكَ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَإِذَا خَلَوْا إِلَى شَيَاطِينِهِمْ قَالُوا إِنَّا مَعَكُمْ [إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ مُسْتَهْزِئُونَ] ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ١٤] وَقَالَ هَاهُنَا: ﴿كُلَّمَا رُدُّوا إِلَى الْفِتْنَةِ أُرْكِسُوا فِيهَا﴾ أَيْ: انْهَمَكُوا فِيهَا.

وَقَالَ السُّدِّيُّ: الْفِتْنَةُ هَاهُنَا: الشِّرْكُ. وَحَكَى ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ: أَنَّهَا نَزَلَتْ فِي قَوْمٍ مِنْ أَهْلِ مَكَّةَ، كَانُوا يَأْتُونَ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ فَيُسَلِّمُونَ رِيَاءً، ثُمَّ يَرْجِعُونَ إِلَى قُرَيْشٍ فَيَرْتَكِسُونَ فِي الْأَوْثَانِ، يَبْتَغُونَ بِذَلِكَ أَنْ يَأْمَنُوا هَاهُنَا وَهَاهُنَا، فَأَمَرَ بِقِتَالِهِمْ إِنْ لَمْ يَعْتَزِلُوا وَيُصْلِحُوا؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَإِنْ لَمْ يَعْتَزِلُوكُمْ وَيُلْقُوا إِلَيْكُمُ السَّلَمَ وَيَكُفُّوا أَيْدِيَهُمْ﴾ أَيْ: عَنِ الْقِتَالِ ﴿فَخُذُوهُمْ وَاقْتُلُوهُمْ حَيْثُ ثَقِفْتُمُوهُمْ﴾ أَيْ: أَيْنَ لَقِيتُمُوهُمْ ﴿وَأُولَئِكُمْ جَعَلْنَا لَكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطَانًا مُبِينًا﴾ أَيْ: بيِّنا وَاضِحًا.

---

## 4:92-93 — verses 92-93 (shared commentary)

### Translation

He, exalted, says: it is not permissible for a believer to kill his believing brother by any manner of means, as is established in the two Sahihs, from Ibn Mas'ud, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The blood of a Muslim man who testifies that there is no god but Allah and that I am the Messenger of Allah is not lawful except in one of three cases: a life for a life, the married person who commits adultery, and the one who abandons his religion and separates from the community."

Then, if one of these three occurs, it is not for any individual among the common people to kill him; that is a matter reserved for the ruler or his deputy.

Regarding His saying, {except by mistake}: they said this is a disconnected exception (istithna munqati'), like the poet's saying:

"From the fair-skinned women, she did not travel far, nor did she tread upon the ground except in a trailing embroidered garment."

There are many parallel examples of this.

There is disagreement about the occasion of revelation of this [verse]. Mujahid and more than one other said: it was revealed concerning Ayyash ibn Abi Rabi'ah, the maternal half-brother of Abu Jahl (their mother being Asma bint Mukharribah). This was because he killed a man who had been torturing him along with his brother on account of Islam, namely al-Harith ibn Yazid al-Amiri. Ayyash harbored ill intent toward him. That man then embraced Islam and emigrated, without Ayyash's knowledge. When the day of the Conquest came, Ayyash saw him and thought he was still upon his former religion, so he attacked him and killed him. Then Allah revealed this verse.

Abd al-Rahman ibn Zayd ibn Aslam said: it was revealed concerning Abu al-Darda', because he killed a man who had spoken the word of Islam just as the sword was raised against him. When he brought the sword down upon him, the man spoke his word of testimony. When this was mentioned to the Prophet ﷺ, it was said, "He only said it seeking protection." The Prophet ﷺ said to him: "Did you split open his heart?" [This story is found in the Sahih concerning someone other than Abu al-Darda'.]

Regarding His saying, {And whoever kills a believer by mistake, then the freeing of a believing slave and blood money handed over to his family, [unless they remit it as charity]}: these are two obligations in the case of killing by mistake. The first is the expiation (kaffarah) for the grave sin he committed, even though it was unintentional. Among its conditions is that it must be the freeing of a believing slave; a non-believing slave does not suffice.

Ibn Jarir related from Ibn Abbas, al-Sha'bi, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, and al-Hasan al-Basri that they said: a young child does not suffice unless he is capable of intending faith. It is related via Abd al-Razzaq, from Ma'mar, from Qatadah, who said: in the reading (harf) of Ubayy, {then the freeing of a believing slave}, a small child does not suffice for it.

Ibn Jarir preferred the view that if the slave was born between two Muslim parents, it suffices; otherwise it does not. What the majority hold is that whenever the slave is a Muslim, freeing him is valid for the expiation, whether he is young or old.

Imam Ahmad said: Abd al-Razzaq informed us, Ma'mar informed us, from al-Zuhri, from Abdullah ibn Abdullah, from a man of the Ansar, that he brought a black slave woman and said: O Messenger of Allah, I owe the freeing of a believing slave; if you consider this woman to be a believer, I will free her. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to her: "Do you testify that there is no god but Allah?" She said: Yes. He said: "Do you testify that I am the Messenger of Allah?" She said: Yes. He said: "Do you believe in resurrection after death?" She said: Yes. He said: "Free her."

This is a sound chain (isnad sahih), and the anonymity of the Companion does no harm.

In the Muwatta of [Imam] Malik, the Musnads of al-Shafi'i and Ahmad, the Sahih of Muslim, and the Sunans of Abu Dawud and al-Nasa'i, via Hilal ibn Abi Maymunah, from Ata' ibn Yasar, from Mu'awiyah ibn al-Hakam, that when he brought that black slave girl, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to her: "Where is Allah?" She said: In the heaven. He said: "Who am I?" She said: You are the Messenger of Allah, may Allah bless him and grant him peace. He said: "Free her, for she is a believer."

Regarding His saying, {and blood money handed over to his family}: this is the second obligation, between the killer and the family of the slain, as compensation for what they have lost of their relative. This blood money (diyah) is obligatory in fifths, as reported by Imam Ahmad and the authors of the Sunan, from the hadith of al-Hajjaj ibn Artah, from Zayd ibn Jubayr, from Khashf ibn Malik, from Ibn Mas'ud, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ ruled, regarding the blood money for killing by mistake, twenty female camels in their first year of pregnancy, twenty male camels of the same age, twenty female camels in their second year, twenty four-year-old camels, and twenty three-year-old camels.

This is the wording of al-Nasa'i. Al-Tirmidhi said: We do not know it as a marfu' (elevated) report except by this route, and it has been related from Abdullah as mawquf (halted report).

It is likewise related from [Ali and] a group of others.

It is also said that it is obligatory in fourths. This blood money is obligatory upon the killer's 'aqilah (paternal kin group liable for blood money), not from his own wealth. Al-Shafi'i, may Allah have mercy on him, said: I do not know of anyone who disputes that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ ruled that the blood money is upon the 'aqilah, and this is more established than the hadith concerning specific individual liability.

What he, may Allah have mercy on him, pointed to is established in more than one hadith. Among them is what is established in the two Sahihs, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: Two women of Hudhayl fought, and one of them threw a stone at the other, killing her and the fetus in her womb. They brought their dispute to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he ruled that the blood money for her fetus was a ghurrah (a male or female slave), and he ruled that the blood money for the woman was upon her 'aqilah.

This indicates that the ruling for "semi-intentional killing" ('amd al-khata) follows the ruling for pure mistake as regards the obligation of blood money, except that in this case the blood money is obligatory in thirds, as with intentional killing, due to its resemblance to it.

In the Sahih of al-Bukhari, from Abdullah ibn Umar, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent Khalid ibn al-Walid to Banu Judhaymah, and he called them to Islam, but they were not able to say properly "We have submitted (aslamna)." Instead they kept saying "We have turned away (saba'na), we have turned away." So Khalid began killing them. When this reached the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he raised his hands and said: "O Allah, I disavow before You what Khalid has done." He sent Ali, who paid blood money for their slain and compensation for what had been destroyed of their property, even down to the drinking vessel of a dog.

From this [hadith] it is derived that when the ruler or his deputy errs, it is borne by the public treasury.

Regarding His saying, {unless they give it as charity}: meaning, the blood money is obligatory, to be handed over to his family, unless they give it away as charity, in which case it is no longer obligatory.

Regarding His saying, {But if he was of a people at war with you and he was a believer, then the freeing of a believing slave}: meaning, if the slain person was a believer, but his kinsmen are among the disbelievers who are people at war, then there is no blood money owed to them, and the killer is obligated only to free a believing slave, nothing else.

Regarding His saying, {And if he was of a people between whom and you there is a covenant, [then blood money handed over to his family and the freeing of a believing slave]}, the verse: meaning, if the slain person's kinsmen are people of a covenant of protection (dhimmah) or a truce, then they are entitled to the blood money for their slain relative. If he was a believer, then it is the full blood money; likewise if he was a disbeliever, according to a group of scholars. It is also said that the blood money obligatory for a disbeliever is half that of a Muslim, and it is also said a third of it, as is detailed in [the Book of Rulings]. The killer is also obligated to free a believing slave.

{And whoever does not find [a slave to free], then the fasting of two consecutive months}: meaning, there is to be no breaking of the fast in between; rather, he fasts continuously to the end of the two months. If he breaks the fast without excuse, such as illness, menstruation, or postpartum bleeding, he must begin again. They differed regarding travel: does it interrupt the continuity or not? There are two views.

Regarding His saying, {as a repentance from Allah, and Allah is Ever Knowing, Wise}: meaning, this is the repentance of one who kills by mistake, if he cannot find a slave to free, he fasts two consecutive months.

They differed regarding one who is unable to fast: is he obligated to feed sixty poor persons, as in the expiation for zihar (a form of unlawful divorce pronouncement)? There are two views. One: yes, as is textually stated in the expiation for zihar, and it was not mentioned here only because this passage is a context of threat, warning, and caution, so it would not be fitting to mention feeding here, given the ease and concession it implies. The second view: one does not resort to feeding, because if it were obligatory, its clarification would not have been delayed past the time of need.

{And Allah is Ever Knowing, Wise}: its interpretation has already preceded more than once.

Then, having clarified the ruling on killing by mistake, He, exalted, proceeded to clarify the ruling on intentional killing, saying: {And whoever kills a believer intentionally, [his recompense is Hell, abiding therein forever, and Allah's wrath is upon him, and He has cursed him, and has prepared for him a tremendous punishment]}. This is a severe threat and firm warning against whoever commits this grave sin, which is coupled with associating partners with Allah in more than one verse in the Book of Allah, as He, glorified is He, says in Surat al-Furqan: {And those who do not invoke besides Allah another deity, nor kill the soul which Allah has forbidden except by right, [nor commit adultery]}, the verse [al-Furqan: 68]. And He, exalted, says: {Say, come, let me recite what your Lord has forbidden to you: that you associate nothing with Him}

[Continuing to where it says:]

{And do not kill the soul which Allah has forbidden except by right. That is what He has enjoined upon you that you might use reason} [al-An'am: 151].

The hadiths concerning the prohibition of killing are very numerous. Among them is what is established in the two Sahihs from Ibn Mas'ud, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "The first matter to be judged between people on the Day of Resurrection will be concerning bloodshed." In another hadith, which Abu Dawud narrated by way of 'Amr ibn al-Walid ibn 'Abadah al-Misri, from 'Ubadah ibn al-Samit, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, "The believer remains at ease with regard to his religion as long as he does not shed forbidden blood, but when he sheds forbidden blood he becomes distressed and burdened." And in another hadith: "The passing away of the entire world is a lesser matter before Allah than the killing of a Muslim man." And in another hadith: "If the inhabitants of the heavens and the earth all joined together to kill a single Muslim man, Allah would cast them all into the Fire." And in another hadith: "Whoever assists in killing a Muslim, even with half a word, will come on the Day of Resurrection with it written between his eyes: despairing of the mercy of Allah."

Ibn 'Abbas, may Allah be pleased with him and his father, held the view that there is no repentance for one who deliberately kills a believer.

Al-Bukhari said: [chain] Adam told us, Shu'bah told us, Mughirah ibn al-Nu'man told us, who said: I heard Ibn Jubayr say: The people of Kufah disagreed about it, so I traveled to Ibn 'Abbas and asked him about it, and he said: This verse was revealed, {And whoever kills a believer deliberately, his recompense is Hell, [abiding therein forever]}; it is the last thing to be revealed on this matter, and nothing has abrogated it.

He also narrated it likewise, as did Muslim and al-Nasa'i, through various chains from Shu'bah. Abu Dawud narrated it from Ahmad ibn Hanbal, from Ibn Mahdi, from Sufyan al-Thawri, from Mughirah ibn al-Nu'man, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas, concerning His words {And whoever kills a believer deliberately, his recompense is Hell, abiding therein forever}, and he said: Nothing has abrogated it.

[Ibn Jarir said: Ibn Bashshar told us, Ibn Abi 'Adiyy told us, Shu'bah told us, from Abu Bishr, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, who said: 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Abzah said: Ibn 'Abbas was asked about His words {And whoever kills a believer deliberately, his recompense is Hell, abiding therein forever}, and he said: Nothing has abrogated it.] And concerning this verse, {And those who do not invoke with Allah another deity [and do not kill the soul which Allah has forbidden except by right, and do not commit unlawful sexual intercourse; and whoever does that will meet a penalty]} [al-Furqan: 68], he said: it was revealed concerning the people of polytheism.

Ibn Jarir said: Ibn Humayd told us, Jarir told us, from Mansur: Sa'id ibn Jubayr told me, or al-Hakam told me from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, who said: I asked Ibn 'Abbas about His words, {And whoever kills a believer deliberately, his recompense is Hell}, and he said: When a man knows Islam and its laws, then deliberately kills a believer, his recompense is Hell and he has no repentance. I mentioned that to Mujahid, and he said: Except one who feels remorse.

Ibn Humayd and Ibn Wakī' told us, both said: Jarir told us, from Yahya al-Jabir, from Salim ibn Abi al-Ja'd, who said: We were with Ibn 'Abbas after his sight had failed, when a man came to him and called out: O 'Abdullah ibn 'Abbas, what do you say about a man who kills a believer deliberately? He said: {his recompense is Hell, abiding therein forever, and Allah has become angry with him and has cursed him and has prepared for him a great punishment}. The man said: What do you think if he repents, does righteous deeds, and then follows guidance? Ibn 'Abbas said: May his mother be bereft of him, how can he have repentance and guidance? By the One in whose hand is my soul, I heard your Prophet ﷺ say: "May his mother be bereft of him: one who deliberately kills a believer will come on the Day of Resurrection holding him by his right hand or his left, his jugular veins gushing blood before the Throne of the Merciful, gripping his killer with his other hand, saying: Ask this one why he killed me." And by the One in whose hand is the soul of 'Abdullah, this verse was revealed and no verse abrogated it until your Prophet ﷺ passed away, and no proof came down after it.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Muhammad ibn Ja'far told us, Shu'bah told us, I heard Yahya ibn al-Mujabbar narrating from Salim ibn Abi al-Ja'd, from Ibn 'Abbas, that a man came to him and said: What do you think of a man who kills another man deliberately? He said: {his recompense is Hell, abiding therein forever, [and Allah has become angry with him and has cursed him and has prepared for him a great punishment]}. He said: It was revealed among the last things to be revealed, and nothing abrogated it until the Messenger of Allah ﷺ passed away, and no revelation came down after the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. The man said: What do you think if he repents, believes, does righteous deeds, and then follows guidance? He said: How could he have repentance? You have heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "May his mother be bereft of him: a man who kills another man deliberately will come on the Day of Resurrection holding his killer by his right hand or his left, and holding his head in his right hand or his left, his jugular veins gushing blood before the Throne, saying: O Lord, ask Your servant why he killed me."

Al-Nasa'i narrated it from Qutaybah, and Ibn Majah from Muhammad ibn al-Sabbah, from Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah, from 'Ammar al-Duhni, and Yahya al-Jabir, and Thabit al-Thumali, from Salim ibn Abi al-Ja'd, from Ibn 'Abbas, and mentioned it. This has been narrated from Ibn 'Abbas through many chains.

Among those of the Salaf who held the view that there is no repentance for him: Zayd ibn Thabit, Abu Hurayrah, 'Abdullah ibn 'Umar, Abu Salamah ibn 'Abd al-Rahman, 'Ubayd ibn 'Umar, al-Hasan, Qatadah, and al-Dahhak ibn Muzahim. Ibn Abi Hatim transmitted this.

On this subject there are many hadiths. Among them is what Abu Bakr ibn Mardawayh the hafiz narrated in his tafsir: [chain] Da'laj ibn Ahmad told us, Muhammad ibn Ibrahim ibn Sa'id al-Bushanji told us, and 'Abdullah ibn Ja'far told us, Ibrahim ibn Fahd told us, both said: 'Ubayd ibn 'Ubaydah told us, Mu'tamir ibn Sulayman told us, from his father, from al-A'mash, from Abu 'Amr ibn Shurahbil, from 'Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "The one killed will come clinging to his killer on the Day of Resurrection, holding his own head in his other hand, and he will say: O Lord, ask this one why he killed me." He said: "So he will say: I killed him so that might would belong to you. He will say: But it belongs to Me." He said: "And another will come clinging to his killer and say: Lord, ask this one why he killed me." He said: "So he will say: I killed him so that might would belong to so-and-so." He said: "It did not belong to him; bear your sin." He said: "And he will plunge into the Fire for seventy autumns."

This was also narrated from al-Nasa'i, from Ibrahim ibn al-Mustamirr al-'Awfi, from 'Amr ibn 'Asim, from Mu'tamir ibn Sulayman, with the same chain.

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Safwan ibn 'Isa told us, Thawr ibn Yazid told us, from Abu 'Awn, from Abu Idris, who said: I heard Mu'awiyah, may Allah be pleased with him, say: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "Every sin, Allah may forgive it, except the man who dies as a disbeliever, or the man who kills a believer deliberately."

Al-Nasa'i likewise narrated it from Muhammad ibn al-Muthanna, from Safwan ibn 'Isa, with the same chain.

Ibn Mardawayh said: [chain] 'Abdullah ibn Ja'far told us, Sammawayh told us, 'Abd al-A'la ibn Mushir told us, Sadaqah ibn Khalid told us, Khalid ibn Dihqan told us, Ibn Abi Zakariyya told us, who said: I heard Umm al-Darda' say: I heard Abu al-Darda' say: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "Every sin, Allah may forgive it, except one who dies a polytheist, or one who kills a believer deliberately."

This is extremely gharib (singular) by way of this chain. What is preserved (well established) is the preceding hadith of Mu'awiyah, and Allah knows best.

Then Ibn Mardawayh narrated by way of Baqiyyah ibn al-Walid, from Nafi' ibn Yazid: Ibn Jubayr al-Ansari told me, from Dawud ibn al-Husayn, from Nafi', from Ibn 'Umar, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: "Whoever deliberately kills a believer has disbelieved in Allah, Mighty and Majestic."

This too is a munkar (rejected) hadith, and its chain has been very heavily criticized.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Al-Nadr told us, Sulayman ibn al-Mughirah told us, Humayd told us, who said: Abu al-'Aliyah came to me, along with a companion of mine, and said to us: Come, for you two are somewhat younger than me and better at retaining hadith than I am. So he took us to Bishr ibn 'Asim, and Abu al-'Aliyah said to him: Tell these two your hadith. He said: 'Uqbah ibn Malik al-Laythi told us, saying: The Prophet ﷺ sent out a raiding detachment, and it raided a people. A man from that people fled, and a man from the detachment pursued him with his sword drawn. The one who fled from the people said: I am a Muslim.

He did not consider what he had said, but struck him and killed him. The report reached the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he spoke about it in harsh terms, and this reached the killer. Then while the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was delivering a sermon, the killer said: "By Allah, he only said what he said to seek refuge from being killed." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ turned away from him and from those near him, and continued his sermon. Then the man said again: "O Messenger of Allah, he only said what he said to seek refuge from being killed." Again he turned away from him and from those near him and continued his sermon. Then the man could not restrain himself, and said a third time: "By Allah, O Messenger of Allah, he only said it to seek refuge from being killed."

The Messenger of Allah ﷺ then turned to him, displeasure visible on his face, and said three times: "Allah refused to accept this from one who has killed a believer."

Al-Nasa'i also related it through the hadith of Sulayman ibn al-Mughirah.

The position held by the majority of the early and later generations of this community is that the killer has a valid path of repentance between himself and his Lord, Mighty and Majestic. If he repents, turns back to Allah, humbles himself, submits, and does righteous deeds, Allah will change his evil deeds into good deeds, compensate the one who was killed for the wrong done to him, and satisfy him regarding his claim.

Allah, Exalted is He, says: {And those who do not invoke another god besides Allah, nor kill the soul which Allah has forbidden except by right, nor commit fornication. And whoever does that will meet a penalty. The punishment will be doubled for him on the Day of Resurrection, and he will abide therein humiliated. Except for one who repents, believes, and does righteous work. For them Allah will replace their evil deeds with good deeds, and Allah is ever Forgiving and Merciful} [al-Furqan: 68-69]. This is a report whose abrogation is not permissible. To interpret it as referring to polytheists, while applying this verse to believers, goes against its apparent meaning, and such an interpretation requires evidence. Allah knows best.

Allah, Exalted is He, also says: {Say: O My servants who have transgressed against themselves, do not despair of the mercy of Allah. Indeed, Allah forgives all sins. Indeed, it is He who is the Forgiving, the Merciful} [al-Zumar: 53]. This is general regarding all sins, whether disbelief, associating partners with Allah, doubt, hypocrisy, killing, wickedness, or anything else. Whoever repents from any of these, Allah accepts his repentance.

Allah, Exalted is He, also says: {Indeed, Allah does not forgive that partners be associated with Him, but He forgives what is less than that for whom He wills} [al-Nisa: 48]. This verse is general regarding all sins except association of partners with Allah, and it is mentioned in this noble surah both after and before this verse, in order to strengthen hope. Allah knows best.

It is established in the two Sahihs, the report of the Israelite who killed one hundred people, then asked a scholar: "Is there repentance for me?" The scholar said: "Who can stand between you and repentance?" He then directed him to a land where Allah was worshipped, so he emigrated toward it, and died on the way. The angels of mercy took his soul. As we have mentioned more than once, if this was the case among the Children of Israel, then it is all the more fitting and more appropriate that repentance be accepted in this community, because Allah has removed from us the shackles and burdens that were placed upon them, and sent our Prophet with the easy, upright religion (al-hanifiyyah al-samhah).

As for the noble verse, which is His statement, Exalted is He: {And whoever kills a believer intentionally, his recompense is Hell, abiding therein forever, and Allah's wrath is upon him, and He has cursed him, and has prepared for him a great punishment}, Abu Hurayrah and a group of the early generations said: this is his recompense, if he is recompensed with it. Ibn Marduyah related it as a marfu' (elevated) report through the chain of Muhammad ibn Jami' al-'Attar, from al-'Ala' ibn Maymun al-'Anbari, from Hajjaj al-Aswad, from Muhammad ibn Sirin, from Abu Hurayrah, marfu'. However, it is not authentic. The meaning of this formulation is that this is his recompense, if he is indeed recompensed for it, and likewise every threat mentioned regarding a sin. But there may be, alongside such a threat, something opposing it from righteous deeds that prevents that recompense from reaching him, according to the two positions of the proponents of "weighing" (muwazanah) or "nullification" (ihbat). This is the best approach to take regarding matters of threat (wa'id), and Allah knows best what is correct.

Even granting that the killer enters the Fire, whether according to the position of Ibn 'Abbas and those who agree with him that he has no repentance, or according to the position of the majority where he has no righteous deed by which he is saved, he does not abide therein forever. Rather, "abiding" here means a lengthy stay. Numerous hadiths have come from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ stating that whoever has in his heart even the smallest particle of faith will be brought out of the Fire.

As for the hadith of Mu'awiyah: "Every sin, Allah may perhaps forgive it, except a man who dies as a disbeliever, or a man who kills a believer intentionally." The word "perhaps" (asa) indicates hope. If hope is negated in these two cases, that does not mean the occurrence of forgiveness is negated in one of them, namely killing, because of the evidence we have mentioned. As for one who dies a disbeliever, the text is explicit that he will never be forgiven at all.

As for the demand the one who was killed will make of the killer on the Day of Resurrection, this is one of the rights of human beings (huquq al-adamiyyin), and such rights are not nullified by repentance. There is no difference in this regard between the one who was killed and one who was robbed, one who was wronged, one who was slandered, and other rights owed to human beings, for there is consensus that these are not nullified by repentance, and it is necessary to fulfill them to the people owed, for the repentance to be valid. If that becomes impossible, then the claim must be settled on the Day of Resurrection. However, the occurrence of the claim does not necessarily mean the occurrence of retribution. The killer may have righteous deeds that are transferred to the one who was killed, or some of them, and there may remain for him a surplus of reward by which he enters Paradise. Or Allah may compensate the one who was killed, out of His bounty, with whatever He wills, from the palaces of Paradise and its bliss, and the raising of his rank therein, and the like. Allah knows best.

Intentional killing carries rulings in this world and rulings in the Hereafter. As for this world, it entails giving authority to the guardians of the one killed over the killer. Allah, Exalted is He, says: {And whoever is killed unjustly, We have given his heir authority, but let him not exceed limits in taking life. Indeed, he has been supported} [al-Isra: 33]. The guardians then have the choice between killing the murderer, pardoning him, or taking a heavier blood money (diyah mughallazah) in three parts: thirty she-camels in their fourth year, thirty in their fifth year, and forty pregnant she-camels, as is established in the books of legal rulings.

The scholars differed regarding whether an expiation (kaffarah) is obligatory upon the killer, whether freeing a slave, fasting two consecutive months, or feeding the poor, following one of two positions, as was mentioned previously regarding the expiation for unintentional killing, according to two views.
