Concerning His saying: {And do not weaken in pursuit of the enemy}, meaning: do not be feeble in seeking out your enemy, but rather strive hard against them, fight them, and lie in wait for them at every point of ambush. {If you are suffering, then indeed they are suffering as you are suffering}, meaning: just as wounds and death befall you, the same happens to them, as He said: {If a wound should touch you, a similar wound has already touched the enemy} [Al Imran: 140].

Then He said: {while you hope from Allah what they do not hope}, meaning: you and they are equal in what befalls each of you of wounds and pains, but you hope from Allah for reward, victory, and support, while they hope for none of that. So you are more deserving of jihad than they are, and more intense in desire to establish the word of Allah and to exalt it.

{And Allah is ever Knowing and Wise}, meaning: He is most knowing and most wise in what He decrees and ordains, and carries out and executes, of His decrees, both cosmic and legislative, and He is praised in every state.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَأْمُرُ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى بِكَثْرَةِ الذِّكْرِ عُقَيْبَ صَلَاةِ الْخَوْفِ، وَإِنْ كَانَ مَشْرُوعًا مُرَغَّبًا فِيهِ أَيْضًا بَعْدَ غَيْرِهَا، وَلَكِنَّ هَاهُنَا آكَدُ لِمَا وَقَعَ فِيهَا مِنَ التَّخْفِيفِ فِي أَرْكَانِهَا، وَمِنَ الرُّخْصَةِ فِي الذَّهَابِ فِيهَا وَالْإِيَابِ وَغَيْرِ ذَلِكَ، مِمَّا لَيْسَ يُوجَدُ فِي غَيْرِهَا، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى فِي الْأَشْهُرِ الْحُرُمَ: ﴿فَلا تَظْلِمُوا فِيهِنَّ أَنْفُسَكُمْ﴾ [التَّوْبَةِ: ٣٦] ، وَإِنْ كَانَ هَذَا مَنْهِيًّا عَنْهُ فِي غَيْرِهَا، وَلَكِنَّ فِيهَا آكَدُ لِشِدَّةِ حُرْمَتِهَا وَعِظَمِهَا؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَإِذَا قَضَيْتُمُ الصَّلاةَ فَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ قِيَامًا وَقُعُودًا وَعَلَى جُنُوبِكُمْ﴾ أَيْ فِي سَائِرِ أَحْوَالِكُمْ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿فَإِذَا اطْمَأْنَنْتُمْ فَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ﴾ أَيْ: فَإِذَا أَمِنْتُمْ وَذَهَبَ الْخَوْفُ، وَحَصَلَتِ الطُّمَأْنِينَةُ ﴿فَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ﴾ أَيْ: فَأَتِمُّوهَا وَأَقِيمُوهَا كَمَا أُمِرْتُمْ بِحُدُودِهَا، وَخُشُوعِهَا، وَسُجُودِهَا وَرُكُوعِهَا، وَجَمِيعِ شُئُونِهَا.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿إِنَّ الصَّلاةَ كَانَتْ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ كِتَابًا مَوْقُوتًا﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: أَيْ مَفْرُوضًا. وَكَذَا رُوِيَ عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، وَسَالِمِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، وَعَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ، وَمُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ، وَالْحَسَنِ، وَمُقَاتِلٍ، وَالسُّدِّيِّ، وَعَطِيَّةَ الْعَوْفِيِّ.

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ: ﴿إِنَّ الصَّلاةَ كَانَتْ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ كِتَابًا مَوْقُوتًا﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ مَسْعُودٍ: إِنَّ لِلصَّلَاةِ وَقْتًا كَوَقْتِ الْحَجِّ.

وَقَالَ زَيْدُ بْنُ أَسْلَمَ: ﴿إِنَّ الصَّلاةَ كَانَتْ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ كِتَابًا مَوْقُوتًا﴾ قَالَ: مُنَجَّمًا، كُلَّمَا مَضَى نَجْمٌ، جَاءَتْهُمْ يَعْنِي: كُلَّمَا مَضَى وَقْتٌ جَاءَ وَقْتٌ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلا تَهِنُوا فِي ابْتِغَاءِ الْقَوْمِ﴾ أَيْ: لَا تَضْعُفُوا فِي طَلَبِ عَدُّوِّكُمْ، بَلْ جِدُّوا فِيهِمْ وَقَاتَلُوهُمْ، وَاقْعُدُوا لَهُمْ كُلَّ مَرْصَدٍ: ﴿إِنْ تَكُونُوا تَأْلَمُونَ فَإِنَّهُمْ يَأْلَمُونَ كَمَا تَأْلَمُونَ﴾ أَيْ: كَمَا يُصِيبُكُمُ الْجِرَاحُ وَالْقَتْلُ، كَذَلِكَ يَحْصُلُ لَهُمْ، كَمَا قَالَ ﴿إِنْ يَمْسَسْكُمْ قَرْحٌ فَقَدْ مَسَّ الْقَوْمَ قَرْحٌ مِثْلُهُ﴾ [آلِ عِمْرَانَ: ١٤٠] .

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿وَتَرْجُونَ مِنَ اللَّهِ مَا لَا يَرْجُونَ﴾ أَيْ: أَنْتُمْ وَإِيَّاهُمْ سَوَاءٌ فِيمَا يُصِيبُكُمْ وَإِيَّاهُمْ من الْجِرَاحِ وَالْآلَامِ، وَلَكِنْ أَنْتُمْ تَرْجُونَ مِنَ اللَّهِ الْمَثُوبَةَ وَالنَّصْرَ وَالتَّأْيِيدَ، وَهُمْ لَا يَرْجُونَ شَيْئًا مِنْ ذَلِكَ، فَأَنْتُمْ أَوْلَى بِالْجِهَادِ مِنْهُمْ، وَأَشَدُّ رَغْبَةً فِي إِقَامَةِ كَلِمَةِ اللَّهِ وَإِعْلَائِهَا.

﴿وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا﴾ أَيْ: هُوَ أَعْلَمُ وَأَحْكَمُ فِيمَا يُقَدِّرُهُ وَيَقْضِيهِ، وَيُنَفِّذُهُ وَيُمْضِيهِ، مِنْ أَحْكَامِهِ الْكَوْنِيَّةِ وَالشَّرْعِيَّةِ، وَهُوَ الْمَحْمُودُ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ.

---

## 4:105-109 — verses 105-109 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Exalted says, addressing His Messenger Muhammad ﷺ: {Indeed We have sent down to you the Book with the truth}, meaning: it is truth from Allah, and it contains truth in both its reports and its commands.

Concerning His saying {so that you may judge between people by that which Allah has shown you}: this was cited as proof by those scholars of legal theory who held that he, peace be upon him, was permitted to judge by independent reasoning (ijtihad), basing this on this verse and on what is established in the two Sahihs through the narration of Hisham ibn Urwah, from his father, from Zaynab bint Umm Salamah, from Umm Salamah: that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ heard the raised voices of two disputants at the door of his chamber, so he went out to them and said: "Indeed I am only a human being, and I judge only according to what I hear. Perhaps one of you is more eloquent in presenting his argument than the other, and so I rule in his favor. So whoever I have given a judgment in his favor that takes from the right of a Muslim, it is only a piece of Fire, so let him take it or leave it."

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Wakia, [chain] Usamah ibn Zayd, from Abdullah ibn Rafi, from Umm Salamah, who said: Two men from the Ansar came disputing before the Messenger of Allah ﷺ over an inheritance between them whose details had become obscure, and neither of them had proof. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "You bring your disputes to me, but I am only a human being. Perhaps one of you is more eloquent in his argument than the other, and I judge between you only according to what I hear. So whoever I give a judgment in his favor that takes something from the right of his brother, let him not take it, for I would only be cutting off for him a piece of the Fire, which he will bring as a burning brand around his neck on the Day of Resurrection." The two men wept, and each of them said: "My right belongs to my brother." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Since you have both said that, go and divide the matter, then seek out what is right, then cast lots, and then let each of you release the other from his claim."

Abu Dawud narrated it from the hadith of Usamah ibn Zayd, with the same chain, adding: "I only judge between you by opinion, in matters concerning which nothing has been revealed to me."

Ibn Mardawayh narrated, via the route of al-Awfi, from Ibn Abbas, who said: A group of the Ansar went out on a military expedition with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in one of his campaigns, and a coat of mail belonging to one of them was stolen. Suspicion fell upon a man from the Ansar. The owner of the coat of mail came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: "Ta'mah ibn Ubayriq has stolen my armor." When the thief realized this, he took the armor and threw it into the house of an innocent man, and said to a group of his own kinsmen: "I have hidden the armor and thrown it into the house of so-and-so, and it will be found with him." So they went to the Prophet of Allah ﷺ by night and said: "O Prophet of Allah, our companion is innocent, and the owner of the armor is so-and-so, and we are certain of this. So excuse our companion before the people and argue on his behalf, for if Allah does not protect him through you, he will perish." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood and declared him innocent and excused him before the people. Then Allah sent down: {Indeed We have sent down to you the Book with the truth so that you may judge between people by that which Allah has shown you, and do not be an advocate for the treacherous} [meaning: judge by what Allah has sent down to you in the Book] {and seek forgiveness of Allah; indeed Allah is ever Forgiving, Merciful. And do not argue on behalf of those who betray themselves [indeed Allah does not love one who is a habitual betrayer and sinner]}. Then He said, concerning those who had come to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, concealing their falsehood: {They seek to hide from people, but they cannot hide from Allah [and He is with them when they plot by night that which He does not approve of in speech, and Allah ever encompasses what they do. Here you are, those who argued on their behalf in the life of this world, but who will argue with Allah on their behalf on the Day of Resurrection, or who will be their representative]}, meaning: those who came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, hiding themselves, arguing on behalf of the traitors. Then He said: {And whoever does evil or wrongs himself [then seeks forgiveness of Allah will find Allah Forgiving, Merciful]}, meaning: those who came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ concealing their falsehood. Then He said: {And whoever earns a fault or a sin, then throws it upon an innocent person, has burdened himself with slander and a manifest sin}, meaning: the thief and those who argued on behalf of the thief.

This is a gharib (singular) chain of transmission. Similarly, Mujahid, Ikrimah, Qatadah, al-Suddi, Ibn Zayd, and others mentioned regarding this verse that it was revealed concerning the thief from Banu Ubayriq, with variations in their accounts, though they are close to one another.

Muhammad ibn Ishaq narrated this story at length. Abu Isa al-Tirmidhi, in the commentary on this verse in his Jami, and Ibn Jarir in his Tafsir, both said:

[chain] al-Hasan ibn Ahmad ibn Abi Shu'ayb Abu Muslim al-Harrani, [chain] Muhammad ibn Salamah al-Harrani, [chain] Muhammad ibn Ishaq, from Asim ibn Umar ibn Qatadah, from his father, from his grandfather Qatadah ibn al-Nu'man, may Allah be pleased with him, who said: There was a household among us called Banu Ubayriq: Bishr, Bushayr, and Mubashshir. Bushayr was a hypocrite who composed poetry satirizing the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ, then attributed it to some of the Arabs, saying: "So-and-so said such and such, and so-and-so said such and such." When the Companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ heard that poetry they would say: "By Allah, none but this vile man could have composed this poetry," or words to that effect, and they would say: "It was Ibn al-Ubayriq who said it."

They said: they were a household of need and poverty both in the Jahiliyyah and in Islam. The people of Madinah at that time ate mostly dates and barley, and if a man had wealth, when a caravan came from Syria carrying fine white flour, he would buy some of it for himself alone, while the family's food remained dates and barley. So a caravan came from Syria, and my uncle Rifa'ah ibn Zayd bought a load of fine flour and stored it in an upper room of his, in which there was also weaponry: a coat of mail and a sword. He was robbed from beneath the house; the upper room was broken into and the food and the weapons were taken. When morning came, my uncle Rifa'ah came to me and said: "O son of my brother, we have been robbed this very night. Our upper room has been broken into and our food and our weapons have been taken."

He said: We made inquiries in the neighborhood and asked around, and we were told: "We saw Banu Ubayriq lighting a fire this night, and we think it was for nothing but some of your food."

He said: And Banu Ubayriq, while we were making our inquiries in the neighborhood, said: "By Allah, we think none other than Labid ibn Sahl is your man," a man among us known for righteousness and Islam. When Labid heard this he drew his sword and said: "I, steal? By Allah, this sword shall meet you, or you shall make this theft clear." They said: "Leave us be, man, you are not the one responsible for it." So we continued asking in the neighborhood until we had no doubt that they were the culprits.

My uncle said to me: "O son of my brother, why do you not go to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and mention this to him?" Qatadah said: So I went to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: "A household among us, people of harshness, set upon my uncle Rifa'ah ibn Zayd, broke into an upper room of his, and took his weapons and his food. Let them return our weapons to us; as for the food, we have no need of it." The Prophet ﷺ said: "I shall see to that matter."

When Banu Ubayriq heard of this, they went to a man among them called Usayr ibn Amr and spoke to him about it. So a group of people from the neighborhood gathered and said: "O Messenger of Allah, Qatadah ibn al-Nu'man and his uncle have set upon a household among us, people of Islam and righteousness, accusing them of theft without proof or certainty." Qatadah said: So I went to the Prophet ﷺ and he spoke to me, saying: "Have you set upon a household known for Islam and righteousness, accusing them of theft without certainty or proof?"

He said: So I returned, and I wished I had rather given up some of my property than have spoken to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ about that matter. My uncle Rifa'ah came to me and said: "O son of my brother, what have you done?" So I informed him of what the Messenger of Allah ﷺ had said to me, and he said: "Allah is the One whose help is sought." We had not long to wait before the Qur'an came down: {Indeed We have sent down to you the Book with the truth so that you may judge between people by that which Allah has shown you, and do not be an advocate for the treacherous}, meaning Banu Ubayriq, {and seek forgiveness of Allah}, for what you said to Qatadah, {indeed Allah is ever Forgiving, Merciful. And do not argue on behalf of those who betray themselves [indeed Allah does not love one who is a habitual betrayer and sinner. They seek to hide from people, but they cannot hide from Allah, and He is with them]} up to His saying {Merciful}, meaning: had they sought Allah's forgiveness He would have forgiven them, {and whoever earns a sin, only earns it against himself} up to His saying {a manifest sin}, referring to their words to Labid: {and were it not for the favor of Allah upon you and His mercy} up to His saying {We will grant him a great reward}.

When the Qur'an came down, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ brought the weapons and returned them to Rifa'ah.

Qatada said: "When I brought the weapon to my uncle, who was an old man, who had grown dim-sighted or hardened with age (the doubt is from Abu 'Isa) during the Jahiliyyah, and I used to consider his Islam suspect. When I brought him the weapon, he said: 'O son of my brother, it is in the path of God.' So I knew that his Islam had been sound. Then when the Qur'an was revealed, Bushayr joined the polytheists and stayed with Sulafa bint Sa'd ibn Sumayya. So God, Exalted is He, revealed: {And whoever opposes the Messenger after guidance has become clear to him and follows other than the way of the believers, We will let him continue in what he has chosen and We will burn him in Hell, and evil it is as a destination. Indeed, God does not forgive association with Him, but He forgives what is less than that for whom He wills. And whoever associates others with God has certainly strayed far astray.} When he stayed with Sulafa, Hassan ibn Thabit attacked her with verses of his poetry. So she took his luggage and put it on her head, then went out with it and threw it down in al-Abtah, and said: 'Have you brought me the poetry of Hassan as a gift? You have never brought me anything good.'"

This is the wording of al-Tirmidhi. Al-Tirmidhi then said: this is a gharib (singular) hadith; we do not know of anyone who transmitted it with a full chain except Muhammad ibn Salama al-Harrani. Yunus ibn Bukayr and more than one other narrated it from Muhammad ibn Ishaq, from 'Asim ibn 'Umar ibn Qatada, as a mursal (report with a broken chain at the end) report, without mentioning "from his father, from his grandfather" in it.

Ibn Abi Hatim also narrated it from Hashim ibn al-Qasim al-Harrani, from Muhammad ibn Salama, with it, in part.

Ibn al-Mundhir narrated it in his tafsir: "Muhammad ibn Isma'il, that is al-Sa'igh, told us: al-Hasan ibn Ahmad ibn Abi Shu'ayb al-Harrani told us: Muhammad ibn Salama told us," and he mentioned it at length.

Abu al-Shaykh al-Asbahani narrated it in his tafsir from Muhammad ibn al-'Abbas ibn Ayyub and al-Hasan ibn Ya'qub, both of them from al-Hasan ibn Ahmad ibn Abi Shu'ayb al-Harrani, from Muhammad ibn Salama, with it. Then he said at the end of it: Muhammad ibn Salama said: "Yahya ibn Ma'in, Ahmad ibn Hanbal, and Ishaq ibn Israil heard this hadith from me."

Al-Hakim Abu 'Abdillah al-Naysaburi narrated this hadith in his book al-Mustadrak, from Abu al-'Abbas al-Asamm, from Ahmad ibn 'Abd al-Jabbar al-'Utaridi, from Yunus ibn Bukayr, from Muhammad ibn Ishaq, with the same meaning but more complete, and it contains the poetry. He then said: this hadith is sound according to the criterion of Muslim, though neither of the two (Bukhari and Muslim) recorded it.

As for His saying: {They hide from people but they do not hide from God, while He is with them when they plot by night that speech which He does not approve} to the end of the verse: this is a rebuke of the hypocrites for hiding their evil deeds from people so that people would not object to them, while they openly commit them before God, because He is fully aware of their inner secrets and knows what is in their hearts. Therefore He said: {while He is with them when they plot by night that speech which He does not approve, and God is ever encompassing of what they do}, as a threat and a warning to them.

Then He said: {Here you are, these ones who argue on their behalf in worldly life, but who will argue with God for them on the Day of Resurrection, or who will be their advocate?} meaning: suppose these people gained victory in this world through what they displayed, or what was displayed on their behalf, before the judges who rule by outward appearance, being obligated to do so by their office, then what will their situation be on the Day of Resurrection before God, Mighty and Majestic, who knows the secret and what is even more hidden? And who is it who will take it upon himself on that day to advance their case? That is, no one will be an advocate for them on that day. Therefore He said: {or who will be their advocate?}

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى مُخَاطِبًا لِرَسُولِهِ مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ: ﴿إِنّا أَنزلْنَا إِلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ بِالْحَقِّ﴾ أَيْ: هُوَ حَقٌّ مِنَ اللَّهِ، وَهُوَ يَتَضَمَّنُ الْحَقَّ فِي خَبَرِهِ وَطَلَبِهِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿لِتَحْكُمَ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ بِمَا أَرَاكَ اللَّهُ﴾ احْتَجَّ بِهِ مَنْ ذَهَبَ مِنْ عُلَمَاءِ الْأُصُولِ إِلَى أَنَّهُ كَانَ، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، لَهُ أَنْ يَحْكُمَ بِالِاجْتِهَادِ بِهَذِهِ الْآيَةِ، وَبِمَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ مِنْ رِوَايَةِ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَة، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ زَيْنَبَ بِنْتِ أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم سمع حَلَبَةَ خَصْمٍ بِبَابِ حُجْرَتِهِ، فَخَرَجَ إِلَيْهِمْ فَقَالَ: "أَلَا إِنَّمَا أَنَا بَشَرٌ، وَإِنَّمَا أَقْضِي بِنَحْوٍ مِمَّا أَسْمَعُ، وَلَعَلَّ أَحَدَكُمْ أَنْ يَكُونَ أَلْحَنَ بِحُجَّتِهِ مِنْ بَعْضٍ، فَأَقْضِي لَهُ، فَمَنْ قَضَيْتُ لَهُ بِحَقِّ مُسْلِمٍ فَإِنَّمَا هِيَ قِطْعَةٌ مِنْ نَارٍ فَلْيَحْمِلَهَا أَوْ لِيَذَرْهَا" .

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيع، حَدَّثَنَا أُسَامَةُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ رَافِعٍ، عَنْ أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ قَالَتْ: جَاءَ رَجُلَانِ مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ يَخْتَصِمَانِ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ فِي مَوَارِيثَ بَيْنَهُمَا قَدْ دَرَسَتْ، لَيْسَ عِنْدَهُمَا بَيِّنَةٌ، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إِنَّكُمْ تَخْتَصِمُونَ إِلَيَّ، وَإِنَّمَا أَنَا بَشَرٌ، وَلَعَلَّ بَعْضَكُمْ ألْحَن بحُجَّتِه مِنْ بَعْضٍ، وَإِنَّمَا أَقْضِي بَيْنَكُمْ عَلَى نَحْوٍ مِمَّا أَسْمَعُ، فَمَنْ قَضَيْتُ لَهُ مِنْ حَقِّ أَخِيهِ شَيْئًا فَلَا يَأْخُذْهُ، فَإِنَّمَا أَقْطَعُ لَهُ قِطْعَةً مِنَ النَّارِ، يَأْتِي بِهَا إِسْطَامًا فِي عُنُقِهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ". فَبَكَى الرَّجُلَانِ وَقَالَ كُلٌّ مِنْهُمَا: حَقِّي لِأَخِي فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "أَمَّا إِذَا قُلْتُمَا فَاذْهَبَا فَاقْتَسِمَا، ثُمَّ تَوَخَّيَا الْحَقَّ، ثُمَّ اسْتَهِمَا، ثُمَّ ليُحْللْ كُلُّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْكُمَا صَاحِبَهُ".

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ مِنْ حَدِيثِ أُسَامَةَ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، بِهِ. وَزَادَ: "إِنِّي إِنَّمَا أَقْضِي بينكما برأي فيما لم يَنْزِلْ عَلَيَّ فِيهِ" .

وَقَدْ رَوَى ابْنُ مَرْدُويه، مِنْ طَرِيقِ الْعَوْفِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: إِنَّ نَفَرًا مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ غَزَوْا مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ فِي بَعْضِ غَزَوَاتِهِ، فَسُرِقَتْ دِرْعٌ لِأَحَدِهِمْ، فَأُظِنَّ بِهَا رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْأَنْصَارِ، فَأَتَى صَاحِبُ الدِّرْعِ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَقَالَ: إِنْ طُعْمةَ بْنَ أُبَيْرق سَرَقَ دِرْعِي، فَلَمَّا رَأَى السَّارِقُ ذَلِكَ عَمَدَ إِلَيْهَا فَأَلْقَاهَا فِي بَيْتِ رَجُلٍ بَرِيءٍ، وَقَالَ لِنَفَرٍ مِنْ عَشِيرَتِهِ: إِنِّي غَيَّبْتُ الدِّرْعَ وَأَلْقَيْتُهَا فِي بَيْتِ فُلَانٍ، وَسَتُوجَدُ عِنْدَهُ. فَانْطَلِقُوا إِلَى نَبِيِّ اللَّهِ ﷺ لَيْلًا فَقَالُوا: يَا نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ، إِنَّ صَاحِبَنَا بَرِيءٌ. وَإِنَّ صَاحِبَ الدِّرْعِ فُلَانٌ، وَقَدْ أحطنا بذلك علما، فاعذُرْ صاحبنا على رءوس النَّاسِ وَجَادِلْ عَنْهُ. فَإِنَّهُ إِلَّا يَعْصِمْهُ اللَّهُ بِكَ يَهْلِكْ، فَقَامَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ فبرأه وعذرَه على رءوس النَّاسِ، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿إِنَّا أَنزلْنَا إِلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ بِالْحَقِّ لِتَحْكُمَ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ بِمَا أَرَاكَ اللَّهُ وَلا تَكُنْ لِلْخَائِنِينَ خَصِيمًا ﴾ [يَقُولُ: احْكُمْ بِمَا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ إِلَيْكَ فِي الْكِتَابِ] ﴿وَاسْتَغْفِرِ اللَّهَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا. وَلا تُجَادِلْ عَنِ الَّذِينَ يَخْتَانُونَ أَنْفُسَهُمْ [إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ مَنْ كَانَ خَوَّانًا أَثِيمًا] ﴾ ثُمَّ قَالَ لِلَّذِينِ أَتَوْا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ مُسْتَخْفين بِالْكَذِبِ: ﴿يَسْتَخْفُونَ مِنَ النَّاسِ وَلا يَسْتَخْفُونَ مِنَ اللَّهِ [وَهُوَ مَعَهُمْ إِذْ يُبَيِّتُونَ مَا لَا يَرْضَى مِنَ الْقَوْلِ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطًا. هَا أَنْتُمْ هَؤُلاءِ جَادَلْتُمْ عَنْهُمْ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا فَمَنْ يُجَادِلُ اللَّهَ عَنْهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ أَمْ مَنْ يَكُونُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَكِيلا] ﴾ يَعْنِي: الَّذِينَ أَتَوْا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ مُسْتَخْفِينَ يُجَادِلُونَ عَنِ الْخَائِنِينَ ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا أَوْ يَظْلِمْ نَفْسَهُ [ثُمَّ يَسْتَغْفِرِ اللَّهَ يَجِدِ اللَّهَ غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا] ﴾ يَعْنِي: الَّذِينَ أَتَوْا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ مُسْتَخْفِينَ بِالْكَذِبِ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿وَمَنْ يَكْسِبْ خَطِيئَةً أَوْ إِثْمًا ثُمَّ يَرْمِ بِهِ بَرِيئًا فَقَدِ احْتَمَلَ بُهْتَانًا وَإِثْمًا مُبِينًا﴾ يَعْنِي: السَّارِقَ وَالَّذِينَ جَادَلُوا عَنِ السَّارِقِ. وَهَذَا سِيَاقٌ غَرِيبٌ وَكَذَا ذَكَرَ مُجَاهِدٌ، وَعِكْرِمَةُ، وَقَتَادَةُ، وَالسُّدِّيُّ، وَابْنُ زَيْدٍ وَغَيْرُهُمْ فِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ أَنَّهَا أُنْزِلَتْ فِي سَارِقِ بَنِي أُبَيْرِقٍ عَلَى اخْتِلَافِ سِيَاقَاتِهِمْ، وَهِيَ مُتَقَارِبَةٌ.

وَقَدْ رَوَى هَذِهِ الْقِصَّةَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ مُطَوَّلَةً، فَقَالَ أَبُو عِيسَى التِّرْمِذِيُّ عِنْدَ تَفْسِيرِ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ مَنْ جَامِعِهِ، وَابْنُ جَرِيرٍ فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ:

حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِي شُعَيْبٍ أَبُو مُسْلِمٍ الحرَّاني، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ الحرَّاني، حَدَّثَنَا محمد بن إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ عُمَر بْنِ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ قَتَادة بْنِ النُّعْمَانِ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: كَانَ أَهْلُ بَيْتٍ مِنَّا يُقَالُ لَهُمْ بَنُو أُبَيْرق: بِشْر وَبُشَيْرٌ ومُبَشّر، وَكَانَ بُشَير رَجُلًا مُنَافِقًا، يَقُولُ الشِّعْرَ يَهْجُو بِهِ أَصْحَابَ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ، ثُمَّ يُنْحِلُهُ بَعْضَ الْعَرَبِ، ثُمَّ يَقُولُ: قَالَ فُلَانٌ كَذَا وَكَذَا، وَقَالَ فُلَانٌ كَذَا وَكَذَا، فَإِذَا سَمِعَ أَصْحَابُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ ذَلِكَ الشِّعْرَ قَالُوا: وَاللَّهِ مَا يَقُولُ هَذَا الشِّعْرَ إِلَّا هَذَا الْخَبِيثُ؟ -أَوْ كَمَا قَالَ الرَّجُلُ -وَقَالُوا ابْنُ الْأُبَيْرِقِ قَالَهَا. قَالُوا: وَكَانُوا أَهْلَ بَيْتِ حَاجَةٍ وَفَاقَةٍ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ وَالْإِسْلَامِ، وَكَانَ النَّاسُ إِنَّمَا طَعَامُهُمْ بِالْمَدِينَةِ التَّمْرُ وَالشَّعِيرُ، وَكَانَ الرَّجُلُ إِذَا كَانَ لَهُ يَسَارٌ فَقَدِمَتْ ضَافِطَةٌ مِنَ الشَّامِ مِنَ الدَّرْمَك ابْتَاعَ الرَّجُلُ مِنْهَا فَخَصَّ بِهَا نَفْسَهُ، وَأَمَّا الْعِيَالُ فَإِنَّمَا طَعَامُهُمُ التَّمْرُ وَالشَّعِيرُ، فَقَدِمَتْ ضَافطة مِنَ الشَّامِ، فَابْتَاعَ عَمِّي رِفَاعَةُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ حِمْلًا مِنَ الدَّرْمَكِ فَحَطَّهُ فِي مَشْربة لَهُ، وَفِي الْمَشْرَبَةِ سِلَاحٌ: دِرْعٌ وَسَيْفٌ، فَعُدى عَلَيْهِ مِنْ تَحْتِ الْبَيْتِ، فَنقّبت الْمَشْرَبَةُ وَأُخِذَ الطَّعَامُ وَالسِّلَاحُ. فَلَمَّا أَصْبَحَ أَتَانِي عَمِّي رِفَاعَةُ فَقَالَ: يَا ابْنَ أَخِي، إِنَّهُ قَدْ عُدِيَ عَلَيْنَا فِي لَيْلَتِنَا هَذِهِ. فَنُقِبَتْ مَشْرَبَتُنَا وَذُهِبَ بِطَعَامِنَا وَسِلَاحِنَا. قَالَ: فَتَجَسَّسْنَا فِي الدَّارِ وَسَأَلْنَا، فَقِيلَ لَنَا: قَدْ رَأَيْنَا بَنِي أُبَيْرق اسْتَوْقَدُوا فِي هَذِهِ اللَّيْلَةِ، وَلَا نَرَى فِيمَا نَرَى إِلَّا عَلَى بَعْضِ طَعَامِكُمْ.

قَالَ: وَكَانَ بَنُو أُبَيْرِقٍ قَالُوا -وَنَحْنُ نَسْأَلُ فِي الدَّارِ -: وَاللَّهِ مَا نَرَى صَاحِبَكُمْ إِلَّا لَبِيد بْنَ سَهْلٍ رَجُلًا مِنَّا لَهُ صَلَاحٌ وَإِسْلَامٌ. فَلَمَّا سَمِعَ لَبِيدٌ اخْتَرَطَ سَيْفَهُ وَقَالَ: أَنَا أَسْرِقُ؟ وَاللَّهِ لَيُخَالِطَنَّكُمْ هَذَا السَّيْفُ، أَوْ لَتُبَيِّنُنَّ هَذِهِ السَّرِقَةَ. قَالُوا: إِلَيْكَ عَنَّا أَيُّهَا الرَّجُلُ، فَمَا أَنْتَ بِصَاحِبِهَا. فَسَأَلْنَا فِي الدَّارِ حَتَّى لَمْ نَشُكَّ أَنَّهُمْ أَصْحَابُهَا.

فَقَالَ لِي عَمِّي: يَا ابْنَ أَخِي، لَوْ أتيتَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَذَكَرْتَ ذَلِكَ لَهُ. قَالَ قَتَادَةُ: فَأَتَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَقَلْتُ: إِنَّ أَهْلَ بَيْتٍ مِنَّا أَهَّلُ جَفَاءٍ عَمَدُوا إِلَى عَمِّي رِفَاعَةَ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، فنَقَبوا مُشْرَبَةً لَهُ، وَأَخَذُوا سِلَاحَهُ وَطَعَامَهُ. فَلْيردوا عَلَيْنَا سِلَاحَنَا، فَأَمَّا الطَّعَامُ فَلَا حَاجَةَ لَنَا فِيهِ. فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ "سآمُرُ فِي ذَلِكَ".

فَلَمَّا سَمِعَ بَنُو أُبَيْرق أَتَوْا رَجُلًا مِنْهُمْ يُقَالُ لَهُ: أُسَير بْنُ عمْرو فَكَلَّمُوهُ فِي ذَلِكَ، فَاجْتَمَعَ فِي ذَلِكَ أُنَاسٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الدَّارِ فَقَالُوا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنَّ قَتَادَةَ بْنَ النُّعْمَانِ وَعَمَّهُ عَمِدَا إِلَى أَهْلِ بَيْتٍ مِنَّا أَهْلِ إِسْلَامٍ وَصَلَاحٍ، يَرْمُونَهُمْ بِالسَّرِقَةِ مِنْ غَيْرِ بَيِّنَةٍ وَلَا ثَبْتٍ. قَالَ قَتَادَةُ: فَأَتَيْتُ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ فَكَلَّمْتُهُ، فَقَالَ: "عَمَدْتَ إِلَى أَهْلِ بَيْتٍ ذُكر مِنْهُمْ إِسْلَامٌ وَصَلَاحٌ، تَرْمِيهِمْ بِالسَّرِقَةِ عَلَى غَيْرِ ثَبَت وَلَا بَيِّنَةٍ؟ ؟

قَالَ: فَرَجَعْتُ ولَوَدِدت أَنِّي خَرَجْتُ مِنْ بَعْضِ مَالِي، وَلَمْ أُكَلِّمْ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ فِي ذَلِكَ، فَأَتَانِي عَمِّي رِفَاعَةُ فَقَالَ: يَا ابْنَ أَخِي، مَا صَنَعْتَ؟ فَأَخْبَرْتُهُ بِمَا قَالَ لِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ، فَقَالَ: اللَّهُ الْمُسْتَعَانُ. فَلَمْ نَلْبَثْ أَنْ نَزَلَ الْقُرْآنُ: ﴿إِنَّا أَنزلْنَا إِلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ بِالْحَقِّ لِتَحْكُمَ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ بِمَا أَرَاكَ اللَّهُ وَلا تَكُنْ لِلْخَائِنِينَ خَصِيمًا﴾ بَنِي أُبَيْرِقٍ ﴿وَاسْتَغْفِرِ اللَّهَ﴾ مِمَّا قُلْتَ لِقَتَادَةَ ﴿إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا. وَلا تُجَادِلْ عَنِ الَّذِينَ يَخْتَانُونَ أَنْفُسَهُمْ [إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ مَنْ كَانَ خَوَّانًا أَثِيمًا. يَسْتَخْفُونَ مِنَ النَّاسِ وَلا يَسْتَخْفُونَ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَهُوَ مَعَهُمْ] ﴾ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿رَحِيمًا﴾ أَيْ: لَوِ اسْتَغْفَرُوا اللَّهَ لَغَفَرَ لَهُمْ ﴿وَمَنْ يَكْسِبْ إِثْمًا فَإِنَّمَا يَكْسِبُهُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ﴾ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿إِثْمًا مُبِينًا﴾ قَوْلُهُمْ لِلَبِيدٍ: ﴿وَلَوْلا فَضْلُ اللهِ عَلَيْكَ وَرَحْمَتُهُ﴾ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿فَسَوْفَ نُؤْتِيهِ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا﴾

فَلَمَّا نَزَلَ الْقُرْآنُ أَتَى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ بِالسِّلَاحِ فردَّه إِلَى رِفَاعَةَ.

فَقَالَ قَتَادَةُ: لَمَّا أَتَيْتُ عَمِّي بِالسِّلَاحِ وَكَانَ شَيْخًا، قَدْ عَشَا أَوْ عَسَا -الشَّكُّ مِنْ أَبِي عِيسَى -فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ وَكُنْتُ أَرَى إِسْلَامَهُ مَدْخُولًا فَلَمَّا أَتَيْتُهُ بِالسِّلَاحِ قَالَ: يَا ابْنَ أَخِي، هُوَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ. فَعَرَفْتُ أَنَّ إِسْلَامَهُ كَانَ صَحِيحًا، فَلَمَّا نَزَلَ الْقُرْآنُ لَحِقَ بُشَيرٌ بِالْمُشْرِكِينَ، فَنَزَلَ عَلَى سُلافةَ بِنْتِ سَعْدِ بْنِ سُمَية، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَنْ يُشَاقِقِ الرَّسُولَ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُ الْهُدَى وَيَتَّبِعْ غَيْرَ سَبِيلِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ نُوَلِّهِ مَا تَوَلَّى وَنُصْلِهِ جَهَنَّمَ وَسَاءَتْ مَصِيرًا. إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَغْفِرُ أَنْ يُشْرَكَ بِهِ وَيَغْفِرُ مَا دُونَ ذَلِكَ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ وَمَنْ يُشْرِكْ بِاللَّهِ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ ضَلالا بَعِيدًا﴾ فَلَمَّا نَزَلَ عَلَى سُلَافَةَ رَمَاهَا حَسَّانُ بْنُ ثَابِتٍ بِأَبْيَاتٍ مِنْ شِعْرِهِ، فَأَخَذَتْ رَحْلَهُ فَوَضَعَتْهُ عَلَى رَأْسِهَا، ثُمَّ خَرَجَتْ بِهِ فَرَمَتْ بِهِ فِي الْأَبْطَحِ، ثُمَّ قَالَتْ: أهديتَ لِي شِعْر حَسَّانَ؟ مَا كنتَ تَأْتِينِي بِخَيْرٍ.

لَفْظُ التِّرْمِذِيِّ، ثُمَّ قَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: هَذَا حَدِيثٌ غَرِيبٌ لَا نَعْلَمُ أَحَدًا أَسْنَدَهُ غَيْرَ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ الْحَرَّانِيِّ: وَرَوَى يُونُسُ بْنُ بُكَير وَغَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ عُمَر بْنِ قَتَادَةَ مُرْسَلًا لَمْ يَذْكُرُوا فِيهِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ.

وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ حَاتِمٍ عَنْ هَاشِمِ بْنِ الْقَاسِمِ الْحَرَّانِيِّ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، بِهِ بِبَعْضِهِ.

وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ الْمُنْذِرِ فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ -يَعْنِي الصَّائِغَ -حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ أحمد ابن أَبِي شُعَيْبٍ الْحَرَّانِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ -فَذَكَرَهُ بِطُولِهِ.

وَرَوَاهُ أَبُو الشَّيْخِ الْأَصْبَهَانِيُّ فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ أَيُّوبَ وَالْحَسَنِ بْنِ يَعْقُوبَ، كِلَاهُمَا عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ أحمد بن أبي شعيب الحراني، عن محمد بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، بِهِ. ثُمَّ قَالَ فِي آخِرِهِ: قَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ: سَمِعَ مِنِّي هَذَا الْحَدِيثَ يَحْيَى بْنُ مَعِينٍ، وَأَحْمَدُ بْنُ حَنْبَلٍ، وَإِسْحَاقُ بْنُ إِسْرَائِيلَ .

وَقَدْ رَوَى الْحَاكِمُ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ النَّيْسَابُورِيُّ هَذَا الْحَدِيثَ فِي كِتَابِهِ "الْمُسْتَدْرَكِ" عَنْ أَبِي الْعَبَّاسِ الْأَصَمِّ، عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْجَبَّارِ العُطاردي، عَنْ يُونُسَ بْنِ بُكَير، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ -بِمَعْنَاهُ أَتَمَّ مِنْهُ، وَفِيهِ الشِّعْرُ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: وَهَذَا حَدِيثٌ صَحِيحٌ عَلَى شَرْطِ مُسْلِمٍ، وَلَمْ يُخْرِجَاهُ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿يَسْتَخْفُونَ مِنَ النَّاسِ وَلا يَسْتَخْفُونَ مِنَ اللَّهِ [وَهُوَ مَعَهُمْ إِذْ يُبَيِّتُونَ مَا لَا يَرْضَى مِنَ الْقَوْلِ] ﴾ الْآيَةَ، هَذَا إِنْكَارٌ عَلَى الْمُنَافِقِينَ فِي كَوْنِهِمْ يَسْتَخْفُونَ بِقَبَائِحِهِمْ مِنَ النَّاسِ لِئَلَّا يُنْكِرُوا عَلَيْهِمْ، وَيُجَاهِرُونَ اللَّهَ بِهَا لِأَنَّهُ مُطَّلِعٌ عَلَى سَرَائِرِهِمْ وَعَالَمٌ بِمَا فِي ضَمَائِرِهِمْ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿وَهُوَ مَعَهُمْ إِذْ يُبَيِّتُونَ مَا لَا يَرْضَى مِنَ الْقَوْلِ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطًا﴾ تَهْدِيدٌ لَهُمْ وَوَعِيدٌ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿هَا أَنْتُمْ هَؤُلاءِ جَادَلْتُمْ عَنْهُمْ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا [فَمَنْ يُجَادِلُ اللَّهَ عَنْهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ أَمْ مَنْ يَكُونُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَكِيلا] ﴾ أَيْ: هَبْ أَنَّ هَؤُلَاءِ انْتَصَرُوا فِي الدُّنْيَا بِمَا أَبْدَوْهُ أَوْ أبدى لهم عند الحكام الذين يَحْكُمُونَ بِالظَّاهِرِ -وَهُمْ مُتَعَبدون بِذَلِكَ -فَمَاذَا يَكُونُ صَنِيعُهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ بَيْنَ يَدَيِ اللَّهِ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، الَّذِي يَعْلَمُ السِّرَّ وَأَخْفَى؟ وَمَنْ ذَا الَّذِي يَتَوَكَّلُ لَهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ فِي تَرْوِيجِ دَعْوَاهُمْ؟ أَيْ: لَا أَحَدَ يَكُونُ يَوْمَئِذٍ لَهُمْ وَكِيلًا وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿أَمْ مَنْ يَكُونُ عَلَيْهِمْ وَكِيلا﴾

---

## 4:110-113 — verses 110-113 (shared commentary)

### Translation

He, exalted is He, informs of His generosity and bounty: that everyone who turns to Him in repentance, He turns to him in acceptance, whatever the sin may be.

He, exalted, said: {And whoever does evil or wrongs himself, then seeks forgiveness from Allah, will find Allah Forgiving and Merciful.}

Ali ibn Abi Talha said, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, that he said concerning this verse: Allah informed His servants of His forbearance, His pardon, His generosity, and the vastness of His mercy and His forgiveness. So whoever commits a sin, small or great, {then seeks forgiveness from Allah, will find Allah Forgiving and Merciful}, even if his sins were greater than the heavens, the earth, and the mountains. Reported by Ibn Jarir.

Ibn Jarir also said: [chain] Muhammad ibn al-Muthanna, from Muhammad ibn Abi Adi, from Shu'bah, from Asim, from Abu Wa'il, who said: Abdullah said: When one of the Children of Israel committed a sin, he would find in the morning that the expiation for that sin had been written on his door, and if urine touched any part of his garment, he would cut it out with scissors. A man then said: Allah has indeed given the Children of Israel something good. Abdullah said: Allah has given you something better than what He gave them. He made water a purifier for you, and He said: {And those who, when they commit an indecency or wrong themselves, remember Allah and seek forgiveness for their sins} [Al Imran: 135], and He said: {And whoever does evil or wrongs himself, then seeks forgiveness from Allah, will find Allah Forgiving and Merciful.}

He also said: [chain] Ya'qub, from Hushaym, from Ibn Awn, from Habib ibn Abi Thabit, who said: A woman came to Abdullah ibn Mughaffal and asked him about a woman who committed fornication and became pregnant, and when she gave birth, she killed her child. Abdullah ibn Mughaffal said: What is there for her? The Fire is for her! She turned away weeping. He then called her back and said: I see your case as one of two matters only: {And whoever does evil or wrongs himself, then seeks forgiveness from Allah, will find Allah Forgiving and Merciful.} He said: She wiped her eyes, and then went on her way.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Abd al-Rahman ibn Mahdi, from Shu'bah, from Uthman ibn al-Mughirah, who said: I heard Ali ibn Rabi'ah of Banu Asad narrating from Asma, or from Ibn Asma of Banu Fazarah, who said: Ali, may Allah be pleased with him, said: When I heard something from the Messenger of Allah, Allah benefited me by it as much as He willed to benefit me. And Abu Bakr told me, and Abu Bakr spoke the truth, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "There is no Muslim who commits a sin, then performs ablution and prays two rak'ahs, and then seeks Allah's forgiveness for that sin, but he is forgiven." And he recited these two verses: {And whoever does evil or wrongs himself, [then seeks forgiveness from Allah, will find Allah Forgiving and Merciful]} and {And those who, when they commit an indecency or wrong themselves} to the end of the verse.

We have already discussed this hadith and attributed it to those among the compilers of the Sunan who reported it, and we mentioned the criticism found in its chain in the section on the reports of Abu Bakr al-Siddiq, may Allah be pleased with him. Some of that was also mentioned earlier in Surat Al Imran.

Ibn Mardawayh reported it in his tafsir by another route from Ali, saying: [chain] Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Ziyad, from Ibrahim ibn Ishaq al-Harbi, from Dawud ibn Mihran al-Dabbagh, from Umar ibn Yazid, from Abu Ishaq, from Abd Khayr, from Ali, who said: I heard Abu Bakr, that is al-Siddiq, say: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "There is no servant who commits a sin, then stands and performs ablution, doing it well, then stands and prays and seeks forgiveness for his sin, but it becomes a duty upon Allah to forgive him, because He says: {And whoever does evil or wrongs himself, [then seeks forgiveness from Allah, will find Allah Forgiving and Merciful]}."

He then reported it by the route of Aban ibn Abi Ayyash, from Abu Ishaq al-Sabi'i, from al-Harith, from Ali, from al-Siddiq, in similar wording. This chain is not sound.

Ibn Mardawayh said: [chain] Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Duhaym, from Ahmad ibn Hazim, from Musa ibn Marwan al-Raqqi, from Mubashshir ibn Isma'il al-Halabi, from Tammam ibn Najih, from Ka'b ibn Dhuhl al-Azdi, who said: I heard Abu al-Darda narrating: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when we sat around him, if he had some need and got up for it intending to return, would leave his sandals in his place of sitting, or something of what he was wearing. On one occasion he got up and left his sandals. Abu al-Darda said: I took a small waterskin and followed him. He went on for a while, then returned without having attended to his need, and said: "One came to me from my Lord and said: 'Indeed, {whoever does evil or wrongs himself, then seeks forgiveness from Allah, will find Allah Forgiving and Merciful}.' So I wanted to give my companions the good news." Abu al-Darda said: The verse before it had been difficult for the people: {Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it}, so I said: O Messenger of Allah, even if he committed fornication or theft, then sought forgiveness from his Lord, will he be forgiven? He said: "Yes." I asked a second time, and he said: "Yes." Then I asked a third time, and he said: "Yes, even if he committed fornication or theft, then sought forgiveness from Allah, he will be forgiven, despite the displeasure of Uwaymir's nose." He said: Then I saw Abu al-Darda striking his own nose with his finger.

This is a very gharib (singular) hadith by this route with this wording, and there is weakness in its chain.

His saying: {And whoever earns a sin, earns it only against himself, [and Allah is Knowing and Wise]} is like His saying, exalted is He: {And no bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another, and if a heavily laden soul calls another to carry its load, nothing of it will be carried, even if he be a close relative} [Fatir: 18]. That is, no one commits a crime against another, and every soul bears only what it has done, no one else carries it for it. Hence He, exalted, said: {And Allah is Knowing and Wise}, meaning that this comes from His knowledge, His wisdom, His justice, and His mercy.

Then He said: {And whoever earns a fault or a sin, then casts it upon an innocent person, [has borne a calumny and a manifest sin]}, meaning as when Banu Ubayriq, through their vile deed, accused that righteous man, who was Labid ibn Sahl, as mentioned earlier in the hadith, or Zayd ibn al-Samin the Jew according to what others have said. He was innocent, while they were the wrongdoers and traitors, as Allah made known to His Messenger ﷺ. This rebuke and reprimand is general, applying to them and to anyone else who is characterized in the same way and commits a fault like theirs, for such a person deserves a punishment like theirs.

His saying: {And were it not for the bounty of Allah upon you and His mercy, a party among them would have resolved to lead you astray, but they lead astray none but themselves, and they cannot harm you in anything}. Imam Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Hashim ibn al-Qasim al-Harrani, in what he wrote to me, from Muhammad ibn Salamah, from Muhammad ibn Ishaq, from Asim ibn Umar ibn Qatadah al-Ansari, from his father, from his grandfather Qatadah ibn al-Nu'man, and he mentioned the story of Banu Ubayriq, whereupon Allah revealed: {a party among them would have resolved to lead you astray, but they lead astray none but themselves, and they cannot harm you in anything}, meaning Usayr ibn Urwah and his companions. That is, when they praised Banu Ubayriq and blamed Qatadah ibn al-Nu'man for having accused them, when they were, in their view, righteous and innocent, and the matter was not as they had reported it to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. Therefore Allah revealed the decisive clarification of the case for His Messenger ﷺ.

Then He bestowed His favor upon him by supporting him in all circumstances, and by protecting him, and by what He had sent down to him of the Book, which is the Qur'an, and the wisdom, which is the Sunnah: {and taught you what you did not know}, that is, before this was revealed to you, as in His saying: {And thus We have revealed to you a spirit from Our command; you did not know what the Book was, nor faith, but We made it a light by which We guide whom We will of Our servants. And indeed, you guide to a straight path, the path of Allah, to whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Indeed, to Allah do all matters return} [al-Shura: 52-53], and He, exalted, said: {And you were not expecting that the Book would be revealed to you, except as a mercy from your Lord} [al-Qasas: 86]. Hence He, exalted, said: {And the bounty of Allah upon you has been great.}

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يُخْبِرُ، تَعَالَى، عَنْ كَرَمِهِ وَجُودِهِ: أَنَّ كُلَّ مَنْ تَابَ إِلَيْهِ تَابَ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَيِّ ذَنْبٍ كَانَ.

فَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا أَوْ يَظْلِمْ نَفْسَهُ ثُمَّ يَسْتَغْفِرِ اللَّهَ يَجِدِ اللَّهَ غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا﴾

قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ فِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: أَخْبَرَ اللَّهُ عِبَادَهُ بِحِلْمِهِ وَعَفْوِهِ وَكَرَمِهِ وَسَعة رَحْمَتِهِ، وَمَغْفِرَتِهِ، فَمَنْ أَذْنَبَ ذَنَبًا صَغِيرًا كَانَ أَوْ كَبِيرًا ﴿ثُمَّ يَسْتَغْفِرِ اللَّهَ يَجِدِ اللَّهَ غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا﴾ وَلَوْ كَانَتْ ذنوبه أعظم من السموات وَالْأَرْضِ وَالْجِبَالِ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُثَنَّى، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي وَائِلٍ قَالَ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: كَانَ بَنُو إِسْرَائِيلَ إِذَا أَصَابَ أحدُهم ذَنْبًا أَصْبَحَ قَدْ كُتب كَفَّارَةُ ذَلِكَ الذَّنْبِ عَلَى بَابِهِ، وَإِذَا أَصَابَ الْبَوْلُ شَيْئًا مِنْهُ قَرَضَهُ بِالْمِقْرَاضِ فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ: لَقَدْ آتَى اللَّهُ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ خَيْرًا -فَقَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: مَا آتَاكُمُ اللَّهُ خَيْرًا مِمَّا آتَاهُمْ، جَعَلَ الْمَاءَ لَكُمْ طَهُورًا، وَقَالَ: ﴿وَالَّذِينَ إِذَا فَعَلُوا فَاحِشَةً أَوْ ظَلَمُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ ذَكَرُوا اللَّهَ فَاسْتَغْفَرُوا لِذُنُوبِهِمْ﴾ [آلِ عِمْرَانَ: ١٣٥] وَقَالَ ﴿وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا أَوْ يَظْلِمْ نَفْسَهُ ثُمَّ يَسْتَغْفِرِ اللَّهَ يَجِدِ اللَّهَ غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا﴾

وَقَالَ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنِي يَعْقُوبُ، حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْم، حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عَوْن، عَنْ حَبِيبِ بْنِ أَبِي ثَابِتٍ قَالَ: جَاءْتِ امْرَأَةٌ إِلَى عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُغَفَّل فَسَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ امْرَأَةٍ فَجَرت فَحَبَلَتْ، فَلَمَّا وَلَدَتْ قَتَلَتْ وَلَدَهَا؟ قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُغَفَّلٍ: مَا لَهَا؟ لَهَا النَّارُ! فَانْصَرَفَتْ وَهِيَ تَبْكِي، فَدَعَاهَا ثُمَّ قَالَ: مَا أَرَى أَمْرَكِ إِلَّا أَحَدَ أَمْرَيْنِ: ﴿وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا أَوْ يَظْلِمْ نَفْسَهُ ثُمَّ يَسْتَغْفِرِ اللَّهَ يَجِدِ اللَّهَ غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا﴾ قَالَ: فَمَسَحَتْ عَيْنَهَا، ثُمَّ مَضَتْ .

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ الْمُغِيرَةِ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عَلِيَّ بْنَ رَبِيعَةَ مِنْ بَنِي أَسَدٍ، يُحَدِّثُ عَنْ أَسْمَاءَ -أَوِ ابْنِ أَسْمَاءَ مِنْ بَنِي فَزَارَةَ -قَالَ: قَالَ عَلِيٌّ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ: كُنْتُ إِذَا سَمِعْتُ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ شَيْئًا نَفَعَنِي اللَّهُ بِمَا شَاءَ أَنْ يَنْفَعَنِي مِنْهُ. وَحَدَّثَنِي أَبُو بَكْرٍ -وَصَدَقَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ -قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَا مِنْ مُسْلِمٍ يُذْنِبُ ذَنْبًا ثُمَّ يَتَوَضَّأُ فَيُصَلِّي رَكْعَتَيْنِ، ثُمَّ يَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ لِذَلِكَ الذَّنْبِ إِلَّا غُفِرَ لَهُ". وَقَرَأَ هَاتَيْنِ الْآيَتَيْنِ: ﴿وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا أَوْ يَظْلِمْ نَفْسَهُ [ثُمَّ يَسْتَغْفِرِ اللَّهَ يَجِدِ اللَّهَ غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا] ﴾ ﴿وَالَّذِينَ إِذَا فَعَلُوا فَاحِشَةً أَوْ ظَلَمُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ﴾ الآية .

وقد تكلما عَلَى هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ، وَعَزَيْنَاهُ إِلَى مَنْ رَوَاهُ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ السُّنَنِ، وَذَكَرَنَا مَا فِي سَنَدِهِ مِنْ مَقَالٍ فِي مُسْنَدِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ الصِّدِّيقِ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ. وَقَدْ تَقَدَّمَ بَعْضُ ذَلِكَ فِي سُورَةِ آلِ عِمْرَانَ أَيْضًا.

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَرْدُويه فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ مِنْ وَجْهٍ آخَرَ عَنْ عَلِيٍّ فَقَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ، حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ الْحَرْبِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا دَاوُدُ بْنُ مِهْران الدَّبَّاغُ، حَدَّثَنَا عُمَرُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ خَيْرٍ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا بَكْرٍ -هُوَ الصِّدِّيقُ - يَقُولُ: سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَقُولُ: "مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ أَذْنَبَ فَقَامَ فَتَوَضَّأَ فَأَحْسَنَ وُضُوءَهُ، ثُمَّ قَامَ فَصَلَّى وَاسْتَغْفَرَ مِنْ ذَنْبِهِ، إِلَّا كَانَ حَقًّا عَلَى اللَّهِ أَنْ يَغْفِرَ لَهُ؛ لِأَنَّهُ يَقُولُ: ﴿وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا أَوْ يَظْلِمْ نَفْسَهُ [ثُمَّ يَسْتَغْفِرِ اللَّهَ يَجِدِ اللَّهَ غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا] ﴾ .

ثُمَّ رَوَاهُ مِنْ طَرِيقِ أَبَانِ بْنِ أَبِي عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ السَّبِيعي، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، عَنِ الصِّدِّيقِ -بِنَحْوِهِ. وَهَذَا إِسْنَادٌ لَا يَصِحُّ .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ دُحَيم حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ حَازِمٍ، حَدَّثَنَا مُوسَى بْنُ مَرْوَانَ الرَّقِّي، حَدَّثَنَا مُبَشِّر بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ الْحَلَبِيُّ، عَنْ تَمَّامِ بْنِ نَجِيح، حَدَّثَنِي كَعْبُ بْنُ ذُهْل الْأَزْدِيُّ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا الدَّرْدَاءِ يُحَدِّثُ قَالَ: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ إِذَا جَلَسْنَا حَوْلَهُ، وَكَانَتْ لَهُ حَاجَةٌ فَقَامَ إِلَيْهَا وَأَرَادَ الرُّجُوعَ، تَرَكَ نَعْلَيْهِ فِي مَجْلِسِهِ أَوْ بَعْضَ مَا عَلَيْهِ، وَإِنَّهُ قَامَ فَتَرَكَ نَعْلَيْهِ. قَالَ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ: فَأَخَذَ رَكْوَة مِنْ مَاءٍ فَاتَّبَعْتُهُ، فَمَضَى سَاعَةً، ثُمَّ رَجَعَ وَلَمْ يَقْضِ حَاجَتَهُ، فَقَالَ: "إِنَّهُ أَتَانِي آتٍ مِنْ رَبِّي فَقَالَ: إِنَّهُ: ﴿مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا أَوْ يَظْلِمْ نَفْسَهُ ثُمَّ يَسْتَغْفِرِ اللَّهَ يَجِدِ اللَّهَ غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا﴾ فَأَرَدْتُ أَنْ أُبَشِّرَ أَصْحَابِي". قَالَ أَبُو الدَّرْدَاءِ: وَكَانَتْ قَدْ شَقَّتْ عَلَى النَّاسِ الْآيَةُ الَّتِي قَبِلَهَا: ﴿مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ﴾ فَقُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، وَإِنَّ زَنَى وَإِنْ سَرَقَ، ثُمَّ اسْتَغْفَرَ رَبَّهُ، غَفَرَ لَهُ؟ قَالَ: "نَعَمْ" قُلْتُ الثَّانِيَةَ، قَالَ: "نَعَمْ"، ثُمَّ قُلْتُ الثَّالِثَةَ، قَالَ: "نَعَمْ، وَإِنَّ زَنَى وَإِنْ سَرَقَ، ثُمَّ اسْتَغْفَرَ اللَّهَ غَفَرَ لَهُ عَلَى رَغْمِ أَنْفِ عُوَيْمِرٍ". قَالَ: فَرَأَيْتُ أَبَا الدَّرْدَاءِ يَضْرِبُ أَنْفَ نَفْسِهِ بأصبعه.

هَذَا حَدِيثٌ غَرِيبٌ جِدًّا مِنْ هَذَا الْوَجْهِ بِهَذَا السِّيَاقِ، وَفِي إِسْنَادِهِ ضَعْفٌ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَمَنْ يَكْسِبْ إِثْمًا فَإِنَّمَا يَكْسِبُهُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ [وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا] ﴾ كَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَلا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَى [وَإِنْ تَدْعُ مُثْقَلَةٌ إِلَى حِمْلِهَا لَا يُحْمَلْ مِنْهُ شَيْءٌ وَلَوْ كَانَ ذَا قُرْبَى] ﴾ الْآيَةَ: [فَاطِرٍ: ١٨] يَعْنِي أَنَّهُ لَا يَجْنِي أَحَدٌ عَلَى أَحَدٍ، وَإِنَّمَا عَلَى كُلِّ نَفْسٍ مَا عَمِلَتْ، لَا يَحْمِلُ عَنْهَا غَيْرُهَا؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَكِيمًا﴾ أَيْ: مِنْ عِلْمِهِ وَحَكْمَتِهِ، وَعَدْلِهِ وَرَحْمَتِهِ كَانَ ذَلِكَ.

ثُمَّ قَالَ: ﴿وَمَنْ يَكْسِبْ خَطِيئَةً أَوْ إِثْمًا ثُمَّ يَرْمِ بِهِ بَرِيئًا [فَقَدِ احْتَمَلَ بُهْتَانًا وَإِثْمًا مُبِينًا] ﴾ يَعْنِي: كَمَا اتَّهَمَ بَنُو أُبَيْرق بِصَنِيعِهِمُ الْقَبِيحِ ذَلِكَ الرَّجُلَ الصَّالِحَ، وَهُوَ لَبِيد بْنُ سَهْلٍ، كَمَا تَقَدَّمَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ، أَوْ زَيْدُ بْنُ السَّمِينِ الْيَهُودِيُّ عَلَى مَا قَالَهُ الْآخَرُونَ، وَقَدْ كَانَ بَرِيئًا وَهُمُ الظَّلَمَةُ الْخَوَنَةُ، كَمَا أطلعَ اللَّهُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ رسولَه ﷺ. ثُمَّ هَذَا التَّقْرِيعُ وَهَذَا التَّوْبِيخُ عَامٌّ فِيهِمْ وَفِي غَيْرِهِمْ مِمَّنِ اتَّصَفَ مِثْلَ صِفَتِهِمْ وَارْتَكَبَ مِثْلَ خَطِيئَتِهِمْ، فَعَلَيْهِ مَثَلَ عُقُوبَتِهِمْ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلَوْلا فَضْلُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكَ وَرَحْمَتُهُ لَهَمَّتْ طَائِفَةٌ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُضِلُّوكَ وَمَا يُضِلُّونَ إِلا أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَمَا يَضُرُّونَكَ مِنْ شَيْءٍ﴾ قَالَ الْإِمَامُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: أَنْبَأَنَا هَاشِمُ بْنُ الْقَاسِمِ الْحَرَّانِيُّ فِيمَا كَتَبَ إليَّ، حَدَّثَنَا محمد بن سلمة، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ. عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ عُمَرَ بْنِ قَتَادَةَ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ عن أبيه، عن جده قتادة بن النعمان -وَذَكَرَ قِصَّةَ بَنِي أُبَيْرِقٍ، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿لَهَمَّتْ طَائِفَةٌ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُضِلُّوكَ وَمَا يُضِلُّونَ إِلا أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَمَا يَضُرُّونَكَ مِنْ شَيْءٍ﴾ يَعْنِي: أُسَيْر بْنَ عُرْوَةَ وَأَصْحَابَهُ. يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ لَمَّا أَثْنَوْا عَلَى بَنِي أُبَيْرِقٍ وَلَامُوا قَتَادَةَ بْنَ النُّعْمَانِ فِي كَوْنِهِ اتَّهَمَهُمْ، وَهُمْ صُلَحَاءُ بُرَآءُ، وَلَمْ يَكُنِ الْأَمْرُ كَمَا أَنْهَوْهُ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ؛ وَلِهَذَا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ فَصْلَ الْقَضِيَّةِ وَجَلَاءَهَا لِرَسُولِهِ ﷺ.

ثُمَّ امْتَنَّ عَلَيْهِ بِتَأْيِيدِهِ إِيَّاهُ فِي جَمِيعِ الْأَحْوَالِ، وَعِصْمَتِهِ لَهُ، وَمَا أَنْزَلَ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ الْكِتَابِ، وَهُوَ الْقُرْآنُ، وَالْحِكْمَةُ، وَهِيَ السُّنَّةُ: ﴿وَعَلَّمَكَ مَا لَمْ تَكُنْ تَعْلَمُ﴾ أَيْ: [مِنْ] قَبِلَ نُزُولِ ذَلِكَ عَلَيْكَ، كَقَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَكَذَلِكَ أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْكَ رُوحًا مِنْ أَمْرِنَا مَا كُنْتَ تَدْرِي مَا الْكِتَابُ [وَلا الإيمَانُ وَلَكِنْ جَعَلْنَاهُ نُورًا نَهْدِي بِهِ مَنْ نَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا وَإِنَّكَ لَتَهْدِي إِلَى صِرَاطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ. صِرَاطِ اللَّهِ الَّذِي لَهُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الأرْضِ أَلا إِلَى اللَّهِ تَصِيرُ الأمُورُ] ﴾ [الشُّورَى: ٥٢، ٥٣] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَا كُنْتَ تَرْجُو أَنْ يُلْقَى إِلَيْكَ الْكِتَابُ إِلا رَحْمَةً مِنْ رَبِّكَ﴾ [الْقَصَصِ: ٨٦] ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَكَانَ فَضْلُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْكَ عَظِيمًا﴾

---

## 4:114-115 — verses 114-115 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Almighty says: {There is no good in much of their private counsel} meaning: people's talk, {except for one who enjoins charity or kindness or reconciliation between people}, that is, except the private counsel of one who says that, as has come in the hadith which Ibn Mardawayh narrated:

[chain] Muhammad ibn Yazid ibn Khunays said: We entered upon Sufyan al-Thawri to visit him while he was ill, and he gestured toward the house of the perfumers, and Sa'id ibn Hassan al-Makhzumi came in to him. Sufyan al-Thawri said to him: The hadith you narrated to me from Umm Salih, repeat it to me. He said: Umm Salih narrated to me, from Safiyya bint Shayba, from Umm Habiba, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "All the speech of the son of Adam counts against him, not for him, except enjoining what is good or forbidding what is evil, or remembrance of Allah, Mighty and Majestic." Sufyan said: So I pressed him, and Muhammad ibn Yazid said: How severe is this hadith! Sufyan said: What is severe about this hadith? It has only come from a woman narrating from a woman. This is in the Book of Allah with which your Prophet ﷺ was sent. Have you not heard Allah say in His Book: {There is no good in much of their private counsel, except for one who enjoins charity or kindness or reconciliation between people}? This is exactly that. Or have you not heard Allah say: {The Day when the Spirit and the angels stand in rows, they do not speak except one whom the Most Merciful permits, and he says what is correct} [al-Naba: 38]? This is exactly that. Or have you not heard Allah say in His Book: {By the time. Indeed, mankind is in loss. Except those who believe and do righteous deeds and enjoin upon one another truth and enjoin upon one another patience} [Surat al-Asr]? This is exactly that.

This hadith was narrated by al-Tirmidhi and Ibn Majah from the hadith of Muhammad ibn Yazid ibn Khunays, from Sa'id ibn Hassan, with this wording, but they did not mention the words of al-Thawri to their end. Al-Tirmidhi then said: gharib (singular); we do not know it except from the hadith of Ibn Khunays.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Yaqub told us, my father told us, Salih ibn Kaysan told us, Muhammad ibn Muslim ibn Ubaydullah ibn Shihab told us, that Humayd ibn Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf informed him, that his mother Umm Kulthum bint Uqba informed him, that she heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "The liar is not one who reconciles between people, spreading good, or he says, speaking good." And she said: I never heard him permit leniency in anything people say except in three matters: in war, and reconciliation between people, and a man's speech to his wife, and a woman's speech to her husband. He said: Umm Kulthum bint Uqba was among the emigrant women who gave the pledge of allegiance to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.

This was narrated by the group (of hadith compilers), other than Ibn Majah, through various chains from al-Zuhri, with similar wording.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Abu Muawiya told us, from al-A'mash, from Amr ibn Murra, from Salim ibn Abi al-Ja'd, from Umm al-Darda, from Abu al-Darda, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Shall I not inform you of something better in rank than prayer, fasting, and charity?" They said: Yes indeed. He said: "Setting right relations between people." He said: "And the corruption of relations between people, that is the shaver (the destroyer)."

This was narrated by Abu Dawud and al-Tirmidhi, from the hadith of Abu Muawiya, and al-Tirmidhi said: hasan sahih (good and authentic).

Al-Hafiz Abu Bakr al-Bazzar said: [chain] Muhammad ibn Abd al-Rahim told us, Surayj ibn Yunus told us, Abd al-Rahman ibn Abdullah ibn Umar told us, my father told us, from Humayd, from Anas, that the Prophet ﷺ said to Abu Ayyub: "Shall I not point you to a trade?" He said: Yes indeed. He said: "You strive to bring about reconciliation between people when they have fallen into discord, and you bring them closer together when they have grown distant." Then al-Bazzar said: Abd al-Rahman ibn Abdullah al-Umari is layyin (weak), and he has narrated hadiths on which he was not corroborated.

Therefore He said: {And whoever does that seeking the pleasure of Allah}, that is, being sincere in that and seeking the reward of that with Allah, Mighty and Majestic, {We will grant him a great reward}, that is, abundant and expansive reward.

His saying: {And whoever opposes the Messenger after guidance has become clear to him}, that is, whoever follows a path other than that of the sacred law which the Messenger ﷺ brought, and thus stands on one side while the sacred law stands on the other side, and that being done deliberately by him after the truth has appeared to him, become clear to him, and become manifest to him. His saying: {and follows a path other than that of the believers}, this is inseparable from the first description, but the opposition may be to the explicit text of the Lawgiver, or it may be to that upon which the Muhammadan community has reached consensus, in matters where their agreement is known with certainty. For protection from error in their collective agreement has been guaranteed to them, as an honor for them and a glorification of their Prophet [ﷺ]. Many authentic hadiths have come regarding this, of which we have mentioned a fair portion in the book "Ahadith al-Usul" (Hadiths of the Foundational Principles). Some scholars have claimed that their meaning reaches the level of tawatur (mass transmission). And that upon which al-Shafi'i, may Allah have mercy on him, relied in arguing that consensus (ijma) is a binding proof whose opposition is forbidden, is this noble verse, after deliberation and lengthy reflection. It is among the best and strongest of deductions, even though some scholars have found this problematic and considered the verse's indication of it to be far-fetched.

Therefore the Almighty threatened concerning this by His saying: {We will let him continue in what he has turned to and We will burn him in Hell, and evil it is as a destination}, that is, if he follows this path, We repay him for that by making it appear good to his heart and adorning it for him, as a gradual leading on (istidraj), just as the Almighty said: {So leave Me with whoever denies this statement. We will progressively lead them on from where they do not know} [al-Qalam: 44]. And the Almighty said: {So when they deviated, Allah caused their hearts to deviate} [al-Saff: 5]. And His saying: {And We leave them in their transgression, wandering blindly} [al-An'am: 110].

And He made the Fire his destination in the Hereafter, because whoever has departed from guidance has no path except to the Fire on the Day of Resurrection, as the Almighty said: {Gather those who wronged and their kinds, and what they used to worship besides Allah, and guide them to the path of the Blazing Fire} [al-Saffat: 22, 23]. And He said: {And the criminals will see the Fire and will be certain that they are to fall into it, and they will not find from it a way of escape} [al-Kahf: 53].

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى: ﴿لَا خَيْرَ فِي كَثِيرٍ مِنْ نَجْوَاهُمْ﴾ يَعْنِي: كَلَامَ النَّاسِ ﴿إِلا مَنْ أَمَرَ بِصَدَقَةٍ أَوْ مَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ إِصْلاحٍ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ﴾ أَيْ: إِلَّا نَجْوَى مَنْ قَالَ ذَلِكَ كَمَا جَاءَ فِي الْحَدِيثِ الَّذِي رَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَرْدُويه:

حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ الْحَارِثِ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ بْنِ خُنَيس قَالَ: دَخَلْنَا عَلَى سُفْيَانَ الثَّوْرِيِّ نَعُودُهُ -وَأَوْمَأَ إِلَى دَارِ الْعَطَّارِينَ -فَدَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ سَعِيدُ بْنُ حَسَّانَ الْمَخْزُومِيُّ فَقَالَ لَهُ سُفْيَانُ الثَّوْرِيُّ: الْحَدِيثُ الَّذِي كُنْتَ حَدَّثْتَنِي بِهِ عَنْ أُمِّ صَالِحٍ اردُدْه عَلَيَّ. فَقَالَ: حَدَّثَتْنِي أُمُّ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ صَفية بِنْتِ شَيْبة، عَنْ أُمِّ حَبيبَة قَالَتْ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "كَلَامُ ابْنِ آدَمَ كُلُّهُ عَلَيْهِ لَا لَهُ مَا خَلَا أَمْرًا بِمَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ نَهْيًا عَنْ مُنْكَرٍ [أَوْ ذِكْرَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ"، قَالَ سُفْيَانُ: فَنَاشَدْتُهُ ] فَقَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ: مَا أَشَدَّ هَذَا الْحَدِيثَ؟ فَقَالَ سُفْيَانُ: وَمَا شِدَّةُ هَذَا الْحَدِيثِ؟ إِنَّمَا جَاءَتْ بِهِ امْرَأَةٌ عَنِ امْرَأَةٍ، هَذَا فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ الَّذِي أُرْسِلَ بِهِ نَبِيُّكُمْ ﷺ أَوَ مَا سَمِعْتَ اللَّهَ يَقُولُ فِي كِتَابِهِ: ﴿لَا خَيْرَ فِي كَثِيرٍ مِنْ نَجْوَاهُمْ إِلا مَنْ أَمَرَ بِصَدَقَةٍ أَوْ مَعْرُوفٍ أَوْ إِصْلاحٍ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ﴾ فَهُوَ هَذَا بِعَيْنِهِ، أَوْ مَا سَمِعْتَ اللَّهَ يَقُولُ: ﴿يَوْمَ يَقُومُ الرُّوحُ وَالْمَلائِكَةُ صَفًّا لَا يَتَكَلَّمُونَ إِلا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ الرَّحْمَنُ وَقَالَ صَوَابًا﴾ [النَّبَأِ: ٣٨] فَهُوَ هَذَا بِعَيْنِهِ، أَوَ مَا سَمِعْتَ اللَّهَ يَقُولُ فِي كِتَابِهِ: ﴿والْعَصْرِ. إِنَّ الإنْسَانَ لَفِي خُسْرٍ. [إِلا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالْحَقِّ وَتَوَاصَوْا بِالصَّبْرِ] ﴾ [سُورَةُ الْعَصْرِ] ، فَهُوَ هَذَا بِعَيْنِهِ.

وَقَدْ رَوَى هَذَا الْحَدِيثَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ وَابْنُ مَاجَهْ مِنْ حَدِيثِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ بْنِ خُنَيس عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ حَسَّانَ، بِهِ. وَلَمْ يَذْكُرَا أَقْوَالَ الثَّوْرِيِّ إِلَى آخِرِهَا، ثُمَّ قَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: غَرِيبٌ لَا نَعْرِفُهُ إِلَّا مِنْ حَدِيثِ ابْنِ خُنَيس . 

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَعْقُوبُ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا صَالِحُ بْنُ كَيْسان، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمِ بْنِ عُبَيد اللَّهِ بْنِ شِهَابٍ: أَنَّ حُمَيْدَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ عَوْفٍ أَخْبَرَهُ، أن أمه أم كلثوم بنت عقبة أَخْبَرَتْهُ: أَنَّهَا سَمِعَتْ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَقُولُ: "لَيْسَ الْكَذَّابُ الَّذِي يُصْلِحُ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ فَيَنْمِي خَيْرًا -أَوْ يَقُولُ خَيْرًا" وَقَالَتْ: لَمْ أَسْمَعْهُ يُرَخِّصُ فِي شَيْءٍ مِمَّا يَقُولُهُ النَّاسُ إِلَّا فِي ثَلَاثٍ: فِي الْحَرْبِ، وَالْإِصْلَاحِ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ، وَحَدِيثِ الرَّجُلِ امْرَأَتَهُ، وَحَدِيثِ الْمَرْأَةِ زَوْجَهَا. قَالَ: وَكَانَتْ أُمُّ كُلْثُومٍ بِنْتُ عُقْبَةَ مِنَ الْمُهَاجِرَاتِ اللَّاتِي بَايَعْنَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ.

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ الْجَمَاعَةُ، سِوَى ابْنِ مَاجَهْ، مِنْ طُرُقٍ، عَنْ الزُّهْرِيِّ، بِهِ نَحْوَهُ .

قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرة عَنْ سَالِمِ بْنِ أَبِي الْجَعْدِ، عَنْ أُمِّ الدَّرْدَاءِ، عَنْ أَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "أَلَا أُخْبِرُكُمْ بِأَفْضَلِ مِنْ دَرَجَةِ الصَّلَاةِ، وَالصِّيَامِ وَالصَّدَقَةِ؟ " قَالُوا: بَلَى. قَالَ: "إِصْلَاحُ ذَاتِ الْبَيْنِ" قَالَ: "وَفَسَادُ ذَاتِ الْبَيْنِ هِيَ الْحَالِقَةُ".

وَرَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ وَالتِّرْمِذِيُّ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ أَبِي مُعَاوِيَةَ، وَقَالَ التِّرْمِذِيُّ: حَسَنٌ صَحِيحٌ .

وَقَالَ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو بَكْرٍ الْبَزَّارُ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحِيمِ، حَدَّثَنَا سُرَيج بْنُ يُونُسَ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ؛ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ ﷺ قَالَ لِأَبِي أَيُّوبَ: "أَلَا أَدُلُّكَ عَلَى تِجَارَةٍ؟ " قَالَ: بَلَى: قَالَ: "تَسْعَى فِي صُلْحٍ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ إِذَا تَفَاسَدُوا، وتُقَارب بَيْنَهُمْ إِذَا تَبَاعَدُوا" ثُمَّ قَالَ الْبَزَّارُ: وَعَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ العُمَري لَيّن، وَقَدْ حَدَّثَ بِأَحَادِيثَ لَمْ يُتَابَعْ عَلَيْهَا .

وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿وَمَنْ يَفْعَلْ ذَلِكَ ابْتِغَاءَ مَرْضَاتِ اللهِ﴾ أَيْ: مُخْلِصًا فِي ذَلِكَ مُحْتَسِبًا ثَوَابَ ذَلِكَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ﴿فَسَوْفَ نُؤْتِيهِ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا﴾ أَيْ: ثَوَابًا كَثِيرًا وَاسِعًا.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَمَنْ يُشَاقِقِ الرَّسُولَ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُ الْهُدَى﴾ أَيْ: وَمَنْ سَلَكَ غَيْرَ طَرِيقِ الشَّرِيعَةِ الَّتِي جَاءَ بِهَا الرَّسُولُ ﷺ، فَصَارَ فِي شِقٍّ وَالشَّرْعُ فِي شِقٍّ، وَذَلِكَ عَنْ عَمْد مِنْهُ بَعْدَمَا ظَهَرَ لَهُ الْحَقُّ وَتَبَيَّنَ لَهُ وَاتَّضَحَ لَهُ. وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَيَتَّبِعْ غَيْرَ سَبِيلِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ﴾ هَذَا مُلَازِمٌ لِلصِّفَةِ الْأُولَى، وَلَكِنْ قَدْ تَكُونُ الْمُخَالَفَةُ لِنَصِّ الشَّارِعِ، وَقَدْ تَكُونُ لِمَا أَجْمَعَتْ عَلَيْهِ الْأُمَّةُ الْمُحَمَّدِيَّةُ، فِيمَا عُلِمَ اتِّفَاقُهُمْ عَلَيْهِ تَحْقِيقًا، فَإِنَّهُ قَدْ ضُمِنت لَهُمُ الْعِصْمَةُ فِي اجْتِمَاعِهِمْ مِنَ الْخَطَأِ، تشريفًا لهم وتعظيما لنبيهم [ﷺ] . وَقَدْ وَرَدَتْ فِي ذَلِكَ أَحَادِيثُ صَحِيحَةٌ كَثِيرَةٌ، قَدْ ذَكَرْنَا مِنْهَا طَرَفًا صَالِحًا فِي كِتَابِ "أَحَادِيثِ الْأُصُولِ"، وَمِنِ الْعُلَمَاءِ مَنِ ادَّعَى تَوَاتُرَ مَعْنَاهَا، وَالَّذِي عَوَّلَ عَلَيْهِ الشَّافِعِيُّ، رَحِمَهُ اللَّهُ، فِي الِاحْتِجَاجِ عَلَى كَوْنِ الْإِجْمَاعِ حُجَّةً تَحْرُم مُخَالَفَتُهُ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ الْكَرِيمَةُ، بَعْدَ التَّرَوِّي وَالْفِكْرِ الطَّوِيلِ. وَهُوَ مِنْ أَحْسَنِ الِاسْتِنْبَاطَاتِ وَأَقْوَاهَا، وَإِنْ كَانَ بَعْضُهُمْ قَدِ اسْتَشْكَلَ ذَلِكَ وَاسْتَبْعَدَ الدِّلَالَةَ مِنْهَا عَلَى ذَلِكَ .

وَلِهَذَا تَوَعَّدَ تَعَالَى عَلَى ذَلِكَ بِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿نُوَلِّهِ مَا تَوَلَّى وَنُصْلِهِ جَهَنَّمَ وَسَاءَتْ مَصِيرًا﴾ أَيْ: إِذَا سَلَكَ هَذِهِ الطَّرِيقَ جَازَيْنَاهُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ، بِأَنْ نُحْسِّنَهَا فِي صَدْرِهِ وَنُزَيِّنَهَا لَهُ -اسْتِدْرَاجًا لَهُ -كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَذَرْنِي وَمَنْ يُكَذِّبُ بِهَذَا الْحَدِيثِ سَنَسْتَدْرِجُهُمْ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ﴾ [الْقَلَمِ: ٤٤] . وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَلَمَّا زَاغُوا أَزَاغَ اللَّهُ قُلُوبَهُمْ﴾ [الصَّفِّ:٥] . وَقَوْلُهُ ﴿وَنَذَرُهُمْ فِي طُغْيَانِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ﴾ [الْأَنْعَامِ: ١١٠] .

وَجَعْلَ النَّارَ مَصِيرَهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ، لِأَنَّ مَنْ خَرَجَ عَنِ الْهُدَى لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ طَرِيقٌ إِلَّا إِلَى النَّارِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿احْشُرُوا الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا وَأَزْوَاجَهُمْ [وَمَا كَانُوا يَعْبُدُونَ. مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ فَاهْدُوهُمْ إِلَى صِرَاطِ الْجَحِيمِ] ﴾ [الصَّافَّاتِ: ٢٢، ٢٣] . وَقَالَ: ﴿وَرَأَى الْمُجْرِمُونَ النَّارَ فَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُمْ مُوَاقِعُوهَا وَلَمْ يَجِدُوا عَنْهَا مَصْرِفًا﴾ [الْكَهْفِ:٥٣] .

---

## 4:116-122 — verses 116-122 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The discussion of this noble verse has already preceded, namely His saying: {Indeed, Allah does not forgive that partners be associated with Him, but He forgives what is less than that [for whom He wills]} the verse [al-Nisa: 48], and we mentioned what pertains to it of hadiths at the beginning of this surah.

Al-Tirmidhi related the hadith of Thuwayr ibn Abi Fakhitah, Sa'id ibn 'Alaqah, from his father, from 'Ali, may Allah be pleased with him, that he said: There is no verse in the Qur'an more beloved to me than this verse: {Indeed, Allah does not forgive that partners be associated with Him, [but He forgives what is less than that for whom He wills]} the verse. Then he said: hasan gharib (a sound, singular report).

And His saying: {And whoever associates partners with Allah has certainly gone far astray} means: he has taken a path other than the true path, has strayed from guidance and become far removed from what is correct, and has destroyed himself and caused himself loss in this world and the next, and has forfeited the happiness of this world and the hereafter.

Regarding His saying: {They call upon nothing but females besides Him}, Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Ubayy ibn Ka'b said regarding {They call upon nothing but females besides Him}: With every idol there is a jinniyyah (female jinn).

[chain] 'A'ishah said regarding {They call upon nothing but females besides Him}: Idols.

Similar statements were related from Abu Salamah ibn 'Abd al-Rahman, 'Urwah ibn al-Zubayr, Mujahid, Abu Malik, al-Suddi, and Muqatil ibn Hayyan.

Juwaybir reported from al-Dahhak concerning [His saying] {They call upon nothing but females besides Him} that he said: The polytheists said: The angels are the daughters of Allah, and we only worship them so that they may bring us near to Allah. He said: They took them as lords and depicted them in the forms of young women, then they carved and adorned them, and said: These resemble the daughters of Allah whom we worship, meaning the angels.

This explanation resembles His saying, Exalted is He: {Have you seen al-Lat and al-'Uzza. And Manat, the third, the other one. Is the male for you and for Him the female? That, then, is an unjust division. They are nothing but names you have named, you and your forefathers, for which Allah has sent down no authority} [al-Najm: 19-23]. And He said, Exalted is He: {And they have made the angels, who are servants of the Most Merciful, females. Did they witness their creation? Their testimony will be recorded, and they will be questioned} [al-Zukhruf: 19]. And He said, Exalted is He: {And they have made between Him and the jinn a lineage, but the jinn have already known that they will be brought [for judgment]. Exalted is Allah above what they describe} [al-Saffat: 158-159].

'Ali ibn Abi Talhah and al-Dahhak reported from Ibn 'Abbas regarding {They call upon nothing but females besides Him} that he said: meaning the dead.

Mubarak, that is, Ibn Fadalah, reported from al-Hasan regarding {They call upon nothing but females besides Him} that al-Hasan said: The females are everything dead that has no spirit in it, whether dry wood or dry stone. Ibn Abi Hatim and Ibn Jarir related this, and it is gharib (a singular report).

Regarding His saying: {And they call upon nothing but a rebellious devil} meaning: he is the one who commanded them to do that, made it appear good to them, and adorned it for them, and they are in truth only worshipping Iblis, as He said, Exalted is He: {Did I not enjoin upon you, O children of Adam, that you should not worship Satan? [Indeed, he is a clear enemy to you]} [Ya-Sin: 60]. And He said, Exalted is He, informing of the angels, that they will say on the Day of Resurrection concerning the polytheists who claimed to worship them in this world: {Rather, they used to worship the jinn; most of them believed in them} [Saba: 41].

Regarding His saying: {Allah has cursed him} meaning: He drove him away and removed him far from His mercy, and expelled him from His nearness.

And He said: {I will surely take from among Your servants a portion appointed} meaning: a specific, determined, known portion. Muqatil ibn Hayyan said: Out of every thousand, nine hundred and ninety-nine go to the Fire, and one to Paradise.

{And I will surely mislead them} meaning: from the truth, {and I will surely arouse in them false hopes} meaning: I will make abandoning repentance appear good to them, and I will promise them false hopes, and I will command them to procrastinate and delay, and I will deceive them regarding themselves.

Regarding His saying: {And I will command them so they will surely slit the ears of cattle}, Qatadah, al-Suddi, and others said: meaning splitting them and making it a mark and sign for the bahirah and the sa'ibah (categories of consecrated livestock among the pre-Islamic Arabs).

{And I will command them so they will surely change the creation of Allah}. Ibn 'Abbas said: this means the castration of animals. The same was related from Ibn 'Umar, Anas, Sa'id ibn al-Musayyib, 'Ikrimah, Abu 'Iyad, Abu Salih, Qatadah, and al-Thawri. A hadith has come forbidding that.

Al-Hasan ibn Abi al-Hasan al-Basri said: this means tattooing. In Sahih Muslim there is a prohibition of tattooing the face, and in one wording: "Allah has cursed whoever does that." And in the Sahih, from Ibn Mas'ud, that he said: Allah has cursed the women who tattoo and those who are tattooed, the women who pluck eyebrows and those who have them plucked, and those who file their teeth apart for beauty, altering the creation of Allah, Mighty and Majestic. Then he said: Should I not curse whom the Messenger of Allah ﷺ cursed, and it is in the Book of Allah, Mighty and Majestic, meaning His saying: {And whatever the Messenger has given you, take it, and whatever he has forbidden you, refrain from it} [al-Hashr: 7].

Ibn 'Abbas said, in one report from him, as did Mujahid, 'Ikrimah as well, Ibrahim al-Nakha'i, al-Hasan, Qatadah, al-Hakam, al-Suddi, al-Dahhak, and 'Ata' al-Khurasani, regarding His saying: {And I will command them so they will surely change the creation of Allah}, meaning: the religion of Allah, Mighty and Majestic. This is like His saying, Exalted is He: {So direct your face toward the religion, inclining to truth. That is the fitrah (natural disposition) of Allah upon which He created mankind. There is no changing the creation of Allah} [al-Rum: 30], according to the view of those who take this as a command, meaning: do not alter the fitrah of Allah, and leave people upon their natural disposition, as is established in the two Sahihs from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Every child is born upon the fitrah, but his parents make him a Jew, a Christian, or a Magian, just as an animal is born whole and intact. Do you perceive in it any deformity?" And in Sahih Muslim, from 'Iyad ibn Himar, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Allah, Mighty and Majestic, said: I created My servants as monotheists inclining to truth, but the devils came to them and turned them away from their religion, and forbade them what I had made lawful for them."

Regarding His saying, Exalted is He: {And whoever takes Satan as an ally instead of Allah has certainly suffered a clear loss} meaning: he has lost this world and the hereafter, and that is a loss that cannot be repaired and whose forfeiture cannot be made up.

Regarding His saying: {He promises them and arouses in them false hopes, but Satan does not promise them except delusion}. This is a report of what actually happens, for Satan promises his allies and gives them false hopes that they are the successful ones in this world and the hereafter, and in that he has lied and fabricated falsehood. Therefore He said: {but Satan does not promise them except delusion}, just as He said, Exalted is He, informing of Iblis on the Day of Return: {And Satan will say, when the matter has been concluded: Indeed, Allah promised you the promise of truth, and I promised you, but I betrayed you. And I had no authority over you [except that I called you and you responded to me. So do not blame me but blame yourselves. I cannot help you, nor can you help me. Indeed, I disbelieved in what you associated with me before]. Indeed, the wrongdoers will have a painful punishment} [Ibrahim: 22].

Regarding His saying, meaning those who find pleasing what he promised them and made them hope for: {their refuge is Hell} meaning: their final destination and outcome on the day of their reckoning, {and they will not find from it any escape} meaning: they have no way out from it, no place to turn, no deliverance, and no refuge.

Then He mentioned the state of the happy, God-fearing ones, and the complete honor that awaits them in their final outcome, saying: {And those who believe and do righteous deeds} meaning: their hearts believed truly and their limbs acted upon what they were commanded of good deeds, and they left what they were forbidden of evil deeds, {We will admit them to gardens beneath which rivers flow} meaning: they direct them wherever they wish and however they wish, {abiding therein forever} meaning: without ceasing and without transferring, {the promise of Allah, in truth} meaning: this is a promise from Allah, and the promise of Allah is known in truth to be something that will certainly occur without fail. Therefore He confirmed it with the verbal noun that indicates the certainty of the report, which is His saying {in truth}. Then He said {And who is more truthful than Allah in speech} meaning: no one is more truthful than Him in speech and report; there is no god but Him, and no lord besides Him. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to say in his sermon: "Indeed, the most truthful speech is the speech of Allah, and the best guidance is the guidance of Muhammad ﷺ, and the worst of matters are the newly invented ones, and every newly invented matter is an innovation, and every innovation is misguidance, and every misguidance is in the Fire."

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

قَدْ تَقَدَّمَ الْكَلَامُ عَلَى هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ الْكَرِيمَةِ، وَهِيَ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَغْفِرُ أَنْ يُشْرَكَ بِهِ وَيَغْفِرُ مَا دُونَ ذَلِكَ [لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ] ﴾ الْآيَةَ [النِّسَاءِ: ٤٨] ، وَذَكَرْنَا مَا يَتَعَلَّقُ بِهَا مِنَ الْأَحَادِيثِ فِي صَدْرِ هَذِهِ السُّورَةِ.

وَقَدْ رَوَى التِّرْمِذِيُّ حَدِيثَ ثُوَيْر بْنِ أَبِي فَاخِتَة سَعِيدِ بْنِ عَلاقَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: مَا فِي الْقُرْآنِ آيَةٌ أَحَبَّ إليَّ مِنْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: ﴿إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَغْفِرُ أَنْ يُشْرَكَ بِهِ [وَيَغْفِرُ مَا دُونَ ذَلِكَ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ] ﴾ الْآيَةَ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: حَسَنٌ غَرِيبٌ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَمَنْ يُشْرِكْ بِاللَّهِ فَقَدْ ضَلَّ ضَلالا بَعِيدًا﴾ أَيْ: فَقَدْ سَلَكَ غَيْرَ الطَّرِيقِ الْحَقِّ، وَضَلَّ عَنِ الْهُدَى وَبَعُدَ عَنِ الصَّوَابِ، وَأَهْلَكَ نَفْسَهُ وَخَسِرَهَا فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ، وَفَاتَتْهُ سَعَادَةُ الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿إِنْ يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ إِلا إِنَاثًا﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا مَحْمُودُ بْنُ غَيْلان، أَنْبَأَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ مُوسَى، أَخْبَرَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ وَاقِدٍ، عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَالِيَةِ، عَنْ أُبَيِّ بْنِ كَعْبٍ: ﴿إِنْ يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ إِلا إِنَاثًا﴾ قَالَ: مَعَ كُلِّ صَنَمٍ جنيَّة.

وَحَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ الْبَاهِلِيُّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ العزيز بن محمد، عَنْ هِشَامٍ -يَعْنِي ابْنَ عُرْوَةَ -عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ عَائِشَةَ: ﴿إِنْ يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ إِلا إِنَاثًا﴾ قَالَتْ: أَوْثَانًا.

وَرَوَى عَنْ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، وَعُرْوَةَ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، وَأَبِي مَالِكٍ، وَالسُّدِّيِّ، وَمُقَاتِلِ بْنِ حَيَّانَ نَحْوُ ذَلِكَ.

وَقَالَ جُوَيْبر عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ فِي [قَوْلِهِ] ﴿إِنْ يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ إِلا إِنَاثًا﴾ قَالَ الْمُشْرِكُونَ: إِنَّ الْمَلَائِكَةَ بَنَاتُ اللَّهِ، وَإِنَّمَا نَعْبُدُهُمْ لِيُقَرِّبُونَا إِلَى اللَّهِ زُلْفَى، قَالَ: اتَّخَذُوهَا أَرْبَابًا وَصَوَّرُوهُنَّ صُوَرَ الْجَوَارِي، فَحَكَمُوا وَقَلَّدُوا، وَقَالُوا: هَؤُلَاءِ يُشْبهن بَنَاتَ اللَّهِ الَّذِي نَعْبُدُهُ، يَعْنُونُ الْمَلَائِكَةَ.

وَهَذَا التَّفْسِيرُ شبيه بقوله تعالى: ﴿أَفَرَأَيْتُمُ اللاتَ وَالْعُزَّى. [مَنَاةَ الثَّالِثَةَ الأخْرَى. أَلَكُمُ الذَّكَرُ وَلَهُ الأنْثَى. تِلْكَ إِذًا قِسْمَةٌ ضِيزَى. إِنْ هِيَ إِلا أَسْمَاءٌ سَمَّيْتُمُوهَا أَنْتُمْ وَآبَاؤُكُمْ مَا أَنزلَ اللَّهُ بِهَا مِنْ سُلْطَانٍ] ﴾ [النَّجْمِ: ١٩-٢٣] ، وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَجَعَلُوا الْمَلائِكَةَ الَّذِينَ هُمْ عِبَادُ الرَّحْمَنِ إِنَاثًا [أشَهِدُوا خَلْقَهُمْ سَتُكْتَبُ شَهَادَتُهُمْ وَيُسْأَلُونَ] ﴾ [الزُّخْرُفِ: ١٩] ، وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَجَعَلُوا بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَ الْجِنَّةِ نَسَبًا وَلَقَدْ عَلِمَتِ الْجِنَّةُ إِنَّهُمْ لَمُحْضَرُونَ. سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ] ﴾ [الصَّافَّاتِ: ١٥٨، ١٥٩] .

وَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ وَالضَّحَّاكُ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿إِنْ يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ إِلا إِنَاثًا﴾ قَالَ: يَعْنِي مَوْتَى.

وَقَالَ مُبَارَكٌ -يَعْنِي ابْنَ فَضَالة -عَنِ الْحَسَنِ: ﴿إِنْ يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ إِلا إِنَاثًا﴾ قَالَ الْحَسَنُ: الْإِنَاثُ كُلُّ شَيْءٍ مَيِّتٍ لَيْسَ فِيهِ رُوحٌ، إِمَّا خَشَبَةٌ يَابِسَةٌ وَإِمَّا حَجَرٌ يَابِسٌ. وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حاتم وابن جرير، وهو غريب.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَإِنْ يَدْعُونَ إِلا شَيْطَانًا مَرِيدًا﴾ أَيْ: هُوَ الَّذِي أَمَرَهُمْ بِذَلِكَ وَحَسَّنَهُ لَهُمْ وَزَيَّنَهُ، وَهُمْ إِنَّمَا يَعْبُدُونَ إِبْلِيسَ فِي نَفْسِ الْأَمْرِ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿أَلَمْ أَعْهَدْ إِلَيْكُمْ يَا بَنِي آدَمَ أَلا تَعْبُدُوا الشَّيْطَانَ [إِنَّهُ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُبِينٌ] ﴾ [يس: ٦٠] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى إِخْبَارًا عَنِ الْمَلَائِكَةِ أَنَّهُمْ يَقُولُونَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ عَنِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ الَّذِينَ ادَّعَوْا عِبَادَتَهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيَا: ﴿بَلْ كَانُوا يَعْبُدُونَ الْجِنَّ أَكْثَرُهُمْ بِهِمْ مُؤْمِنُونَ﴾ [سَبَأٍ: ٤١] .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿لَعَنَهُ اللَّهُ﴾ أَيْ: طَرَدَهُ وَأَبْعَدَهُ مِنْ رَحْمَتِهِ، وَأَخْرَجَهُ مِنْ جِوَارِهِ.

وَقَالَ: ﴿لأتَّخِذَنَّ مِنْ عِبَادِكَ نَصِيبًا مَفْرُوضًا﴾ أَيْ: مُعَيَّنا مقدَّرًا مَعْلُومًا. قَالَ مُقَاتِلُ بْنُ حَيَّانَ: مِنْ كُلِّ أَلْفٍ تِسْعُمِائَةٌ وَتِسْعَةٌ وَتِسْعُونَ إِلَى النَّارِ، وَوَاحِدٌ إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ.

﴿وَلأضِلَّنَّهُمْ﴾ أَيْ: عَنِ الْحَقِّ ﴿وَلأمَنِّيَنَّهُمْ﴾ أَيْ: أُزَيِّنُ لَهُمْ تَرْكَ التَّوْبَةِ، وَأَعِدُهُمُ الْأَمَانِيَ، وَآمُرُهُمْ بِالتَّسْوِيفِ وَالتَّأْخِيرِ، وَأَغُرُّهُمْ مِنْ أَنْفُسِهِمْ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلآمُرَنَّهُمْ فَلَيُبَتِّكُنَّ آذَانَ الأنْعَامِ﴾ قَالَ قَتَادَةُ وَالسُّدِّيُّ وَغَيْرُهُمَا: يَعْنِي تَشْقِيقَهَا وَجَعْلَهَا سِمَةً وَعَلَامَةً لِلْبَحِيرَةِ وَالسَّائِبَةِ.

﴿وَلآمُرَنَّهُمْ فَلَيُغَيِّرُنَّ خَلْقَ اللَّهِ﴾ قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ خِصَاءَ الدَّوَابِّ. وَكَذَا رُوِيَ عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، وَأَنَسٍ، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيَّبِ، وَعِكْرِمَةَ، وَأَبِي عِيَاضٍ، وَأَبِي صَالِحٍ، وَقَتَادَةَ، وَالثَّوْرِيِّ. وَقَدْ وَرَدَ فِي حَدِيثٍ النَّهْيُ عن ذلك .

وقال الحسن ابن أَبِي الْحَسَنِ الْبَصْرِيُّ: يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ الوَشْم. وَفِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ النَّهْيُ عَنِ الْوَشْمِ فِي الْوَجْهِ وَفِي لفظٍ: "لَعَنَ اللَّهُ مَنْ فَعَلَ ذَلِكَ". وَفِي الصَّحِيحِ عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: لَعَنَ اللَّهُ الْوَاشِمَاتِ والمستوشِمات، وَالنَّامِصَاتِ والمُتَنَمِّصَاتِ، والمُتَفَلِّجات للحُسْن الْمُغَيِّرَاتِ خَلْقَ اللَّهِ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، ثُمَّ قَالَ: أَلَا أَلْعَنُ مَنْ لَعَنَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ وَهُوَ فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، يَعْنِي قَوْلَهُ: ﴿وَمَا آتَاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَمَا نَهَاكُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا﴾ [الْحَشْرِ: ٧] .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ فِي رِوَايَةٍ عَنْهُ، وَمُجَاهِدٌ، وَعِكْرِمَةُ أَيْضًا وَإِبْرَاهِيمُ النخَعي، وَالْحَسَنُ، وَقَتَادَةُ، وَالْحَكَمُ، وَالسُّدِّيُّ، وَالضَّحَّاكُ، وَعَطَاءٌ الخُراساني فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَلآمُرَنَّهُمْ فَلَيُغَيِّرُنَّ خَلْقَ اللَّهِ﴾ يَعْنِي: دِينَ اللَّهِ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ. وَهَذَا كَقَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَأَقِمْ وَجْهَكَ لِلدِّينِ حَنِيفًا فِطْرَتَ اللَّهِ الَّتِي فَطَرَ النَّاسَ عَلَيْهَا لَا تَبْدِيلَ لِخَلْقِ اللَّهِ﴾ [الرُّومِ: ٣٠] عَلَى قَوْلِ مَنْ جَعَلَ ذَلِكَ أَمْرًا، أَيْ: لَا تُبَدِّلُوا فِطْرَةَ اللَّهِ، وَدَعُوا النَّاسَ عَلَى فِطْرَتِهِمْ، كَمَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "كُلُّ مَوْلُودٍ يُولَدُ على الفِطْرَة، فَأَبَوَاهُ يُهَوِّدانه، ويُنَصِّرَانه، ويُمَجِّسَانه، كَمَا تُولَدُ الْبَهِيمَةُ بَهِيمَةً جَمْعاء، هَلْ يَحُسّون فِيهَا مِنْ جَدْعَاءَ؟ " وَفِي صَحِيحِ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ عِيَاضِ بْنِ حِمَار قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "قَالَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: إِنِّي خلقتُ عِبَادِي حُنَفَاء، فَجَاءَتْهُمُ الشَّيَاطِينُ فْاجْتَالَتْهُم عَنْ دِينِهِمْ، وحَرّمت عَلَيْهِمْ مَا أَحْلَلْتُ لَهُمْ" .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَنْ يَتَّخِذِ الشَّيْطَانَ وَلِيًّا مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ فَقَدْ خَسِرَ خُسْرَانًا مُبِينًا﴾ أَيْ: فَقَدْ خَسِرَ الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ، وَتِلْكَ خَسَارَةٌ لَا جَبْرَ لَهَا وَلَا اسْتِدْرَاكَ لِفَائِتِهَا.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿يَعِدُهُمْ وَيُمَنِّيهِمْ وَمَا يَعِدُهُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ إِلا غُرُورًا﴾ وَهَذَا إِخْبَارٌ عَنِ الْوَاقِعِ؛ لِأَنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ يَعِدُ أَوْلِيَاءَهُ وَيُمَنِّيهِمْ بِأَنَّهُمْ هُمُ الْفَائِزُونَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ، وَقَدْ كَذَبَ وَافْتَرَى فِي ذَلِكَ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿وَمَا يَعِدُهُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ إِلا غُرُورًا﴾ كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى مُخْبِرًا عَنْ إِبْلِيسَ يَوْمَ الْمَعَادِ: ﴿وَقَالَ الشَّيْطَانُ لَمَّا قُضِيَ الأمْرُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ وَعَدَكُمْ وَعْدَ الْحَقِّ وَوَعَدْتُكُمْ فَأَخْلَفْتُكُمْ وَمَا كَانَ لِي عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْ سُلْطَانٍ [إِلا أَنْ دَعَوْتُكُمْ فَاسْتَجَبْتُمْ لِي فَلا تَلُومُونِي وَلُومُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ مَا أَنَا بِمُصْرِخِكُمْ وَمَا أَنْتُمْ بِمُصْرِخِيَّ إِنِّي كَفَرْتُ بِمَا أَشْرَكْتُمُونِ مِنْ قَبْلُ] إِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ﴾ [إِبْرَاهِيمَ: ٢٢] .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: أَيِ: الْمُسْتَحْسِنُونَ لَهُ فِيمَا وَعَدَهُمْ وَمَنَّاهُمْ ﴿مَأْوَاهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ﴾ أَيْ: مَصِيرُهُمْ وَمَآلُهُمْ يَوْمَ حِسَابِهِمْ ﴿وَلا يَجِدُونَ عَنْهَا مَحِيصًا﴾ أَيْ: لَيْسَ لَهُمْ عَنْهَا مَنْدُوحَةٌ وَلَا مَصْرِفٌ، وَلَا خَلَاصٌ وَلَا مَنَاصٌ.
