ثُمَّ ذَكَرَ حَالَ السُّعَدَاءِ الْأَتْقِيَاءِ وَمَا لَهُمْ فِي مَآلِهِمْ مِنَ الْكَرَامَةِ التَّامَّةِ، فَقَالَ: ﴿وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ﴾ أَيْ: صَدّقت قُلُوبُهُمْ وَعَمِلَتْ جَوَارِحُهُمْ بِمَا أُمِرُوا بِهِ مِنَ الْخَيِّرَاتِ، وَتَرَكُوا مَا نُهُوا عَنْهُ مِنَ الْمُنْكَرَاتِ ﴿سَنُدْخِلُهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الأنْهَارُ﴾ أَيْ: يصرفونها حيث شاءوا وأين شاءوا ﴿خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدًا﴾ أَيْ: بِلَا زَوَالٍ وَلَا انْتِقَالٍ ﴿وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقًّا﴾ أَيْ: هَذَا وَعْدٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَوَعْدُ اللَّهِ مَعْلُومٌ حَقِيقَةً أَنَّهُ وَاقِعٌ لَا مَحَالَةَ، وَلِهَذَا أَكَّدَهُ بِالْمَصْدَرِ الدَّالِّ عَلَى تَحْقِيقِ الْخَبَرِ، وَهُوَ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿حَقًّا﴾ ثُمَّ قَال ﴿وَمَنْ أَصْدَقُ مِنَ اللَّهِ قِيلا﴾ أَيْ: لَا أَحَدَ أَصْدَقُ مِنْهُ قَوْلًا وَخَبَرًا، لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ، وَلَا رَبَّ سِوَاهُ. وَكَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ يقول فِي خُطْبَتِهِ: "إِنَّ أَصْدَقَ الْحَدِيثِ كَلَامُ اللَّهِ، وَخَيْرَ الهَدْي هَدْي مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ، وَشَرَّ الْأُمُورِ مُحْدَثاتها، وَكُلَّ مُحْدَثة بِدْعَةٌ وَكُلَّ بِدْعَةٍ ضَلَالَةٌ، وَكُلَّ ضَلَالَةٍ فِي النَّارِ".

---

## 4:123-126 — verses 123-126 (shared commentary)

### Translation

Qatadah said: it was mentioned to us that the Muslims and the People of the Book boasted against one another. The People of the Book said: our prophet came before your prophet, and our book came before your book, so we have a greater claim upon Allah than you. And the Muslims said: we have a greater claim upon Allah than you, our prophet is the seal of the prophets, and our book supersedes the books that came before it. So Allah sent down: {It is not by your wishful thinking nor by the wishful thinking of the People of the Book. Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it} {And who is better in religion than one who submits his face to Allah while being a doer of good [and follows the religion of Ibrahim, inclining toward truth]} to the end of the verse. So Allah made triumphant the argument of the Muslims over whoever opposed them among the followers of other religions.

The same has been related from al-Suddi, Masruq, al-Dahhak, Abu Salih, and others. Likewise al-'Awfi related from Ibn 'Abbas that he said concerning this verse: the followers of the various religions disputed with one another. The people of the Torah said: our book is the best of books, and our prophet is the best of prophets. And the people of the Gospel said the same. And the people of Islam said: there is no religion except Islam, our book has abrogated every other book, our prophet is the seal of the prophets, and you have been commanded, as we have been commanded, to believe in your book and to act according to our book. So Allah judged between them and said: {It is not by your wishful thinking nor by the wishful thinking of the People of the Book. Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it}, and He gave the choice among religions when He said: {And who is better in religion than one who submits his face to Allah while being a doer of good [and follows the religion of Ibrahim, inclining toward truth]} up to His saying: {And Allah took Ibrahim as a close friend}.

Mujahid said: the Arabs said, we will never be resurrected and we will never be punished. And the Jews and Christians said: {None will enter Paradise except one who is a Jew or a Christian} [al-Baqarah: 111], and they said: {The Fire will not touch us except for a few numbered days} [al-Baqarah: 80].

The meaning in this verse is that religion is not attained by mere adornment or wishful longing, and not everyone who claims something obtains it by his claim alone, nor is everyone who says "I am the one upon truth" to be heeded merely for saying so, until he has proof from Allah. Therefore Allah, exalted is He, said: {It is not by your wishful thinking nor by the wishful thinking of the People of the Book}, meaning: neither you nor they attain salvation by mere wishing. Rather, what matters is obedience to Allah and following what He has legislated through the tongues of His noble messengers. Hence He said after it: {Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it}, similar to His saying: {So whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it, and whoever does an atom's weight of evil will see it} [al-Zalzalah: 7-8].

It has been related that when this verse was revealed, it weighed heavily upon many of the Companions. Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Abu Bakr ibn Abi Zuhayr said: I was informed that Abu Bakr said: O Messenger of Allah, how can righteousness be attained after this verse: {It is not by your wishful thinking nor by the wishful thinking of the People of the Book. Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it}? So for every evil we do, shall we be recompensed for it? The Prophet ﷺ said: "May Allah forgive you, O Abu Bakr. Do you not fall ill? Do you not grow weary? Do you not grieve? Do you not suffer hardship?" He said: yes indeed. He said: "That is what you are recompensed with."

Sa'id ibn Mansur related it from Khalaf ibn Khalifah, from Isma'il ibn Abi Khalid, with the same chain. Ibn Hibban related it in his Sahih, from Abu Ya'la, from Abu Khaythamah, from Yahya ibn Sa'id, from Isma'il ibn Abi Khalid, with the same chain. Al-Hakim related it through the route of Sufyan al-Thawri, from Isma'il, with the same chain.

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Ibn 'Umar said: I heard Abu Bakr say: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it in this world."

Abu Bakr ibn Mardawayh said: [chain] Mujahid said: 'Abdullah ibn 'Umar said: look at the place where 'Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr is crucified, and do not pass by it. He said: the young man forgot, and suddenly Ibn 'Umar found himself looking at Ibn al-Zubayr. He said: may Allah forgive you, three times. By Allah, I never knew you except as one who fasted much, prayed much at night, and maintained ties of kinship. By Allah, I hope that, given the equal weight of what you did wrong, Allah will not punish you beyond this. He said: then he turned to me and said: I heard Abu Bakr al-Siddiq say: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever does evil in this world will be recompensed for it."

Abu Bakr al-Bazzar related it in his Musnad, from al-Fadl ibn Sahl, from 'Abd al-Wahhab ibn 'Ata', with the same chain, in abridged form. And he said in the Musnad of Ibn al-Zubayr: [chain] Hayyan ibn Bistam said: I was with Ibn 'Umar, and he passed by 'Abdullah ibn al-Zubayr while he was crucified, and he said: may Allah have mercy on you, Abu Khubayb. I heard your father, meaning al-Zubayr, say: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it in this world and the next." Then he said: we do not know it to be related from al-Zubayr except by this route.

Abu Bakr ibn Mardawayh said: [chain] the freedman of Ibn Sibaʿ said: I heard Ibn 'Umar relating from Abu Bakr al-Siddiq who said: I was with the Prophet ﷺ when this verse was revealed: {Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it, and will not find for himself besides Allah any protector or helper}. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "O Abu Bakr, shall I recite to you a verse that has been revealed to me?" He said: I said, yes indeed, O Messenger of Allah. So he recited it to me, and I know only that I felt a breaking in my back until I bent over. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "What is wrong with you, Abu Bakr?" I said: may my father and mother be sacrificed for you, O Messenger of Allah, which of us has not done evil, and shall we be recompensed for every evil we have done? The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "As for you and your companions, O Abu Bakr, the believers, you will be recompensed for that in this world until you meet Allah, and you will have no sins remaining. As for the others, that will be gathered for them until they are recompensed for it on the Day of Resurrection."

Al-Tirmidhi related it likewise from Yahya ibn Musa and 'Abd ibn Humayd, from Rawh ibn 'Ubadah, with the same chain. Then he said: Musa ibn 'Ubaydah is graded weak, and the freedman of Ibn Sibaʿ is unknown.

[Ibn Jarir said: [chain] 'Ata' ibn Abi Rabah said: when it was revealed, Abu Bakr said: O Messenger of Allah, the back-breaking verse has come. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "It refers only to the calamities of this world."]

Another route from al-Siddiq: Ibn Mardawayh said: [chain] Masruq said: Abu Bakr [al-Siddiq] said: O Messenger of Allah, how severe is this verse: {Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it}! The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The calamities, illnesses, and griefs of this world are the recompense."

Another route: Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Muhammad ibn Zayd ibn Qunfudh, from 'A'ishah, from Abu Bakr, who said: when {Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it} was revealed, Abu Bakr said: O Messenger of Allah, shall we be held accountable for everything we do? He said: "O Abu Bakr, does not such and such befall you? That is an expiation."

Another hadith: Sa'id ibn Mansur said: [chain] from 'Ubayd ibn 'Umayr, from 'A'ishah: that a man recited this verse: {Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it}, and said: shall we then be recompensed for every deed? Then we are ruined. This reached the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he said: "Yes, the believer is recompensed for it in this world, in himself, in his body, in what afflicts him."

Another route: Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] 'A'ishah said: I said, O Messenger of Allah, I know the most severe verse in the Qur'an. He said: "What is it, O 'A'ishah?" I said: {Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it}. He said: "It refers to what befalls the believing servant, even the stumble he stumbles."

Ibn Jarir related it from the hadith of Hushaym, with the same chain. Abu Dawud also related it, from the hadith of Abu Amir Salih ibn Rustum al-Khazzaz, with the same chain.

Another route: Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi said: Hammad ibn Salama narrated to us, from Ali ibn Zayd, from Umayya, that she asked Aisha about this verse: {Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it}. She said: No one has asked me about this verse since I asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ about it. I asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and he said: "O Aisha, this is Allah's covenant of exchange with His servant, through what befalls him of fever, misfortune, and a thorn's prick, even to the point that a small item of merchandise which he places in his sleeve and then becomes alarmed about, only to find it in his pocket, so that indeed the believer emerges from his sins just as pure red gold emerges from the furnace."

Another route: Ibn Marduyah said: Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Ibrahim narrated to us, Abu al-Qasim narrated to us, Surayj ibn Yunus narrated to us, Abu Mu'awiya narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Isma'il, from Muhammad ibn Zayd ibn al-Muhajir, from Aisha, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was asked about this verse: {Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it}. He said: "Indeed the believer is rewarded for everything, even for the exhalation of breath at the moment of death."

Imam Ahmad said: Husayn narrated to us, from Za'ida, from Layth, from Mujahid, from Aisha, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "When the sins of a servant multiply and he has nothing to expiate them, Allah afflicts him with grief so as to expiate them for him."

Another hadith: Sa'id ibn Mansur said, from Sufyan ibn Uyayna, from Umar ibn Abd al-Rahman ibn Muhaysin, who heard Muhammad ibn Qays ibn Makhrama report that Abu Hurayra, may Allah be pleased with him, said: When {Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it} was revealed, this weighed heavily on the Muslims, so the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to them: "Be upright and moderate, for in everything that afflicts the Muslim there is an expiation, even the thorn that pricks him and the misfortune that befalls him."

Ahmad related it likewise from Sufyan ibn Uyayna, and Muslim, al-Tirmidhi, and al-Nasa'i related it from the hadith of Sufyan ibn Uyayna, with the same chain. Ibn Marduyah related it from the hadith of Rawh and Mu'tamir, both of them from Ibrahim ibn Yazid, from Abdullah ibn Ibrahim, who said: I heard Abu Hurayra say: When this verse was revealed: {Not according to your wishes, nor the wishes of the People of the Book. Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it}, we wept and grieved and said: O Messenger of Allah, this verse has left nothing unaccounted for. He said: "Indeed, by the One in whose hand is my soul, it is exactly as it was revealed, but rejoice, be moderate, and be upright, for nothing befalls any of you in this world except that Allah expiates thereby his sin, even the thorn that pricks one of you in his foot."

Ata ibn Yasar reported, from Abu Sa'id and Abu Hurayra, that they both heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "Nothing befalls the believer of fatigue, illness, sickness, or grief, even the anxiety that worries him, except that some of his sins are expiated thereby." Both al-Bukhari and Muslim related it.

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Yahya narrated to us, from Sa'd ibn Ishaq, Zaynab bint Ka'b ibn Ujra narrated to me, from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, who said: A man said to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ: What do you think of these illnesses that afflict us, what benefit do we have from them? He said: "Expiations." Ubayy said: Even if they are slight? He said: "Even a thorn's prick and anything above it." He said: So Ubayy prayed against himself that fever would not leave him until he died, on condition that it would not distract him from Hajj, Umra, jihad in the way of Allah, or the obligatory prayer in congregation. So no person touched him without finding his heat, until he died, may Allah be pleased with him. Ahmad alone related this.

Another hadith: Ibn Marduyah related, through the route of Husayn ibn Waqid, from al-Kalbi, from Abu Salih, from Ibn Abbas, who said: It was said: O Messenger of Allah, {Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it}? He said: "Yes, and whoever does a good deed will be recompensed for it tenfold. So ruined is he whose single [evil deed] outweighs ten [good deeds]."

Ibn Jarir said: Ibn Wakee narrated to us, Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, Hammad ibn Salama narrated to us, from Humayd, from al-Hasan, regarding {Whoever does evil will be recompensed for it}: he said, this refers to the disbeliever, then he recited: {And do We recompense except the ungrateful?} [Saba: 17].

Likewise it was related from Ibn Abbas and Sa'id ibn Jubayr, that they both interpreted "evil" here as referring to shirk (polytheism) as well.

Regarding His saying: {and will not find for himself, besides Allah, any protector or helper}, Ali ibn Abi Talha said, from Ibn Abbas: except that he repents, in which case Allah will accept his repentance. Ibn Abi Hatim related this.

The correct view is that this is general, applying to all deeds, based on the hadiths mentioned previously. This is the view that Ibn Jarir chose. And Allah knows best.

Regarding His saying: {And whoever does righteous deeds, whether male or female, while being a believer, [those will enter Paradise and will not be wronged even as much as the hollow of a date pit]}: having mentioned the recompense for evil deeds, and that Allah must inevitably exact what is due from the servant for them, whether in this world (which is the better option for him) or in the Hereafter (we seek refuge with Allah from that, and we ask Him for well-being in this world and the next, and for His pardon, forgiveness, and leniency), He proceeded to explain His kindness, generosity, and mercy in accepting righteous deeds from His servants, both male and female, on condition of faith, and that He will admit them to Paradise and will not wrong them of their good deeds even by the measure of a naqir. This is the tiny groove found on the back of a date pit. Discussion of the fatil has preceded, which is the thread found in the crevice of the date pit. This naqir, along with that fatil, are both parts of a single date pit, and likewise the qitmir, which is the thin membrane covering the date pit. All three of these terms occur in the Qur'an.

Then the Almighty says: {And who is better in religion than one who submits his face to Allah}, meaning he devotes his deeds sincerely to his Lord, the Mighty and Majestic, doing so out of faith and in hope of reward, {while being a doer of good}, meaning he follows in his deeds what Allah has prescribed for him and what He sent His Messenger with, of guidance and the true religion. These two conditions are such that no deed of any doer can be sound without them, that is, the deed must be sincere and correct. Sincerity means that it is for Allah alone, and correctness means that it follows the sacred law, so that its outward form is sound through conformity, and its inward reality is sound through sincerity. Whoever's deed lacks one of these two conditions is corrupted. Whoever lacks sincerity is a hypocrite, and such are those who show off before people, and whoever lacks conformity is misguided and ignorant. Whenever the two are combined, that is the deed of the believers: {those from whom We accept the best of what they did and overlook their bad deeds, [among the companions of Paradise, a true promise which they were promised]} [al-Ahqaf: 16]. For this reason the Almighty says: {and followed the religion of Abraham, inclining toward truth}, and these are Muhammad and his followers until the Day of Resurrection, as the Almighty says: {Indeed, the people most worthy of Abraham are those who followed him, and this Prophet [and those who believe, and Allah is the protecting friend of the believers]} [Al Imran: 68], and the Almighty says: {[Say: Indeed, my Lord has guided me to a straight path, an upright religion, the way of Abraham, inclining toward truth, and he was not among the polytheists]} [al-An'am: 161], and {Then We revealed to you: follow the religion of Abraham, inclining toward truth, and he was not among the polytheists} [al-Nahl: 123]. Al-hanif is one who deliberately turns away from shirk, that is, abandoning it with clear insight, and turns wholly toward the truth, unswayed by any obstruction and unturned by any deterrent.

Regarding His saying: {And Allah took Abraham as a close friend}: this is intended as an encouragement to follow him, since he is a leader to be emulated, having reached the utmost degree by which servants draw near to Him. For he attained the rank of khulla (intimate friendship), which is the highest station of love, and that was due to nothing but the abundance of his obedience to his Lord, as He described him in His saying: {and Abraham, who fulfilled [his covenant]} [al-Najm: 37]. Many of the early scholars said: meaning he carried out all that he was commanded, and fulfilled every station of worship completely, so that no grave matter distracted him from a minor one, nor any great matter from a small one. The Almighty says: {And [mention] when Abraham's Lord tested him with commands, and he fulfilled them, [He said: Indeed, I will make you a leader for the people]} [al-Baqara: 124], and the Almighty says: {Indeed, Abraham was a nation obedient to Allah, inclining toward truth, and he was not among the polytheists, [being grateful for His favors. He chose him and guided him to a straight path. And We gave him good in this world, and indeed, in the Hereafter he is among the righteous]} [al-Nahl: 120-122].

Al-Bukhari said: Sulayman ibn Harb narrated to us, Shu'ba narrated to us, from Habib ibn Abi Thabit, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Amr ibn Maymun, who said: When Mu'adh arrived in Yemen, he led them in the dawn prayer and recited: {And Allah took Abraham as a close friend}.

A man among the people said, "The eye of the mother of Ibrahim has indeed found coolness."

Ibn Jarir mentioned in his tafsir, on the authority of some of them, that Allah only named him a khalil (intimate friend) because the people of his region were struck by drought, so he journeyed to a khalil of his among the people of Mosul, and some of them said among the people of Egypt, in order to obtain provisions of food for his household from him, but he did not find what he needed with him. When he drew near to his family, he passed by a desert tract of sand, and he said, "If only I filled my sacks with this sand, so that I do not grieve my family by returning to them without provisions, and so that they may think I have brought them what they love." So he did that, and what was in his sacks turned from sand into flour. When he arrived at his dwelling he slept, and his family arose and opened the sacks, and they found flour, so they kneaded it and baked bread from it. Then he woke and asked them about the flour from which they had baked. They said, "From the flour you brought from your khalil." He said, "Yes, it is from my khalil, Allah." So Allah named him a khalil on account of that.

There is reason to question the soundness and occurrence of this, and at most it may be counted as an Isra'iliyyat report (a narration derived from Jewish or Israelite sources) which is neither confirmed nor denied. Rather, he was named the khalil of Allah because of the intensity of his Lord's love, Mighty and Majestic is He, for him, on account of the obedience he fulfilled which He loves and is pleased with. For this reason it is established in the two Sahihs, from the hadith of Abu Sa'id al-Khudri, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when he addressed them in the last sermon he delivered, said: "To proceed, O people, if I were to take a khalil from among the people of the earth, I would have taken Abu Bakr ibn Abi Quhafah as a khalil, but your companion is the khalil of Allah."

It has also come by the route of Jundub ibn Abdullah al-Bajali, Abdullah ibn Amr ibn al-As, and Abdullah ibn Mas'ud, from the Prophet ﷺ: "Indeed Allah has taken me as a khalil just as He took Ibrahim as a khalil."

Abu Bakr ibn Mardawayh said: [chain] Ibn Abbas said: A group of the companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ were sitting, awaiting him. He came out, and when he drew near them he heard them discussing among themselves, and he listened to what they were saying. One of them was saying, "How wondrous that Allah took a khalil from among His creation, for Ibrahim is His khalil!" Another said, "What is more wondrous than that Allah spoke to Musa directly!" Another said, "And Isa is the spirit of Allah and His word!" Another said, "Adam, Allah chose him!" So he came out to them, gave the greeting of peace, and said: "I have heard your talk and your wonder that Ibrahim is the khalil of Allah, and he is indeed so, and that Musa is His one spoken to, and that Isa is His spirit and His word, and that Adam, Allah chose him, and he is indeed so. But indeed, I am the beloved of Allah, and this is no boast. I am the bearer of the banner of praise on the Day of Resurrection, and this is no boast. I am the first intercessor and the first whose intercession is accepted, and this is no boast. I am the first to move the rings of the gate of Paradise, so Allah will open it and admit me into it, and with me the poor among the believers, and this is no boast. I am the most honored of the first and the last on the Day of Resurrection, and this is no boast."

This is a gharib (singular) hadith from this route, though some of it has corroborating reports in the Sahih collections and elsewhere.

Qatadah said, on the authority of Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas, that he said: "Do you wonder that the khullah (intimate friendship) belongs to Ibrahim, the direct speech belongs to Musa, and the vision belongs to Muhammad?" May the prayers and peace of Allah be upon them all.

Al-Hakim related it in his Mustadrak and said, "Sound according to the criterion of al-Bukhari," though the two of them (al-Bukhari and Muslim) did not include it. It has likewise been related from Anas ibn Malik and more than one of the Companions, the Successors, and the leading scholars among the earlier and later generations.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Ubayd ibn Umayr said: Ibrahim (peace be upon him) used to host guests. One day he went out seeking someone to host, but he found no one to host. So he returned to his house and found a man standing in it. He said, "O servant of Allah, what has brought you into my house without my permission?" The man said, "I entered it by the permission of its Lord." He said, "And who are you?" He said, "I am the angel of death. My Lord sent me to a servant among His servants to give him the good news that Allah has taken him as a khalil." He said, "Who is he? By Allah, if you tell me who he is, even if he were in the farthest of lands, I would go to him and never cease being his neighbor until death separates us." He said, "That servant is you." He said, "Me?" He said, "Yes." He said, "For what has Allah taken me as a khalil?" He said, "Because you give to people and do not ask them for anything."

He also said: [chain] Ishaq ibn Yasar said: When Allah took Ibrahim as a khalil, He cast fear into his heart, such that the pounding of his heart could be heard from afar, just as the fluttering of a bird's wings is heard in the air. And it has likewise come in the description of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ that a rumbling could be heard from his chest, like the rumbling of a boiling pot, from weeping.

His statement: {And to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth} means, all of it is His possession, His servants, and His creation, and He is the One who disposes of all of it. There is none who can reject what He has decreed, and none who can override what He has judged, and He is not questioned about what He does, on account of His greatness, His power, His justice, His wisdom, His gentleness, and His mercy.

His statement: {And Allah encompasses all things} means, His knowledge penetrates all of that. Nothing of His servants is hidden from Him, and not even the weight of an atom in the heavens or on the earth escapes His knowledge, nor anything smaller than that or greater. Not even a single atom is hidden from Him, whether it is apparent to those who look or concealed from them.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

قَالَ قَتَادَةُ: ذُكرَ لَنَا أَنَّ الْمُسْلِمِينَ وَأَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ افْتَخَرُوا، فَقَالَ أَهْلُ الْكِتَابِ: نَبِيُّنَا قَبْلَ نَبِيِّكُمْ، وَكِتَابُنَا قَبْلَ كِتَابِكُمْ، فَنَحْنُ أَوْلَى بِاللَّهِ مِنْكُمْ. وَقَالَ الْمُسْلِمُونَ: نَحْنُ أَوْلَى بِاللَّهِ مِنْكُمْ نَبِيُّنَا خَاتَمُ النَّبِيِّينَ، وَكِتَابُنَا يَقْضِي عَلَى الْكُتُبِ الَّتِي كَانَتْ قَبْلَهُ فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿لَيْسَ بِأَمَانِيِّكُمْ وَلا أَمَانِيِّ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ﴾ ﴿وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ دِينًا مِمَّنْ أَسْلَمَ وَجْهَهُ لِلَّهِ وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ [وَاتَّبَعَ مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا] ﴾ الْآيَةَ. فَأَفْلَجَ اللَّهُ حُجَّةَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ عَلَى مَنْ نَاوَأَهُمْ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْأَدْيَانِ.

وَكَذَا رُوِيَ عَنِ السُّدِّيِّ، وَمَسْرُوقٍ، وَالضَّحَّاكِ وَأَبِي صَالِحٍ، وَغَيْرِهِمْ وَكَذَا رَوَى العَوْفيّ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ فِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: تخاصَمَ أَهْلُ الْأَدْيَانِ فَقَالَ أَهْلُ التَّوْرَاةِ: كِتَابُنَا خَيْرُ الْكُتُبِ، وَنَبِيُّنَا خَيْرُ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ. وَقَالَ أَهْلُ الْإِنْجِيلِ مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ. وَقَالَ أَهْلُ الْإِسْلَامِ: لَا دِينَ إِلَّا الْإِسْلَامُ. وَكِتَابُنَا نَسَخَ كُلَّ كِتَابٍ، وَنَبِيُّنَا خَاتَمُ النَّبِيِّينَ، وأُمرْتُم وَأُمِرْنَا أَنْ نُؤْمِنَ بِكِتَابِكُمْ وَنَعْمَلَ بِكِتَابِنَا. فَقَضَى اللَّهُ بَيْنَهُمْ فَقَالَ: ﴿لَيْسَ بِأَمَانِيِّكُمْ وَلا أَمَانِيِّ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ﴾ وخَيَّر بَيْنَ الْأَدْيَانِ فَقَالَ: ﴿وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ دِينًا مِمَّنْ أَسْلَمَ وَجْهَهُ لِلَّهِ وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ [وَاتَّبَعَ مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا] ﴾ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَاتَّخَذَ اللَّهُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ خَلِيلا﴾

وَقَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ: قَالَتِ الْعَرَبُ: لَنْ نبْعث وَلَنْ نُعذَّب. وَقَالَتِ الْيَهُودُ وَالنَّصَارَى: ﴿لَنْ يَدْخُلَ الْجَنَّةَ إِلا مَنْ كَانَ هُودًا أَوْ نَصَارَى﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ١١١] وَقَالُوا ﴿لَنْ تَمَسَّنَا النَّارُ إِلا أَيَّامًا مَعْدُودَةً﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ٨٠] .

وَالْمَعْنَى فِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: أَنَّ الدِّينَ لَيْسَ بِالتَّحَلِّي وَلَا بِالتَّمَنِّي، وَلَيْسَ كُلّ مَنِ ادَّعَى شَيْئًا حَصَلَ لَهُ بِمُجَرَّدِ دَعْوَاهُ، وَلَا كُلُّ مَنْ قَالَ: "إِنَّهُ هُوَ المُحق" سُمِعَ قَوْلُهُ بِمُجَرَّدِ ذَلِكَ، حَتَّى يَكُونَ لَهُ مِنَ اللَّهِ بُرْهَانٌ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿لَيْسَ بِأَمَانِيِّكُمْ وَلا أَمَانِيِّ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ﴾ أَيْ: لَيْسَ لَكُمْ وَلَا لَهُمُ النَّجَاةُ بِمُجَرَّدِ التَّمَنِّي، بَلِ الْعِبْرَةُ بِطَاعَةِ اللَّهِ، وَاتِّبَاعِ مَا شَرَعَهُ عَلَى أَلْسِنَةِ رُسُلِهِ الْكِرَامِ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ بَعْدَهُ: ﴿مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ﴾ كَقَوْلِهِ ﴿فَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيْرًا يَرَهُ. وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِثْقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ شَرًّا يَرَهُ﴾ [الزَّلْزَلَةِ: ٧، ٨] .

وَقَدْ رُوِيَ أَنَّ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ لَمَّا نَزَلَتْ شَقَّ ذَلِكَ عَلَى كَثِيرٍ مِنَ الصَّحَابَةِ. قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ نُمَيْر، حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ، عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ أَبِي زُهَيْرٍ قَالَ: أخْبرْتُ أَنَّ أَبَا بَكْرٍ قَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، كَيْفَ الصَّلَاحُ بَعْدَ هذه الآية: ﴿لَيْسَ بِأَمَانِيِّكُمْ وَلا أَمَانِيِّ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ﴾ فَكُل سُوءٍ عَمِلْنَاهُ جُزِينَا بِهِ؟ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ: "غَفَر اللَّهُ لكَ يَا أَبَا بَكْرٍ، ألستَ تَمْرضُ؟ ألستَ تَنْصَب؟ أَلَسْتَ تَحْزَن؟ أَلَسْتَ تُصيبك اللَّأْوَاءُ ؟ " قَالَ: بَلَى. قَالَ: "فهو ما تُجْزَوْنَ به".

وَرَوَاهُ سَعِيدُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ خَلَفِ بْنِ خَلِيفَةَ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، بِهِ. وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ حِبَّانَ فِي صَحِيحِهِ، عَنْ أَبِي يَعلى، عَنْ أَبِي خَيْثَمة، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، بِهِ. وَرَوَاهُ الْحَاكِمُ مِنْ طَرِيقِ سُفْيَانَ الثَّوْرِيِّ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بِهِ .

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ بْنُ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ زِيَادٍ الْجَصَّاصِ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا بَكْرٍ يَقُولُ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم: "من يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ فِي الدُّنْيَا" .

وَقَالَ أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ مَرْدُويه: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ هُشَيْم بْنِ جُهَيْمَة، حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ بْنُ عَطَاءٍ، حَدَّثَنَا زياد الجصاص، عن علي بن زيد، عن مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ عُمَرَ: انْظُرُوا الْمَكَانَ الَّذِي بِهِ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ مَصْلُوبًا وَلَا تمرُّنَّ عَلَيْهِ. قَالَ: فَسَهَا الْغُلَامُ، فَإِذَا ابْنُ عُمَرَ يَنْظُرُ إِلَى ابْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ فَقَالَ: يَغْفِرُ اللَّهُ لَكَ ثَلَاثًا، أَمَا وَاللَّهِ مَا عَلِمْتُكَ إِلَّا صَوَّامًا قَوَّامًا وَصَّالًا لِلرَّحِمِ، أَمَا وَاللَّهِ إِنِّي لَأَرْجُوَ مَعَ مُتَسَاوِي مَا أصبتَ أَلَّا يُعَذِّبَكَ اللَّهُ بَعْدَهَا. قَالَ: ثُمَّ الْتَفَتَ إِلَيَّ فَقَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا بَكْرٍ الصِّدِّيقَ يَقُولُ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا فِي الدُّنْيَا يُجْزَ بِهِ".

وَرَوَاهُ أَبُو بَكْرٍ الْبَزَّارُ فِي مُسْنَدِهِ، عَنِ الْفَضْلِ بْنِ سَهْلٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْوَهَّابِ بْنِ عَطَاءٍ، بِهِ مُخْتَصَرًا. وَقَدْ قَالَ فِي مُسْنَدِ ابْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ: حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ الْمُسْتَمِرِّ العُروفي حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ سُلَيْمِ بْنِ حَيّان، حَدَّثَنِي أَبِي، عَنْ جَدِّي حَيَّانَ بْنِ بِسْطَامٍ، قَالَ: كُنْتُ مَعَ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، فَمَرَّ بِعَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ وَهُوَ مَصْلُوبٌ، فَقَالَ: رَحِمَكَ اللَّهُ أَبَا خُبيب، سَمِعْتُ أَبَاكَ -يَعْنِي الزُّبَيْرَ-يَقُولُ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْأُخْرَى" ثُمَّ قَالَ: لَا نَعْلَمُهُ يُرْوَى عَنِ الزُّبَيْرِ إِلَّا مِنْ هَذَا الْوَجْهِ. 

وَقَالَ أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ: حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ كَامِلٍ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ الْعَوْفِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا رَوْحُ بْنُ عُبَادَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا مُوسَى بْنُ عبيدة، حدثني مولى بن سِبَاع قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ يُحَدِّثُ، عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرٍ الصَّدِّيقِ قَالَ: كُنْتُ عِنْدَ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ فَنَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ: ﴿مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ وَلا يَجِدْ لَهُ مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ وَلِيًّا وَلا نَصِيرًا﴾ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "يَا أَبَا بَكْرٍ، هَلْ أُقْرِئُكَ آيَةً نَزَلَتْ عَلَيَّ؟ " قَالَ: قُلْتُ: بَلَى يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ. فَأَقْرَأَنِيهَا فَلَا أَعْلَمُ إِلَّا أَنِّي وَجَدْتُ انقصَامًا فِي ظَهْرِي حَتَّى تَمَطَّأْتُ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "مالك يَا أَبَا بَكْرٍ؟ " قُلْتُ: بِأَبِي أَنْتَ وَأُمِّي يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، وَأَيُّنَا لَمْ يَعْمَلِ السُّوءَ، وَإِنَّا لمجْزيُّون بِكُلِّ سُوءٍ عَمِلْنَاهُ؟! فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "أَمَّا أَنْتَ وَأَصْحَابُكَ يَا أَبَا بَكْرٍ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ فَتُجْزَوْنَ بِذَلِكَ في الدُّنْيَا حَتَّى تَلْقَوُا اللَّهَ، وَلَيْسَ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبٌ، وَأَمَّا الْآخَرُونَ فَيُجْمَعُ لَهُمْ ذَلِكَ حَتَّى يُجْزَوْا بِهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ".

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ مُوسَى، وَعَبْدِ بْنِ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنْ رَوْحِ بْنِ عُبَادَةَ، بِهِ. ثُمَّ قَالَ: وَمُوسَى بن عبيدة يضعف، ومولى بن سِبَاعٍ مَجْهُولٌ .

[وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا الْغُلَامُ، حَدَّثَنَا الْحُسَيْنُ، حَدَّثَنَا الْحَجَّاجُ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، أَخْبَرَنِي عَطَاءُ بْنُ أَبِي رَبَاحٍ قَالَ: لمَّا نَزَلَتْ قَالَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، جَاءَتْ قَاصِمَةُ الظَّهْرِ، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إِنَّمَا هِيَ الْمَصَائِبُ فِي الدُّنْيَا"] .

طَرِيقٌ أُخْرَى عَنِ الصِّدِّيقِ: قَالَ ابْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ الْعَسْكَرِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَامِرٍ السَّعْدِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ يَحْيَى، حَدَّثَنَا فُضَيْلُ بْنُ عِيَاضٍ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ مِهْرَانَ، عَنْ مُسْلِمِ بْنِ صُبَيح، عَنْ مَسْرُوقٍ قَالَ: قَالَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ [الصِّدِّيقُ] يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، مَا أَشَدَّ هَذِهِ الْآيَةَ: ﴿مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ﴾ ! فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "الْمَصَائِبُ وَالْأَمْرَاضُ وَالْأَحْزَانُ فِي الدُّنْيَا جَزَاءٌ" .

طَرِيقٌ أُخْرَى: قَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ أَبِي زِيَادٍ وَأَحْمَدُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ قَالَا حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ الحُبَاب، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْمَلِكِ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الْحَارِثِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ زَيْدِ بْنِ قُنْفُذ عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي بَكْرٍ قَالَ: لَمَّا نَزَلَتْ: ﴿مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ﴾ قَالَ أَبُو بَكْرٍ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، كُلُّ مَا نَعْمَلُ نُؤَاخَذُ بِهِ؟ فَقَالَ: "يَا أَبَا بَكْرٍ، أَلَيْسَ يُصِيبُكَ كَذَا وَكَذَا؟ فَهُوَ كَفَّارَةٌ" .

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ: أَنْبَأَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ وَهْبٍ، أَخْبَرَنِي عَمْرُو بْنُ الْحَارِثِ، أَنَّ بَكْرَ بْنَ سِوَادَةَ حَدَّثَهُ، أَنَّ يَزِيدَ بْنَ أَبِي يَزِيدَ حَدَّثَهُ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ بْنِ عُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ: أَنَّ رَجُلًا تَلَا هذه الآية: ﴿مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ﴾ فَقَالَ: إِنَّا لنُجْزَى بِكُلِّ عَمَل ؟ هَلَكْنَا إذًا. فَبَلَغَ ذَلِكَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ فَقَالَ: "نَعَمْ، يُجْزَى بِهِ الْمُؤْمِنُ فِي الدُّنْيَا، فِي نَفْسِهِ، فِي جَسَدِهِ، فِيمَا يُؤْذِيهِ" .

طَرِيقٌ أُخْرَى: قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا سَلَمَةُ بْنُ بَشِيرٍ، حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْم، عَنْ أَبِي عَامِرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي مُلَيْكة، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ قَالَتْ: قُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنِّي لَأَعْلَمُ أَشَدَّ آيَةٍ فِي الْقُرْآنِ. فَقَالَ: "مَا هِيَ يَا عَائِشَةُ؟ " قُلْتُ: ﴿مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ﴾ فَقَالَ: "هُوَ مَا يُصِيبُ العبد المؤمن حتى النَّكْبَة يَنْكُبها".

رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ مِنْ حَدِيثِ هُشَيْمٍ، بِهِ. وَرَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ أَبِي عَامِرٍ صَالِحِ بْنِ رُسْتُمَ الْخَزَّازِ بِهِ .

طَرِيقٌ أُخْرَى: قَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ أُمَيَّةَ أَنَّهَا سَأَلَتْ عَائِشَةَ عَنْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: ﴿مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ﴾ فَقَالَتْ: مَا سَأَلَنِي عَنْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ أَحَدٌ مُنْذُ سَأَلْتُ عَنْهَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ، سَأَلْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ فقال: "يَا عَائِشَةُ، هَذِهِ مُبَايَعَةُ اللَّهِ لِلْعَبْدِ، مِمَّا يُصِيبُهُ مِنَ الْحُمَّى والنَّكْبَة وَالشَّوْكَةِ، حَتَّى الْبِضَاعَةُ فيضعها فِي كُمِّه فَيَفْزَعُ لَهَا، فَيَجِدُهَا فِي جَيْبِهِ، حَتَّى إِنَّ الْمُؤْمِنَ لِيَخْرُجُ مِنْ ذُنُوبِهِ كَمَا يَخْرُجُ التِّبْرُ الْأَحْمَرُ مِنَ الكِير" .

طَرِيقٌ أُخْرَى: قَالَ ابْنُ مَرْدُويه: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْقَاسِمِ، حَدَّثَنَا سُرَيج بْنُ يُونُسَ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ الْمُهَاجِرِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ قَالَتْ: سُئل رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ عَنْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: ﴿مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ﴾ قَالَ: "إِنَّ الْمُؤْمِنَ يُؤْجَرُ فِي كُلِّ شَيْءٍ حَتَّى فِي الفَيْظ عِنْدَ الْمَوْتِ".

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا حُسَيْنٌ، عَنْ زَائِدَةَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ قَالَتْ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "إِذَا كَثُرَتْ ذُنُوبُ الْعَبْدِ، وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ مَا يُكَفِّرُهَا، ابْتَلَاهُ اللَّهُ بالحَزَن ليُكَفِّرها عَنْهُ" .

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنِ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ بْنِ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ مُحَيْصِن، سَمِعَ مُحَمَّدَ بْنَ قَيْسِ بْنِ مَخْرَمَة، يُخْبِرُ أَنَّ أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: لَمَّا نَزَلَتْ: ﴿مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ﴾ شَقّ ذَلِكَ عَلَى الْمُسْلِمِينَ، فَقَالَ لَهُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ: "سَدِّدوا وَقَارِبُوا، فَإِنَّ فِي كُلِّ مَا يُصَابُ بِهِ الْمُسْلِمُ كَفَّارَةٌ حَتَّى الشَّوْكَةِ يُشَاكها، والنَّكْبَة يَنْكُبُهَا".

وَهَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَحْمَدُ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ بْنِ عُيَيْنَةَ، وَمُسْلِمٍ وَالتِّرْمِذِيِّ وَالنَّسَائِيِّ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ سُفْيَانَ بْنِ عُيَيْنَةَ، بِهِ وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ مَردُويه مِنْ حَدِيثِ رَوْحٍ وَمُعْتَمِرٍ كِلَاهُمَا، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ يَزِيدَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، سَمِعْتُ أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ يَقُولُ: لَمَّا نَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ: ﴿لَيْسَ بِأَمَانِيِّكُمْ وَلا أَمَانِيِّ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ﴾ بَكَيْنَا وَحَزِنَّا وَقُلْنَا: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، مَا أَبْقَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ مِنْ شَيْءٍ. قَالَ: "أَمَا وَالَّذِي نَفْسِي بِيَدِهِ إِنَّهَا لَكَمَا نَزَلَتْ، وَلَكِنْ أَبْشِرُوا وَقَارِبُوا وسَدِّدوا؛ فَإِنَّهُ لَا يُصِيبُ أحدًا منكم فِي الدُّنْيَا إِلَّا كفَّر اللَّهُ بِهَا خَطِيئَتَهُ، حَتَّى الشَّوْكَةُ يُشَاكها أَحَدُكُمْ فِي قَدَمِهِ" .

وَقَالَ عَطَاءُ بْنُ يَسَارٍ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ وَأَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ: أَنَّهُمَا سَمِعَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَقُولُ: "مَا يُصِيبُ الْمُؤْمِنَ مِنْ نَصب وَلَا وَصَب وَلَا سَقَم وَلَا حَزَن، حَتَّى الْهَمِّ يُهَمّه، إِلَّا كُفّر بِهِ مِنْ سَيِّئَاتِهِ" أَخْرَجَاهُ .

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: قَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى، عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، حَدَّثَتْنِي زَيْنَبُ بِنْتُ كَعْبِ بنُ عُجْرَة، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ قَالَ: قَالَ رَجُلٌ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ: أَرَأَيْتَ هَذِهِ الْأَمْرَاضَ الَّتِي تُصِيبُنَا؟ مَا لَنَا بِهَا؟ قَالَ: "كَفَّارَاتٌ". قَالَ أُبَيٌّ: وَإِنْ قَلَّتْ؟ قَالَ: "وَإِنْ شَوْكَةً فَمَا فَوْقَهَا" قَالَ: فَدَعَا أُبَيٌّ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ أَنَّهُ لَا يُفَارِقُهُ الْوَعْك حَتَّى يَمُوتَ، فِي أَلَّا يَشْغَلَهُ عَنْ حَجٍّ وَلَا عُمْرَةٍ، وَلَا جِهَادٍ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ، وَلَا صَلَاةٍ مَكْتُوبَةٍ فِي جَمَاعَةٍ، فَمَا مَسَّهُ إِنْسَانٌ إِلَّا وَجَدَ حَرَّهُ، حَتَّى مَاتَ، رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ. تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ أَحْمَدُ .

حَدِيثٌ آخَرُ: رَوَى ابْنُ مَرْدَوَيْهِ مِنْ طَرِيقِ حُسَيْنِ بْنِ وَاقِدٍ، عَنِ الْكَلْبِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: قِيلَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ: ﴿مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ﴾ ؟ قَالَ: "نَعَمْ، وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ حَسَنَةً يُجزَ بِهَا عَشْرًا. فَهَلَكَ مَنْ غَلَبَ وَاحِدَتُهُ عَشْرًا" .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ وَكِيع، حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنْ الْحَسَنِ: ﴿مَنْ يَعْمَلْ سُوءًا يُجْزَ بِهِ﴾ قَالَ: الْكَافِرُ، ثُمَّ قَرَأَ: ﴿وَهَلْ نُجَازِي إِلا الْكَفُورَ﴾ [سَبَأٍ: ١٧] .

وَهَكَذَا رُوي عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، وَسَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ: أَنَّهُمَا فَسَّرَا السُّوءَ هَاهُنَا بِالشِّرْكِ أَيْضًا.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَلا يَجِدْ لَهُ مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ وَلِيًّا وَلا نَصِيرًا﴾ قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: إِلَّا أَنْ يَتُوبَ فَيَتُوبَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ. رَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ.

وَالصَّحِيحُ أَنَّ ذَلِكَ عامٌّ فِي جَمِيعِ الْأَعْمَالِ، لِمَا تَقَدَّمَ مِنَ الْأَحَادِيثِ، وَهَذَا اخْتِيَارُ ابْنِ جَرِيرٍ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَمَنْ يَعْمَلْ مِنَ الصَّالِحَاتِ مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثَى وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ [فَأُولَئِكَ يَدْخُلُونَ الْجَنَّةَ وَلا يُظْلَمُونَ نَقِيرًا] ﴾ لَمَّا ذَكَرَ الْجَزَاءَ عَلَى السَّيِّئَاتِ، وَأَنَّهُ لَا بُدَّ أَنْ يَأْخُذَ مُسْتَحَقَّهَا مِنَ الْعَبْدِ إِمَّا فِي الدُّنْيَا -وَهُوَ الْأَجْوَدُ لَهُ -وَإِمَّا فِي الْآخِرَةِ -وَالْعِيَاذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنْ ذَلِكَ، وَنَسْأَلُهُ الْعَافِيَةَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ، وَالصَّفْحَ وَالْعَفْوَ وَالْمُسَامَحَةَ -شَرَعَ فِي بَيَانِ إِحْسَانِهِ وَكَرَمِهِ وَرَحْمَتِهِ فِي قَبُولِ الْأَعْمَالِ الصَّالِحَةِ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ ذُكْرَانهم وَإِنَاثِهِمْ، بِشَرْطِ الْإِيمَانِ، وَأَنَّهُ سَيُدْخِلُهُمُ الْجَنَّةَ وَلَا يَظْلِمُهُمْ مِنْ حَسَنَاتِهِمْ وَلَا مِقْدَارَ النَّقِيرِ، وَهُوَ: النَّقْرَةُ الَّتِي فِي ظَهْرِ نَوَاةِ التَّمْرَةِ، وَقَدْ تَقَدَّمَ الْكَلَامُ عَلَى الْفَتِيلِ، وَهُوَ الْخَيْطُ الَّذِي فِي شِقِّ النَّوَاةِ، وَهَذَا النَّقِيرُ وَهُمَا فِي نَوَاةِ التَّمْرَةِ، وَكَذَا الْقِطْمِيرُ وَهُوَ اللِّفَافَةُ الَّتِي عَلَى نَوَاةِ التَّمْرَةِ، الثَّلَاثَةُ فِي القرآن.

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ دِينًا مِمَّنْ أَسْلَمَ وَجْهَهُ لِلَّهِ﴾ أَخْلَصَ الْعَمَلَ لِرَبِّهِ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، فَعَمِلَ إِيمَانًا وَاحْتِسَابًا ﴿وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ﴾ أَيِ: اتَّبَعَ فِي عَمَلِهِ مَا شَرَعَهُ اللَّهُ لَهُ، وَمَا أَرْسَلَ بِهِ رَسُولَهُ مِنَ الْهُدَى وَدِينِ الْحَقِّ، وَهَذَانَ الشَّرْطَانِ لَا يَصِحُّ عَمَلُ عَامِلٍ بِدُونِهِمَا، أَيْ: يَكُونُ خَالِصًا صَوَابًا، وَالْخَالِصُ أَنْ يَكُونَ لِلَّهِ، وَالصَّوَابُ أَنْ يَكُونَ مُتَّبِعًا لِلشَّرِيعَةِ فيصح ظاهره بالمتابعة، وباطنه بالإخلاص، فمن فَقَدَ الْعَمَلُ أَحَدَ هَذَيْنِ الشَّرْطَيْنِ فَسَدَ. فَمَنْ فقد الإخلاص كان منافقًا، وهم الذين يراءون النَّاسَ، وَمَنْ فَقَدَ الْمُتَابَعَةَ كَانَ ضَالًّا جَاهِلًا. وَمَتَى جَمَعَهُمَا فَهُوَ عَمَلُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ: ﴿الَّذِينَ نَتَقَبَّلُ عَنْهُمْ أَحْسَنَ مَا عَمِلُوا وَنَتَجاوَزُ عَنْ سَيِّئَاتِهِمْ [فِي أَصْحَابِ الْجَنَّةِ وَعْدَ الصِّدْقِ الَّذِي كَانُوا يُوعَدُونَ] ﴾ [الْأَحْقَافِ: ١٦] ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَاتَّبَعَ مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا﴾ وَهُمْ مُحَمَّدٌ وَأَتْبَاعُهُ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِنَّ أَوْلَى النَّاسِ بِإِبْرَاهِيمَ لَلَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ وَهَذَا النَّبِيُّ [وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَاللَّهُ وَلِيُّ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ] ﴾ [آلِ عِمْرَانَ: ٦٨] وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿ [قُلْ إِنَّنِي هَدَانِي رَبِّي إِلَى صِرَاطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ دِينًا قِيَمًا مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ] ﴾ [الْأَنْعَامِ: ١٦١] وَ ﴿ثُمَّ أَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْكَ أَنِ اتَّبِعْ مِلَّةَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ حَنِيفًا وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ﴾ [النَّحْلِ: ١٢٣] وَالْحَنِيفُ: هُوَ الْمَائِلُ عَنِ الشِّرْكِ قَصْدًا، أَيْ تَارِكًا لَهُ عَنْ بَصِيرَةٍ، وَمُقْبِلٌ عَلَى الْحَقِّ بِكُلِّيَّتِهِ، لَا يَصُدُّهُ عَنْهُ صَادٌّ، وَلَا يَرُدُّهُ عَنْهُ رَادٌّ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَاتَّخَذَ اللَّهُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ خَلِيلا﴾ وَهَذَا مِنْ بَابِ التَّرْغِيبِ فِي اتِّبَاعِهِ؛ لِأَنَّهُ إِمَامٌ يُقْتَدَى بِهِ، حَيْثُ وَصَلَ إِلَى غَايَةِ مَا يَتَقَرَّبُ بِهِ الْعِبَادُ لَهُ، فَإِنَّهُ انْتَهَى إِلَى دَرَجَةِ الخُلَّة الَّتِي هِيَ أَرْفَعُ مَقَامَاتِ الْمُحِبَّةِ، وَمَا ذَاكَ إِلَّا لِكَثْرَةِ طَاعَتِهِ لِرَبِّهِ، كَمَا وَصَفَهُ بِهِ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ الَّذِي وَفَّى﴾ [النَّجْمِ: ٣٧] قَالَ كَثِيرُونَ مِنَ السَّلَفِ: أَيْ قَامَ بِجَمِيعِ مَا أُمِرَ بِهِ ووفَّى كُلَّ مَقَامٍ مِنْ مَقَامَاتِ الْعِبَادَةِ، فَكَانَ لَا يَشْغَلُهُ أَمْرٌ جَلِيلٌ عَنْ حَقِيرٍ، وَلَا كَبِيرٌ عَنْ صَغِيرٍ. وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَإِذِ ابْتَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ رَبُّهُ بِكَلِمَاتٍ فَأَتَمَّهُنَّ [قَالَ إِنِّي جَاعِلُكَ لِلنَّاسِ إِمَامًا] ﴾ الْآيَةَ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ١٢٤] . وَقَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ كَانَ أُمَّةً قَانِتًا لِلَّهِ حَنِيفًا وَلَمْ يَكُ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ [شَاكِرًا لأنْعُمِهِ اجْتَبَاهُ وَهَدَاهُ إِلَى صِرَاطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ. وَآتَيْنَاهُ فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَإِنَّهُ فِي الآخِرَةِ لَمِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ] ﴾ [النَّحْلِ: ١٢٠-١٢٢] .

وَقَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ حَرْبٍ، حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، عَنْ حَبِيبِ بْنِ أَبِي ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ قَالَ: إِنَّ مُعَاذًا لَمَّا قَدِمَ الْيَمَنَ صَلَّى الصُّبْحَ بِهِمْ: فَقَرَأَ: ﴿وَاتَّخَذَ اللَّهُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ خَلِيلا﴾ فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ: لَقَدْ قَرّت عينُ أُمِّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ.

وَقَدْ ذَكَرَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ فِي تَفْسِيرِهِ، عَنْ بَعْضِهِمْ أَنَّهُ إِنَّمَا سَمَّاهُ اللَّهُ خَلِيلًا مِنْ أَجْلِ أَنَّهُ أَصَابَ أَهْلَ نَاحِيَتِهِ جَدْب، فَارْتَحَلَ إِلَى خَلِيلٍ لَهُ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْمَوْصِلِ -وَقَالَ بَعْضُهُمْ: مِنْ أَهْلِ مِصْرَ -لِيَمْتَارَ طَعَامًا لِأَهْلِهِ مِنْ قِبَله، فَلَمْ يُصِبْ عِنْدَهُ حَاجَتَهُ. فَلَمَّا قَرُب مِنْ أَهْلِهِ مَرَّ بِمَفَازَةٍ ذَاتِ رَمْلٍ، فَقَالَ: لَوْ مَلَأْتُ غَرَائري مِنْ هَذَا الرَّمْلِ، لِئَلَّا أغُمّ أَهْلِي بِرُجُوعِي إِلَيْهِمْ بِغَيْرِ مِيرَةٍ، وَلِيَظُنُّوا أَنِّي أَتَيْتُهُمْ بِمَا يُحِبُّونَ. فَفَعَلَ ذَلِكَ، فَتَحَوَّلَ مَا فِي غَرَائِرِهِ مِنَ الرَّمْلِ دَقِيقًا، فَلَمَّا صَارَ إِلَى مَنْزِلِهِ نَامَ وقام أهله ففتحوا الغرائر، فَوَجَدُوا دَقِيقًا فَعَجَنُوا وَخَبَزُوا مِنْهُ فَاسْتَيْقَظَ، فَسَأَلَهُمْ عَنِ الدَّقِيقِ الَّذِي مِنْهُ خَبَزُوا، فَقَالُوا: مِنَ الدَّقِيقِ الَّذِي جِئْتَ بِهِ مِنْ عِنْدِ خَلِيلِكَ فَقَالَ: نَعَمْ، هُوَ مِنْ خَلِيلِي اللَّهِ. فَسَمَّاهُ اللَّهُ بِذَلِكَ خَلِيلًا.

وَفِي صِحَّةِ هَذَا وَوُقُوعِهِ نَظَرٌ، وَغَايَتُهُ أَنْ يَكُونَ خَبَرًا إِسْرَائِيلِيًّا لَا يُصدَّق وَلَا يُكذَّب، وَإِنَّمَا سُمّي خَلِيلَ اللَّهِ لِشِدَّةِ مَحَبَّةِ رَبِّهِ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، لَهُ، لِمَا قَامَ لَهُ مِنَ الطَّاعَةِ الَّتِي يُحِبُّهَا وَيَرْضَاهَا؛ وَلِهَذَا ثَبَتَ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى الله عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لَمَّا خَطَبَهُمْ فِي آخِرِ خُطْبَةٍ خَطَبَهَا قَالَ: "أَمَّا بَعْدُ، أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ، فَلَوْ كُنْتُ مُتَّخِذًا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْأَرْضِ خَلِيلًا لَاتَّخَذْتُ أَبَا بَكْرِ بْنَ أَبِي قُحَافَةَ خَلِيلًا وَلَكِنَّ صَاحِبَكُمْ خَلِيلُ اللَّهِ" .

وَجَاءَ مِنْ طَرِيقِ جُنْدُب بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ البَجَلي، وَعَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمرو بْنِ الْعَاصِ، وَعَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ: "إن اللَّهَ اتَّخَذَنِي خَلِيلًا كَمَا اتَّخَذَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ خَلِيلًا" .

وَقَالَ أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ مَرْدُويه: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أُسَيْد، حَدَّثَنَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ الجَوْزجاني بِمَكَّةَ، حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيد اللَّهِ الحَنَفي، حَدَّثَنَا زَمْعة بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ وَهْرَام، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: جَلَسَ نَاسٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَنْتَظِرُونَهُ، فَخَرَجَ حَتَّى إِذَا دَنَا مِنْهُمْ سَمِعَهُمْ يَتَذَاكَرُونَ، فَسَمِعَ حَدِيثَهُمْ، وَإِذَا بَعْضُهُمْ يَقُولُ: عَجَبًا إِنِ اللَّهَ اتَّخَذَ مِنْ خَلْقِهِ خَلِيلًا فَإِبْرَاهِيمُ خَلِيلُهُ! وَقَالَ آخَرُ: مَاذَا بِأَعْجَبِ مِنْ أَنَّ اللَّهَ كَلَّمَ مُوسَى تَكْلِيمًا! وَقَالَ آخَرُ: فَعِيسَى رُوحُ اللَّهِ وَكَلِمَتُهُ! وَقَالَ آخَرُ: آدَمُ اصْطَفَاهُ اللَّهُ! فَخَرَجَ عَلَيْهِمْ فَسَلَّمَ وَقَالَ: "قَدْ سَمِعْتُ كَلَامَكُمْ وَتَعَجُّبَكُمْ أَنَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ خَلِيلُ اللَّهِ، وَهُوَ كَذَلِكَ، وَمُوسَى كَلِيمُهُ، وَعِيسَى رُوحُهُ وَكَلِمَتُهُ، وَآدَمَ اصْطَفَاهُ اللَّهُ، وَهُوَ كَذَلِكَ أَلَا وَإِنِّي حَبِيبُ اللَّهِ وَلَا فَخْرَ، وَأَنَا حَامِلُ لِوَاءِ الْحَمْدِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَلَا فَخْرَ، وَأَنَا أَوَّلُ شَافِعٍ، وَأَوَّلُ مشَفع وَلَا فَخْرَ، وَأَنَا أَوَّلُ مَنْ يُحَرِّكُ حِلَق الْجَنَّةِ، فَيَفْتَحُ اللَّهُ فَيُدْخِلُنِيهَا وَمَعِي فُقَرَاءُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَلَا فَخْرَ، وَأَنَا أَكْرَمُ الْأَوَّلِينَ وَالْآخَرِينَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَلَا فَخْرَ".

وَهَذَا حَدِيثٌ غَرِيبٌ مِنْ هَذَا الْوَجْهِ، وَلِبَعْضِهِ شَوَاهِدُ فِي الصِّحَاحِ وَغَيْرِهَا.

وَقَالَ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: أَتَعْجَبُونَ مِنْ أَنْ تَكُونَ الخُلَّة لِإِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَالْكَلَامُ لِمُوسَى، وَالرُّؤْيَةُ لِمُحَمَّدٍ، صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ وَسَلَامُهُ عَلَيْهِمْ أَجْمَعِينَ.

رَوَاهُ الْحَاكِمُ فِي مُسْتَدْرَكِهِ وَقَالَ: صَحِيحٌ عَلَى شَرْطِ الْبُخَارِيِّ، وَلَمْ يُخْرِجَاهُ. وَكَذَا رُوِيَ عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، وَغَيْرِ وَاحِدٍ مِنَ الصَّحَابَةِ وَالتَّابِعَيْنَ، وَالْأَئِمَّةِ مِنَ السَّلَفِ وَالْخَلْفِ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ عَبْدَكَ الْقَزْوِينِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدٌ -يَعْنِي ابْنَ سَعِيدِ بْنِ سابق - حَدَّثَنَا عَمْرٌو -يَعْنِي ابْنَ أَبِي قَيْسٍ -عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي رَاشِدٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْد بْنِ عُمَير قَالَ: كَانَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ يُضِيفُ النَّاسَ، فَخَرَجَ يَوْمًا يَلْتَمِسُ إِنْسَانًا يُضِيفُهُ، فَلَمْ يَجِدْ أَحَدًا يُضِيفُهُ، فَرَجَعَ إِلَى دَارِهِ فَوَجَدَ فِيهَا رَجُلًا قَائِمًا، فَقَالَ: يَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ، مَا أَدْخَلَكَ دَارِي بِغَيْرِ إِذْنِي؟ قَالَ: دَخَلْتُهَا بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهَا. قَالَ: وَمَنْ أَنْتَ؟ قَالَ: أَنَا مَلَكُ الْمَوْتِ، أَرْسَلَنِي رَبِّي إِلَى عَبْدٍ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ أُبَشِّرُهُ أَنَّ اللَّهَ قَدِ اتَّخَذَهُ خَلِيلًا. قَالَ: مَنْ هُوَ؟ فَوَاللَّهِ إِنْ أَخْبَرْتَنِي بِهِ ثُمَّ كَانَ بِأَقْصَى الْبِلَادِ لَآتِيَنَّهُ ثُمَّ لَا أَبْرَحُ لَهُ جَارًا حَتَّى يُفَرِّقَ بَيْنَنَا الْمَوْتُ. قَالَ: ذَلِكَ الْعَبْدُ أَنْتَ. قَالَ: أَنَا؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ. قَالَ: فِيمَ اتَّخَذَنِي اللَّهُ خَلِيلًا؟ قَالَ: إِنَّكَ تُعْطِي النَّاسَ وَلَا تَسْأَلُهُمْ .

وَحَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا مَحْمُودُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ السُّلَمِيُّ، حَدَّثَنَا الْوَلِيدُ، عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ يَسَارٍ قَالَ: لَمَّا اتَّخَذَ اللَّهُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ خَلِيلًا أَلْقَى فِي قَلْبِهِ الوَجَل، حَتَّى إِنْ كَانَ خفقانُ قَلْبِهِ لَيُسْمَعُ مِنْ بَعِيدٍ كَمَا يُسْمَعُ خَفَقَانُ الطَّيْرِ فِي الْهَوَاءِ. وَهَكَذَا جَاءَ فِي صِفَةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُسْمَعُ لِصَدْرِهِ أَزِيزٌ كَأَزِيزِ المرْجل مِنَ الْبُكَاءِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَللهِ مَا فِي السَّمَوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الأرْضِ﴾ أَيِ: الْجَمِيعُ مِلْكُهُ وَعَبِيدُهُ وَخَلْقُهُ، وَهُوَ الْمُتَصَرِّفُ فِي جَمِيعِ ذَلِكَ، لَا رَادَّ لِمَا قَضَى، وَلَا مُعَقِّبَ لِمَا حَكَمَ، وَلَا يُسْأَلُ عَمَّا يَفْعَلُ، لِعَظَمَتِهِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ وَعَدْلِهِ وَحِكْمَتِهِ وَلُطْفِهِ وَرَحْمَتِهِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَكَانَ اللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ مُحِيطًا﴾ أَيْ: عِلْمُهُ نَافِذٌ فِي جَمِيعِ ذَلِكَ، لَا تَخْفَى عَلَيْهِ خَافِيَةٌ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ، وَلَا يعْزُب عَنْ عِلْمِهِ مِثْقَالُ ذَرَّةٍ فِي السموات وَلَا فِي الْأَرْضِ، وَلَا أَصْغَرَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ وَلَا أَكْبَرَ، وَلَا تَخْفَى عَلَيْهِ ذَرَّةٌ لِمَا تَرَاءَى لِلنَّاظِرِينَ وَمَا تَوَارَى.

---

## 4:127 — verse 127

### Translation

Al-Bukhari said: Ubayd ibn Isma'il narrated to us, Abu Usamah narrated to us, he said: Hisham ibn Urwah narrated to us, my father informed me, from A'ishah, regarding {They ask you for a ruling concerning women. Say: Allah gives you a ruling concerning them} up to His saying {and you desire to marry them}. She said: This concerns the man who has an orphan girl in his care, he being her guardian and heir, who has a share with her in her property, even in the palm cluster, and he desires to marry her but dislikes marrying her to another man who would then share with him in the property in which she already shares with him, so he withholds her from marriage. So this verse was revealed.

Muslim likewise related it, from Abu Kurayb and from Abu Bakr ibn Abi Shaybah, both from Abu Usamah.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: I read to Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Abd al-Hakam, Ibn Wahb informed us, Yunus informed me, from Ibn Shihab, Urwah ibn al-Zubayr informed me, A'ishah said: Then the people sought a ruling from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ after this verse concerning them, so Allah revealed: {They ask you for a ruling concerning women. Say: Allah gives you a ruling concerning them, and what is recited to you in the Book} the verse. She said: What Allah mentioned as being recited to them in the Book is the earlier verse in which Allah [the Exalted] said: {And if you fear that you will not deal justly with the orphans, then marry those that are pleasing to you of women} [al-Nisa: 3].

And with this same chain, from A'ishah, she said: The saying of Allah, Mighty and Majestic, {and you desire to marry them} refers to the aversion one of you might feel toward his orphan girl who is under his guardianship, when she has little wealth and little beauty. So they were forbidden to marry those orphan women whose wealth and beauty they desired, except with fairness, precisely because of their aversion toward the ones lacking these qualities.

Its basis is firmly established in the two Sahihs, through the route of Yunus ibn Yazid al-Ayli, with the same content.

The point is that when a man has an orphan girl under his guardianship whom it is lawful for him to marry, he sometimes desires to marry her. Allah, Mighty and Majestic, commanded him in that case to give her a dower equal to that of women like her; and if he will not do that, then let him turn instead to another woman, for Allah, Mighty and Majestic, has made things ample. This meaning is found in the first verse at the beginning of the surah. And at other times the man has no desire for her, because he finds her unattractive, or because this is simply the reality of the matter, so Allah, Mighty and Majestic, forbade him to withhold her from marrying others, out of fear that they would then share with him in the property that lies between him and her. This is as Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas, concerning His saying: {concerning the orphan women [to whom you do not give what has been decreed for them, and whom you desire to marry]} the verse: In the Jahiliyyah (pre-Islamic era), a man might have an orphan girl in his care, and he would throw his garment over her; once he did that to her, no one else could ever marry her. If she was beautiful and he desired her, he would marry her and consume her wealth; and if she was unattractive, he would keep her from marrying other men forever, until she died, and then he would inherit from her. So Allah forbade that and prohibited it.

And he said concerning His saying: {and the weak among the children}: In the Jahiliyyah they did not give inheritance to the young or to daughters, and that is the meaning of His saying: {you do not give them what has been decreed for them}. So Allah forbade that, and made clear to every rightful heir his due share, saying: {for the male is the equivalent share of two females} [al-Nisa: 11], whether young or old.

Sa'id ibn Jubayr and others said likewise. Sa'id ibn Jubayr said concerning His saying: {and that you maintain justice for the orphans}: Just as when she has beauty and wealth you marry her and keep her for yourself, so too when she has neither beauty nor wealth, marry her and keep her for yourself.

And His saying: {And whatever good you do, indeed Allah is ever Knowing of it} is meant to spur people on to doing good deeds and complying with the command, and to affirm that Allah, Mighty and Majestic, knows all of that, and will reward it with the fullest and most complete recompense.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

قَالَ الْبُخَارِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هِشَامُ بْنُ عُرْوَةَ، أَخْبَرَنِي أَبِي عَنْ عَائِشَةَ: ﴿وَيَسْتَفْتُونَكَ فِي النِّسَاءِ قُلِ اللَّهُ يُفْتِيكُمْ فِيهِنَّ﴾ إِلَى قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَتَرْغَبُونَ أَنْ تَنْكِحُوهُنَّ﴾ قَالَتْ: هُوَ الرَّجُلُ تَكُونُ عِنْدَهُ الْيَتِيمَةُ، هُوَ وَلِيُّهَا وَوَارِثُهَا قَدْ شَرِكته فِي مَالِهِ، حَتَّى فِي العَذْق، فَيَرْغَبُ أَنْ يَنْكِحَهَا، وَيَكْرَهُ أَنْ يزوِّجها رَجُلًا فَيَشْرَكَهُ فِي مَالِهِ بِمَا شَرِكَتْهُ فَيَعْضُلُهَا، فَنَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ.

وَكَذَلِكَ رَوَاهُ مُسْلِمٌ، عَنْ أَبِي كُرَيب، وَعَنْ أَبِي بَكْرِ بْنِ أَبِي شَيْبَةَ، كِلَاهُمَا عَنْ أَبِي أُسَامَةَ .

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: قَرَأْتُ عَلَى مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْحَكَمِ، أَخْبَرَنَا ابْنُ وَهْبٍ، أَخْبَرَنِي يُونُسُ، عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ، أَخْبَرَنِي عُرْوَةُ بْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ، قَالَتْ عَائِشَةُ: ثُمَّ إِنَّ النَّاسَ استفْتَوْا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم بَعْدَ هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ فِيهِنَّ، فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: ﴿وَيَسْتَفْتُونَكَ فِي النِّسَاءِ قُلِ اللَّهُ يُفْتِيكُمْ فِيهِنَّ وَمَا يُتْلَى عَلَيْكُمْ فِي الْكِتَابِ﴾ الْآيَةَ، قَالَتْ: وَالَّذِي ذَكَرَ اللَّهُ أَنَّهُ يُتْلَى عَلَيْهِمْ فِي الْكِتَابِ الْآيَةُ الْأَوْلَى الَّتِي قَالَ اللَّهِ [تَعَالَى] ﴿وَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلا تُقْسِطُوا فِي الْيَتَامَى فَانْكِحُوا مَا طَابَ لَكُمْ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ﴾ [النِّسَاءِ: ٣] .

وَبِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ قَالَتْ: وَقَوْلُ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: ﴿وَتَرْغَبُونَ أَنْ تَنْكِحُوهُنَّ﴾ رَغْبَةَ أَحَدِكُمْ عَنْ يَتِيمَتِهِ الَّتِي تَكُونُ فِي حِجْرِهِ حِينَ تَكُونُ قَلِيلَةَ الْمَالِ وَالْجِمَالِ، فَنُهُوا أَنْ يَنْكِحُوا مَا رَغِبُوا فِي مَالِهَا وَجِمَالِهَا مِنْ يَتَامَى النِّسَاءِ إِلَّا بِالْقِسْطِ، مِنْ أَجْلِ رَغْبَتِهِمْ عَنْهُنَّ.

وَأَصْلُهُ ثَابِتٌ فِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ يُونُسَ بْنِ يَزِيدَ الأيْلي، بِهِ .

وَالْمَقْصُودُ أَنَّ الرَّجُلَ إِذَا كَانَ فِي حِجْرِهِ يَتِيمَةٌ يَحِلُّ لَهُ تَزْوِيجُهَا، فَتَارَةً يَرْغَبُ فِي أَنْ يَتَزَوَّجَهَا، فَأَمَرَهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ أَنْ يُمْهِرَهَا أُسْوَةَ أَمْثَالِهَا مِنَ النِّسَاءِ، فَإِنْ لَمْ يَفْعَلْ فَلْيَعْدِلْ إِلَى غَيْرِهَا مِنَ النِّسَاءِ، فَقَدْ وَسَّعَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ. وَهَذَا الْمَعْنَى فِي الْآيَةِ الْأُولَى الَّتِي فِي أَوَّلِ السُّورَةِ. وَتَارَةً لَا يَكُونُ لِلرَّجُلِ فِيهَا رَغْبَةٌ لِدَمَامَتِهَا عِنْدَهُ، أَوْ فِي نَفْسِ الْأَمْرِ، فَنَهَاهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ أَنْ يُعضِلها عَنِ الْأَزْوَاجِ خَشْيَةَ أَنْ يَشْركوه فِي مَالِهِ الَّذِي بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَهَا، كَمَا قَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿فِي يَتَامَى النِّسَاء [اللَّاتِي لَا تُؤْتُونَهُنَّ مَا كُتِبَ لَهُنَّ وَتَرْغَبُونَ أَنْ تَنْكِحُوهُنَّ] ﴾ الْآيَةَ، فَكَانَ الرَّجُلُ فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ تَكُونُ عِنْدَهُ الْيَتِيمَةُ، فَيُلْقِي عَلَيْهَا ثَوْبَهُ، فَإِذَا فَعَلَ ذَلِكَ [بِهَا] لَمْ يَقْدِرْ أَحَدٌ أَنْ يتَزَوّجها أَبَدًا، فَإِنْ كَانَتْ جَمِيلَةً وَهَوِيَهَا تَزَوَّجَها وَأَكَلَ مَالَهَا، وَإِنْ كَانَتْ دَمِيمَةً مَنْعَهَا الرِّجَالَ أَبَدًا حَتَّى تَمُوتَ، فَإِذَا مَاتَتْ وَرِثَهَا. فَحَرَّم اللَّهُ ذَلِكَ وَنَهَى عَنْهُ.

وَقَالَ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَالْمُسْتَضْعَفِينَ مِنَ الْوِلْدَانِ﴾ كَانُوا فِي الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ لَا يُورِثُونَ الصِّغَارَ وَلَا الْبَنَاتَ، وَذَلِكَ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿لَا تُؤْتُونَهُنَّ مَا كُتِبَ لَهُنَّ﴾ فَنَهَى اللَّهُ عَنْ ذَلِكَ، وبيَّن لِكُلِّ ذِي سَهْمٍ سَهْمَهُ، فَقَالَ: ﴿لِلذَّكَرِ مِثْلُ حَظِّ الأنْثَيَيْنِ﴾ [النِّسَاءِ: ١١] صَغِيرًا أَوْ كَبِيرًا.

وَكَذَا قَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ وَغَيْرُهُ، قَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَأَنْ تَقُومُوا لِلْيَتَامَى بِالْقِسْطِ﴾ كَمَا إِذَا كَانَتْ ذَاتَ جِمَالٍ وَمَالٍ نَكَحْتَهَا وَاسْتَأْثَرْتَ بِهَا، كَذَلِكَ إِذَا لَمْ تَكُنْ ذَاتَ جِمَالٍ وَلَا مَالٍ فَانْكِحْهَا وَاسْتَأْثِرْ بِهَا.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَمَا تَفْعَلُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ بِهِ عَلِيمًا﴾ تهييجًا عَلَى فِعْلِ الْخَيْرَاتِ وَامْتِثَالُ الْأَمْرِ وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ عَالِمٌ بِجَمِيعِ ذَلِكَ، وَسَيَجْزِي عَلَيْهِ أوفر الجزاء وأتمه.

---

## 4:128-130 — verses 128-130 (shared commentary)

### Translation

The Exalted speaks, informing and legislating, about the condition of spouses: sometimes concerning the man's aversion toward the woman, sometimes concerning his agreement with her, and sometimes concerning his separation from her.

The first case is when the woman fears that her husband will turn away from her or avert himself from her. In that case she may relinquish her right, or part of it, whether maintenance, clothing, nightly turn, or other rights owed to her by him, and he may accept that from her. There is no blame on her for offering that to him, nor on him for accepting it from her. This is why the Exalted said: {There is no blame upon them if they reach a settlement between themselves}, then He said: {and settlement is better}, meaning better than separation. And His saying {and souls are ever present with greed} means that settlement, in the presence of stinginess, is better than separation.

This is why, when Sawdah bint Zam'ah grew old, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ resolved to separate from her, so she made a settlement with him that he would keep her while she would give up her turn to Aisha, and he accepted that from her and kept her on that basis.

Mention of the report concerning that:

Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi said: [chain] Sulayman ibn Mu'adh, from Simak ibn Harb, from Ikrimah, from Ibn Abbas, who said: Sawdah feared that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ would divorce her, so she said: "O Messenger of Allah, do not divorce me, and give my turn to Aisha." He did so, and this verse was revealed: {And if a woman fears from her husband ill treatment or aversion...} to the end of the verse. Ibn Abbas said: "Whatever settlement they agree upon is permissible."

Al-Tirmidhi also related it, from Muhammad ibn al-Muthanna, from Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi, with the same chain, and said: hasan gharib (sound but singular in its chain).

Al-Shafi'i said: [chain] Muslim, from Ibn Jurayj, from Ata, from Ibn Abbas, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ died leaving nine wives, and he used to divide his time among eight of them.

In the two Sahihs, from the hadith of Hisham ibn Urwah, from his father, from Aisha, who said: When Sawdah bint Zam'ah grew old, she gave her turn to Aisha, so the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to allot Aisha's share on Sawdah's day.

In Sahih al-Bukhari, from the hadith of al-Zuhri, from Urwah, from Aisha, similarly.

Sa'id ibn Mansur said: [chain] Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi al-Zinad, from Hisham, from his father Urwah, who said: Allah the Exalted revealed concerning Sawdah and those like her: {And if a woman fears from her husband ill treatment or aversion...}. This was because Sawdah was a woman who had grown old, and she was alarmed that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ might separate from her, and she was reluctant to lose her place with him, and she knew of the Messenger of Allah's ﷺ love for Aisha and her standing with him, so she gave her turn from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ to Aisha, and the Prophet ﷺ accepted that.

Al-Bayhaqi said: Ahmad ibn Yunus related it from Ibn Abi al-Zinad as a connected chain. This route was related by al-Hakim in his Mustadrak, where he said:

[chain] Abu Bakr ibn Ishaq al-Faqih, al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Ziyad, Ahmad ibn Yunus, Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi al-Zinad, from Hisham ibn Urwah, from his father, from Aisha, that she said to him: "O son of my sister, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not favor some of us over others in the time he spent with us. Rarely would a day pass without him going around to all of us, coming near each woman without intimate contact, until he reached the one whose turn it was, and he would spend the night with her. Sawdah bint Zam'ah, when she grew old and feared that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ might separate from her, said: 'O Messenger of Allah, this turn of mine is for Aisha.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ accepted that." Aisha said: "It was concerning that, that Allah revealed: {And if a woman fears from her husband ill treatment or aversion...}"

Abu Dawud related it likewise, from Ahmad ibn Yunus, with the same chain. Then al-Hakim said: sound chain, though the two sheikhs [Bukhari and Muslim] did not include it in their collections.

Al-Hafiz Abu Bakr ibn Mardawayh related it through the route of Abu Bilal al-Ash'ari, from Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi al-Zinad, with the same chain, similarly. And through the narration of Abd al-Aziz ibn Muhammad al-Darawardi, from Hisham ibn Urwah, similarly but abridged. And Allah knows best.

Abu al-Abbas Muhammad ibn Abd al-Rahman al-Daghuli said at the beginning of his Mu'jam: [chain] Muhammad ibn Yahya, Muslim ibn Ibrahim, Hisham al-Dastuwa'i, al-Qasim ibn Abi Bazzah, who said: The Prophet ﷺ sent word to Sawdah bint Zam'ah of her divorce. When he came to her, she sat for him along Aisha's path, and when she saw him she said to him: "I adjure you by the One who revealed His speech to you and chose you above His creation, take me back. I have grown old and have no need of men, but I wish to be raised among your wives on the Day of Resurrection." So he took her back. She then said: "I have made my day and night for the beloved of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ [meaning Aisha]." This report is gharib (singular) and mursal (with a missing link in the chain, going directly to a Successor).

Al-Bukhari said: [chain] Muhammad ibn Muqatil, Abdullah, Hisham ibn Urwah, from his father, from Aisha, concerning {And if a woman fears from her husband ill treatment or aversion}: she said, "This is the man who has a wife with him and does not desire much of her, and wants to separate from her, so she says: 'I release you from your obligation regarding me.'" So this verse was revealed.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Ibn Wakee, his father, from Hisham ibn Urwah, from his father, from Aisha, concerning {And if a woman fears from her husband ill treatment or aversion, there is no blame upon them if they reach a settlement between themselves, and settlement is better}: she said, "This concerns the woman who is with a man, and perhaps he does not desire much of her, and she has no child, but she has companionship with him, so she says: 'Do not divorce me, and you are released from your obligation regarding me.'"

[Ibn Jarir continued:] Al-Muthanna told me: [chain] Hajjaj ibn Minhal, Hammad ibn Salamah, from Hisham, from Urwah, from Aisha, concerning His saying {And if a woman fears from her husband ill treatment or aversion}: she said, "This is the man who has two wives, one of whom has grown old, or is unattractive, and he does not desire much of her, so she says: 'Do not divorce me, and you are released from your obligation regarding me.'"

This hadith is established in the two Sahihs, through more than one route, from Hisham ibn Urwah, from his father, from Aisha, in a manner similar to what has preceded. To Allah belongs praise and grace.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Ibn Humayd and Ibn Wakee, both said: Jarir, from Ash'ath, from Ibn Sirin, who said: A man came to Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, and asked him about a verse, and Umar disliked that and struck him with the whip. Then another man asked him about this verse: {And if a woman fears from her husband ill treatment or aversion}, so Umar said: "Ask about matters like this." Then he said: "This is the woman who is with a man, and her youth has passed, so he marries a young woman seeking children from her. Whatever settlement they agree upon is permissible."

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Ali ibn al-Hasan al-Hisinjani, Musaddad, Abu al-Ahwas, from Simak ibn Harb, from Khalid ibn Ar'arah, who said: A man came to Ali ibn Abi Talib, may Allah be pleased with him, and asked him about the saying of Allah, mighty and exalted: {And if a woman fears from her husband ill treatment or aversion, there is no blame upon them...}. Ali said: "This is the man who has a wife, and his eyes turn away from her because of her unattractiveness, or her old age, or her bad character, or her defects, but she dislikes separation from him. If she gives up part of her dower for him, that is lawful for him, and if she assigns him some of her allotted days, there is no harm in it."

Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi related it likewise, from Shu'bah, from Hammad ibn Salamah and Abu al-Ahwas. Ibn Jarir related it through the route of Isra'il, all four of them from Simak, with the same chain. Ibn Abbas, Ubaydah al-Salmani, Mujahid ibn Jabr, al-Sha'bi, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, Ata, Atiyyah al-Awfi, Makhul, al-Hakam ibn Utaybah, al-Hasan, Qatadah, and more than one of the early scholars and leading imams interpreted it likewise, and I do not know of any disagreement that this is what is meant by this verse. And Allah knows best.

Al-Shafi'i said: [chain] Ibn Uyaynah, from al-Zuhri, from Ibn al-Musayyab: that the daughter of Muhammad ibn Maslamah was married to Rafi' ibn Khadij, and he disliked something about her, either her old age or something else, and he wanted to divorce her. She said: "Do not divorce me, and allot to me whatever portion you see fit." So Allah, mighty and exalted, revealed: {And if a woman fears from her husband ill treatment or aversion...} to the end of the verse.

Al-Hakim related it in his Mustadrak, through the route of Abd al-Razzaq, from Ma'mar, from al-Zuhri, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab and Sulayman ibn Yasar, in a longer version than this account.

The hafiz Abu Bakr al-Bayhaqi said: Abu Sa'id ibn Abi Amr informed us, Abu Muhammad Ahmad ibn Abdullah al-Muzani related to us, Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Isa informed us, Abu al-Yaman related to us, Shu'ayb ibn Abi Hamza informed me, from al-Zuhri, Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab and Sulayman ibn Yasar informed me: that the established practice (sunnah) concerning these two verses in which Allah mentions a man's aversion and turning away from his wife, in His saying {And if a woman fears from her husband nushuz (ill treatment) or aversion} to the end of the two verses, is that when a man turns away from his wife and prefers another over her, it is her right that he offer her either that he divorce her, or that she remain with him despite the preference he shows to another in the division of his wealth and himself. If she remains with him on that basis and dislikes that he divorce her, there is no blame upon him for the preference he shows over her in that regard. And if he does not offer her divorce, but instead reaches a settlement with her whereby he gives her from his wealth what satisfies her, and she remains with him accepting the preference shown to another in the division of his wealth and himself, that is permissible for him, and her settlement with him on that basis is valid. This is how Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab and Sulayman described the settlement (sulh) that Allah, Mighty and Majestic, mentioned: {There is no blame upon them if they reach a settlement between themselves, and settlement is better}.

It has been mentioned to me that Rafi' ibn Khadij al-Ansari, who was one of the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ, had a wife, and when she grew old he married a young woman in addition to her, and he showed preference to the young woman over her. So she pressed him for divorce, and he divorced her once. Then he gave her time, and when her waiting period was nearly complete he took her back. Then he again showed preference to the young woman over her, so she pressed him for divorce again, and he divorced her a second time. Then he gave her time again, and when her waiting period was nearly complete he took her back. Then he again showed preference to the young woman over her, so she pressed him for divorce. He said to her: "As you wish. Only one divorce remains for you. If you wish, you may remain accepting the preference you see, or if you wish, I will separate from you." She said: "No, rather I will remain accepting the preference." So he kept her on that basis, and that was their settlement. Rafi' did not consider there to be any sin upon him once she was content to remain with him accepting the preference he showed over her.

This was narrated in full by Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Hatim, from his father, from Abu al-Yaman, from Shu'ayb, from al-Zuhri, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab and Sulayman ibn Yasar, who related it at length. And Allah knows best.

Concerning His saying {and settlement is better}: Ali ibn Abi Talha said, from Ibn Abbas: it means giving her the choice, that the husband gives her the choice between remaining and separating, which is better than the husband continuing to show preference to another over her.

The apparent meaning of the verse is that a settlement between them, in which she relinquishes some of her right in favor of the husband, and the husband accepts that, is better than complete separation. This is like how the Prophet ﷺ kept Sawda bint Zam'a after she gave up her night to Aisha, may Allah be pleased with her, and he did not separate from her but kept her among his wives. He did this so that his community might follow his example in the permissibility and legitimacy of such an arrangement, and it was more excellent as a course of action for him, upon him be blessings and peace. And since agreement is more beloved to Allah, Mighty and Majestic, than separation, He said: {and settlement is better}.

Rather, divorce is hateful to Him, glorified and exalted is He. For this reason it has come in the hadith narrated by Abu Dawud and Ibn Majah together, from Kathir ibn Ubayd, from Muhammad ibn Khalid, from Mu'arrif ibn Wasil, from Muharib ibn Dithar, from Abdullah ibn Umar, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The most hateful of permissible things to Allah is divorce."

Abu Dawud then narrated it from Ahmad ibn Yunus, from Mu'arrif, from Muharib, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said... and mentioned its meaning as a mursal (report with a missing Companion link) report.

Concerning His saying {And if you do good and are mindful of Allah, indeed Allah is ever Aware of what you do}: that is, if you bear the difficulty of patience with those of them whom you dislike, and you give them a fair share like their counterparts, then Allah knows this and will reward you for it with the fullest reward.

Then Allah, exalted is He, says: {And you will never be able to be equitable between wives, even if you strive to do so}, that is: you will never be able, O people, to make wives completely equal in every respect, for even if the outward division is achieved, night for night, there must inevitably be disparity in love, desire, and intimacy, as Ibn Abbas, Ubayda al-Salmani, Mujahid, al-Hasan al-Basri, and al-Dahhak ibn Muzahim have said.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: Abu Zur'a related to us, Ibn Abi Shayba related to us, Husayn al-Ju'fi related to us, from Za'ida, from Abd al-Aziz ibn Rufay', from Ibn Abi Mulayka, who said: This verse was revealed, {And you will never be able to be equitable between wives, even if you strive to do so}, concerning Aisha. That is, because the Prophet ﷺ loved her more than the others, as has come in the hadith narrated by Imam Ahmad and the compilers of the Sunan, from the report of Hammad ibn Salama, from Ayyub, from Abu Qilaba, from Abdullah ibn Yazid, from Aisha, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to divide his time among his wives and was fair, then he would say: "O Allah, this is my division in what I possess, so do not blame me for what You possess and I do not possess," meaning the heart.

This is the wording of Abu Dawud, and this chain is sound (sahih). However, al-Tirmidhi said: it was narrated by Hammad ibn Zayd and more than one other, from Ayyub, from Abu Qilaba, as a mursal report, and said: this is more authentic.

Concerning His saying {so do not incline completely}: that is, if you incline toward one of them, do not go to the extreme of complete inclination, {so as to leave her hanging}, that is, so that the other one remains suspended.

Ibn Abbas, Mujahid, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, al-Hasan, al-Dahhak, al-Rabi' ibn Anas, al-Suddi, and Muqatil ibn Hayyan said: its meaning is neither having a husband nor being divorced.

Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi said: Hammam informed us, from Qatada, from al-Nadr ibn Anas, from Bashir ibn Nahik, from Abu Hurayra, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever has two wives and inclines toward one of them will come on the Day of Resurrection with one of his sides fallen."

This is likewise narrated by Imam Ahmad and the compilers of the Sunan, from the report of Hammam ibn Yahya, from Qatada, similarly. Al-Tirmidhi said: only Hammam attributed it as a marfu (elevated report), and Hisham al-Dastawa'i narrated it from Qatada, who said: "It used to be said..." We do not know this hadith as a marfu report except through Hammam's report.

Concerning His saying {And if you reconcile and are mindful of Allah, indeed Allah is ever Forgiving and Merciful}: that is, if you set your affairs right and divide fairly in what you possess, and you are mindful of Allah in all circumstances, Allah will forgive you for whatever inclination occurred toward some wives rather than others.

Then Allah, exalted is He, says: {And if they separate, Allah will enrich each of them from His abundance, and Allah is ever All Encompassing, Wise}. This is the third state, the state of separation. Allah, exalted is He, has informed that if the two separate, Allah will make him independent of her and make her independent of him, by compensating him with someone better for him than her, and compensating her with someone better for her than him: {and Allah is ever All Encompassing, Wise}, that is, vast in favor, tremendous in bestowal, wise in all His acts, decrees, and legislation.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَقُولُ تَعَالَى مُخْبِرًا وَمُشَرِّعًا عَنْ حَالِ الزَّوْجَيْنِ: تَارَةً فِي حَالِ نُفُورِ الرَّجُلِ عَنِ الْمَرْأَةِ، وَتَارَةً فِي حَالِ اتِّفَاقِهِ مَعَهَا، وَتَارَةً فِي حَالِ فِرَاقِهِ لَهَا.

فَالْحَالَةُ الْأُولَى: مَا إِذَا خَافَتِ الْمَرْأَةُ مِنْ زَوْجِهَا أَنْ يَنْفِرَ عَنْهَا، أَوْ يُعْرِضَ عَنْهَا، فَلَهَا أَنْ تُسْقِطَ حَقَّهَا أَوْ بَعْضَهُ، مِنْ نَفَقَةٍ أَوْ كُسْوَةٍ، أَوْ مَبِيتٍ، أَوْ غَيْرِ ذَلِكَ مِنَ الْحُقُوقِ عَلَيْهِ، وَلَهُ أَنْ يَقْبَلَ ذَلِكَ مِنْهَا فَلَا جَنَاحَ عَلَيْهَا فِي بَذْلِهَا ذَلِكَ لَهُ، وَلَا عَلَيْهِ فِي قَبُولِهِ مِنْهَا؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَلا جُنَاحَ عَلَيْهِمَا أَنْ يُصْلِحَا بَيْنَهُمَا صُلْحًا﴾ ثُمَّ قَالَ ﴿وَالصُّلْحُ خَيْرٌ﴾ أَيْ: مِنَ الْفِرَاقِ. وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَأُحْضِرَتِ الأنْفُسُ الشُّحَّ﴾ أَيِ الصُّلْحُ عِنْدَ المُشَاحَّة خَيْرٌ مِنَ الْفِرَاقِ؛ وَلِهَذَا لَمَّا كَبِرَتْ سَوْدَةُ بِنْتُ زَمْعَة عَزْمَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ عَلَى فِرَاقِهَا، فَصَالَحَتْهُ عَلَى أَنْ يُمْسِكَهَا، وَتَتْرُكَ يَوْمَهَا لِعَائِشَةَ، فَقَبِل ذَلِكَ مِنْهَا وَأَبْقَاهَا عَلَى ذَلِكَ.

ذِكْرُ الرِّوَايَةِ بِذَلِكَ:

قَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيُّ: حَدَّثَنَا سُلَيْمَانُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، عَنْ سِمَاك بْنِ حَرْبٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: خَشيت سَوْدَة أَنْ يُطَلِّقَهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ، فَقَالَتْ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، لَا تُطَلِّقْنِي وَاجْعَلْ يَوْمِي لِعَائِشَةَ. فَفَعَلَ، وَنَزَلَتْ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ: ﴿وَإِنِ امْرَأَةٌ خَافَتْ مِنْ بَعْلِهَا نُشُوزًا أَوْ إِعْرَاضًا﴾ الْآيَةُ، قَالَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ: فَمَا اصْطَلَحَا عَلَيْهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَهُوَ جَائِزٌ.

وَرَوَاهُ التِّرْمِذِيُّ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْمُثَنَّى، عَنْ أَبِي دَاوُدَ الطَّيَالِسِيِّ، بِهِ. وَقَالَ: حَسَنٌ غَرِيبٌ 

وَقَالَ الشَّافِعِيُّ أَخْبَرَنَا مُسْلِمٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْج، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وسلم تُوُفِّيَ عَنْ تِسْعِ نِسْوَةٍ، وَكَانَ يَقْسِمُ لِثَمَانٍ .

وَفِي الصَّحِيحَيْنِ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوة، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ قَالَتْ: لَمَّا كَبرْت سودةُ بنتُ زَمعة وهبَتْ يَوْمَهَا لِعَائِشَةَ، فَكَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَقْسِمُ لَهَا بِيَوْمِ سَوْدَةَ .

وَفِي صَحِيحِ الْبُخَارِيِّ، مِنْ حَدِيثِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ، نَحْوُهُ.

وَقَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ: أَنْبَأَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي الزِّناد، عَنْ هِشَامِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ عُرْوَةَ قَالَ: أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى فِي سَوْدَةَ وَأَشْبَاهِهَا: ﴿وَإِنِ امْرَأَةٌ خَافَتْ مِنْ بَعْلِهَا نُشُوزًا أَوْ إِعْرَاضًا﴾ وذلك أن سَوْدَةَ كَانَتِ امْرَأَةً قَدْ أَسَنَّتْ، فَفَزِعَتْ أَنْ يُفَارِقَهَا رسولُ اللَّهِ ﷺ، وضنَّت بِمَكَانِهَا مِنْهُ، وَعَرَفَتْ مِنْ حُبِّ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ عَائِشَةَ وَمَنْزِلَتِهَا مِنْهُ، فَوَهَبَتْ يَوْمَهَا مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ لِعَائِشَةَ، فَقَبِلَ ذَلِكَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ .

قَالَ الْبَيْهَقِيُّ: وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ أَحْمَدُ بْنُ يُونُسَ: عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي الزِّناد مَوْصُولًا. وَهَذِهِ الطَّرِيقُ رَوَاهَا الْحَاكِمُ فِي مُسْتَدْرَكِهِ فَقَالَ:
