يَنْهَى تَعَالَى عِبَادَهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَنْ اتِّخَاذِ الْكَافِرِينَ أَوْلِيَاءَ مِنْ دُونِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ، يَعْنِي مُصَاحَبَتَهُمْ وَمُصَادَقَتَهُمْ وَمُنَاصَحَتَهُمْ وَإِسْرَارَ الْمَوَدَّةِ إِلَيْهِمْ، وَإِفْشَاءَ أَحْوَالِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ الْبَاطِنَةِ إِلَيْهِمْ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿لَا يَتَّخِذِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ الْكَافِرِينَ أَوْلِيَاءَ مِنْ دُونِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَمَنْ يَفْعَلْ ذَلِكَ فَلَيْسَ مِنَ اللَّهِ فِي شَيْءٍ إِلا أَنْ تَتَّقُوا مِنْهُمْ تُقَاةً وَيُحَذِّرُكُمُ اللَّهُ نَفْسَهُ﴾ [آلِ عِمْرَانَ: ٢٨] أَيْ: يُحَذِّرُكُمْ عُقُوبَتَهُ فِي ارْتِكَابِكُمْ نَهْيَهُ. وَلِهَذَا قَالَ هَاهُنَا: ﴿أَتُرِيدُونَ أَنْ تَجْعَلُوا لِلَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ سُلْطَانًا مُبِينًا﴾ أَيْ: حُجَّةً عَلَيْكُمْ فِي عُقُوبَتِهِ إِيَّاكُمْ.

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا مَالِكُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ دِينَارٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿سُلْطَانًا مُبِينًا﴾ [قَالَ] كُلُّ سُلْطَانٍ فِي الْقُرْآنِ حُجَّةٌ.

وَهَذَا إِسْنَادٌ صَحِيحٌ. وَكَذَا قَالَ مُجَاهِدٌ، وَعِكْرِمَةُ، وَسَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ، وَمُحَمَّدُ بْنُ كَعْبٍ القُرَظي، وَالضَّحَّاكُ، وَالسُّدِّيُّ وَالنَّضْرُ بْنُ عَرَبي.

ثُمَّ أَخْبَرَ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ فِي الدَّرْكِ الأسْفَلِ مِنَ النَّارِ﴾ أَيْ: يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ، جَزَاءً عَلَى كُفْرِهِمُ الْغَلِيظِ. قَالَ الْوَالِبِيِّ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿فِي الدَّرْكِ الأسْفَلِ مِنَ النَّارِ﴾ أَيْ: فِي أَسْفَلِ النَّارِ. وَقَالَ غَيْرُهُ: النَّارُ دَرَكَاتٌ، كَمَا أَنَّ الْجَنَّةَ دَرَجَاتٌ. "وَقَالَ سُفْيَانُ الثَّوْرِيُّ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ ذَكْوان أَبِي صَالِحٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ: ﴿إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ فِي الدَّرْكِ الأسْفَلِ مِنَ النَّارِ﴾ قَالَ: فِي تَوَابِيتَ تُرْتَجُ عَلَيْهِمْ. كَذَا رَوَاهُ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ وَكِيع، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ يَمَانٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، بِهِ. وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ، عَنِ الْمُنْذِرِ بْنِ شَاذَانَ، عَنْ عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُوسَى، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي صَالِحٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ: ﴿إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ فِي الدَّرْكِ الأسْفَلِ مِنَ النَّارِ﴾ قَالَ: الدَّرْكُ الْأَسْفَلُ بُيُوتٌ لَهَا أَبْوَابٌ تُطْبَقُ عَلَيْهِمْ، فَتُوقَدُ مِنْ تَحْتِهِمْ وَمِنْ فَوْقِهِمْ.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ جَرِيرٍ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ بَشَّارٍ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ كُهَيْل، عَنْ خَيْثَمَة، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ -يَعْنِي ابْنَ مَسْعُودٍ: ﴿إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ فِي الدَّرْكِ الأسْفَلِ مِنَ النَّارِ﴾ قال: في توابيت مِنْ نَارٍ تُطْبِقُ عَلَيْهِمْ. وَرَوَاهُ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْأَشَجِّ، عَنْ وَكِيعٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ خَيْثَمَةَ، عَنِ ابن مسعود: ﴿إِنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ فِي الدَّرْكِ الأسْفَلِ مِنَ النَّارِ﴾ قَالَ: فِي تَوَابِيتَ مِنْ حَدِيدٍ مُبْهَمَةٍ عَلَيْهِمْ، وَمَعْنَى قَوْلِهِ: (مُبْهَمَةٍ) أَيْ: مُغْلَقَةٍ مُقْفَلَةٍ لَا يُهْتَدَى لِمَكَانِ فَتْحِهَا.

وَقَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو سَلَمَةَ، حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ سَلَمَةَ، أَخْبَرَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ: أَنَّ ابْنَ مَسْعُودٍ سُئِلَ عَنِ الْمُنَافِقِينَ، فَقَالَ: يُجْعَلُونَ فِي تَوَابِيتَ مِنْ نَارٍ، فَتُطْبَقُ عَلَيْهِمْ فِي أَسْفَلِ دَرَكٍ مِنَ النَّارِ.

﴿وَلَنْ تَجِدَ لَهُمْ نَصِيرًا﴾ أَيْ: يُنْقِذَهُمْ مِمَّا هُمْ فِيهِ، وَيُخْرِجَهُمْ مِنْ أَلِيمِ الْعَذَابِ.

ثُمَّ أَخْبَرَ تَعَالَى أَنَّ مَنْ تَابَ [مِنْهُمْ] فِي الدُّنْيَا تَابَ عَلَيْهِ وقَبِلَ نَدَمَهُ إِذَا أَخْلَصَ فِي تَوْبَتِهِ وَأَصْلَحَ عَمَلَهُ، وَاعْتَصَمَ بِرَبِّهِ فِي جَمِيعِ أَمْرِهِ، فَقَالَ: ﴿إِلا الَّذِينَ تَابُوا وَأَصْلَحُوا وَاعْتَصَمُوا بِاللَّهِ وَأَخْلَصُوا دِينَهُمْ لِلَّهِ﴾ أَيْ: بَدَّلوا الرِّيَاءَ بِالْإِخْلَاصِ، فَيَنْفَعُهُمُ الْعَمَلُ الصَّالِحُ وَإِنَّ قَلَّ.

قَالَ ابْنُ أَبِي حَاتِمٍ: أَخْبَرَنَا يُونُسُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى قِرَاءَةً، أَنْبَأَنَا ابْنُ وَهْبٍ، أَخْبَرَنِي يَحْيَى بْنُ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ زَحْر، عَنْ خَالِدِ بْنِ أَبِي عِمْران، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ، عَنْ مُعَاذِ بْنِ جَبَلٍ: أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "أَخْلِصْ دِينَكَ، يَكْفِكَ الْقَلِيلُ مِنَ الْعَمَلِ" .

﴿فَأُولَئِكَ مَعَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ﴾ أَيْ: فِي زُمْرَتِهِمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ﴿وَسَوْفَ يُؤْتِ اللَّهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَجْرًا عَظِيمًا﴾

ثُمَّ قَالَ مُخْبِرًا عَنْ غِنَاهُ عَمَّا سِوَاهُ، وَأَنَّهُ إِنَّمَا يُعَذِّبُ الْعِبَادَ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ، فَقَالَ: ﴿مَا يَفْعَلُ اللَّهُ بِعَذَابِكُمْ إِنْ شَكَرْتُمْ وَآمَنْتُمْ﴾ أَيْ: أَصْلَحْتُمِ الْعَمَلَ وَآمَنْتُمْ بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ، ﴿وَكَانَ اللَّهُ شَاكِرًا عَلِيمًا﴾ أَيْ: مَنْ شَكَرَ شَكَرَ لَهُ وَمَنْ آمَنَ قَلْبُهُ بِهِ عَلِمَهُ، وَجَازَاهُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ أَوْفَرَ الْجَزَاءِ.

---

## 4:148-149 — verses 148-149 (shared commentary)

### Translation

[Ali] ibn Abi Talhah said, on the authority of Ibn Abbas, regarding {God does not love the public utterance of evil speech}: he says, God does not love that anyone should invoke a curse upon another, except one who has been wronged, for he has been given license to invoke a curse upon the one who wronged him, and that is His saying {except one who has been wronged}, but if he bears it patiently, that is better for him.

Abu Dawud said: [chain] Aishah said: something was stolen from her, and she began to invoke curses upon the thief, so the Prophet ﷺ said, "Do not diminish his sin against him by cursing him."

Al-Hasan al-Basri said: he should not curse him, but rather should say, "O God, help me against him, and extract my right from him." In another report from him he said: he has been given license to invoke a curse upon the one who wronged him, provided he does not transgress against him.

Abd al-Karim ibn Malik al-Jazari said regarding this verse: it refers to a man who reviles you, so you revile him back, but if he slanders you, do not slander him in return, on account of His saying {And whoever defends himself after having been wronged, such ones, there is no cause of blame against them} [al-Shura: 41].

Abu Dawud said: [chain] Abu Hurayrah reported that the Messenger of God ﷺ said: "When two people revile each other, the burden of what they say falls upon the one who initiated it, so long as the wronged party does not transgress."

Abd al-Razzaq said: [chain] Mujahid said regarding His saying {God does not love the public utterance of evil speech, except from one who has been wronged}: a man was hosted by another man, but the host did not fulfill his duty of hospitality toward him. When the guest left, he informed people, saying, "I was hosted by so-and-so, but he did not fulfill his duty of hospitality toward me." That is the meaning of the public utterance of evil speech, except from one who has been wronged, since the other had not fulfilled his duty of hospitality toward him.

Muhammad ibn Ishaq said, on the authority of Ibn Abi Najih, from Mujahid, regarding {God does not love the public utterance of evil speech, except from one who has been wronged}: he said, this refers to a man who stays with another man, and the latter does not treat him well as a guest, so he leaves and says, "He treated me badly as a guest, and did not treat me well." In another report: it refers to the guest whose baggage has been moved elsewhere, for he then publicly voices evil speech about his host.

The like of this has been reported from more than one narrator on the authority of Mujahid. The group of hadith compilers, other than al-Nasa'i and al-Tirmidhi, transmitted, by way of al-Layth ibn Sa'd, and al-Tirmidhi by way of the hadith of Ibn Lahi'ah, both from Yazid ibn Abi Habib, from Abu al-Khayr Marthad ibn Abdullah, from Uqbah ibn Amir, who said: We said, "O Messenger of God, you send us out and we stay among a people who do not offer us hospitality; what do you think about that?" He said: "If you stay among a people and they order for you what is due to a guest, accept it from them, but if they do not do so, then take from them the guest's due that is rightfully theirs to give."

Imam Ahmad said: [chain] al-Miqdam Abu Karimah reported from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: "Whichever Muslim hosts a people, and the guest ends up deprived, it is a right upon every Muslim to support him until he takes his night's provision from the host's crops and wealth."

Ahmad alone transmitted this by this route. Ahmad also said: [chain] al-Miqdam Abu Karimah heard the Messenger of God ﷺ say: "A guest's night is obligatory upon every Muslim, and if he ends up at his courtyard deprived, it becomes a debt owed to him by the host, which he may choose to collect or to forgo."

Then he also transmitted it from Ghundar from Shu'bah, and from Ziyad ibn Abdullah al-Bakka'i, from Waki' and Abu Nu'aym, from Sufyan al-Thawri, all three from Mansur, with the same wording. Abu Dawud likewise transmitted it from the hadith of Abu Awanah, from Mansur, with the same wording.

On the basis of these hadiths and their like, Ahmad and others held the view that hospitality is obligatory. Of this same kind is the hadith transmitted by the hafiz Abu Bakr al-Bazzar.

[chain] Abu Hurayrah reported that a man came to the Prophet ﷺ and said, "I have a neighbor who harms me." He said to him, "Take out your belongings and place them in the road." The man took his belongings and threw them into the road, and everyone who passed by asked him, "What is the matter with you?" He said, "My neighbor harms me." So they said, "O God, curse him! O God, disgrace him!" He said: then the man said, "Go back to your house," and said, "I will never harm you again."

Abu Dawud transmitted it in the Book of Manners, [chain] from Muhammad ibn Ajlan, with the same wording.

Then al-Bazzar said: we do not know it to be transmitted from Abu Hurayrah except by this chain. It was also transmitted by Abu Juhayfah Wahb ibn Abdullah, from the Prophet ﷺ, and by Yusuf ibn Abdullah ibn Salam, from the Prophet ﷺ.

As for His saying, {If you disclose a good deed or conceal it, or pardon an evil, then indeed God is ever Pardoning, Powerful}, that is: if you, O people, manifest good, or conceal it, or pardon one who has wronged you, that is among the things that draw you nearer to God and multiply your reward with Him, for it is among His attributes, exalted is He, that He pardons His servants despite His power to punish them. Therefore He said, {Indeed God is ever Pardoning, Powerful}. It has therefore come in a report that the bearers of the Throne glorify God, and some of them say, "Glory be to You for Your forbearance after Your knowledge," and others say, "Glory be to You for Your pardon after Your power." And in the sound hadith: "Wealth does not decrease through charity, and God does not increase a servant in pardon except in honor, and whoever humbles himself for the sake of God, God raises him."

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

قَالَ [عَلِيُّ] بْنُ أَبِي طَلْحَةَ عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: ﴿لَا يُحِبُّ اللَّهُ الْجَهْرَ بِالسُّوءِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ﴾ يَقُولُ: لَا يُحِبُّ اللَّهُ أَنْ يَدْعُوَ أَحَدٌ عَلَى أَحَدٍ، إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ مَظْلُومًا، فَإِنَّهُ قَدْ أُرْخِصَ لَهُ أَنْ يَدْعُوَ عَلَى مَنْ ظَلَمَهُ، وَذَلِكَ قَوْلُهُ: ﴿إِلا مَنْ ظُلِمَ﴾ وَإِنْ صَبَرَ فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَهُ.

وَقَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ: حَدَّثَنَا عُبَيْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، حَدَّثَنَا أَبِي، حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ، عَنْ حَبِيبٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ قَالَتْ: سُرق لَهَا شَيْءٌ، فَجَعَلَتْ تَدْعُو عَلَيْهِ، فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ ﷺ "لَا تُسَبّخي عنه" .

وَقَالَ الْحَسَنُ الْبَصْرِيُّ: لَا يَدْعُ عَلَيْهِ، وَلْيَقُلْ: اللَّهُمَّ أَعِنِّي عَلَيْهِ، وَاسْتَخْرِجْ حَقِّي مِنْهُ. وَفِي رِوَايَةٍ عَنْهُ قَالَ: قَدْ أُرْخِصَ لَهُ أَنْ يَدْعُوَ عَلَى مَنْ ظَلَمَهُ مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يَعْتَدِيَ عَلَيْهِ.

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الْكَرِيمِ بْنُ مَالِكٍ الجَزَريّ فِي هَذِهِ الْآيَةِ: هُوَ الرَّجُلُ يَشْتُمُكَ فَتَشْتُمُهُ، وَلَكِنْ إِنِ افْتَرَى عَلَيْكَ فَلَا تَفْتَرِ عَلَيْهِ؛ لِقَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَلَمَنِ انْتَصَرَ بَعْدَ ظُلْمِهِ فَأُولَئِكَ مَا عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ سَبِيلٍ﴾ [الشُّورَى: ٤١] .

وَقَالَ أَبُو دَاوُدَ: حَدَّثَنَا القَعْنَبِيّ، حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْعَزِيزِ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنِ الْعَلَاءِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ؛ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ قَالَ: "المُسْتَبَّانِ مَا قَالَا فَعَلَى الْبَادِئِ مِنْهُمَا، مَا لَمْ يَعْتَدِ الْمَظْلُومُ" .

وَقَالَ عَبْدُ الرَّزَّاقِ: أَنْبَأَنَا الْمُثَنَّى بْنُ الصَّبَّاحِ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ فِي قَوْلِهِ: ﴿لَا يُحِبُّ اللَّهُ الْجَهْرَ بِالسُّوءِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ إِلا مَنْ ظُلِمَ﴾ قَالَ: ضَافَ رَجُلٌ رَجُلًا فَلَمْ يُؤَدِّ إِلَيْهِ حَقَّ ضِيَافَتِهِ، فَلَمَّا خَرَجَ أَخْبَرَ النَّاسَ، فَقَالَ: "ضِفْتُ فُلَانًا فَلَمْ يُؤَدِّ إِلَيَّ حَقَّ ضِيَافَتِي". فَذَلِكَ الْجَهْرُ بِالسُّوءِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ إِلَّا مِنْ ظُلِمَ، حِينَ لَمْ يُؤَدِّ الْآخَرُ إِلَيْهِ حَقَّ ضِيَافَتِهِ.

وَقَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيح، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ: ﴿لَا يُحِبُّ اللَّهُ الْجَهْرَ بِالسُّوءِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ إِلا مَنْ ظُلِمَ﴾ قَالَ: قَالَ هُوَ الرَّجُلُ يَنْزِلُ بِالرَّجُلِ فَلَا يُحْسِنُ ضِيَافَتَهُ، فَيَخْرُجُ فَيَقُولُ: "أَسَاءَ ضِيَافَتِي، وَلَمْ يُحْسِنْ". وَفِي رِوَايَةٍ هُوَ الضَّيْفُ الْمُحَوَّلُ رحلُه، فَإِنَّهُ يَجْهَرُ لِصَاحِبِهِ بِالسُّوءِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ.

وَكَذَا رُوِيَ عَنْ غَيْرِ وَاحِدٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، نَحْوُ هَذَا. وَقَدْ رَوَى الْجَمَاعَةُ سِوَى النَّسَائِيِّ وَالتِّرْمِذِيِّ، مِنْ طَرِيقِ اللَّيْثِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ -وَالتِّرْمِذِيِّ مِنْ حَدِيثِ ابْنِ لَهِيعة-كِلَاهُمَا عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي حَبِيبٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الْخَيْرِ مَرْثَد بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ عُقْبَةَ بْنِ عَامِرٍ قَالَ: قُلْنَا يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنَّكَ تَبْعَثُنَا فَنَنْزِلُ بِقَوْمٍ فَلَا يَقْرُونا، فَمَا تَرَى فِي ذَلِكَ؟ قَالَ: "إِذَا نَزَلْتُمْ بِقَوْمٍ فأمَرُوا لَكُمْ بِمَا يَنْبَغِي لِلضَّيْفِ، فَاقْبَلُوا مِنْهُمْ، وَإِنْ لَمْ يَفْعَلُوا فَخُذُوا مِنْهُمْ حَقَّ الضَّيْفِ الَّذِي يَنْبَغِي لَهُمْ" .

وَقَالَ الْإِمَامُ أَحْمَدُ: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، حدثنا شعبة، سَمِعْتُ أَبَا الْجُودِيِّ يُحَدِّثُ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُهَاجِرِ، عَنِ الْمِقْدَامِ أَبِي كَرِيمَةَ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: "أَيُّمَا مسلمٍ ضَافَ قَوْمًا، فَأَصْبَحَ الضَّيْفُ مَحْرُومًا، فَإِنَّ حَقًا عَلَى كُلِّ مُسْلِمٍ نَصْرَه حَتَّى يَأْخُذَ بقِرى لَيْلَتِهِ مِنْ زَرْعِهِ وَمَالِهِ".

تَفَرَّدَ بِهِ أَحْمَدُ مِنْ هَذَا الْوَجْهِ وَقَالَ أَحْمَدُ أَيْضًا: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، حَدَّثَنَا شُعْبَةُ، حَدَّثَنِي مَنْصُورٌ، عَنِ الشَّعْبي عَنِ الْمِقْدَامِ أَبِي كَرِيمَةَ، سَمِعَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ يَقُولُ: "لَيْلَةُ الضَّيْفِ وَاجِبَةٌ عَلَى كُلِّ مُسْلِمٍ، فَإِنْ أَصْبَحَ بفِنَائه مَحْرُومًا كَانَ دَيْنًا لَهُ عَلَيْهِ، إِنْ شَاءَ اقْتَضَاهُ وَإِنْ شَاءَ تَرَكَهُ".

ثُمَّ رَوَاهُ أَيْضًا عَنْ غُنْدَر عَنْ شُعْبَةَ. وَعَنْ زِيَادِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ البكَّائي. عَنْ وَكِيع، وأبي نُعَيْم، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ الثَّوْرِيِّ -ثَلَاثَتُهُمْ عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، بِهِ. وَكَذَا رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ مِنْ حَدِيثِ أَبِي عَوَانة، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، بِهِ .

وَمِنْ هَذِهِ الْأَحَادِيثِ وَأَمْثَالِهَا ذَهَبَ أَحْمَدُ وَغَيْرُهُ إِلَى وُجُوبِ الضِّيَافَةِ، وَمِنْ هَذَا الْقَبِيلِ الْحَدِيثُ الذِي رَوَاهُ الْحَافِظُ أَبُو بَكْرٍ البزَّار.

حَدَّثَنَا عَمْرُو بْنُ عَلِيٍّ، حَدَّثَنَا صَفْوَانُ بْنُ عِيسَى، حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَجْلان، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ؛ أَنَّ رَجُلًا أَتَى النَّبِيَّ ﷺ فَقَالَ: إِنْ لِي جَارًا يُؤْذِينِي، فَقَالَ لَهُ: "أَخْرِجْ مَتَاعَكَ فَضَعْهُ عَلَى الطَّرِيقِ". فَأَخَذَ الرَّجُلُ مَتَاعَهُ فَطَرَحَهُ عَلَى الطَّرِيقِ، فَجَعَلَ كُلُّ مَنْ مَرَّ بِهِ قَالَ: مَالَكَ؟ قَالَ: جَارِي يُؤْذِينِي. فَيَقُولُ: اللَّهُمَّ الْعَنْهُ، اللَّهُمَّ أَخْزِهِ! قَالَ: فَقَالَ الرَّجُلُ: ارْجِعْ إِلَى مَنْزِلِكَ، وَقَالَ لَا أُوذِيكَ أَبَدًا".

وَقَدْ رَوَاهُ أَبُو دَاوُدَ فِي كِتَابِ الْأَدَبِ، عَنْ أَبِي تَوْبَةَ الرَّبِيعِ بْنِ نَافِعٍ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ حَيَّانَ أَبِي خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرِ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَجْلَانَ بِهِ .

ثُمَّ قَالَ الْبَزَّارُ: لَا نَعْلَمُهُ يُرْوَى عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ إِلَّا بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ، وَرَوَاهُ أَبُو جُحَيفة وَهْبُ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ وَيُوسُفُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سَلَامٍ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ ﷺ .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿إِنْ تُبْدُوا خَيْرًا أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ أَوْ تَعْفُوا عَنْ سُوءٍ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ عَفُوًّا قَدِيرًا﴾ أَيْ: إِنْ تُظْهِرُوا -أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ-خَيْرًا، أَوْ أَخْفَيْتُمُوهُ، أَوْ عَفَوْتُمْ عَمَّنْ أَسَاءَ إِلَيْكُمْ، فَإِنَّ ذَلِكَ مِمَّا يُقَرِّبُكُمْ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ وَيُجْزِلُ ثَوَابَكُمْ لَدَيْهِ، فَإِنَّ مِنْ صِفَاتِهِ تَعَالَى أَنْ يَعْفُوَ عَنْ عِبَادِهِ مَعَ قُدْرَتِهِ عَلَى عِقَابِهِمْ. وَلِهَذَا قَالَ: ﴿إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ عَفُوًّا قَدِيرًا﴾ ؛ وَلِهَذَا وَرَدَ فِي الْأَثَرِ: أَنَّ حَمَلَةَ الْعَرْشِ يُسَبِّحُونَ اللَّهَ، فَيَقُولُ بَعْضُهُمْ: سُبْحَانَكَ عَلَى حِلْمِكَ بَعْدَ عِلْمِكَ. وَيَقُولُ بَعْضُهُمْ: سُبْحَانَكَ عَلَى عَفْوِكَ بَعْدَ قُدْرَتِكَ. وَفِي الْحَدِيثِ الصَّحِيحِ: "مَا نَقَصَ مَالٌ مِنْ صَدَقَةٍ، وَلَا زَادَ اللَّهُ عَبْدًا بِعَفْوٍ إِلَّا عِزًّا، وَمَنْ تَوَاضَعَ لِلَّهِ رَفَعَهُ الله" .

---

## 4:150-152 — verses 150-152 (shared commentary)

### Translation

[Blessed and] Exalted, He threatens with punishment those who disbelieve in Him and in His messengers from among the Jews and the Christians, in that they distinguished between Allah and His messengers in matters of faith, believing in some prophets and disbelieving in others, out of mere whim and habit and what they found their fathers upon, not because of any proof that led them to this. For indeed there is no path for them to such a thing; rather it is by mere caprice and tribal partisanship. The Jews, upon them be the curses of Allah, believed in the prophets except Jesus and Muhammad (peace be upon them both), while the Christians believed in the prophets but disbelieved in the last and noblest of them, Muhammad ﷺ. The Samaritans do not believe in any prophet after Joshua, the successor of Moses son of Imran. As for the Magians, it is said that they used to believe in a prophet of theirs called Zoroaster, then they disbelieved in his law, so it was lifted from among them. And Allah knows best.

The point is that whoever disbelieves in one of the prophets has thereby disbelieved in all the prophets, for faith is obligatory in every prophet whom Allah sent to the people of the earth. So whoever rejects a prophet's prophethood out of envy, partisanship, or mere whim, it becomes clear that his faith in whichever prophets he does believe in is not a faith that is religiously valid; rather it stems from a personal motive, caprice, and partisanship. This is why the Exalted said: {Indeed, those who disbelieve in Allah and His messengers}, branding them as disbelievers in Allah and His messengers, {and wish to make a distinction between Allah and His messengers}, that is, in matters of faith, {and say, "We believe in some and disbelieve in others," and wish to take a path in between}, meaning a way and a course.

Then the Exalted informs about them, saying: {Those, they are the disbelievers in truth}, meaning their disbelief is confirmed without doubt even regarding the one whom they claim to believe in, because their belief is not religiously valid. For had they truly believed in him because he was a messenger of Allah, they would have believed in his counterpart, and in one whose proof was clearer and whose evidence was stronger than his, had they looked with proper consideration into his prophethood.

His saying: {And We have prepared for the disbelievers a humiliating punishment} means: just as they held in contempt those in whom they disbelieved, whether from their failure to consider what had come to them from Allah, and their turning away from it while devoting themselves to amassing the worthless goods of this world which they had no real need for, or from their disbelief in him after they had come to know of his prophethood, as many of the rabbis of the Jews used to do in the time of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. They envied him for the great prophethood Allah had granted him, opposed him, denied him, showed him enmity, and fought him. So Allah imposed upon them humiliation in this world joined to humiliation in the Hereafter: {And they were struck with humiliation and poverty, and they drew upon themselves wrath from Allah} [al-Baqarah: 61], in this world and in the Hereafter.

His saying: {And those who believe in Allah and His messengers and do not distinguish between any of them} refers to the community (ummah) of Muhammad ﷺ, for they believe in every book that Allah sent down and in every prophet that Allah raised up, as the Exalted said: {The Messenger has believed in what was revealed to him from his Lord, and the believers as well. All of them have believed in Allah [and His angels and His books and His messengers, saying, "We make no distinction between any of His messengers." And they say, "We hear and we obey. Grant us Your forgiveness, our Lord, and to You is the return"]} [al-Baqarah: 285].

Then the Exalted informs that He has prepared for them abundant recompense, magnificent reward, and beautiful bestowal, saying: {Those, He will grant them their rewards}, for what they believed regarding Allah and His messengers, {and Allah is ever Forgiving and Merciful}, meaning, of their sins, that is, if some of them had sins.

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

يَتَوَعَّدُ [تَبَارَكَ وَ] تَعَالَى الْكَافِرِينَ بِهِ وَبِرُسُلِهِ مِنَ الْيَهُودِ وَالنَّصَارَى، حَيْثُ فَرّقوا بَيْنَ اللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِ فِي الْإِيمَانِ، فَآمَنُوا بِبَعْضِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ وَكَفَرُوا بِبَعْضٍ، بِمُجَرَّدِ التَّشَهِّي وَالْعَادَةِ، وَمَا أَلْفَوْا عَلَيْهِ آبَاءَهُمْ، لَا عَنْ دَلِيلٍ قَادَهُمْ إِلَى ذَلِكَ، فَإِنَّهُ لَا سَبِيلَ لَهُمْ إِلَى ذَلِكَ بَلْ بِمُجَرَّدِ الْهَوَى وَالْعَصَبِيَّةِ. فَالْيَهُودُ -عَلَيْهِمْ لَعَائِنُ اللَّهِ-آمَنُوا بِالْأَنْبِيَاءِ إلا عيسى ومحمد عَلَيْهِمَا الصَّلَاةُ وَالسَّلَامُ، وَالنَّصَارَى آمَنُوا بِالْأَنْبِيَاءِ وَكَفَرُوا بِخَاتَمِهِمْ وَأَشْرَفِهِمْ مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ، وَالسَّامِرَةُ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِنَبِيٍّ بَعْدَ يُوشَعَ خَلِيفَةِ مُوسَى بْنِ عِمْرَانَ، وَالْمَجُوسُ يُقَالُ: إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِنَبِيٍّ لَهُمْ يُقَالُ لَهُ زَرَادِشْتُ، ثُمَّ كَفَرُوا بِشَرْعِهِ، فَرُفِعَ مِنْ بَيْنِ أَظْهُرِهِمْ، وَاللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ.

وَالْمَقْصُودُ أَنَّ مَنْ كَفَرَ بِنَبِيٍّ مِنَ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ، فَقَدْ كَفَرَ بِسَائِرِ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ، فَإِنَّ الْإِيمَانَ وَاجِبٌ بِكُلِّ نَبِيٍّ بَعَثَهُ اللَّهُ إِلَى أَهْلِ الْأَرْضِ، فَمَنْ رَدَّ نُبُوَّتَهُ لِلْحَسَدِ أَوِ الْعَصَبِيَّةِ أَوِ التَّشَهِّي تَبَيَّنَ أَنَّ إِيمَانَهُ بِمَنْ آمَنَ بِهِ مِنَ الْأَنْبِيَاءِ لَيْسَ إِيمَانًا شَرْعِيًّا، إِنَّمَا هُوَ عَنْ غَرَضٍ وَهَوًى وَعَصَبِيَّةٍ؛ وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْفُرُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِ﴾ فَوَسْمَهُمْ بِأَنَّهُمْ كُفَّارٌ بِاللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِ ﴿وَيُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يُفَرِّقُوا بَيْنَ اللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِ﴾ أَيْ: فِي الْإِيمَانِ ﴿وَيَقُولُونَ نُؤْمِنُ بِبَعْضٍ وَنَكْفُرُ بِبَعْضٍ وَيُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يَتَّخِذُوا بَيْنَ ذَلِكَ سَبِيلا﴾ أَيْ: طَرِيقًا وَمَسْلَكًا.

ثُمَّ أَخْبَرَ تَعَالَى عَنْهُمْ، فَقَالَ: ﴿أُولَئِكَ هُمُ الْكَافِرُونَ حَقًّا﴾ أَيْ: كُفْرُهُمْ مُحَقَّقٌ لَا مَحَالَةَ بِمَنِ ادَّعَوُا الْإِيمَانَ بِهِ؛ لِأَنَّهُ لَيْسَ شَرْعِيًّا، إِذْ لَوْ كَانُوا مُؤْمِنِينَ بِهِ لِكَوْنِهِ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ لَآمَنُوا بِنَظِيرِهِ، وَبِمَنْ هُوَ أَوْضَحُ دَلِيلًا وَأَقْوَى بُرْهَانًا مِنْهُ، لَوْ نَظَرُوا حَقَّ النَّظَرِ فِي نُبُوَّتِهِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَأَعْتَدْنَا لِلْكَافِرِينَ عَذَابًا مُهِينًا﴾ أَيْ: كَمَا اسْتَهَانُوا بِمَنْ كَفَرُوا بِهِ إِمَّا لِعَدَمِ نَظَرِهِمْ فِيمَا جَاءَهُمْ بِهِ مِنَ اللَّهِ، وَإِعْرَاضِهِمْ عَنْهُ وَإِقْبَالِهِمْ عَلَى جَمْعِ حُطَامِ الدُّنْيَا مِمَّا لَا ضَرُورَةَ بِهِمْ إِلَيْهِ، وَإِمَّا بِكُفْرِهِمْ بِهِ بَعْدَ عِلْمِهِمْ بِنُبُوَّتِهِ، كَمَا كَانَ يَفْعَلُهُ كَثِيرٌ مِنْ أَحْبَارِ الْيَهُودِ فِي زَمَانِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ﷺ حَيْثُ حَسَدُوهُ عَلَى مَا آتَاهُ اللَّهُ مِنَ النُّبُوَّةِ الْعَظِيمَةِ، وَخَالَفُوهُ وَكَذَّبُوهُ وَعَادُوهُ وَقَاتَلُوهُ، فَسَلَّطَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمُ الذُّلَّ الدُّنْيَوِيَّ الْمَوْصُولَ بِالذُّلِّ الْأُخْرَوِيِّ: ﴿وَضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الذِّلَّةُ وَالْمَسْكَنَةُ وَبَاءُوا بِغَضَبٍ مِنَ اللَّهِ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ٦١] فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ.

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ: ﴿وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِ وَلَمْ يُفَرِّقُوا بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِنْهُمْ﴾ يَعْنِي بِذَلِكَ: أُمَّةَ مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ، فَإِنَّهُمْ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِكُلِّ كِتَابٍ أَنْزَلَهُ اللَّهُ وَبِكُلِّ نَبِيٍّ بَعَثَهُ اللَّهُ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿آمَنَ الرَّسُولُ بِمَا أُنزلَ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ رَبِّهِ وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ [وَمَلائِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِنْ رُسُلِهِ وَقَالُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا غُفْرَانَكَ رَبَّنَا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ] ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ٢٨٥] .

ثُمَّ أَخْبَرَ تَعَالَى بِأَنَّهُ قَدْ أَعَدَّ لَهُمُ الْجَزَاءَ الْجَزِيلَ وَالثَّوَابَ الْجَلِيلَ وَالْعَطَاءَ الْجَمِيلَ، فَقَالَ: ﴿أُولَئِكَ سَوْفَ يُؤْتِيهِمْ أُجُورَهُمْ﴾ عَلَى مَا آمَنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِ ﴿وَكَانَ اللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَحِيمًا﴾ أَيْ: لِذُنُوبِهِمْ أَيْ: إِنْ كَانَ لبعضهم ذنوب.

---

## 4:153-154 — verses 153-154 (shared commentary)

### Translation

Muhammad ibn Ka'b al-Qurazi, al-Suddi, and Qatadah said: the Jews asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ to send down upon them a book from heaven, just as the Torah had been sent down upon Moses written out.

Ibn Jurayj said: they asked him to send down upon them written pages from Allah addressed to so-and-so and so-and-so and so-and-so, confirming him in what he had brought them. They said this only as a form of stubbornness, obstinacy, disbelief, and deviation, just as the disbelievers of Quraysh before them had asked for something similar, as is mentioned in Surat "Subhan": {And they said, "We will never believe in you until you make a spring gush forth for us from the earth"} [al-Isra: 90, 93] and the following verses. That is why Allah, Exalted is He, said: {They had already asked Moses for something greater than that, for they said, "Show us Allah plainly," and the thunderbolt seized them for their wrongdoing}, meaning: for their transgression, their aggression, their insolence, and their obstinacy. This is explained in Surat al-Baqarah where Allah, Exalted is He, says: {And when you said, "O Moses, we will never believe in you until we see Allah plainly," so the thunderbolt seized you while you were looking on. Then We revived you after your death so that you might be grateful} [al-Baqarah: 55, 56].

Allah, Exalted is He, said: {Then they took up the calf after clear proofs had come to them}, meaning: after they had seen the dazzling signs and overwhelming proofs at the hand of Moses (peace be upon him) in the land of Egypt, and after the destruction of the enemy of Allah, Pharaoh, and all his troops in the sea. They had scarcely gone beyond that when they came upon a people who were devoted to idols of theirs, and they said to Moses, {"Make for us a god just as they have gods."} [He said, "Indeed you are a people who are ignorant. Indeed, that which these people are engaged in is destined for ruin, and what they have been doing is false] [al-A'raf: 138, 139]. Allah, Exalted is He, then relates the story of their taking up the calf at length in Surat al-A'raf, and in Surat "Ta Ha," after Moses had gone to converse with Allah, Mighty and Majestic is He. Then when he returned and what happened happened, Allah made their repentance for what they had made and innovated consist of this: that those among them who had not worshipped the calf should kill those who had worshipped it. So they began killing one another, then Allah, Mighty and Majestic is He, brought them back to life. Allah, Mighty and Majestic is He, said: {So We pardoned that, and We gave Moses clear authority.}

Then Allah, Exalted is He, said: {And We raised the mount above them by their covenant}, and that was when they refused to commit themselves to the rulings of the Torah, and refusal appeared among them regarding what Moses (peace be upon him) had brought them. Allah raised a mountain over their heads, and then they were compelled, so they committed themselves and prostrated, and they kept looking up above their heads for fear that it would fall upon them, as Allah, Exalted is He, said: {And when We shook the mountain above them as if it were a canopy, and they thought it was going to fall upon them. Take what We have given you with determination [and remember what is in it, so that you may be mindful]} [al-A'raf: 171].

{And We said to them, "Enter the gate prostrating,"} meaning: they contravened what they had been commanded in both word and deed. For they had been commanded to enter the gate of Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem) prostrating, while saying: hittah, meaning: "O Allah, remove from us our sins for our having abandoned struggle and shrunk back from it," until we wandered lost in the wilderness for forty years. Instead, they entered crawling on their buttocks, saying: "hintah fi sha'rah" (wheat in a hair, a mocking distortion of the word).

{And We said to them, "Do not transgress in the matter of the Sabbath,"} meaning: We charged them to keep the Sabbath and to hold to what Allah had forbidden them, for as long as it remained legislated for them. {And We took from them a solemn covenant}, meaning: a firm one. But they contravened it, disobeyed, and devised stratagems to commit what Allah, Mighty and Majestic is He, had forbidden, as is set out at length in Surat al-A'raf at the words: {And ask them about the town that was by the sea, [when they transgressed in the matter of the Sabbath]} [al-A'raf: 163-166] and the following verses. The hadith of Safwan ibn 'Assal will come in Surat "Subhan" at the words: {And We had certainly given Moses nine clear signs} [al-Isra: 101], in which it is said: "and upon you, O Jews specifically, is that you not transgress in the matter of the Sabbath."

### Source (Arabic) — review against this

قَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ كَعْبٍ الْقُرَظِيُّ، وَالسُّدِّيُّ، وَقَتَادَةُ: سَأَلَ الْيَهُودُ رسولَ اللَّهِ ﷺ أَنْ يُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْهِمْ كِتَابًا مِنَ السَّمَاءِ. كَمَا نَزَلَتِ التَّوْرَاةُ عَلَى مُوسَى مَكْتُوبَةً.

قَالَ ابْنُ جُرَيج: سَأَلُوهُ أَنْ يُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْهِمْ صُحُفًا مِنَ اللَّهِ مَكْتُوبَةً إِلَى فُلَانٍ وَفُلَانٍ وَفُلَانٍ، بِتَصْدِيقِهِ فِيمَا جَاءَهُمْ بِهِ. وَهَذَا إِنَّمَا قَالُوهُ عَلَى سَبِيلِ التَّعَنُّتِ وَالْعِنَادِ وَالْكُفْرِ وَالْإِلْحَادِ، كَمَا سَأَلَ كفارُ قُرَيْشٍ قَبْلَهُمْ نَظِيرَ ذَلِكَ، كَمَا هُوَ مَذْكُورٌ فِي سُورَةِ "سُبْحَانَ": ﴿وَقَالُوا لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّى تَفْجُرَ لَنَا مِنَ الأرْضِ يَنْبُوعًا﴾ [الْإِسْرَاءِ: ٩٠، ٩٣] الْآيَاتِ. وَلِهَذَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿فَقَدْ سَأَلُوا مُوسَى أَكْبَرَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ فَقَالُوا أَرِنَا اللَّهَ جَهْرَةً فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الصَّاعِقَةُ بِظُلْمِهِمْ﴾ أَيْ: بِطُغْيَانِهِمْ وَبَغْيِهِمْ، وَعُتُوِّهِمْ وَعِنَادِهِمْ. وَهَذَا مُفَسَّرٌ فِي سُورَةِ "الْبَقَرَةِ" حَيْثُ يَقُولُ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَإِذْ قُلْتُمْ يَا مُوسَى لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّى نَرَى اللَّهَ جَهْرَةً فَأَخَذَتْكُمُ الصَّاعِقَةُ وَأَنْتُمْ تَنْظُرُونَ. ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَاكُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَوْتِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ﴾ [الْبَقَرَةِ: ٥٥، ٥٦] .

* *

وَقَوْلُهُ تَعَالَى: ﴿ثُمَّ اتَّخَذُوا الْعِجْلَ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَتْهُمُ الْبَيِّنَاتُ﴾ أَيْ: مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا رَأَوْا مِنَ الْآيَاتِ الْبَاهِرَةِ وَالْأَدِلَّةِ الْقَاهِرَةِ عَلَى يَدِ مُوسَى، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، فِي بِلَادِ مِصْرَ وَمَا كَانَ مِنْ إِهْلَاكِ عَدُوِّ اللَّهِ فِرْعَوْنَ وَجَمِيعِ جُنُودِهِ فِي الْيَمِّ، فَمَا جَاوَزُوهُ إِلَّا يَسِيرًا حَتَّى أَتَوْا عَلَى قَوْمٍ يَعْكُفُونَ عَلَى أَصْنَامٍ لَهُمْ، فَقَالُوا لِمُوسَى ﴿اجْعَلْ لَنَا إِلَهًا كَمَا لَهُمْ آلِهَةٌ [قَالَ إِنَّكُمْ قَوْمٌ تَجْهَلُونَ. إِنَّ هَؤُلاءِ مُتَبَّرٌ مَا هُمْ فِيهِ وَبَاطِلٌ مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ] ﴾ [الْأَعْرَافِ: ١٣٨، ١٣٩] . ثُمَّ ذَكَرَ تَعَالَى قِصَّةَ اتِّخَاذِهِمُ الْعِجْلَ مَبْسُوطَةً فِي سُورَةِ "الْأَعْرَافِ"، وَفِي سُورَةِ "طَهَ" بَعْدَ ذَهَابِ مُوسَى إِلَى مُنَاجَاةِ اللَّهِ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، ثُمَّ لَمَّا رَجَعَ وَكَانَ مَا كَانَ، جَعَلَ اللَّهُ تَوْبَتَهُمْ مِنَ الَّذِي صَنَعُوهُ وَابْتَدَعُوهُ: أَنْ يقتُلَ مَنْ لَمْ يَعْبُدِ الْعِجْلَ مِنْهُمْ مَنْ عَبَدَهُ، فَجَعَلَ يَقْتُلُ بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا ثُمَّ أَحْيَاهُمُ اللَّهُ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، فَقَالَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ ﴿فَعَفَوْنَا عَنْ ذَلِكَ وَآتَيْنَا مُوسَى سُلْطَانًا مُبِينًا﴾

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَرَفَعْنَا فَوْقَهُمُ الطُّورَ بِمِيثَاقِهِمْ﴾ وَذَلِكَ حِينَ امْتَنَعُوا مِنَ الِالْتِزَامِ بِأَحْكَامِ التَّوْرَاةِ، وَظَهَرَ مِنْهُمْ إِبَاءٌ عَمَّا جَاءَهُمْ بِهِ مُوسَى، عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ، وَرَفَعَ اللَّهُ عَلَى رُؤُوسِهِمْ جَبَلًا ثُمَّ أُلْزِمُوا فَالْتَزَمُوا وَسَجَدُوا، وَجَعَلُوا يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَى فَوْقِ رُؤُوسِهِمْ خَشْيَةَ أَنْ يَسْقُطَ عَلَيْهِمْ، كَمَا قَالَ تَعَالَى: ﴿وَإِذْ نَتَقْنَا الْجَبَلَ فَوْقَهُمْ كَأَنَّهُ ظُلَّةٌ وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُ وَاقِعٌ بِهِمْ خُذُوا مَا آتَيْنَاكُمْ بِقُوَّةٍ [وَاذْكُرُوا مَا فِيهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ] ﴾ [الأعراف: ١٧١] .

﴿وَقُلْنَا لَهُمُ ادْخُلُوا الْبَابَ سُجَّدًا﴾ أَيْ: فَخَالَفُوا مَا أُمِرُوا بِهِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ وَالْفِعْلِ، فَإِنَّهُمْ أُمِرُوا أَنْ يَدْخُلُوا بَابَ بَيْتِ الْقُدْسِ سُجَّدًا، وَهُمْ يَقُولُونَ: حِطَّةٌ. أَيْ: اللَّهُمَّ حُطَّ عَنَّا ذُنُوبَنَا فِي تَرْكِنَا الْجِهَادَ وَنُكُولِنَا عَنْهُ، حَتَّى تُهْنَا فِي التِّيهِ أَرْبَعِينَ سَنَةً. فَدَخَلُوا يَزْحَفُونَ عَلَى اسْتَاهِهِمْ، وَهُمْ يَقُولُونَ: حِنْطَةٌ فِي شَعْرَةٍ.

﴿وَقُلْنَا لَهُمْ لَا تَعْدُوا فِي السَّبْتِ﴾ أَيْ: وُصَّيْنَاهُمْ بِحِفْظِ السَّبْتِ وَالْتِزَامِ مَا حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ، مَا دَامَ مَشْرُوعًا لَهُمْ ﴿وَأَخَذْنَا مِنْهُمْ مِيثَاقًا غَلِيظًا﴾ أَيْ: شَدِيدًا، فَخَالَفُوا وعَصَوْا وَتَحَيَّلُوا عَلَى ارْتِكَابِ مَنَاهِي اللَّهِ، عَزَّ وَجَلَّ، كَمَا هُوَ مَبْسُوطٌ فِي سُورَةِ الْأَعْرَافِ عِنْدَ قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَاسْأَلْهُمْ عَنِ الْقَرْيَةِ الَّتِي كَانَتْ حَاضِرَةَ الْبَحْرِ [إِذْ يَعْدُونَ فِي السَّبْتِ] ﴾ [الْأَعْرَافِ: ١٦٣-١٦٦] الْآيَاتِ، وَسَيَأْتِي حَدِيثُ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ عَسَّالٍ، فِي سُورَةِ "سُبْحَانَ" عِنْدَ قَوْلِهِ: ﴿وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا مُوسَى تِسْعَ آيَاتٍ بَيِّنَاتٍ﴾ [الْإِسْرَاءِ: ١٠١] ، وفيه: "وعليكم -خاصة يهود-أن لا تعدوا في السبت".

---

## 4:155-159 — verses 155-159 (shared commentary)

### Translation

This is one of the sins they committed, among the things that necessitated their being cursed, driven away, and distanced from guidance: their breaking of the covenants and pledges that had been taken from them, and their disbelief in the signs of God, that is, His proofs and evidences, and the miracles which they witnessed at the hands of the prophets (peace be upon them).

His saying, {and their killing of the prophets without right}: this was due to the abundance of their crimes and their boldness against the prophets of God, for they killed a great multitude of the prophets [without right] (peace be upon them).

As for their saying, {our hearts are ghulf}: Ibn Abbas, Mujahid, Sa'id ibn Jubayr, Ikrimah, al-Suddi, Qatadah, and others said it means "covered." This is like the statement of the polytheists: {and they said, our hearts are within coverings from that to which you call us, [and in our ears is deafness, and between us and you is a veil, so work; indeed we are working]} [Fussilat: 5]. It has also been said that its meaning is that they claimed their hearts were vessels (ghulf) for knowledge, that is, receptacles for knowledge which had contained and attained it. This was narrated by al-Kalbi, from Abu Salih, from Ibn Abbas. Its like has already been discussed in Surat al-Baqarah.

God Most High says: {rather, God has sealed them because of their disbelief}. According to the first interpretation, it is as if they were excusing themselves to Him by claiming that their hearts could not comprehend what He said, because they were covered and veiled. God says: rather, they are sealed because of their disbelief. According to the second interpretation, He turned back upon them, in every respect, what they had claimed. Discussion of the like of this has already preceded in Surat al-Baqarah.

{So they do not believe, except a few}: that is, their hearts became hardened in disbelief, transgression, and scant faith.

{And for their disbelief and their saying against Mary a great slander}: Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn Abbas: "meaning that they accused her of fornication." Al-Suddi, Juwaybir, Muhammad ibn Ishaq, and others said likewise. This is evident from the verse: that they accused her and her son of enormities, making her out to be an adulteress who had conceived her child from that, some added: while she was menstruating. Upon them be the successive curses of God until the Day of Resurrection.

As for their saying, {Indeed we killed the Messiah, Jesus son of Mary, the Messenger of God}: that is, this one who claims for himself this rank, we killed him. This on their part is a form of mockery and derision, like the saying of the polytheists: {O you upon whom the Reminder has been sent down, indeed you are mad} [al-Hijr: 6].

Now, part of the account of the Jews, upon them the curses of God and His wrath, anger, and punishment, is that when God sent Jesus son of Mary with clear proofs and guidance, they envied him for the prophethood and dazzling miracles God had granted him, by which he healed the blind and the leper and raised the dead by God's permission, and fashioned from clay a bird, then breathed into it so that it became a bird whose flight was witnessed by God's permission, mighty and majestic is He, along with other miracles with which God honored him and which He caused to occur at his hands. Despite this, they disbelieved him, opposed him, and strove to harm him by every means possible, until the Prophet of God Jesus (peace be upon him) would not dwell with them in any town, but instead traveled much, he and his mother (peace be upon them both). Yet even this did not satisfy them, until they went to the king of Damascus of that time, who was a polytheist man from among the star-worshippers, and the people of his religion were called the Greeks, and they reported to him: that in Jerusalem there was a man who was seducing the people, leading them astray, and corrupting the king's subjects against him. The king became angry at this, and wrote to his deputy in Jerusalem, ordering him to seize this man, crucify him, place thorns upon his head, and put a stop to his harm to the people. When the letter arrived, the governor of Jerusalem complied, and he and a group of Jews went to the house in which Jesus (peace be upon him) was, he being with a group of his companions, twelve or thirteen, and it is also said seventeen persons. This was on Friday after the afternoon prayer, the eve of Saturday, and they besieged him there. When he perceived them and realized that there was no escape from either their entering upon him or his going out to them, he said to his companions: "Which of you will have my likeness cast upon him, and be my companion in Paradise?" A young man among them volunteered for this, but it seemed he was considered too young for it, so Jesus repeated the offer a second and third time, and each time only that same young man volunteered. So he said: "You are the one." God then cast upon him the likeness of Jesus, until he appeared to be Jesus himself. A skylight was opened in the roof of the house, and Jesus (peace be upon him) was overtaken by a drowsiness, and he was raised up to heaven in that state, as God Most High said: {when God said, O Jesus, indeed I will take you and raise you to Me, [and purify you from those who disbelieved]} the verse [Al Imran: 55].

When he was raised up, those men came out, and when they saw that young man they thought he was Jesus. So they seized him at night and crucified him, and placed thorns upon his head. The Jews then proclaimed that they had brought about his crucifixion and boasted of it, and groups among the Christians, out of their ignorance and lack of understanding, accepted this from them, except for those who had been in the house with the Messiah, for they had witnessed his being raised up. As for the rest, they thought, just as the Jews thought, that the one crucified was the Messiah son of Mary, to the point that they mention that Mary sat beneath that crucified man and wept, and it is said that he spoke to her. God knows best.

All of this was a trial from God for His servants, for in it there is His far-reaching wisdom, and God has made the matter plain, clarified it, explained it, and made it manifest in the Mighty Qur'an, which He sent down upon His noble Messenger, who was supported with miracles, clear proofs, and evident signs. God Most High says, and He is the most truthful of speakers, the Lord of the worlds, the One who is aware of secrets and inmost thoughts, who knows what is secret in the heavens and the earth, the Knower of what has been and what will be, and what has not been, how it would be if it were: {and they did not kill him, nor did they crucify him, but it was made to appear so to them}, that is, they saw his likeness and thought it was him. This is why He said: {and indeed those who differed concerning him are in doubt about it, they have no knowledge of it except the following of assumption, [and they did not kill him for certain, rather God raised him to Himself]}, meaning by this: those among the Jews who claimed to have killed him, and those among the ignorant Christians who accepted this claim, all of them are in doubt, confusion, misguidance, and bewilderment about this. This is why He said: {and they did not kill him for certain}, that is, they did not kill him with certainty that it was truly him, but rather while doubting and merely supposing. {Rather, God raised him to Himself, and God is Ever Mighty, Ever Wise}, that is, of impregnable dignity, whose sanctuary cannot be violated, and whoever takes refuge at His door will not be wronged. {Wise}, that is, in all that He decrees and ordains of the matters He creates, and His is the far-reaching wisdom, the decisive proof, the mighty authority, and the eternal command.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] Ahmad ibn Sinan told us, Abu Mu'awiyah told us, from al-A'mash, from al-Minhal ibn Amr, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn Abbas, who said: when God intended to raise Jesus up to heaven, he went out to his companions, and there were twelve men of the disciples in the house, meaning he came out to them from a spring in the house, with his head dripping water, and he said: "Among you is one who will disbelieve in me twelve times, after having believed in me." Then he said: "Which of you will have my likeness cast upon him, and be killed in my place, and be with me in my rank?" A young man, the youngest of them in age, stood up, and he said to him: "Sit down." He repeated it to them again, and that same young man stood up, and he said: "Sit down." He repeated it to them a third time, and the young man stood up and said: "I will." So he said: "You are that one." So the likeness of Jesus was cast upon him, and Jesus was raised up from a skylight in the house to heaven. He said: and the pursuit came from the Jews, and they seized the likeness and killed him, then crucified him, and some of them disbelieved in him twelve times after having believed in him. And they split into three factions. One faction said: "God was among us as He willed, then He ascended to heaven," and these are the Jacobites (Ya'qubiyyah). Another faction said: "The son of God was among us as He willed, then God raised him to Himself," and these are the Nestorians. And another faction said: "The servant of God and His Messenger was among us as He willed, then God raised him to Himself," and these are the Muslims. Then the two disbelieving factions joined together against the believing faction, and killed it, so that Islam remained obscured until God sent Muhammad ﷺ.

This is a sound (sahih) chain of transmission going back to Ibn Abbas. Al-Nasa'i also related it from Abu Kurayb, from Abu Mu'awiyah, in similar wording. Likewise, more than one of the Salaf mentioned that he said to them: "Which of you will have my likeness cast upon him, so that he is killed in my place, and he will be my companion in Paradise?"

Ibn Jarir said: Ibn Humayd narrated to us, Ya'qub al-Qummi narrated to us, from Harun ibn Antarah, from Wahb ibn Munabbih, who said: Isa was approached while he was with seventeen of the disciples in a house, and they were surrounded. When they entered upon him, God, mighty and exalted, made all of them appear in the form of Isa. So they said to them: "You have bewitched us. Either Isa comes out to us, or we will kill all of you." Isa said to his companions: "Who among you will sell his soul today for Paradise?" A man among them said: "I will." So he went out to them and said: "I am Isa," for God had shaped him into the form of Isa. They seized him, killed him, and crucified him. From that, the likeness was cast upon them, and they thought they had killed Isa, and the Christians likewise supposed that it was Isa. God raised Isa up on that very day. This is a very strange (gharib) account.

Ibn Jarir said: A similar report has been related from Wahb, which is what al-Muthanna narrated to me, Ishaq narrated to us, Isma'il ibn Abd al-Karim narrated to us, Abd al-Samad ibn Mi'qal narrated to me, that he heard Wahb say: When God informed Isa son of Maryam that he was going to depart from this world, he was distressed by death and it weighed heavily upon him. So he called the disciples and prepared food for them, saying: "Be present with me tonight, for I have a need of you." When they gathered to him at night, he served them dinner and stood to serve them himself. When they finished eating, he began to wash their hands and perform ablution for them with his own hand, and wipe their hands with his garment. They found this exceedingly burdensome and disliked it. So he said: "Whoever rejects anything I do tonight is not of me, nor am I of him." So they accepted it. When he finished with that, he said: "As for what I have done for you tonight, in serving you at the meal and washing your hands with my own hand, let it be an example for you to follow. You regard me as the best among you, so let none of you exalt himself over another, and let each of you give of himself for the sake of another, just as I have given myself for you. As for my need tonight, in which I seek your help, it is that you pray to God for me, and strive earnestly in supplication that my appointed time be delayed."

When they set themselves to pray and intended to strive earnestly, sleep overtook them so that they were unable to make supplication. He kept waking them, saying: "Glory be to God! Can you not endure one night with me, helping me in it?" They said: "By God, we do not know what is wrong with us. We used to stay up in conversation at night and prolong it greatly, yet tonight we cannot bear even to converse, and whenever we wish to pray, something comes between us and it." He said: "The shepherd will be taken away and the sheep will be scattered," and he continued speaking in this manner, foretelling his own departure.

Then he said: "Truly, one of you will disbelieve in me before the rooster crows three times, and one of you will sell me for a paltry sum of dirhams and consume its price." Then they went out and dispersed. The Jews were seeking him, and they seized Sham'un, one of the disciples, saying: "This is one of his companions." He denied it, saying: "I am not his companion," so they let him go. Then others seized him, and he denied it likewise. Then he heard the crowing of a rooster, and he wept and was grieved.

When morning came, one of the disciples went to the Jews and said: "What will you give me if I show you where the Messiah is?" They agreed on thirty dirhams for him. He took it and showed them where he was. But the likeness had already been cast upon them before that. They seized him, bound him securely, tied him with a rope, and led him along saying to him: "You used to bring the dead to life, rebuke the devil, and cure the insane, so why do you not save yourself from this rope?" They spat upon him and threw thorns at him, until they brought him to the wooden structure upon which they intended to crucify him. God raised him up to Himself, and they crucified the one upon whom the likeness had been cast. He remained there for seven days.

Then his mother, and the woman whom Isa (peace be upon him) used to treat, whom God had cured of madness, came weeping to the place of the crucified one. Isa came to them and said: "Why are you weeping?" They said: "For you." He said: "God has indeed raised me up to Himself, and nothing has befallen me but good. This one was made to resemble me for them. So instruct the disciples to meet me at such and such a place." Eleven of them went to meet him at that place. They found that the one who had sold him and shown the Jews where he was, was missing. He asked his companions about him, and they said: "He regretted what he had done and strangled himself, killing himself." He said: "Had he repented, God would have accepted his repentance."

Then he asked them about a young boy who had almost followed them, called Yahya. He said: "He is with you. Go forth, for tomorrow every person will speak in the language of his own people, so let him warn them and then leave them." This is a very strange (gharib) account.

Then Ibn Jarir said: Ibn Humayd narrated to us, Salamah narrated to us, from Ibn Ishaq, who said: The name of the king of the Children of Israel who was sent to kill Isa was a man among them called Dawud. When they resolved upon this against him, no servant of God, as it was related to me, found death more terrible than he did, nor was more anguished by it, nor prayed more fervently to God to turn it away from him, to the point that he would say, as they claim: "O God, if You are going to turn this cup away from anyone among Your creation, then turn it away from me." And to the point that his skin, from the anguish of that, would ooze with blood.

He entered the place where they had agreed to come upon him to kill him, he and his companions, who together with Isa (peace be upon him) numbered thirteen. When he became certain that they were about to come in upon him, he said to his companions among the disciples, who were twelve men: Futrus, Ya'qub ibn Zabdi, Yuhannas the brother of Ya'qub, Andarabis, Filibs, Abrathulma, Mina, Tumas, Ya'qub ibn Halfiya, Tadawusis, Qathaniya, and Yudas Zakariyya Yuta.

Ibn Humayd said: Salamah said, Ibn Ishaq said: Among them, as was related to me, was a man named Sirjis, so they numbered thirteen men besides Isa (peace be upon him), whom the Christians deny, because he is the one upon whom the likeness of Isa (peace be upon him) was cast in place of Isa for the Jews. He said: I do not know which one it was, whether he was one of these twelve, or whether he was a thirteenth. They denied him when they affirmed to the Jews that Isa had been crucified, and disbelieved in what Muhammad ﷺ brought concerning this matter. So if they were thirteen, then when they entered the place, together with Isa they were fourteen; and if they were twelve, then when they entered the place they were thirteen.

Ibn Ishaq said: A man who had been a Christian and then embraced Islam narrated to me that when there came to Isa from God {Indeed, I will take you and raise you to Myself}, he said: "O company of disciples, which of you would love to be my companion in Paradise, on condition that the likeness be cast upon him in my form for the people, so that they kill him in my place?" Sirjis said: "I will, O Spirit of God." He said: "Then sit in my seat." So he sat in it, and Isa (peace be upon him) was raised up. They came in upon him, seized him, and crucified him. So it was he whom they crucified, and the likeness was cast upon them through him. Their number, when they had entered along with Isa, was known, for they had seen them and counted their number. When they entered to seize him, they found, as they supposed, Isa and his companions, but they were missing one man from the count. It was he over whom they later disputed. They did not know Isa by sight, so they arranged with Yudas Zakariyya Yuta thirty dirhams on condition that he show them and identify him for them. He told them: "When you come in upon him, I will kiss him, and whomever I kiss, seize him." So when they entered, Isa had already been raised up, and they saw Sirjis in the form of Isa, so it did not seem doubtful to them that he was Isa. He bent over him and kissed him, so they seized him and crucified him.

Then Yudas Zakariyya Yuta regretted what he had done, and strangled himself with a rope until he killed himself. He is cursed among the Christians, though he had been counted as one of his companions. Some Christians claim that it was Yudas Zakariyya Yuta himself upon whom the likeness was cast, and that they crucified him while he was saying: "I am not your man. I am the one who showed you where he was." God knows best which of these it was.

Ibn Jarir related, from Mujahid: They crucified a man whom they made to resemble Isa, and God, mighty and exalted, raised Isa up to heaven alive.

Ibn Jarir preferred the view that the likeness of Isa was cast upon all of his companions.

The word of the Most High: {And there is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him before his death, and on the Day of Resurrection he will be a witness against them.}

Ibn Jarir said: The people of interpretation differed concerning the meaning of this. Some of them said: the meaning of it is: {And there is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him} meaning in Jesus, {before his death} meaning before the death of Jesus. This is directed toward the view that all of them will affirm him when he descends to kill the Dajjal (the Antichrist), so that all the religious communities become one, namely the religion of Islam, the Hanifiyyah (the pure monotheistic way), the religion of Abraham (peace be upon him).

Mention of those who said this:

[chain] Ibn Bashshar, from 'Abd al-Rahman, from Sufyan, from Abu Husayn, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas: {And there is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him before his death} he said: before the death of Jesus son of Mary. And al-'Awfi related the like of this from Ibn 'Abbas.

And Abu Malik said concerning His saying: {but will surely believe in him before his death}, he said: that is at the descent of Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon him). No one among the People of the Book will remain except that he believes in him.

And al-Dahhak said, from Ibn 'Abbas: {And there is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him before his death} meaning: the Jews specifically. And al-Hasan al-Basri said: meaning the Negus and his companions. Ibn Abi Hatim related both of these.

Ibn Jarir said: [chain] Ya'qub, from Ibn 'Ulayyah, from Abu Raja', from al-Hasan: {And there is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him before his death} he said: before the death of Jesus. By God, he is now alive with God, but when he descends they will all believe in him.

Ibn Abi Hatim said: [chain] my father, from 'Ali ibn 'Uthman al-Lahiqi, from Juwayriyah ibn Bishr who said: I heard a man say to al-Hasan: O Abu Sa'id, concerning the saying of God [Mighty and Majestic]: {And there is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him before his death}, he said: "Before the death of Jesus. God raised Jesus up to Himself, and He will send him forth before the Day of Resurrection to a station in which the righteous and the wicked alike will believe in him."

And Qatadah and 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Zayd ibn Aslam and others besides them said likewise. This view is the truth, as we shall clarify afterward with a decisive proof, God willing. In Him is trust, and upon Him is reliance.

Ibn Jarir said: Others said: the meaning of this is: {And there is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him} before the death of the person of the Book. Mention of those who directed this toward the idea that when a person witnesses (death directly) he comes to know the truth from falsehood, because everyone whom death comes upon does not have his soul depart until the truth becomes clear to him from falsehood regarding his religion.

'Ali ibn Abi Talhah said, from Ibn 'Abbas concerning His saying: {And there is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him before his death}, he said: No Jew dies until he believes in Jesus.

[chain] al-Muthanna, from Abu Hudhayfah, from Shibl, from Ibn Abi Najih, from Mujahid concerning His saying: {but will surely believe in him before his death}: every person of the Book believes in Jesus before his death, meaning before the death of the person of the Book. And Ibn 'Abbas said: Even if you struck off his neck, his soul would not depart until he believed in Jesus.

[chain] Ibn Humayd, from Abu Numaylah Yahya ibn Wadih, from Husayn ibn Waqid, from Yazid al-Nahwi, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas, who said: No Jew dies until he testifies that Jesus is the servant of God and His messenger, even if a weapon were hastened against him.

[chain] Ishaq ibn Ibrahim ibn Habib ibn al-Shahid, from 'Attab ibn Bashir, from Khusayf, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn 'Abbas: {And there is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him before his death}, he said: it is in the recitation of Ubayy: {before their death}. No Jew ever dies until he believes in Jesus. It was said to Ibn 'Abbas: What do you think if he falls from the top of a house? He said: He speaks it while falling. Then it was said: What do you think if the neck of one of them is struck? He said: His tongue stammers it out.

And likewise Sufyan al-Thawri related from Khasif, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas: {And there is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him before his death}, he said: No Jew dies until he believes in Jesus (peace be upon him), and if he is struck with the sword he speaks it, he said: and if he falls he speaks it as he is falling.

And likewise Abu Dawud al-Tayalisi related, from Shu'bah, from Abu Harun al-Ghanawi, from 'Ikrimah, from Ibn 'Abbas. All of these are sound chains going back to Ibn 'Abbas, and likewise it is authentically established from Mujahid, 'Ikrimah, and Muhammad ibn Sirin. Al-Dahhak, Juwaybir, and al-Suddi hold this view, and it was related from Ibn 'Abbas, who also transmitted the recitation of Ubayy ibn Ka'b: "before their death."

'Abd al-Razzaq said, from Isra'il, from Furat al-Qazzaz, from al-Hasan concerning His saying: {but will surely believe in him before his death}, he said: none of them dies until he believes in Jesus before he dies.

It is possible that al-Hasan's intent here is what was mentioned from him previously, and it is possible that his intent is what these others intended.

Ibn Jarir said: Others said: the meaning of this is: there is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in Muhammad ﷺ before the death of the person of the Book.

Mention of those who said this:

[chain] Ibn al-Muthanna, from al-Hajjaj ibn Minhal, from Hammad, from Humayd, who said: 'Ikrimah said: Neither the Christian nor the Jew dies until he believes in Muhammad ﷺ, concerning His saying: {And there is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him before his death}.

Then Ibn Jarir said: The most fitting of these views for correctness is the first view, which is that no one among the People of the Book will remain after the descent of Jesus (peace be upon him) except that he will believe in him before his death, that is, before the death of Jesus (peace be upon him). There is no doubt that what Ibn Jarir, may God have mercy on him, said is correct, because it is what is intended by the context of the verses in establishing the falsity of what the Jews claimed regarding the killing and crucifixion of Jesus, and the acceptance of that by the ignorant among the Christians who conceded it to them. God informed that the matter was not so, but rather it was made to appear similar to them, so they killed the one who resembled him without realizing it. Then God raised him up to Himself, and he remains alive, and he will descend before the Day of Resurrection, as the mutawatir (mass-transmitted) hadiths indicate, which we shall present soon, God willing. He will kill the false messiah, break the cross, kill the swine, and abolish the jizyah, meaning he will not accept it from anyone among the People of the religions, but will accept nothing but Islam or the sword. So this noble verse informs that all the People of the Book will believe in him at that time, and not a single one of them will fail to affirm him. This is why He said: {And there is none among the People of the Book but will surely believe in him before his death}, that is, before the death of Jesus, whom the Jews and those Christians who agreed with them claimed had been killed and crucified.

{And on the Day of Resurrection he will be a witness against them} meaning: concerning their deeds which he witnessed from them before his being raised up to heaven and after his descent to earth. As for those who interpreted this verse to mean that every person of the Book does not die until he believes in Jesus or in Muhammad, upon them both be peace, this is indeed what actually occurs, for everyone at the moment of death has revealed to him what he was ignorant of, and he believes in it. But this is not a faith that benefits him, since he has already witnessed the angel, as God Most High said at the beginning of this surah: {And repentance is not for those who do evil deeds until, when death comes to one of them, he says, "Indeed, I repent now," nor for those who die while they are disbelievers} the verse [al-Nisa: 18]. And God Most High said: {But when they saw Our punishment, they said, "We believe in God alone and disbelieve in that which we used to associate with Him." But their faith did not benefit them when they saw Our punishment} the two verses [Ghafir: 84-85]. This indicates the weakness of what Ibn Jarir used as an argument in rejecting this view, where he said: If this were the intended meaning of this verse, then everyone who believed in Muhammad or in the Messiah, from among those who had disbelieved in them, would be considered as being upon their religion, and in that case his relatives from among the people of his own religion would not inherit from him, because the Truthful One has informed that he believes in him before his death. This is not sound, since it does not follow from his believing in a state in which his belief does not benefit him that he thereby becomes a Muslim. Do you not see the statement of Ibn 'Abbas: "Even if he fell from a height, or was struck with a sword, or was devoured by a predator, he would inevitably believe in Jesus." Faith in such circumstances is not beneficial, and it does not remove its possessor from his disbelief, for the reasons we have already presented. And God knows best.

Whoever examines this well and looks carefully will see clearly that this, although it is indeed what has occurred, does not necessarily mean that this is what is meant by this verse. Rather, what is meant by it is what we have mentioned: the establishment of the existence of Jesus (peace be upon him), the continuation of his life in the heavens, and that he will descend to the earth before the Day of Resurrection, in order to refute both these and those, namely the Jews and the Christians, whose statements about him diverged, contradicted one another, ran counter to one another, and departed from the truth. These Jews fell short and those Christians went to excess: the Jews belittled him through the terrible things they attributed to him and to his mother, while the Christians exaggerated him to the point of claiming for him what is not true of him, raising him, in opposition to the Jews, from the station of prophethood to the station of divinity. God is exalted far above what both these and those say, exalted greatly; He is transcendent and holy, there is no god but He.

Mention of the hadiths reported concerning the descent of Jesus son of Mary from heaven to earth at the end of time before the Day of Resurrection, and that he will call to the worship of God alone, having no partner:

Al-Bukhari, may God have mercy on him, said in the Book of the Mention of the Prophets in his Sahih, which has been received with acceptance, under the heading "The Descent of Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon him)": [chain] Ishaq ibn Ibrahim, Ya'qub ibn Ibrahim, his father, from Salih, from Ibn Shihab, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said, "By the One in whose hand is my soul, the son of Mary will soon descend among you as a just judge. He will break the cross, kill the pig, abolish the jizyah, and wealth will overflow until no one accepts it, so that a single prostration will be better than this world and all it contains." Abu Hurayrah would then say: "Recite, if you wish: {And there is none among the People of the Book but will believe in him before his death, and on the Day of Resurrection he will be a witness against them}."

Muslim likewise narrated it from al-Hasan al-Hulwani and 'Abd ibn Humayd, both from Ya'qub, with the same chain. Al-Bukhari and Muslim also transmitted it from the hadith of Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah, from al-Zuhri, with the same chain, and both transmitted it also through al-Layth from al-Zuhri with the same chain. Ibn Mardawayh narrated it through Muhammad ibn Abi Hafsah, from al-Zuhri, from Sa'id ibn al-Musayyab, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said, "The son of Mary will soon be among you as a just judge; he will kill the Dajjal, kill the pig, break the cross, abolish the jizyah, and wealth will overflow, and prostration will be one, for God, Lord of the worlds." Abu Hurayrah said: "Recite, if you wish: {And there is none among the People of the Book but will believe in him before} the death of Jesus son of Mary," and Abu Hurayrah repeated this three times.

Another chain from Abu Hurayrah: Imam Ahmad said: [chain] Rawh, Muhammad ibn Abi Hafsah, from al-Zuhri, from Hanzalah ibn 'Ali al-Aslami, from Abu Hurayrah, that the Messenger of God ﷺ said, "Jesus son of Mary will surely enter the state of consecration for hajj or 'umrah at Fajj al-Rawha', or he will surely combine both."

Muslim likewise narrated it alone from the hadith of Sufyan ibn 'Uyaynah, al-Layth ibn Sa'd, and Yunus ibn Yazid, all three from al-Zuhri with the same chain.

Ahmad said: [chain] Yazid, Sufyan (that is, ibn Husayn), from al-Zuhri, from Hanzalah, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said, "Jesus son of Mary will descend and kill the pig, erase the cross, prayer will be gathered together for him, he will give away wealth until it is no longer accepted, he will abolish the land tax, and he will descend at al-Rawha', from where he will perform hajj or 'umrah or combine both." He said: Abu Hurayrah then recited: {And there is none among the People of the Book but will believe in him before his death, [and on the Day of Resurrection he will be a witness against them]}. Hanzalah claimed that Abu Hurayrah said "he will believe in him before the death of Jesus," so I do not know whether all of this is the hadith of the Prophet ﷺ or something Abu Hurayrah himself said.

Ibn Abi Hatim likewise narrated it from his father, from Abu Musa Muhammad ibn al-Muthanna, from Yazid ibn Harun, from Sufyan ibn Husayn, from al-Zuhri, with the same chain.

Another chain: Al-Bukhari said: [chain] Ibn Bukayr, al-Layth, from Yunus, from Ibn Shihab, from Nafi', the freedman of Abu Qatadah al-Ansari, that Abu Hurayrah said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said, "How will you be when the Messiah son of Mary descends among you while your leader is one of your own?" 'Uqayl and al-Awza'i corroborated him.

Imam Ahmad likewise narrated it from 'Abd al-Razzaq, from Ma'mar, and from 'Uthman ibn 'Umar, from Ibn Abi Dhi'b, both from al-Zuhri with the same chain. Muslim transmitted it from the reports of Yunus, al-Awza'i, and Ibn Abi Dhi'b, with the same chain.

Another chain: Imam Ahmad said: [chain] 'Affan, Hammam, Qatadah, from 'Abd al-Rahman, from Abu Hurayrah, that the Prophet ﷺ said, "The prophets are brothers from different mothers with one religion. I am the closest of people to Jesus son of Mary, for there was no prophet between him and myself. He will indeed descend, so when you see him, know him: a man of medium height, reddish-white in complexion, wearing two garments dyed with saffron, as though his head were dripping although no moisture has touched it. He will crush the cross, kill the pig, abolish the jizyah, and call people to Islam. God will destroy in his time all religions except Islam, and God will destroy the false Messiah, the Dajjal, in his time. Then security will settle upon the earth, so that lions will graze with camels, leopards with cattle, and wolves with sheep, and children will play with snakes without being harmed. He will remain forty years, then he will die, and the Muslims will perform the funeral prayer over him."

Abu Dawud likewise narrated it from Hudbah ibn Khalid, from Hammam ibn Yahya. Ibn Jarir narrated it, and cited no other report under this verse, from Bishr ibn Mu'adh, from Yazid ibn Harun, from Sa'id ibn Abi 'Arubah, both from Qatadah, from 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Adam, the freedman of Umm Burthun, the keeper of the water supply, from Abu Hurayrah, from the Prophet ﷺ, mentioning something similar, and he said: "So the people will fight for Islam."

Al-Bukhari also narrated from Abu al-Yaman, from Shu'ayb, from al-Zuhri, from Abu Salamah, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: I heard the Messenger of God ﷺ say, "I am the closest of people to Jesus son of Mary, and the prophets are children of different mothers; there is no prophet between him and myself."

He then narrated from Muhammad ibn Sinan, from Fulayh ibn Sulayman, from Hilal ibn 'Ali, from 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi 'Amrah, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said, "I am the closest of people to Jesus son of Mary in this world and the next, and the prophets are brothers from different mothers, their mothers being various while their religion is one." Ibrahim ibn Tahman said, from Musa ibn 'Uqbah, from Safwan ibn Sulaym, from 'Ata' ibn Yasar, from Abu Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of God ﷺ said...

Another hadith: Muslim said in his Sahih: [chain] Zuhayr ibn Harb, Mu'alla ibn Mansur, Sulayman ibn Bilal, Suhayl, from his father, from Abu Hurayrah, that the Messenger of God ﷺ said, "The Hour will not come until the Romans land at al-A'maq, or at Dabiq. An army from Medina, consisting of the best people of the earth at that time, will go out to meet them. When they line up in ranks, the Romans will say: 'Leave us and those of our people whom you have taken captive, that we may fight them.' The Muslims will say: 'No, by God, we will not leave you and our brothers.' So they will fight them, and a third of the Muslim army will be routed, and God will never forgive them; a third will be killed, the best of martyrs before God, may He be exalted; and a third will achieve victory, never to be tempted again, and they will conquer Constantinople. Then, while they are dividing the spoils, having hung their swords on olive trees, Satan will cry out among them: 'The Messiah has taken your place among your families!' So they will set out, but that will be false. When they reach Syria, he will appear. Then, while they are preparing for battle, straightening their ranks, the call to prayer will be given, and Jesus son of Mary will descend and lead them in prayer. When the enemy of God sees him, he will melt as salt melts in water. If he were left, he would go on melting until he perished, but God will kill him by His own hand, and He will show them his blood upon his spear."

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Hushaym related to us, from al-Awwam ibn Hawshab, from Jabala ibn Suhaym, from Muthir ibn Afaza, from Ibn Mas'ud, from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, who said: "On the night I was taken on the Night Journey, I met Ibrahim, Musa, and Isa (peace be upon them). They discussed the matter of the Hour among themselves, and referred the matter to Ibrahim. He said: I have no knowledge of it. They referred the matter to Musa. He said: I have no knowledge of it. They referred the matter to Isa, and he said: As for its coming to pass, no one knows of it except Allah. But among what my Lord, Mighty and Majestic, entrusted to me is that the Dajjal will emerge." He said: "And I will have with me two staffs, and when he sees me he will melt as lead melts." He said: "Allah will destroy him when he sees me, so much so that even the stone and the tree will say: O Muslim, there is a disbeliever beneath me, so come and kill him. He said: Allah will destroy them, then the people will return to their lands and homelands. At that point Ya'juj and Ma'juj will emerge, {and they will come rushing down from every mound}, and they will trample their lands, coming upon nothing without destroying it, and passing by no water without drinking it. He said: Then the people will come back to me complaining of them, and I will call upon Allah against them, and He will destroy them and cause them to die, until the earth becomes fetid from the stench of their decay. Allah will send down rain, which will sweep away their bodies until we cast them into the sea. Among what my Lord, Mighty and Majestic, entrusted to me is that when that occurs, the Hour will be like a pregnant woman at full term: her family does not know when she will suddenly deliver, by night or by day."

Ibn Majah related it, from Muhammad ibn Bashshar, from Yazid ibn Harun, from al-Awwam ibn Hawshab, with similar wording.

Another hadith: Imam Ahmad said: Yazid ibn Harun related to us, Hammad ibn Salama informed us, from Ali ibn Zayd, from Abu Nadra, who said: We came to Uthman ibn Abi al-As on a Friday, to present to him a copy of our Qur'an alongside his copy. When the time for the Friday prayer arrived, he ordered us, so we performed ritual washing, then we brought perfume and scented ourselves, then we came to the mosque and sat with a man, who related to us about the Dajjal. Then Uthman ibn Abi al-As arrived, so we rose to greet him, and we sat down, and he said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "There will be for the Muslims three great cities: a city at the meeting point of the two seas, a city at al-Hira, and a city in al-Sham. The people will be struck with terror three times. The Dajjal will emerge among the outskirts of the people, and he will be driven back from the direction of the east. The first city he will come to is the city at the meeting point of the two seas, and its people will split into three factions: a faction that will stay, saying, 'Let us associate with him and see what he is'; a faction that will join the bedouin; and a faction that will join the city next to them. With the Dajjal will be seventy thousand wearing striped garments, and most of those with him will be Jews and women. Then he will come to the city next to it, and its people will split into three factions: a faction that says, 'Let us associate with him and see what he is'; a faction that will join the bedouin; and a faction that will join the city next to them in the west of al-Sham. The Muslims will withdraw to the pass of Afiq, and they will send out a grazing herd of theirs, which will be struck, and this will be very hard on them, and a severe famine and hardship will afflict them, such that one of them will burn the string of his bow and eat it. While they are in that state, a caller will call out at dawn: 'O people, help has come to you,' three times. Some of them will say to each other: This is the voice of a man who is well fed. Then Isa son of Mary (peace be upon him) will descend at the time of the dawn prayer, and their commander will say to him: Spirit of Allah, step forward and lead the prayer. He will say: This community has its own commanders, one over another. So their commander will step forward and lead the prayer. When he finishes his prayer, Isa will take his spear and go toward the Dajjal. When the Dajjal sees him, he will melt as lead melts. Isa will place his spear between his shoulder blades and kill him, and his companions will be routed. On that day nothing will be able to conceal anyone, so much so that the tree will say: O believer, here is a disbeliever. And the stone will say: O believer, here is a disbeliever."

Ahmad alone related it in this manner.

Another hadith: Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Yazid ibn Majah said in his well known Sunan: Ali ibn Muhammad related to us, Abd al-Rahman al-Muharibi related to us, from Isma'il ibn Rafi' Abu Rafi', from Abu Zur'a al-Shaybani Yahya ibn Abi Amr, from Abu Umama al-Bahili, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ delivered a sermon to us, and most of his sermon was a discourse he related to us about the Dajjal, warning us against him. Among what he said was:

"There has never been a trial on earth, since Allah created the offspring of Adam (peace be upon him), greater than the trial of the Dajjal. Allah never sent a prophet without him warning his community against the Dajjal. I am the last of the prophets, and you are the last of the communities, and he will inevitably emerge among you. If he emerges while I am still among you, then I will be the one to argue on behalf of every Muslim, and if he emerges after me, then every person will be his own advocate, and Allah is my successor over every Muslim. He will emerge from a gap between al-Sham and Iraq, and he will cause corruption to the right and cause corruption to the left."

"Beware, O servants of Allah, O people, so stand firm. I will describe him to you with a description that no prophet before me has described. He will begin by saying: I am a prophet, but there is no prophet after me. Then he will repeat himself and say: I am your lord, but you will not see your Lord until you die. He is one eyed, and your Lord, Mighty and Majestic, is not one eyed. Written between his eyes will be: disbeliever, which every believer will read, whether literate or illiterate. Among his trials is that he will have with him a paradise and a fire, but his fire is a paradise and his paradise is a fire. Whoever is tried by his fire, let him seek refuge with Allah and recite the opening verses of Surat al-Kahf, and it will become for him coolness and safety, just as the fire was for Ibrahim (peace be upon him). Among his trials is that he will say to a bedouin: What do you think, if I bring back to life your father and your mother, will you testify that I am your lord? He will say: Yes. So two devils will appear to him in the form of his father and mother, and they will say: O my son, follow him, for he is your lord. Among his trials is that he will be given power over a single soul, and he will kill him and saw him in two with a saw, until he is cast down in two halves, then he will say: Look at this servant of mine, I will now bring him back to life, then he will claim that he has a lord other than me. Allah will bring him back to life, and the wicked one will say to him: Who is your lord? He will say: My Lord is Allah, and you are the enemy of Allah, the Dajjal. By Allah, I have never had clearer insight into you than I have today."

Abu Hasan al-Tanafisi said: al-Muharibi related to us, Ubaydullah ibn al-Walid al-Wassafi related to us, from Atiyya, from Abu Sa'id, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "That man will have the highest rank in Paradise among my community."

He said: Abu Sa'id said: By Allah, we thought that man was none other than Umar ibn al-Khattab, until he passed away.

Al-Muharibi said: Then we returned to the hadith of Abu Rafi', who said: "Among his trials is that he will command the sky to rain, and it will rain, and he will command the earth to bring forth vegetation, and it will bring it forth. And among his trials is that he will pass by a settlement and they will disbelieve him, so none of their grazing livestock will remain except that it perishes.
